- Goal: FathomDB 0.8.3 Slice 15a is a $0, CPU-only, offline embedder-ceiling probe. It A/Bs a base

  pass function it must NOT redefine: src/python/eval/decision_rule_083.py::probe_15a_pass (+ EU7_FLOOR).
- The design under review: dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md (on this branch).
- Frozen pre-registration: dev/design/0.8.3-mem0-parity.md §5; the rule module decision_rule_083.py.
- Existing IR-eval references the design reuses: dev/design/ir-recall-measure.md (eu8 = doc-id recall
  reduction), src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/support/ir_eval.rs (evidence_recall_at_k),
  src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/ir_c_gold_diagnostics.rs (the bucket()/hard-subset def),
  data/corpus-data/eval/ir_gold/all.gold.json (qrels, corpus_hash fe973fcd), tests/corpus/snapshot.json.

QUESTIONS to answer explicitly (one verdict each: OK / CONCERN / BLOCK + why):
1. Does the design's eu8 recall@10 definition (strict all-of doc-id reduction over
   expected_top_k_doc_ids, negatives excluded) faithfully match ir_eval.rs::evidence_recall_at_k and
   ir-recall-measure.md? Any divergence that would make the probe's eu8 number non-comparable?
2. Is the HARD subset defined in a genuinely MODEL-INDEPENDENT way (lexical-BM25-miss set), so the
   A/B across embedders is fair? Does relying on bm25_rank (not dense_rank) correctly avoid the
   circularity in ir_c_gold_diagnostics.rs::bucket() (which mixes bm25 AND dense rank)? Is the "~596
   governs but reproduce-actual" honesty rule sound?
3. Is projected_eu7 (1-bit sign-quant -> Hamming K=192 -> f32 rerank, recall@10 vs the model's OWN
   f32-exact top-10) the CORRECT eu7 ANN-fidelity proxy per eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs / ADR-0.7.0, and
   is it right that it must be measured PER candidate embedder?
4. Is the paired bootstrap margin-CI method (resample query indices; mean(cand_hit-base_hit); 95%
   percentile lower bound) the right gated quantity, and does it match how probe_15a_pass consumes
   eu8_margin_ci_lo/hard_margin_ci_lo? Any way the harness could accidentally gate on a raw-point
   check (which Slice-0 fix-1 explicitly removed)?
5. Pooling/prefix table (bge CLS + "Represent this sentence..."; gte CLS?; e5 mean + query:/passage:;
   nomic mean + search_query:/search_document:) — any per-model error that would invalidate a candidate?
6. Any way this probe could produce a MISLEADING verdict (false pass / false "no swap"), or violate
   the consume-don't-redefine discipline, the $0/CPU/no-GPU/no-engine footprint, or determinism?

Output a tight bulleted findings list with severities. No code edits.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: FathomDB 0.8.3 — Slice-15a design (embedder-ceiling probe, $0 / CPU / EVAL-ONLY)
     3	status: decision-ready
     4	date: 2026-06-22
     5	slice: 15a (PRIMARY $0 triage lever pick)
     6	plan: dev/plans/plan-0.8.3.md §4 Slice 15
     7	preregistration: dev/design/0.8.3-mem0-parity.md §5 (frozen) + src/python/eval/decision_rule_083.py
     8	consumes (does NOT redefine): probe_15a_pass(), EU7_FLOOR=0.90, decision_rule_083.py, the gold artifacts
     9	memory: [[embed-completeness-and-gpu-readiness]], [[0.8.3-0.8.4-competitor-parity-reframe]], [[priced-runs-need-resilience-before-spend]]
    10	---
    11	
    12	# 0.8.3 Slice-15a — embedder-ceiling probe (design + TDD contract)
    13	
    14	> **Orchestrator draft for implementer (TDD) + codex review.** This grounds the §5-frozen 15a
    15	> probe into an executable, deterministic, $0/CPU/offline measurement. The implementer makes it
    16	> executable + reviewable under TDD; codex reviews; the orchestrator gates. **The gate semantics
    17	> are frozen in `decision_rule_083.probe_15a_pass` — this slice CONSUMES that function and must not
    18	> reimplement or relax it.**
    19	
    20	## 1. Purpose & non-goals
    21	
    22	**Purpose.** Pick the Slice-20 embedder by measuring which CPU/ONNX-class embedder is projected to
    23	close the most Mem0 gap, subject to **CPU-feasibility** and **1-bit (Hamming) survivability**. This
    24	is the **PRIMARY** $0 lever (ADJ-6: the dense axis is embedder-bound; fused only *ties* BM25, eu8
    25	ceiling ≈0.571). FathomDB's FTS recall trails plain BM25 on every memory class
    26	(`runs/0.8.3-d0b-powered-recall.json` / corpus-adequacy note), so the embedder is the lever this
    27	probe sizes.
    28	
    29	**Non-goals / hard constraints (footprint invariant).**
    30	- **$0, LLM-free, NO airlock.** No gpt-5.4 / qwen / `localhost:4000`. Pure offline embedding+recall.
    31	- **CPU only — NO GPU.** The sibling D0b run owns the GPU. All embedding on CPU
    32	  (`CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=""`); CPU is the library footprint anyway.
    33	- **No engine / Rust change.** Pure-Python eval harness. The engine embedder swap is **Slice 20**.
    34	- **Do NOT modify** `src/python/eval/decision_rule_083.py` or the gold artifacts. Consume only.
    35	- **Do NOT merge to main.** Commit on `0.8.3-slice-15a`, write closure `output.json`, run codex §9,
    36	  STOP. The human/primary lands it after D0b.
    37	- Footprint tag: this entire slice is **OFFLINE-BUILD / EVAL-ONLY**.
    38	
    39	## 2. Frozen pass criterion (consumed verbatim from `probe_15a_pass`)
    40	
    41	A candidate **clears** iff ALL hold (design §5 / `decision_rule_083.probe_15a_pass`):
    42	1. `eu8_margin_ci_lo > 0` — paired (cand − base) eu8 recall@10 margin, 95% bootstrap CI **lower bound > 0**;
    43	2. `hard_margin_ci_lo > 0` — same for the hard-subset recall@10 margin;
    44	3. `cpu_feasible == True`;
    45	4. `projected_eu7 ≥ EU7_FLOOR (0.90)` (1-bit survivability).
    46	
    47	The harness builds `cand = {eu8, hard, eu8_margin_ci_lo, hard_margin_ci_lo, projected_eu7,
    48	cpu_feasible}` and `base = {eu8, hard}` per candidate and calls `probe_15a_pass(cand, base)`. It
    49	**must not** add extra gates or a raw-point check (the Slice-0 fix-1 review explicitly dropped
    50	raw-point gates; gate on the CI lower bound only).
    51	
    52	**Decision output (frozen gate semantics).** The Slice-20 embedder = the candidate that **passes**
    53	with the **largest headroom** (ranked by the binding margin = `min(eu8_margin_ci_lo,
    54	hard_margin_ci_lo)`, with projected_eu7 headroom as tiebreak); if **none** passes, the pick is the
    55	**CLS-corrected `bge-small`** — *no swap*. A clean negative (no candidate beats the corrected base at
    56	power) is reported as such, not papered over.
    57	
    58	## 3. Reference (base) and candidates
    59	
    60	**Base (reference) = CLS-corrected `bge-small-en-v1.5`.** The known Slice-20 bug is mean pooling for
    61	a CLS-trained BGE model; the reference here MUST use **CLS pooling** (`Pooling::Cls` in
    62	`fathomdb-embedder`'s `CandleBgeEmbedder`) + the BGE query prefix. Mirroring the wrong (mean)
    63	pooling would invalidate the entire A/B — the base must be the *corrected* model.
    64	
    65	**Candidates** (4, per plan §4 + one memory/retrieval-tuned of our choice):
    66	
    67	| name | HF id | dim | pooling | query prefix | passage prefix |
    68	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    69	| **base** bge-small | `BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5` | 384 | **CLS** | `Represent this sentence for searching relevant passages: ` | — |
    70	| bge-base | `BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5` | 768 | **CLS** | same BGE query prefix | — |
    71	| gte-base | `Alibaba-NLP/gte-base-en-v1.5` | 768 | **CLS**¹ | — | — |
    72	| e5-base-v2 | `intfloat/e5-base-v2` | 768 | **mean** | `query: ` | `passage: ` |
    73	| **nomic** (memory-tuned) | `nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5` | 768 | **mean** | `search_query: ` | `search_document: ` |
    74	
    75	¹ `gte-base-en-v1.5` (the `Alibaba-NLP/new-impl` family) is trained with **CLS** pooling; confirm
    76	against the model card at pin time. If the card specifies mean, use mean — a pooling error
    77	invalidates the A/B (see §9 determinism test). The exact correct pooling/prefix per model is a
    78	**RED-test fixture** (§8): a fixture catches a mean-vs-CLS or missing-prefix mistake.
    79	
    80	**Why nomic-embed-text-v1.5 as the memory/retrieval-tuned candidate.** It is explicitly
    81	retrieval/instruction-tuned (task-prefixed `search_query:`/`search_document:`), Matryoshka, 768-d,
    82	CPU-friendly, and **already has a first-class engine path** (`NomicEmbedder` referenced in
    83	`tests/ir_c_gold_diagnostics.rs`), so a Slice-20 adoption is low-friction. It is the strongest
    84	in-footprint "memory-tuned" option that does not require a larger-than-base model.
    85	
    86	All candidates are sentence-transformer/ONNX-class, CPU-runnable, ≤768-d (1-bit-index-friendly).
    87	**Pinned HF revisions** are recorded per model in the output (commit SHA / revision hash) for
    88	determinism.
    89	
    90	## 4. Corpus, gold, and the hard subset (frozen, model-independent)
    91	
    92	- **Corpus:** the frozen IR snapshot `tests/corpus/snapshot.json` (`corpus_hash` =
    93	  `fe973fcd49fbbda083158f69fe720f17858ab8528e171fa2188eec84131c7d4e`); doc bodies in
    94	  `data/corpus-data/raw/*.jsonl`. The probe records the resolved doc count + re-verifies the corpus
    95	  hash before embedding (the pinning principle, `ir-recall-measure.md` §f — a relevance number across
    96	  a different corpus is meaningless). Reuse the existing corpus loader/membership the IR harness uses
    97	  (`tests/.../support/corpus_subset.rs` membership; Python side resolves doc_id→body from the same
    98	  raw jsonl + snapshot per-source sha256).
    99	- **eu8 gold (qrels):** `data/corpus-data/eval/ir_gold/all.gold.json` (`qrels_version`
   100	  `ir-c-reused-v1`, 4597 queries). Each query carries `expected_top_k_doc_ids` (the doc-id qrels).
   101	  **eu8 relevance recall@10** = the legacy doc-id reduction of Evidence Recall (mirror
   102	  `support/ir_eval.rs::evidence_recall_at_k`): per query, **strict all-of** — `1` iff every
   103	  `expected_top_k_doc_ids` doc is in the f32-exact top-10, else `0`; averaged over non-`negative`
   104	  queries. Negative-class queries are excluded from the recall mean (abstention-correctness, §d of
   105	  the measure doc).
   106	- **Hard subset (= deep-exploratory recall).** Frozen as a **model-INDEPENDENT** query-id set so the
   107	  A/B is fair: the queries whose gold doc is **missed by lexical BM25 within the cap** — i.e. the
   108	  non-`lexical` bucket of `ir_c_gold_diagnostics.rs::bucket()` (`bm25_rank` is `None` or `> cap`).
   109	  This is exactly where dense/the embedder is the only lever. The implementer reproduces the BM25
   110	  ranking deterministically over the frozen corpus (FTS5 `bm25()` ascending, or a deterministic
   111	  rank-BM25 over the same tokenization), freezes `hard_qids = {qid : bm25_rank None or > cap}`, and
   112	  records the **cap used + the actual count**. The design references a **~596-q** hard subset; the
   113	  **actual reproduced frozen count governs** and is reported transparently — do NOT force 596 if the
   114	  deterministic reproduction yields a different number (honesty prior; a forced count would be a
   115	  silent fudge). Metrics on the hard subset: **recall@10** (the gated one), **recall@50**, and
   116	  **median gold rank**.
   117	- **Memory-class strict recall (where cheaply derivable, $0).** On the re-pinned LME gold
   118	  (`gold_repin.py` output, `repin_hash 2916cace`, per-class factoid/knowledge_update/multi_session/
   119	  temporal), compute per-class strict recall@10 offline if the LME passages embed cheaply within the
   120	  same harness. This is **report-only attribution** (it is NOT a `probe_15a_pass` input); if it is
   121	  not cheaply derivable on CPU within the slice, it is recorded as "not computed — deferred to Slice
   122	  20 re-embed" rather than faked.
   123	
   124	## 5. Metrics computed per embedder (deterministic, CPU)
   125	
   126	For each model (base + 4 candidates), with **pinned HF revision + fixed seed**, on CPU:
   127	
   128	1. **eu8 recall@10** (§4) — f32-exact cosine top-10 over the full corpus, strict all-of.
   129	2. **hard-subset** recall@10 (gated), recall@50, median gold rank (§4).
   130	3. **memory-class strict recall@10** (report-only, where cheap; §4).
   131	4. **projected_eu7 (1-bit survivability):** quantize the model's f32 vectors to **1-bit sign**
   132	   (`vec >= 0`), Hamming top-**K=192** (the production fanout, `ADR-0.7.0`) → fetch f32 → cosine
   133	   rerank → top-10; **recall@10 of this 1-bit path vs the model's own f32-exact top-10** (the eu7
   134	   ANN-fidelity definition, exactly as `eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs` / `dev/research/eu-0/run_sweep.py`).
   135	   This is the per-embedder eu7 proxy; must be **≥ 0.90**. (Mean-centering before sign-quant is the
   136	   production pipeline; apply it iff the production path does — record whether it was applied.)
   137	5. **cpu_feasible:** measured CPU embed latency **ms/query** and **ms/doc** (median over a fixed
   138	   sample, fixed thread count recorded), judged against the AC012/AC013 library budget ballpark.
   139	   Report the numbers **and** the bool; a 768-d model that embeds within budget on CPU is feasible.
   140	
   141	### Paired margin CIs (the gated quantity)
   142	
   143	`eu8_margin_ci_lo` / `hard_margin_ci_lo` = the **paired (candidate − base) per-query delta**, 95%
   144	**bootstrap** CI **lower bound**, fixed bootstrap seed, ≥1000 resamples, percentile method (mirror
   145	`eu8_ir_validation.rs:283-304`). Paired = resample query indices, compute mean(cand_hit − base_hit)
   146	on the same resampled queries (the same query set for eu8; the frozen `hard_qids` for hard). This is
   147	the only quantity `probe_15a_pass` gates on.
   148	
   149	## 6. Two-stage split (explicit, per the prompt)
   150	
   151	- **Stage 1 (this slice, mechanical):** emit the per-candidate metrics, the paired margin CIs, and
   152	  the mechanical `probe_15a_pass` verdict + the ranking + projected lifts. **The chosen Slice-20
   153	  embedder (or "no swap") is decided here** by the frozen gate.
   154	- **Stage 2 (after D0b lands, orchestrator/HITL):** the gap-relative "is the projected lift *enough*
   155	  to close the Mem0 gap / does it *overshoot* (surpass)" interpretation. D0b's final per-class gap is
   156	  not yet available; this slice records the **surpass flag as provisional** (a candidate whose
   157	  projected eu8/hard lift is large is flagged a *candidate* surpass lever) and defers the
   158	  gap-relative finalization. The output makes this split explicit.
   159	
   160	## 7. Determinism & environment
   161	
   162	- **Determinism:** pinned HF revisions; fixed seeds (embedding is eval-mode, no dropout → forward is
   163	  deterministic on fixed CPU/lib versions); fixed bootstrap seed; fixed `corpus_hash` + `hard_qids`.
   164	  Re-running the embed for a model + revision yields **byte-identical** vectors (asserted, §8a).
   165	- **Environment:** the canonical `.venv` lacks torch/transformers; reuse `dev/research/eu-0/.venv`
   166	  (torch 2.12+cpu, transformers 5.9) with `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=""`, `HF_HOME` pointed at
   167	  `dev/research/eu-0/hf_cache` (bge-small/base + e5-small cached; gte-base, e5-base-v2, nomic-v1.5
   168	  download once — network is allowed for OFFLINE-BUILD model fetch, never for the library path). Do
   169	  NOT install into the canonical `.venv`, do NOT `maturin develop`/`pip install -e` (protect the
   170	  sibling run). ONNXRuntime is present in the canonical venv; torch-CPU and ONNX-CPU produce
   171	  numerically equivalent f32 vectors for these models, so torch-CPU is a valid ceiling measurement —
   172	  this equivalence is recorded as a caveat.
   173	- **Resilience ([[priced-runs-need-resilience-before-spend]]):** the runner checkpoints per-model
   174	  doc/query f32 vectors to `.npy` (atomic write) and supports `--resume`; a multi-hour CPU embed
   175	  must survive a restart. `--smoke` (small corpus subset + 2 models) proves the pipeline green fast;
   176	  `--full` runs the real measurement.
   177	
   178	## 8. TDD plan (RED → GREEN, backend-light)
   179	
   180	**RED (commit first; tiny fixed vectors / fixtures where possible, no model download in unit tests):**
   181	- (a) **embedding determinism:** same input + pinned revision → byte-identical vectors (a small
   182	  fixture or a marked/integration test that loads the smallest model once).
   183	- (b) **pooling/prefix correctness:** a fixture that FAILS on a mean-vs-CLS swap or a missing
   184	  `query:`/`search_query:` prefix (e.g. assert the documented pooling + prefix string per model from
   185	  a config table; a known-vector fixture for one model).
   186	- (c) **probe wiring:** the harness builds the `cand`/`base` dicts and calls
   187	  `probe_15a_pass`, emitting a mechanical PASS/FAIL + the per-candidate margin CIs; assert against a
   188	  synthetic case where the margins/projected_eu7 are known (truth-table: passes / fails-on-eu8 /
   189	  fails-on-hard / fails-on-cpu / fails-on-eu7). **Must call the real `probe_15a_pass`, not a copy.**
   190	- (d) **1-bit eu7-proxy computation:** on tiny fixed f32 vectors, assert the sign-quant→Hamming→f32-
   191	  rerank recall@10-vs-f32 equals a hand-computed value (and that identical f32 ⇒ proxy 1.0).
   192	- (e) **paired bootstrap CI:** deterministic (fixed seed) lower bound on a tiny fixed paired array;
   193	  assert a known value + that a strictly-positive paired delta yields ci_lo > 0 and a zero delta does
   194	  not.
   195	
   196	**GREEN:** the probe runner producing the outputs (§9), `--smoke` green end-to-end.
   197	
   198	Tests run in the eu-0 venv (or canonical venv for the pure-logic ones). `ruff` + `pyright`/typecheck
   199	green on new files. `mkdocs build` green if docs touched.
   200	
   201	## 9. Outputs
   202	
   203	- `dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-s15a-embedder.json` — per candidate: `{eu8, hard:{r@10,r@50,median_rank},
   204	  memory_class_recall|null, projected_eu7, cpu_latency:{ms_per_query,ms_per_doc,threads,feasible},
   205	  eu8_margin_ci:{lo,hi,point}, hard_margin_ci:{lo,hi,point}, probe_15a_pass}`; plus `chosen_embedder`,
   206	  `ranking`, `surpass_flag` (provisional), `corpus_hash`, `qrels_version`, `hard_subset:{cap,count,
   207	  definition}`, `model_revisions`, `seeds`, `pooling_prefix_table`, `caveats`.
   208	- `dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-s15a-report.md` — short narrative: ranking, the chosen Slice-20 embedder (or
   209	  "no swap"), projected lifts, the (provisional) surpass note, the two-stage split, and any blocker.
   210	- `dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-s15a-output.json` — closure: `{phase:"0.8.3-slice-15a", base_sha, branch,
   211	  head_sha, commits[], chosen_embedder, per_candidate_summary, probe_15a_pass_verdicts, surpass_flag,
   212	  codex_verdict, gate_results, next_step_for_orchestrator:"land on main after D0b lands; feed the
   213	  chosen embedder into Slice 20"}`.
   214	
   215	## 10. DoD / guardrails
   216	
   217	EVAL-ONLY / $0 / CPU-only / deterministic / no airlock / no GPU / no engine change.
   218	`decision_rule_083.py` + gold untouched. Honesty: margins reported with CIs; a tie inside the CI is a
   219	tie; if **no** candidate beats the corrected base at power → clean negative → "no swap". Cross-check
   220	every "green"/number against printed output ([[background-exit-masks-real-exit]]). codex §9 on the
   221	slice branch (primary reviewer); remediate [P1]/[P2] (fix-N); re-review. STOP un-merged.
   222	
   223	## 11. Risks / open items (for the reviewer to pressure-test)
   224	
   225	1. **Hard-subset reproduction.** The frozen `hard_qids` count may not be exactly 596; the
   226	   deterministic reproduction governs and is reported. Risk: the BM25 tokenization used here may not
   227	   match the engine's FTS5 tokenizer exactly → a slightly different bucket. Mitigation: use FTS5
   228	   `bm25()` via the engine read path if cheaply available, else a documented deterministic BM25 and
   229	   flag the tokenizer caveat.
   230	2. **gte-base pooling.** Confirm CLS vs mean at pin time against the card; a wrong choice invalidates
   231	   that candidate (not the whole probe).
   232	3. **torch-CPU vs ONNX equivalence** for the ceiling measurement — recorded as a caveat; the ceiling
   233	   is a model-weights property, runtime-independent to f32 tolerance.
   234	4. **Mean-centering in the eu7 proxy** must match the production sign-quant pipeline; record whether
   235	   applied so projected_eu7 is comparable to the real eu7.
   236	5. **CPU wall-clock** for 5×(7.7k–10.5k docs): mitigated by checkpointing + `--resume`; run `--full`
   237	   as a resilient background job.
   238	</content>

     1	# IR / agentic evidence-recall MEASURE (definition + methodology) · `[design / consensus]`
     2	
     3	> **Status:** Phase-1 DEFINITION, Claude↔codex consensus-signed (see § Consensus).
     4	> **Scope:** This document *defines the measure and its methodology only.* It mints no AC,
     5	> builds no gold set, runs no experiment, picks no threshold number, and **does not touch the
     6	> eu7 / AC-075 fidelity gate.** Every number here is **TBD** and is settled downstream by the
     7	> Phase-4 experiments + the IR-2 / HITL gate. It does **not** commit to any corpus snapshot —
     8	> the corpus basis is ruled separately by B-1 + the corpus freeze.
     9	>
    10	> **This is the input to IR-1 Phases 2–4 (AC-077 mint, gold set, experiments) + IR-2.**
    11	> **Initiative:** IR-eval. **Prompt:** `dev/plans/prompts/0.8.x-IR-1-phase1-measure-consensus.md`.
    12	> **Charter:** `dev/notes/recall-eval-framework-assessment-20260607T174821Z.md`.
    13	
    14	## 0. Why this measure exists (the one-paragraph frame)
    15	
    16	FathomDB has exactly **one** gated recall number today — eu7 / AC-075 — and it measures
    17	**ANN/quantization FIDELITY**: does the cheap 1-bit sign-quant index (K=192 Hamming + f32
    18	rerank) reproduce the *exact-f32 top-10 of the same embedder*
    19	(`dev/adr/ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md:151-158`; `eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs`). That is a
    20	**system-health** property — necessary but **not sufficient** for "is the agent's memory
    21	useful." A store can sit at 0.99 fidelity and still bury the one fact the agent needed.
    22	The **product-value** axis — *when the agent needs a memory to act, is the required evidence
    23	in the retrieved context?* — is measured today only by eu8 (`eu8_ir_validation.rs`), which is
    24	**report-only** and capped at the embedder's IR ceiling (≈0.571,
    25	`ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md:169-179`). This document defines the **product-value
    26	recall measure** so it can later become a tracked signal and (post-experiment, HITL-ruled)
    27	a gate — **complementary to, never a replacement for, eu7.**
    28	
    29	---
    30	
    31	## (a) What is measured — the primary measure + its relationships
    32	
    33	**Primary measure: Evidence Recall@K.**
    34	
    35	> **Evidence Recall@K** = the fraction of evaluation queries for which the retrieved
    36	> top-K context **contains *all* the evidence units required to correctly act on / answer
    37	> the query.** It is computed against a **gold set** of queries each labelled with its
    38	> *required-evidence set* (the unit of relevance is defined in (b)).
    39	
    40	Two reporting forms, both required (they answer different questions):
    41	
    42	- **Strict (all-of) Evidence Recall@K** — per query, `1` iff **every** required-evidence unit
    43	  is present in top-K, else `0`; then averaged. This is the headline product-value number
    44	  ("could the agent have acted?"). It is *all-or-nothing per query* because a commitment with
    45	  the date but not the obligor is not actionable.
    46	- **Graded (fraction-of) evidence recall@K** — per query, **over the `required` evidence set
    47	  only**: `|required ∩ retrieved@K| / |required|`; averaged. This is the **diagnostic** companion
    48	  (how close did we get when we missed?), and it degrades gracefully so it is useful below the
    49	  all-of threshold. *Both* strict and graded use the **same `required`-only denominator** — they
    50	  differ only in all-or-nothing vs. fractional — so they are directly comparable. `supporting`
    51	  evidence is **not** in either recall number (see (b)); it is reported as a separate
    52	  supporting-coverage diagnostic.
    53	
    54	**Relationship to plain retrieval Recall@K (eu8 today).** Plain Recall@K is *doc-id* recall —
    55	"did a labelled-relevant *document* appear?" (`eu8_ir_validation.rs:227-253`, binary relevance
    56	over `expected_doc_ids`). Evidence Recall is *evidence-unit* recall — "did the labelled-required
    57	*fact* appear?" Plain Recall@K is the **layer-1 retriever-health** signal and is **subsumed**:
    58	when the unit of relevance is the whole document (the degenerate label, see (b)), Evidence
    59	Recall reduces exactly to eu8's doc-id Recall@K. So eu8 is **not discarded** — it is the
    60	document-granularity special case and the migration path.
    61	
    62	**Relationship to MRR / nDCG.** MRR and nDCG are **ranking-quality** signals (where in the
    63	list did relevant items land), already computed by eu8 (`eu8_ir_validation.rs:244-278`). They
    64	are reported alongside Evidence Recall as **secondary** signals, never as the headline: a high
    65	MRR with incomplete evidence is still an un-actionable result. nDCG's graded value needs graded
    66	qrels, which the current chain labels do not carry — flagged, report-only until graded labels
    67	exist.
    68	
    69	**Relationship to the eu7 fidelity gate (explicit, load-bearing).** Evidence Recall and eu7
    70	are on **different axes** and measure **different ground truths**:
    71	
    72	| | eu7 / AC-075 (the GA gate) | Evidence Recall@K (this measure) |
    73	|---|---|---|
    74	| Ground truth | exact-f32 top-10 of the **same** embedder | **externally-labelled required-evidence** sets |
    75	| Question | does quant reproduce exact-f32? | is the evidence needed to act present? |
    76	| Axis | **ANN / quantization fidelity** | **IR / agentic relevance (product value)** |
    77	| Role | system-health (necessary) | product-value (sufficiency of context) |
    78	| Ceiling | ~1.0 achievable (index faithfulness) | embedder/graph-bound (~0.571-class for vector-only) |
    79	
    80	**They are complementary and orthogonal. This measure does NOT replace, relax, alter, or
    81	re-anchor eu7 / AC-075. The 0.90 fidelity floor stays exactly as it is.** (§ (g) restates why.)
    82	
    83	---
    84	
    85	## (b) Unit of relevance + the gold-set encoding protocol
    86	
    87	**Unit of relevance = the atomic evidence unit (`evidence_id`).** Because FathomDB stores
    88	**whole node bodies** and **does not chunk** (`fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs:1010-1019,922-928`;
    89	charter §1), the "chunk recall" axis collapses — *document recall and chunk recall are the
    90	same thing here.* The genuinely missing axis is **fact-level / evidence-level** recall: a long
    91	`enron` / `qmsum` / `cnn_dailymail` body can rank highly as a *document* while the *specific
    92	fact* needed to act is buried. The unit of relevance is therefore the **atomic evidence unit**,
    93	not the document.
    94	
    95	**The gold-set encoding protocol (schema, NOT the labels — labeling is Phase 3).** The schema
    96	is **additive over the existing chain `ground_truth_queries` shape** (`IRQuery`:
    97	`support/corpus_subset.rs:214-220`; chains carry `expected_top_k_doc_ids` + `relation_type` +
    98	`chain_shape` today), so eu8 stays the document-granularity special case and the gold set is a
    99	strict superset:
   100	
   101	```jsonc
   102	// per gold query (additive superset of today's ground_truth_queries entry)
   103	{
   104	  "query": "...",                        // SAME key the eu8 parser reads (corpus_subset.rs:239 q.get("query")) — additive superset, stays parseable
   105	  "query_id": "stable-unique-id",        // additive (new; optional for existing eu8 entries)
   106	  "query_class": "commitment | action | exact_fact | preference | exploratory | negative",
   107	  "required_evidence": [                 // the "all evidence required to act" set
   108	    {
   109	      "evidence_id": "stable-unique-id",
   110	      "doc_id": "<the body that carries this fact>",   // which node body must be retrieved
   111	      "necessity": "required | supporting",            // required = must be in top-K for strict recall
   112	      "locator": { "kind": "span|whole_body", "...": "OPTIONAL provenance, not used for scoring in a non-chunking store" }
   113	    }
   114	  ],
   115	  // back-compat / migration: a query whose required_evidence is exactly its
   116	  // distinct doc_ids with necessity=required degenerates to today's eu8 doc-id qrels.
   117	  "expected_top_k_doc_ids": ["..."],     // PRESERVED: the eu8 doc-id view
   118	  "relation_type": "action_from | contradicts | follows_up_on | mentions | summarizes | ...",
   119	  "chain_shape": "..."
   120	}
   121	```
   122	
   123	Scoring contract: an `evidence_id` counts as **retrieved@K** iff its `doc_id` appears in the
   124	engine's top-K result bodies (body→doc_id mapping is in-harness today,
   125	`eu8_ir_validation.rs:189-216`). Because FathomDB returns whole bodies, **presence is at
   126	doc-body granularity**; the `locator` is recorded for label provenance/audit but is **not**
   127	load-bearing for the score in a non-chunking store (it becomes load-bearing only if/when
   128	FathomDB chunks — not planned for 0.8.0/0.8.1). **Only `necessity=required` units are in the
   129	recall denominator** — they gate the strict all-of recall *and* form the `required`-only
   130	denominator of the graded recall (a). **`supporting` units are NOT in either recall number**;
   131	they are reported as a separate **supporting-coverage** diagnostic (how much corroborating
   132	context was also retrieved). This keeps strict and graded recall on one consistent denominator
   133	and removes any ambiguity about whether supporting evidence inflates the headline. **The label
   134	*values* (which facts are required for which query) are produced in Phase 3 by human/HITL
   135	labeling — this document defines only the slot they go in.**
   136	
   137	---
   138	
   139	## (c) The K-ladder + the headline K
   140	
   141	Report at **K ∈ {5, 10, 20, 50}** (extensible to 200 as a pure diagnostic). Rationale,
   142	anchored to FathomDB's real surface:
   143	
   144	| K | Role | Anchor |
   145	|---|------|--------|
   146	| **@5** | UX-proximal (what the agent reads first) | — |
   147	| **@10** | **HEADLINE** — the eval/reporting convention; aligns with the vector-branch phase-2 rerank depth `SEARCH_RERANK_LIMIT`=10 and with eu7/eu8's K=10 | `lib.rs:3388` (`SEARCH_RERANK_LIMIT`); eu7 `:444-445`; eu8 `K=10` (`:70`) |
   148	| **@20** | near-UX / reranker-target band (when a real reranker lands) | charter §3 item 1 |
   149	| **@50** | **retriever-health** (is the evidence anywhere in the candidate pool?) | charter §5 #3 |
   150	| (@200) | deep diagnostic only — not a UX surface | charter §3 (chunk/fact note) |
   151	
   152	**Headline = Evidence Recall@10**, as the **eval/reporting convention** — *not* an
   153	API-enforced result-set cap. **Accuracy note (codex round-7 [P2]):** `search()` does **not**
   154	truncate its final result set to 10. Only the **vector branch's phase-2 rerank** is limited to
   155	`SEARCH_RERANK_LIMIT`=10 (`lib.rs:3388`); the **FTS/text branch is unbounded by default** and the
   156	fused `SearchResult.results` is returned **without a final top-K truncation** (`lib.rs:2206`;
   157	`read_search_in_tx` returns the full fused list). So @10 is chosen as the headline because it is
   158	the natural UX-proximal depth *and* it matches the vector-rerank depth + the eu7/eu8 K=10 the rest
   159	of FathomDB's recall measurement already uses — the eval applies the @K cut itself; it must not
   160	assume the API enforces a top-10. @50 is the **retriever-health** companion (separating "the
   161	ranker buried it" from "retrieval never surfaced it"); @5 is the UX-proximal stress; @200 is a
   162	diagnostic ceiling, never a UX claim. **No threshold number is assigned to any K here** — the
   163	pass/fail lines are Phase-4 experiment output + IR-2/HITL.
   164	
   165	> **Vector-fanout requirement for K>10 (codex round-8 [P2]).** In production the vector branch
   166	> reranks only `SEARCH_RERANK_LIMIT`=10 candidates. If the eval computed vector-only or
   167	> hybrid recall at @20/@50 with that default, those depths would see **only 10 vector hits** and
   168	> @50 would no longer measure "is the evidence anywhere in the candidate pool." The eval harness
   169	> **must raise the vector phase-2 rerank fanout to ≥ the deepest K on the ladder** for the
   170	> vector branch and the vector contribution to hybrid — via the existing
   171	> `set_search_limit_for_test` seam (`lib.rs:2873`, which stores `search_limit_override` and only
   172	> ever *raises* above the production floor, never shrinks). This is exactly how eu7 pulls
   173	> top-(K+slack) (`eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs:440-445`). The eval records the fanout it used alongside
   174	> each number. (This is a measurement-harness setting, **not** a production-behavior change.)
   175	
   176	---
   177	
   178	## (d) Per-class structure (structure, not numbers)
   179	
   180	Evidence Recall is **stratified by query class**, because the stakes differ by class and a
   181	single mean hides must-not-miss failures. The classes (the *structure*; the per-class
   182	thresholds are **TBD**, Phase 4 / IR-2):
   183	
   184	| Class | What it asks of memory | Maps onto existing chain `relation_type` |
   185	|-------|------------------------|------------------------------------------|
   186	| **commitment** | a promise/obligation + its parties + due date | `action_from`, `follows_up_on` |
   187	| **action** | the next action a note/email implies | `action_from` |
   188	| **exact-fact** | a specific retrievable fact (number, name, date) | `mentions`, `summarizes` |
   189	| **preference** | a stated user preference / standing instruction | (new — not in current chains) |
   190	| **exploratory** | open-ended "what do I know about X" recall | `summarizes`, `mentions` |
   191	| **negative ("not-found")** | the answer is *absent*; correct behaviour is to return nothing / abstain | `contradicts` (partial), (new) |
   192	
   193	Two structural notes:
   194	
   195	1. **Must-not-miss classes** (commitment / exact-fact) are reported **separately** and are the
   196	   natural future stricter-gate candidates — but their thresholds are **TBD** and **must be
   197	   re-anchored to FathomDB's embedder class** before any gate (the charter is explicit: against
   198	   a measured 0.571 IR ceiling, importing generic-RAG "≥98–99%" lines would produce a
   199	   permanently-red, uninformative gate — §3 item 2, §5 #7).
   200	2. **The negative class is first-class.** "Correctly returns nothing when nothing is relevant"
   201	   is a real product property (avoiding confident retrieval of irrelevant memory). It is scored
   202	   as **abstention-correctness**, not recall, and reported separately. The existing
   203	   per-relation / per-chain-shape buckets (`eu8_ir_validation.rs:321-344`) are the mechanism
   204	   this stratification grows into.
   205	
   206	---
   207	
   208	## (e) Retrieval modes to compare (with today-vs-future flags)
   209	
   210	The measure is reported **per retrieval mode**, so the eval separates embedder/ranker effects
   211	from retrieval-architecture effects:
   212	
   213	| Mode | Status today | Anchor |
   214	|------|--------------|--------|
   215	| **FTS branch** (FTS5 `MATCH` filter) | **EXISTS, but NOT bm25-ranked in production** — the text branch carries `bm25()` only as a *score*; it is `ORDER BY write_cursor` (insertion order), and RRF fuses on that insertion-order rank | `lib.rs:3905-3928` (text SQL: `ORDER BY write_cursor`, `bm25()` selected as score) |
   216	| **BM25-ranked FTS-only baseline** | **HARNESS-CONSTRUCTIBLE, not the production path** — a true BM25 baseline needs the eval to order by the already-carried `bm25()` score **ascending** (FTS5 `bm25()` is *smaller = better*, the opposite of the `SearchHit.score` larger-is-better convention — codex round-8 [P2]: use `ORDER BY bm25(search_index) ASC`, do **not** reuse a descending score sort or the ranking inverts); **no engine change** (score is present), but it is NOT what production search ranks by today | `lib.rs:3918-3920` (score carried, order is write_cursor) |
   217	| **vector-only** (bit-KNN K=192 → f32 rerank) | **EXISTS** | `ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md:97-106` |
   218	| **RRF-hybrid** (the production fused ranking) | **EXISTS** — the unconditional ranking; fuses the vector branch and the **write-cursor-ordered** text branch on rank | `lib.rs:3955-3956,3584-3623` |
   219	| **+reranker** (rerank top-N → top-K) | **STUB** — `rerank_fused()` is identity | `lib.rs:3653-3660` |
   220	| **+graph expansion** (`expand=N` / neighbors) | **0.8.1** — designed, deferred | `ADR-0.8.0-graph-traversal-scope.md`; `dev/roadmap/0.8.1.md` |

     1	"""Frozen RESOLUTION decision rule for the 0.8.3 Mem0-parity gate.
     2	
     3	This module is the *executable core* of the 0.8.3 Slice-0 pre-registration. It
     4	freezes — before any number is seen, so a downstream slice (10/20/25/30) cannot
     5	post-hoc switch the endpoint — three things as code:
     6	
     7	1. :func:`decide_083` — the **REACHED / NOT_REACHED** resolution computation over
     8	   the external per-memory-class ``FathomDB − Mem0`` deltas, with the two hard
     9	   BLOCK gates (eu7 fidelity floor; latency budget), a per-class power guard, and
    10	   the near-parity band ε. **Slices 10/20/25/30 import this; they may not redefine
    11	   the rule.**
    12	2. :func:`probe_15a_pass` / :func:`probe_15b_pass` — the two $0, LLM-free triage
    13	   probe pass criteria (15a embedder-ceiling, PRIMARY; 15b D2 content-at-scale
    14	   proxy) from design §5.
    15	3. :func:`lint_preregistration_083` — the schema lint asserting the design doc
    16	   ``dev/design/0.8.3-mem0-parity.md`` carries every required frozen, dated
    17	   pre-registration field.
    18	
    19	The two-level reframe (design §2): the **external** Mem0 delta is THE GATE
    20	(decides *done*); internal lever deltas are attribution only. ``decide_083``
    21	consumes **already-computed summary statistics** — the parity harness runs the
    22	paired bootstrap; this function stays pure/deterministic (no RNG, no I/O).
    23	
    24	Binding spec: ``dev/design/0.8.3-mem0-parity.md`` §5 (frozen pre-registration) and
    25	§9 (this module's contract); ``dev/plans/plan-0.8.3.md`` §4 (Slice 0).
    26	
    27	Mirrors :mod:`eval.m1_decision_rule`'s style — pure stdlib, **no ``fathomdb`` /
    28	``scipy`` / ``numpy`` / ``networkx`` import** — so it (and its test) run anywhere,
    29	independent of the native-extension build or the ``.venv`` binding. Deterministic:
    30	no clock, no RNG, no I/O.
    31	"""
    32	
    33	from __future__ import annotations
    34	
    35	import math
    36	import re
    37	from collections.abc import Mapping
    38	from typing import Literal, TypedDict
    39	
    40	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    41	# Frozen constants (the rule must be auditable). See design §5 (frozen-field:
    42	# near-parity-band) + §4 (hard gates).
    43	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    44	
    45	#: Near-parity band ε (5 percentage points). "Near-parity-or-better" on a class =
    46	#: the external ``FathomDB − Mem0`` paired-CI **lower bound ≥ −ε**; "better" =
    47	#: lower bound **> 0**. Frozen 2026-06-21 (design §5; do NOT change downstream).
    48	EPS_NEAR_PARITY: float = 0.05
    49	
    50	#: The eu7 ANN-quantization fidelity recall@10 hard floor — any embedder/pooling
    51	#: change must re-clear this once (design §4 BLOCK gate / §5 eu7-break fork).
    52	EU7_FLOOR: float = 0.90
    53	
    54	#: The four agentic-memory classes scored independently (design §3). The
    55	#: resolution requires *every* class to clear the near-parity band.
    56	MEMORY_CLASSES: tuple[str, ...] = (
    57	    "factoid",
    58	    "knowledge_update",
    59	    "multi_session",
    60	    "temporal",
    61	)
    62	
    63	Verdict = Literal["REACHED", "NOT_REACHED"]
    64	_REACHED: Verdict = "REACHED"
    65	_NOT_REACHED: Verdict = "NOT_REACHED"
    66	
    67	BlockedBy = Literal["eu7", "latency"]
    68	
    69	
    70	class ClassFacts(TypedDict):
    71	    """Per-class echo of the input delta plus the three derived gate flags."""
    72	
    73	    point: float
    74	    ci_lo: float
    75	    ci_hi: float
    76	    mde: float
    77	    n: int
    78	    near_parity: bool
    79	    better: bool
    80	    underpowered: bool
    81	
    82	
    83	class Resolution(TypedDict):
    84	    """The frozen resolution verdict (design §9)."""
    85	
    86	    verdict: Verdict
    87	    per_class: dict[str, ClassFacts]
    88	    binding_constraint: str | None
    89	    surpass_candidates: list[str]
    90	    blocked_by: BlockedBy | None
    91	
    92	
    93	def _require_finite(value: float, name: str) -> float:
    94	    """Return ``float(value)``; raise ``ValueError`` if it is non-finite.
    95	
    96	    A ``NaN`` slips past every ``<`` / ``>=`` comparison (``nan < x`` and
    97	    ``nan >= x`` are both False), so without this guard a malformed endpoint could
    98	    silently yield a verdict. A malformed endpoint must fail loudly instead.
    99	    """
   100	    v = float(value)
   101	    if not math.isfinite(v):
   102	        raise ValueError(
   103	            f"non-finite {name}: {value!r} (a malformed endpoint must fail loudly)"
   104	        )
   105	    return v
   106	
   107	
   108	def decide_083(
   109	    external_per_class: Mapping[str, Mapping[str, float]],
   110	    eu7_recall: float,
   111	    latency_ok: bool,
   112	) -> Resolution:
   113	    """Return the frozen RESOLUTION verdict for the 0.8.3 Mem0-parity gate.
   114	
   115	    ``external_per_class`` maps **each** of :data:`MEMORY_CLASSES` to an
   116	    already-computed delta ``{"point", "ci_lo", "ci_hi", "mde", "n"}`` — the
   117	    external ``FathomDB − Mem0`` paired delta on that class with its 95% CI bounds,
   118	    the per-class paired MDE, and the class N. ``eu7_recall`` is the measured eu7
   119	    ANN-fidelity recall@10; ``latency_ok`` is the AC012/AC013/AC020 budget verdict.
   120	
   121	    The rule (design §5 decision-rule, §9 truth table) — in order:
   122	
   123	    1. **BLOCK (hard gates, checked first):** ``eu7_recall <`` :data:`EU7_FLOOR`
   124	       ⇒ ``NOT_REACHED`` with ``blocked_by="eu7"``; else ``not latency_ok`` ⇒
   125	       ``NOT_REACHED`` with ``blocked_by="latency"``. A block never makes a parity
   126	       claim (``binding_constraint`` stays ``None``; ``blocked_by`` carries it).
   127	    2. **Power guard:** any class with ``mde >`` :data:`EPS_NEAR_PARITY` ⇒
   128	       ``NOT_REACHED`` with ``binding_constraint="underpowered:<class>"`` (the
   129	       first such class in :data:`MEMORY_CLASSES` order) — no parity claim on an
   130	       under-powered class.
   131	    3. **Resolution:** ``REACHED`` iff *every* class clears the near-parity band
   132	       (``ci_lo >= -EPS_NEAR_PARITY``); otherwise ``NOT_REACHED`` with
   133	       ``binding_constraint="below_parity:<class>,<class>..."`` (the mechanical
   134	       failing-class identifier; the HITL names the *semantic* binding constraint —
   135	       embedder ceiling / reader / a class-specific lever — per design §5).
   136	
   137	    ``surpass_candidates`` is always the classes whose ``ci_lo > 0`` (strictly
   138	    better than Mem0). Deterministic: same input → same Resolution.
   139	
   140	    Raises :class:`KeyError` if a class or a delta key is missing, and
   141	    :class:`ValueError` if any float is non-finite (``NaN`` / ``±inf``) — a
   142	    malformed endpoint must fail loudly, never silently return a verdict.
   143	    """
   144	    eu7 = _require_finite(eu7_recall, "eu7_recall")
   145	    latency_intact = bool(latency_ok)
   146	
   147	    # Echo + derive every class up front (KeyError if a class/key is missing;
   148	    # ValueError if any float is non-finite) — fail loudly on a malformed endpoint.
   149	    per_class: dict[str, ClassFacts] = {}
   150	    for cls in MEMORY_CLASSES:
   151	        delta = external_per_class[cls]
   152	        point = _require_finite(delta["point"], f"{cls}.point")
   153	        ci_lo = _require_finite(delta["ci_lo"], f"{cls}.ci_lo")
   154	        ci_hi = _require_finite(delta["ci_hi"], f"{cls}.ci_hi")
   155	        mde = _require_finite(delta["mde"], f"{cls}.mde")
   156	        n = int(delta["n"])
   157	        per_class[cls] = ClassFacts(
   158	            point=point,
   159	            ci_lo=ci_lo,
   160	            ci_hi=ci_hi,
   161	            mde=mde,
   162	            n=n,
   163	            near_parity=ci_lo >= -EPS_NEAR_PARITY,
   164	            better=ci_lo > 0.0,
   165	            underpowered=mde > EPS_NEAR_PARITY,
   166	        )
   167	
   168	    surpass_candidates = [cls for cls in MEMORY_CLASSES if per_class[cls]["better"]]
   169	
   170	    def _result(
   171	        verdict: Verdict,
   172	        binding_constraint: str | None,
   173	        blocked_by: BlockedBy | None,
   174	    ) -> Resolution:
   175	        return Resolution(
   176	            verdict=verdict,
   177	            per_class=per_class,
   178	            binding_constraint=binding_constraint,
   179	            surpass_candidates=surpass_candidates,
   180	            blocked_by=blocked_by,
   181	        )
   182	
   183	    # 1 — hard-gate BLOCK (checked first; eu7 before latency).
   184	    if eu7 < EU7_FLOOR:
   185	        return _result(_NOT_REACHED, None, "eu7")
   186	    if not latency_intact:
   187	        return _result(_NOT_REACHED, None, "latency")
   188	
   189	    # 2 — power guard: no parity claim on an under-powered class.
   190	    for cls in MEMORY_CLASSES:
   191	        if per_class[cls]["underpowered"]:
   192	            return _result(_NOT_REACHED, f"underpowered:{cls}", None)
   193	
   194	    # 3 — resolution: every class must clear the near-parity band.
   195	    below = [cls for cls in MEMORY_CLASSES if not per_class[cls]["near_parity"]]
   196	    if below:
   197	        return _result(_NOT_REACHED, "below_parity:" + ",".join(below), None)
   198	
   199	    return _result(_REACHED, None, None)
   200	
   201	
   202	def probe_15a_pass(
   203	    cand: Mapping[str, float],
   204	    base: Mapping[str, float],
   205	) -> bool:
   206	    """15a embedder-ceiling probe (PRIMARY) — design §5.
   207	
   208	    A candidate embedder *clears* iff it beats the CLS-corrected ``bge-small``
   209	    reference on **both** eu8 relevance recall@10 and the ~596-q hard subset by a
   210	    margin whose paired-CI **lower bound > 0**, **and** is CPU-feasible, **and** is
   211	    1-bit-survivable (projected eu7 ≥ :data:`EU7_FLOOR`).
   212	
   213	    ``cand`` carries ``{"eu8", "hard"}`` (candidate point estimates),
   214	    ``{"eu8_margin_ci_lo", "hard_margin_ci_lo"}`` (the paired ``cand − base`` margin
   215	    CI lower bounds, already computed), ``cpu_feasible`` (bool), and
   216	    ``projected_eu7`` (float). ``base`` carries the CLS-corrected ``bge-small``
   217	    reference points ``{"eu8", "hard"}``.
   218	
   219	    Returns ``True`` only when every criterion holds. Non-finite floats raise.
   220	    """
   221	    # Validate the carried point estimates for finiteness (a malformed endpoint
   222	    # must fail loudly) but do NOT gate on a raw cand-point > base-point check:
   223	    # frozen design §5 (15a) gates on the paired margin CI lower bound only.
   224	    _require_finite(cand["eu8"], "cand.eu8")
   225	    _require_finite(cand["hard"], "cand.hard")
   226	    _require_finite(base["eu8"], "base.eu8")
   227	    _require_finite(base["hard"], "base.hard")
   228	    eu8_margin_ci_lo = _require_finite(cand["eu8_margin_ci_lo"], "cand.eu8_margin_ci_lo")
   229	    hard_margin_ci_lo = _require_finite(
   230	        cand["hard_margin_ci_lo"], "cand.hard_margin_ci_lo"
   231	    )
   232	    projected_eu7 = _require_finite(cand["projected_eu7"], "cand.projected_eu7")
   233	    cpu_feasible = bool(cand["cpu_feasible"])
   234	
   235	    one_bit_survivable = projected_eu7 >= EU7_FLOOR
   236	    return (
   237	        eu8_margin_ci_lo > 0.0
   238	        and hard_margin_ci_lo > 0.0
   239	        and cpu_feasible
   240	        and one_bit_survivable

 succeeded in 0ms:

     4	date: 2026-06-21

    10	---
    11	
    12	# 0.8.3 Slice-0 — Mem0-parity resolution design + pre-registration
    13	
    14	> **Orchestrator draft.** This is the substantive design the Slice-0 implementer freezes into code
    15	> (`eval/decision_rule_083.py`) + a falsifiable AC list, then the HITL signs. Per the three-role model
    16	> (`dev/design/orchestration.md` §1) the orchestrator authors the contract; the implementer makes it
    17	> executable + reviewable (TDD), the reviewer (codex §9) checks it, the HITL gates it.
    18	
    19	## 1. Purpose — the resolution, not a lever bake-off
    20	
    21	0.8.3 succeeds iff **FathomDB reaches near-parity-or-better with Mem0 on agentic memory**, end-to-end,
    22	under an identical-answerer protocol, with **Graphiti/Zep** as a second comparator on the same axis. The
    23	non-graph levers M1 surfaced (D1 dense/embedder, D2 fielded enrichment) are **means to close a measured
    24	competitor gap**, not ends. History: M1 closed NO-GO (graph multi-hop refuted, `runs/0.8.2-m1-FINDINGS.md`);
    25	the 2026-06-21 capability report found the product's defining number — identical-answerer accuracy vs
    26	Mem0/Zep — was **never measured**, with the four memory classes at **N=0 gold** since 0.8.1 Slice 25.
    27	0.8.3 makes that number exist (Phase A) and drives it to parity (Phase B).
    28	
    29	## 2. Two-level measurement (frozen)
    30	
    31	| Level | Quantity | Role |
    32	|---|---|---|
    33	| **External** | per-class `FathomDB − {Mem0, Graphiti/Zep, naive-RAG}` (strict full-gold Recall@K **and** identical-answerer accuracy) on the re-pinned, power-sized gold | **THE GATE** — decides *done* |
    34	| **Internal** | each lever vs the corrected-fused baseline (the M1 comparator), MDE-feasible materiality | **attribution only** — explains *which* lever closed the gap; never, by itself, "done" |
    35	
    36	The external Mem0 delta is the resolution gate. Internal lever deltas are reported per lever so gap-closure
    37	is explainable, but an internal win is **not** a resolution.
    38	
    39	## 3. Datasets, arms, adapters
    40	
    41	- **Corpus:** LongMemEval-derived gold, **re-pinned + power-sized** at Slice 5 so each of the four memory
    42	  classes (`factoid`, `knowledge_update`, `multi_session`, `temporal`) carries **≥ N_min gold** (see §5).
    43	  New `corpus_hash` recorded in `runs/0.8.3-d0a-corpus-manifest.json`.
    44	- **Arms (identical-answerer):** `fathomdb` · `mem0` (Mem0-OSS, local) · `graphiti_zep` · `naive_rag` (BM25).
    45	  Adapters expose only `retrieve(question, k)`; **one answerer / one prompt / one context budget** across all
    46	  arms (the R2 invariant) so any delta is retrieval, not prompt divergence. Reader = `gpt-5.4` (temp 0,
    47	  seed 0), the single priced EVAL-ONLY seam; cheap-validate = `gemini-2.5-flash-lite`.
    48	- **Harness:** reuse `eval/r2_parity_eval.py` (identical-answerer, shared adapter/metric contract).
    49	
    50	## 4. Endpoints
    51	
    52	- **External GATE (resolution):** per memory class, `FathomDB − Mem0` on (a) strict full-gold Recall@K and
    53	  (b) identical-answerer accuracy, with a paired 95% CI. Graphiti/Zep reported alongside (2nd comparator).
    54	- **Internal attribution (lever signals):** corrected-fused baseline deltas on eu8 relevance recall@10, the
    55	  ~596-q hard subset, and MuSiQue co-primaries **all-bridges@K (presence)** and **F1-given-all-bridges
    56	  (composition)** (ADJ-10) — a lever that wins presence but loses composition is not progress.
    57	- **Hard gates (BLOCK):** eu7 ANN-quantization fidelity recall@10 **≥ 0.90**; AC012/AC013/AC020 latency
    58	  budgets intact on read-path slices (ADJ-9).
    59	
    60	## 5. Pre-registration — frozen fields (dated 2026-06-21)
    61	
    62	> Frozen here in prose; the **implementer freezes the executable copy in `eval/decision_rule_083.py`** and a
    63	> lint test asserts this doc carries every field below. No downstream slice may redefine these.
    64	
    65	- **frozen-field: near-parity-band ε = 0.05** (frozen 2026-06-21) (5 percentage points). "Near-parity-or-better" on a class =
    66	  the external `FathomDB − Mem0` paired-CI **lower bound ≥ −ε**. "Better" = CI lower bound **> 0**.
    67	- **frozen-field: power-sizing rule.** (frozen 2026-06-21) Per-class N is the smallest N whose paired **MDE ≤ ε** at the
    68	  **Slice-5-measured** per-class variance + pairing ρ. The *rule* is frozen now; the *number* is computed
    69	  deterministically from the frozen variance at Slice 5 (provisional target per-class **N ≥ 150**, pooled
    70	  ≥ 600 — to be confirmed/escalated at Slice 5; base-retrieval MDE was ≈21pp at n=160, far too coarse, so
    71	  N must rise). Slice-10 power-check **asserts MDE ≤ ε per class** or **escalates to HITL** (no parity
    72	  claim on an under-powered class).
    73	- **frozen-field: decision-rule** (frozen 2026-06-21) (`decide_083`, §9): **REACHED** iff *every* class clears the near-parity
    74	  band (external CI lower ≥ −ε) **and** eu7 ≥ 0.90 **and** latency intact ⇒ present the surpass-option
    75	  package; HITL signs "ship at parity" or "pursue surpass". **NOT_REACHED** ⇒ record the **residual per-class
    76	  gap + the named binding constraint** (most-likely: embedder ceiling / reader / a class-specific lever) +
    77	  the explicit fork: (a) carry a stronger embedder, (b) the caller-side reader axis (out of library
    78	  footprint), (c) a memory-class-specific lever. **This replaces the retired "both-null fallback": 0.8.3
    79	  always ends in a decision, never an open redirect.**
    80	- **frozen-field: $0-probe pass/fail.** (frozen 2026-06-21)
    81	  - **15a embedder-ceiling (PRIMARY):** a candidate embedder *clears* iff it beats CLS-corrected `bge-small`
    82	    on eu8 + the hard subset by a margin whose CI lower > 0 **and** is CPU-feasible **and** 1-bit-survivable
    83	    (projected eu7 ≥ 0.90). Output = the embedder projected to close the most Mem0 gap; if none clears,
    84	    Slice 20 keeps CLS-corrected `bge-small`.
    85	  - **15b D2 content-at-scale proxy:** enrichment *passes* iff fielded content beats the **length-matched
    86	    placebo** at power (CI lower > 0) **and** fielding removes the FTS length-norm penalty. FAIL ⇒ Slice 25
    87	    (D2 engine) defers/drops.
    88	- **frozen-field: eu7-break fork.** (frozen 2026-06-21) Any embedder/pooling change rewrites stored vectors ⇒ re-clear eu7 ≥0.90
    89	  **once** on the chosen embedder. Breach = **BLOCK → HITL** + the fidelity-recovery fork (rotation/whitening
    90	  before 1-bit sign-quant; raise ANN fan-out K>192; a 2-bit option) **and** a **0.937→0.896 bisect** (is CLS
    91	  the cause, or the pre-existing unbisected vector-path regression?). Today's point eu7 = 0.896 (CI-hi 0.925).
    92	- **frozen-field: surpass-option protocol.** (frozen 2026-06-21) At every Phase-B gate the orchestrator records whether a
    93	  credible surpass-Mem0 lever exists (a 15a embedder with headroom; a class-specific lever; the caller-side
    94	  reader axis). The **Slice-30 sign-off package presents it** with cost + expected lift, so HITL chooses
    95	  "ship at parity" vs "spend to surpass" — never a silent stop at the parity line.
    96	
    97	## 6. ADJ-2 — F5 / tunable-`b` scope ruling
    98	
    99	**RULING (RATIFIED by the HITL Slice-0 gate 2026-06-21): PROMOTE** F5 (fielded-FTS/BM25F) + the
   100	tunable-/lower-`b` length-norm path into 0.8.3 Slice 25 **as the strong form of D2**, conditional on the
   101	**15b proxy passing** and **Slice 20 leaving a Mem0 gap**. If 15b fails or Slice 20 already reaches parity,
   102	D2 **defers to 0.8.5** (revert path retained, `dev/roadmap/0.8.5.md` §4). The ADR
   103	`dev/adr/ADR-0.8.1-deferred-f5-fielded-fts-bm25f.md` is re-pointed to 0.8.3 Slice 25 (binding). (The weak
   104	body-append R6 form already underperformed FTS as-is — not worth running.)
   105	
   106	## 7. Budget (aggregate, frozen at this gate)
   107	
   108	Three priced runs, all reader = `gpt-5.4`, all cheap-validated first, all on the resilient harness
   109	([[priced-runs-need-resilience-before-spend]]): **(1)** D0b parity (Slice 10), **(2)** D1 confirmatory
   110	(Slice 20), **(3)** D2 confirmatory (Slice 25, conditional). Aggregate **target ≤ $30**; per-run
   111	cheap-validate gate; running ledger in `runs/STATUS-0.8.3.md`. M1 reference: a full n=300 pass was $2.50.
   112	
   113	## 8. Footprint tags (per technique)
   114	
   115	CLS-pooling fix = **IN-LIBRARY**; embedder swap = **IN-LIBRARY** (candle/ONNX, `EmbedderChoice::Caller`);
   116	vector PRF = **IN-LIBRARY** (deterministic, bounded); fielded-FTS/BM25F + tunable-`b` = **IN-LIBRARY**;
   117	enrichment-key extraction = **OFFLINE-BUILD** (local Qwen, $0); the answerer = **EVAL-ONLY**. **No in-library
   118	LLM.** Query rewrite / HyDE / decomposition / IRCoT = **CALLER-SIDE BYO-LLM** only (out of 0.8.3).
   119	
   120	## 9. `eval/decision_rule_083.py` — module spec (implementer freezes this)
   121	
   122	Extend `eval/m1_decision_rule.py`'s style: pure stdlib (no `fathomdb`/`scipy`/`networkx`/RNG/I/O),
   123	deterministic, consumes already-computed summary stats.
   124	
   125	- `EPS_NEAR_PARITY: float = 0.05`; `MEMORY_CLASSES = ("factoid","knowledge_update","multi_session","temporal")`.
   126	- `decide_083(external_per_class: Mapping[str, ClassDelta], eu7_recall: float, latency_ok: bool) -> Resolution`
   127	  where `ClassDelta` carries `point`, `ci_lo`, `ci_hi`, `mde`, `n`. Returns
   128	  `{"verdict": "REACHED"|"NOT_REACHED", "per_class": {...}, "binding_constraint": str|None,
   129	  "surpass_candidates": [...], "blocked_by": "eu7"|"latency"|None}`.
   130	  - **BLOCK** first: `eu7_recall < 0.90` → `blocked_by="eu7"`; `not latency_ok` → `blocked_by="latency"`.
   131	  - **Power guard:** any class with `mde > EPS_NEAR_PARITY` → `NOT_REACHED`, `binding_constraint="underpowered:<class>"`.
   132	  - **REACHED** iff every class `ci_lo >= -EPS_NEAR_PARITY` (and not blocked/underpowered).
   133	  - **surpass_candidates:** classes with `ci_lo > 0`.
   134	- `probe_15a_pass(cand, base) -> bool`, `probe_15b_pass(enriched, placebo) -> bool` (the §5 criteria).
   135	- `lint_preregistration_083(design_md_text: str) -> None` — assert this doc carries each `frozen-field:`
   136	  tag + a date (mirrors `m1_decision_rule.lint_preregistration`).
   137	- Tests (RED→GREEN, pure-Python, **no extension build**): rule truth-table (REACHED / NOT_REACHED /
   138	  blocked / underpowered / surpass), probe criteria, and the lint over this file.
   139	
   140	## 10. Falsifiable AC list (per slice — the implementer pins these as the slice acceptance bar)
   141	
   142	- **S5 (D0a):** each class ≥ N_min gold (no silent N=0); answerer seam smoke passes; Mem0-OSS adapter
   143	  conformance (returns top-K under the shared contract); `corpus_hash` emitted.
   144	- **S10 (D0b):** per-class external deltas + paired CI emitted for all arms on one `corpus_hash`+answerer;
   145	  power-check `mde ≤ ε` per class (else HITL escalation); the gap lands in `runs/0.8.3-report.md`.
   146	- **S15:** 15a emits a ranked embedder table with the pass criterion applied; 15b emits enriched-vs-placebo
   147	  at power with the pass/fail; both mechanical.
   148	- **S20:** CLS pooling pinned (Rust); eu7 ≥ 0.90 re-cleared (else BLOCK→HITL + fork); external Mem0 delta +
   149	  internal co-primaries re-measured; latency intact; PRF added only on a remaining gap (composition guard).
   150	- **S25 (conditional):** additive migration + `SCHEMA_VERSION` bump + EXPLAIN no-SCAN; enriched-beats-BOTH-
   151	  plain-AND-placebo; body channel unaffected; external delta re-measured; latency intact.
   152	- **S30:** final external delta per class; eu7 + latency re-checked; `decide_083` emits the parity decision
   153	  mechanically; surpass-option package assembled.

     1	//! IR-B (IR-1 **Phase 2 / CODE**) — Evidence Recall@K measure + gold-set schema.
     2	//!
     3	//! Implements the **corpus-INDEPENDENT** half of the Phase-1 consensus measure
     4	//! `dev/design/ir-recall-measure.md`: the gold-set encoding schema (§(b)), the
     5	//! Evidence Recall@K math (§(a) — strict all-of headline + graded diagnostic,
     6	//! single `required`-only denominator), the K-ladder (§(c) — @5/@10/@20/@50,
     7	//! headline @10), the per-class stratification (§(d)), and the retrieval-mode
     8	//! plumbing (§(e)). It computes the metric GIVEN a gold set + retrieved results;
     9	//! it MINTS NO AC, PICKS NO THRESHOLD, and touches NOTHING in the eu7/AC-075
    10	//! fidelity gate or the engine vector path.
    11	//!
    12	//! ## What is DEFERRED to the COR-2 corpus freeze (NOT in this module)
    13	//! - the **real** fact-level gold-set LABELS (which facts are required for which
    14	//!   query) — Phase 3 / human-HITL labeling on the FROZEN corpus;
    15	//! - the **real-corpus experiment RUNS** + any real recall numbers — IR-C;
    16	//! - the production **FTS write-cursor** and **BM25-ordered** retrieval modes,
    17	//!   which need harness-level FTS5 SQL + the frozen corpus to be meaningful
    18	//!   (`TODO(COR-2-freeze)` in [`run_mode_bodies`]).
    19	//!
    20	//! The fixture under `tests/fixtures/ir_gold/synthetic_gold.json` is a tiny
    21	//! **illustrative** gold set (synthetic, NOT real labels) used only to exercise
    22	//! the loader/validator and pin the schema shape.
    23	//!
    24	//! Zero new crate dependencies: the gold set is parsed manually from
    25	//! `serde_json::Value` (mirroring `support/corpus_subset.rs`), since the engine
    26	//! test crate carries `serde_json` but not `serde` derive.
    27	
    28	#![allow(dead_code)] // referenced by the sibling `ir_recall_eval.rs` test; cargo lints each include in isolation
    29	
    30	use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet, HashMap, HashSet};
    31	use std::path::Path;
    32	
    33	use fathomdb_engine::{rerank_fused, Engine};
    34	use serde_json::{json, Value};
    35	
    36	// ── K-ladder (§(c)) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	/// The reported K-ladder: @5 (UX-proximal), @10 (HEADLINE / reporting
    38	/// convention), @20 (reranker band), @50 (retriever-health). No threshold is
    39	/// attached to any K here — pass/fail lines are Phase-4 experiment + IR-2/HITL.
    40	pub const K_LADDER: [usize; 4] = [5, 10, 20, 50];
    41	/// The headline K — the eval/reporting convention (NOT an API-enforced cap; the
    42	/// eval applies the @K cut itself, see measure doc §(c) codex round-7).
    43	pub const HEADLINE_K: usize = 10;
    44	/// Vector phase-2 rerank fanout floor for the eval (≥ the deepest ladder K) so
    45	/// @20/@50 vector/hybrid depths actually see >10 candidates (measure doc §(c)
    46	/// codex round-8). A measurement-harness setting via `set_search_limit_for_test`
    47	/// — never a production-behavior change.
    48	pub const DEFAULT_FANOUT: usize = 50;
    49	
    50	// ── Gold-set schema (§(b)) — additive superset of eu8 ground_truth_queries ──
    51	
    52	/// Whether an evidence unit is part of the recall denominator (`required`) or a
    53	/// separate corroboration diagnostic (`supporting`). Only `required` units gate
    54	/// strict recall AND form the graded recall denominator; `supporting` is in
    55	/// neither recall number (measure doc §(a)/(b)).
    56	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
    57	pub enum Necessity {
    58	    Required,
    59	    Supporting,
    60	}
    61	
    62	impl Necessity {
    63	    fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {
    64	        match s {
    65	            "required" => Some(Self::Required),
    66	            "supporting" => Some(Self::Supporting),
    67	            _ => None,
    68	        }
    69	    }
    70	}
    71	
    72	/// The six query classes (measure doc §(d)). `Negative` ("not-found") is scored
    73	/// as abstention-correctness, NOT recall, and is reported separately.
    74	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
    75	pub enum QueryClass {
    76	    Commitment,
    77	    Action,
    78	    ExactFact,
    79	    Preference,
    80	    Exploratory,
    81	    Negative,
    82	}
    83	
    84	impl QueryClass {
    85	    pub fn label(&self) -> &'static str {
    86	        match self {
    87	            Self::Commitment => "commitment",
    88	            Self::Action => "action",
    89	            Self::ExactFact => "exact_fact",
    90	            Self::Preference => "preference",
    91	            Self::Exploratory => "exploratory",
    92	            Self::Negative => "negative",
    93	        }
    94	    }
    95	    fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {
    96	        Some(match s {
    97	            "commitment" => Self::Commitment,
    98	            "action" => Self::Action,
    99	            "exact_fact" => Self::ExactFact,
   100	            "preference" => Self::Preference,
   101	            "exploratory" => Self::Exploratory,
   102	            "negative" => Self::Negative,
   103	            _ => return None,
   104	        })
   105	    }
   106	}
   107	
   108	/// Where the query text came from — *leakage provenance*. `human_dataset` is a
   109	/// human/benchmark-authored question (no doc-text leakage); `templated` is
   110	/// derived from the evidence doc (HIGH lexical-leakage risk — must be held to a
   111	/// higher validation bar); `llm_generated` is model-synthesized (needs the
   112	/// label-audit caveats in `dev/notes/IR-C-fact-level-gold-labels-research.md`).
   113	/// Locked now so any synthetic query is flagged the moment it appears.
   114	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
   115	pub enum QueryOrigin {
   116	    HumanDataset,
   117	    LlmGenerated,
   118	    Templated,
   119	}
   120	
   121	impl QueryOrigin {
   122	    pub fn label(&self) -> &'static str {
   123	        match self {
   124	            Self::HumanDataset => "human_dataset",
   125	            Self::LlmGenerated => "llm_generated",
   126	            Self::Templated => "templated",
   127	        }
   128	    }
   129	    fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {
   130	        Some(match s {
   131	            "human_dataset" => Self::HumanDataset,
   132	            "llm_generated" => Self::LlmGenerated,
   133	            "templated" => Self::Templated,
   134	            _ => return None,
   135	        })
   136	    }
   137	}
   138	
   139	/// A character span within a doc body, carried through from the source QA
   140	/// `evidence_spans`. NOT load-bearing for the doc-body-granularity score; it
   141	/// powers the passage↔evidence-span overlap diagnostic (IR-C instrumentation
   142	/// plan WI-3 — `dev/plans/IR-C-test-query-quality-instrumentation-plan.md`).

   144	pub struct Span {

   146	    pub start: usize,
   147	    pub end: usize,
   148	}
   149	
   150	/// Provenance of an evidence unit. In a non-chunking store this is recorded for

   152	/// doc-body granularity, measure doc §(b)).
   153	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
   154	pub struct Locator {
   155	    /// "span" | "whole_body".
   156	    pub kind: String,
   157	    /// Char spans within the doc body (present when `kind == "span"`); `None` for
   158	    /// `whole_body`. Carried from the dataset `evidence_spans` for span-level
   159	    /// diagnostics — never affects the recall score.
   160	    pub spans: Option<Vec<Span>>,
   161	}
   162	
   163	/// One atomic evidence unit — the unit of relevance (measure doc §(b)).
   164	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
   165	pub struct EvidenceUnit {

   170	    pub necessity: Necessity,
   171	    pub locator: Option<Locator>,
   172	}
   173	
   174	/// One gold query — an additive superset of today's eu8 `ground_truth_queries`

   177	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
   178	pub struct GoldQuery {
   179	    pub query: String,
   180	    pub query_id: Option<String>,
   181	    pub query_class: QueryClass,
   182	    /// The atomic-evidence layer (additive). May be empty for a legacy/eu8 entry
   183	    /// (then the denominator falls back to `expected_top_k_doc_ids`, §(f)) or for
   184	    /// a `Negative` query (abstention class).
   185	    pub required_evidence: Vec<EvidenceUnit>,
   186	    /// PRESERVED eu8 doc-id view — the legacy/fallback denominator.
   187	    pub expected_top_k_doc_ids: Vec<String>,
   188	    pub relation_type: Option<String>,
   189	    pub chain_shape: Option<String>,

   197	    pub answer_type: Option<String>,

   200	    pub query_origin: QueryOrigin,
   201	}

   203	/// A versioned, corpus-pinned gold set. The pinning PRINCIPLE (measure doc §(f),

   205	/// set so a relevance number can never silently drift under corpus expansion.

   208	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
   209	pub struct GoldSet {

   211	    pub qrels_version: String,

   213	    pub queries: Vec<GoldQuery>,

   216	/// Sentinel used by the synthetic fixture until the COR-2 freeze pins a real

   219	pub const UNPINNED_PLACEHOLDER: &str = "TODO(COR-2-freeze)";

   221	// ── Loader (§(b)) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   222	
   223	/// Load + parse a gold set from a JSON file on disk.
   224	pub fn load_gold_set(path: &Path) -> Result<GoldSet, String> {
   225	    let text =
   226	        std::fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| format!("read {}: {e}", path.display()))?;
   227	    parse_gold_set(&text)
   228	}
   229	
   230	/// Parse a gold set from a JSON string. Manual `serde_json::Value` walk (no
   231	/// `serde` derive dep), mirroring `corpus_subset::extract_ground_truth_queries`.
   232	pub fn parse_gold_set(text: &str) -> Result<GoldSet, String> {
   233	    let v: Value = serde_json::from_str(text).map_err(|e| format!("gold set JSON: {e}"))?;
   234	    let corpus_hash = v.get("corpus_hash").and_then(Value::as_str).unwrap_or_default().to_string();
   235	    let qrels_version =
   236	        v.get("qrels_version").and_then(Value::as_str).unwrap_or_default().to_string();
   237	    let note = v.get("note").and_then(Value::as_str).map(str::to_string);
   238	    let arr = v
   239	        .get("queries")
   240	        .and_then(Value::as_array)
   241	        .ok_or_else(|| "gold set: missing `queries` array".to_string())?;
   242	    let mut queries = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());
   243	    for (i, q) in arr.iter().enumerate() {
   244	        queries.push(parse_query(q).map_err(|e| format!("queries[{i}]: {e}"))?);
   245	    }
   246	    Ok(GoldSet { corpus_hash, qrels_version, note, queries })
   247	}

   260	            required_evidence
