 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! IR-C — gold test-query-quality diagnostics: the LEXICAL tier (WI-1L).
     2	//!
     3	//! Plan: `dev/plans/IR-C-test-query-quality-instrumentation-plan.md`. Quantifies
     4	//! how lexically *easy* each gold query is — i.e. how much of the "hybrid is
     5	//! lexical-bound" result is a property of the test set vs the retriever — using
     6	//! a MODEL-FREE pass over the frozen corpus (seed + FTS5 `bm25()`, NO embedding),
     7	//! so it is cheap and CI-able. The model-dependent DENSE tier (dense_gold_rank,
     8	//! lexical/semantic/hard buckets, span-overlap) is WI-1D and lands separately.
     9	//!
    10	//! Per positive query it records:
    11	//!   - `bm25_gold_rank` — rank of the best gold doc under content-OR + `bm25()`
    12	//!     over the whole corpus (`null` past the scan cap). rank 1 ⇒ trivially lexical.
    13	//!   - `idf_overlap`    — IDF-weighted query∩gold-doc content-token coverage
    14	//!     (raw coverage saturates on long docs; IDF-weight measures the *rare* terms).
    15	//!   - `gold_doc_tokens`, `gold_locator_kind` — difficulty context.
    16	//!
    17	//! Writes the `lexical` section of `…/ir_gold/all.gold.diagnostics.json`, pinned
    18	//! to `corpus_hash` (refuses on snapshot mismatch). The retrieval seams are the
    19	//! SAME ones the fusion experiment uses (`support/ir_retrieval.rs`) so ranks are
    20	//! comparable.
    21	//!
    22	//! The full-corpus run is gated `IRC_RUN=1` and skips when the (gitignored) corpus
    23	//! is absent; the unit tests below run in the DEFAULT `cargo test` pass (no
    24	//! feature, no corpus) and pin the metric math.
    25	
    26	#[path = "support/corpus_subset.rs"]
    27	mod corpus_subset;
    28	#[path = "support/ir_eval.rs"]
    29	mod ir_eval;
    30	#[path = "support/ir_retrieval.rs"]
    31	mod ir_retrieval;
    32	
    33	use std::collections::{BTreeSet, HashMap, HashSet};
    34	
    35	use corpus_subset::{ingest, load_subset_or_skip, repo_root, Doc, VECTOR_KIND};
    36	use fathomdb_engine::PreparedWrite;
    37	use ir_eval::{
    38	    load_gold_set, required_doc_ids, validate_gold_set, QueryClass, UNPINNED_PLACEHOLDER,
    39	};
    40	use ir_retrieval::{compile_content_or, content_tokens, fts_bodies, map_bodies, tokenize_set};
    41	use rusqlite::{Connection, OpenFlags};
    42	use serde_json::{json, Value};
    43	
    44	/// How deep the content-OR `bm25()` scan goes before a gold doc counts as
    45	/// "unranked" (`bm25_gold_rank = null`). Deep enough that anything past it is
    46	/// lexically hopeless regardless of the exact value.
    47	const RANK_CAP: usize = 1000;
    48	
    49	fn env_usize(key: &str, default: usize) -> usize {
    50	    std::env::var(key).ok().and_then(|v| v.parse().ok()).unwrap_or(default)
    51	}
    52	
    53	// ── Pure metric helpers (unit-tested below) ─────────────────────────────────
    54	
    55	/// 1-based rank of the best (earliest) gold doc in a ranked doc_id list; `None`
    56	/// if no gold doc appears.
    57	fn bm25_gold_rank(ranked: &[String], gold: &BTreeSet<String>) -> Option<usize> {
    58	    ranked.iter().position(|d| gold.contains(d)).map(|p| p + 1)
    59	}
    60	
    61	/// BM25 IDF of a term: `ln((N − df + 0.5)/(df + 0.5) + 1)`. Rarer ⇒ higher.
    62	fn idf(df: usize, n_docs: usize) -> f64 {
    63	    let n = n_docs as f64;
    64	    let df = df as f64;
    65	    ((n - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5) + 1.0).ln()
    66	}
    67	
    68	/// IDF-weighted coverage of the query's content tokens by the gold doc:
    69	/// `Σ_{t∈Q∩D} idf(t) / Σ_{t∈Q} idf(t)` ∈ [0,1]. Empty query ⇒ 0.0.
    70	fn idf_overlap(
    71	    q_tokens: &HashSet<String>,
    72	    doc_tokens: &HashSet<String>,
    73	    df: &HashMap<String, usize>,
    74	    n_docs: usize,
    75	) -> f64 {
    76	    let idf_of = |t: &str| idf(df.get(t).copied().unwrap_or(0), n_docs);
    77	    let denom: f64 = q_tokens.iter().map(|t| idf_of(t)).sum();
    78	    if denom <= 0.0 {
    79	        return 0.0;
    80	    }
    81	    let numer: f64 =
    82	        q_tokens.iter().filter(|t| doc_tokens.contains(t.as_str())).map(|t| idf_of(t)).sum();
    83	    numer / denom
    84	}
    85	
    86	/// Guard: the gold's pin must match the frozen snapshot and not be the
    87	/// fixture placeholder — otherwise we'd diagnose a corpus the gold wasn't built
    88	/// against. Returns the validated hash to stamp into the sidecar.
    89	fn check_corpus_hash(gold_hash: &str, snapshot_hash: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
    90	    if gold_hash.trim().is_empty() {
    91	        return Err("gold corpus_hash is empty".into());
    92	    }
    93	    if gold_hash == UNPINNED_PLACEHOLDER {
    94	        return Err("gold corpus_hash is the unpinned fixture placeholder".into());
    95	    }
    96	    if snapshot_hash.trim().is_empty() {
    97	        return Err("snapshot corpus_hash is empty".into());
    98	    }
    99	    if gold_hash != snapshot_hash {
   100	        return Err(format!("gold corpus_hash {gold_hash} != snapshot {snapshot_hash}"));
   101	    }
   102	    Ok(gold_hash.to_string())
   103	}
   104	
   105	// ── Sidecar assembly (unit-tested for shape) ────────────────────────────────
   106	
   107	struct LexRecord {
   108	    query_id: String,
   109	    query_class: String,
   110	    source: Option<String>,
   111	    bm25_gold_rank: Option<usize>,
   112	    idf_overlap: f64,
   113	    gold_doc_tokens: usize,
   114	    gold_locator_kind: String,
   115	}
   116	
   117	fn median(mut v: Vec<usize>) -> Option<f64> {
   118	    if v.is_empty() {
   119	        return None;
   120	    }
   121	    v.sort_unstable();
   122	    let n = v.len();
   123	    Some(if n % 2 == 1 { v[n / 2] as f64 } else { (v[n / 2 - 1] + v[n / 2]) as f64 / 2.0 })
   124	}
   125	
   126	/// Per-class + overall summary over a slice of records.
   127	fn summarize(recs: &[&LexRecord]) -> Value {
   128	    let n = recs.len();
   129	    if n == 0 {
   130	        return json!({ "n": 0 });
   131	    }
   132	    let rank1 = recs.iter().filter(|r| r.bm25_gold_rank == Some(1)).count();
   133	    let found: Vec<usize> = recs.iter().filter_map(|r| r.bm25_gold_rank).collect();
   134	    let mean_overlap: f64 = recs.iter().map(|r| r.idf_overlap).sum::<f64>() / n as f64;
   135	    json!({
   136	        "n": n,
   137	        "bm25_rank1_frac": rank1 as f64 / n as f64,
   138	        "bm25_found_frac": found.len() as f64 / n as f64,
   139	        "median_bm25_gold_rank": median(found),
   140	        "mean_idf_overlap": (mean_overlap * 1e4).round() / 1e4,
   141	    })
   142	}
   143	
   144	fn build_lexical_sidecar(
   145	    corpus_hash: &str,
   146	    qrels_version: &str,
   147	    scope: &str,
   148	    n_docs: usize,
   149	    recs: &[LexRecord],
   150	) -> Value {
   151	    let per_query: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = recs
   152	        .iter()
   153	        .map(|r| {
   154	            (
   155	                r.query_id.clone(),
   156	                json!({
   157	                    "query_class": r.query_class,
   158	                    "source": r.source,
   159	                    "bm25_gold_rank": r.bm25_gold_rank,
   160	                    "idf_overlap": (r.idf_overlap * 1e4).round() / 1e4,
   161	                    "gold_doc_tokens": r.gold_doc_tokens,
   162	                    "gold_locator_kind": r.gold_locator_kind,
   163	                }),
   164	            )
   165	        })
   166	        .collect();
   167	
   168	    // Overall + per-class summaries.
   169	    let mut summary = serde_json::Map::new();
   170	    summary.insert("overall".into(), summarize(&recs.iter().collect::<Vec<_>>()));
   171	    let mut by_class: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = serde_json::Map::new();
   172	    for class in ["exact_fact", "exploratory"] {
   173	        let sel: Vec<&LexRecord> = recs.iter().filter(|r| r.query_class == class).collect();
   174	        if !sel.is_empty() {
   175	            by_class.insert(class.into(), summarize(&sel));
   176	        }
   177	    }
   178	    summary.insert("per_class".into(), Value::Object(by_class));
   179	
   180	    json!({
   181	        "corpus_hash": corpus_hash,
   182	        "qrels_version": qrels_version,
   183	        "_comment": "IR-C gold diagnostics — LEXICAL tier (model-free, full-corpus). \
   184	                     bm25_gold_rank/idf_overlap measure per-query lexical easiness; the \
   185	                     dense tier (buckets, span overlap) is WI-1D. Pinned to corpus_hash.",
   186	        "lexical": {
   187	            "scope": scope,
   188	            "n_docs": n_docs,
   189	            "summary": Value::Object(summary),
   190	            "per_query": Value::Object(per_query),
   191	        },
   192	    })
   193	}
   194	
   195	// ── Dense tier (WI-1D/WI-3b): ranks, buckets, passage↔span overlap ──────────
   196	// Pure helpers (unit-tested); the embedding loop that feeds them is feature-gated.
   197	
   198	/// Classify a query by which arm can find its gold doc within `cap`:
   199	/// `lexical` (bm25 reaches it), else `semantic` (only the chunked dense arm
   200	/// reaches it — the stratum that justifies a vector arm), else `hard`.
   201	fn bucket(bm25_rank: Option<usize>, dense_rank: Option<usize>, cap: usize) -> &'static str {
   202	    let within = |r: Option<usize>| matches!(r, Some(x) if x <= cap);
   203	    if within(bm25_rank) {
   204	        "lexical"
   205	    } else if within(dense_rank) {
   206	        "semantic"
   207	    } else {
   208	        "hard"
   209	    }
   210	}
   211	
   212	/// Char-span IoU of two `[start, end)` byte ranges. Disjoint ⇒ 0.0.
   213	fn span_iou(a: (usize, usize), b: (usize, usize)) -> f64 {
   214	    let inter = a.1.min(b.1).saturating_sub(a.0.max(b.0)) as f64;
   215	    let la = a.1.saturating_sub(a.0) as f64;
   216	    let lb = b.1.saturating_sub(b.0) as f64;
   217	    let union = la + lb - inter;
   218	    if union <= 0.0 {
   219	        0.0
   220	    } else {
   221	        inter / union
   222	    }
   223	}
   224	
   225	/// Max-pool passage→doc ranking that also tracks each doc's best-scoring
   226	/// passage span (for the span-overlap metric). Returns `(doc_id, best_span)` in
   227	/// descending doc-score order. Same max-pool the experiment's `v_*_max` uses.
   228	fn maxpool_ranking(
   229	    qv: &[f32],
   230	    passages: &[(String, usize, usize, Vec<f32>)],
   231	) -> Vec<(String, (usize, usize))> {
   232	    struct B {
   233	        score: f32,
   234	        span: (usize, usize),
   235	    }
   236	    let mut by: HashMap<&str, B> = HashMap::new();
   237	    for (doc, s, e, v) in passages {
   238	        let dot: f32 = qv.iter().zip(v).map(|(a, b)| a * b).sum();
   239	        let entry = by.entry(doc.as_str()).or_insert(B { score: f32::MIN, span: (0, 0) });
   240	        if dot > entry.score {
   241	            entry.score = dot;
   242	            entry.span = (*s, *e);
   243	        }
   244	    }
   245	    let mut v: Vec<(&str, f32, (usize, usize))> =
   246	        by.into_iter().map(|(d, b)| (d, b.score, b.span)).collect();
   247	    v.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
   248	    v.into_iter().map(|(d, _, sp)| (d.to_string(), sp)).collect()
   249	}
   250	
   251	/// Rank (1-based) of the best gold doc in a doc ranking, plus that doc's best
   252	/// passage span. `(None, None)` if no gold doc appears.
   253	fn dense_gold_rank_and_span(
   254	    ranking: &[(String, (usize, usize))],
   255	    gold: &BTreeSet<String>,
   256	) -> (Option<usize>, Option<(usize, usize)>) {
   257	    for (i, (doc, span)) in ranking.iter().enumerate() {
   258	        if gold.contains(doc) {
   259	            return (Some(i + 1), Some(*span));
   260	        }
   261	    }
   262	    (None, None)
   263	}
   264	
   265	struct DenseRecord {
   266	    query_id: String,
   267	    dense_gold_rank_whole: Option<usize>,
   268	    dense_gold_rank_128_96: Option<usize>,
   269	    bucket: &'static str,
   270	    passage_evidence_iou: Option<f64>,
   271	}
   272	
   273	fn build_dense_section(
   274	    identity: &str,
   275	    scope: &str,
   276	    bucket_cap: usize,
   277	    recs: &[DenseRecord],
   278	) -> Value {
   279	    let per_query: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = recs
   280	        .iter()
   281	        .map(|r| {
   282	            (
   283	                r.query_id.clone(),
   284	                json!({
   285	                    "dense_gold_rank_whole": r.dense_gold_rank_whole,
   286	                    "dense_gold_rank_128_96": r.dense_gold_rank_128_96,
   287	                    "bucket": r.bucket,
   288	                    "passage_evidence_iou": r.passage_evidence_iou.map(|x| (x * 1e4).round() / 1e4),
   289	                }),
   290	            )
   291	        })
   292	        .collect();
   293	    let count = |b: &str| recs.iter().filter(|r| r.bucket == b).count();
   294	    let ious: Vec<f64> = recs.iter().filter_map(|r| r.passage_evidence_iou).collect();
   295	    let mean_iou = if ious.is_empty() {
   296	        Value::Null
   297	    } else {
   298	        json!((ious.iter().sum::<f64>() / ious.len() as f64 * 1e4).round() / 1e4)
   299	    };
   300	    json!({

dev/adr/ADR-0.8.1-deferred-f9-confidence-importance.md:5:desc: Framing ADR for F9 — confidence-vs-importance weighting on graph-retrieved nodes and edges. Deferred pending consumer signal + eval results from the BYO-LLM ingest track (Slices 0/15) and the R2 parity eval (Slice 25). Captures what Slice 15 now provides, what signal is needed before deciding, and what the implementation would look like. RE-POINTED 2026-06-16 from 0.8.2+ → 0.8.5+ (0.8.2–0.8.4 re-sequenced for the graph-adjudication track); F9 may surface earlier only as an ablation knob inside the 0.8.2 M1 / 0.8.3 M2 PPR arm (confidence-weighted seeds/edges), not as an opened slice.

dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy.md:6:blast_radius: every module in fathomdb-engine; every binding error-mapping table; design/*.md error-handling sections; thiserror dep
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy.md:14:Phase 2 #18 design ADR. Decides Rust error design across the engine and its bindings.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy.md:72:Distinctness rationale: each is owned by its producing ADR (clean coupling); each maps to a different user remediation (fix input shape vs fix payload contents vs resolve an open-time embedder mismatch); `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError` doesn't even surface at write time. Collapsing into one `WriteError` would lose this signal and force `EmbedderIdentityMismatch` (an `Engine.open` error) into a misnamed bucket.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy.md:108:- `design/*.md` per-subsystem docs each list their owned error type + variants.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy.md:112:- `WriteValidationError`, `SchemaValidationError`, `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError` stay as distinct module errors per § Module-error boundary table; specific shapes live in design/engine.md, design/op-store.md, and design/embedder.md respectively.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-subprocess-bridge-deferral.md:6:blast_radius: bindings.md; future tooling work; design/*.md (no IPC layer)
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-subprocess-bridge-deferral.md:18:0.5.x rewrite proposal mentioned a possible subprocess bridge for non-Rust language bindings (or operator tools). 0.6.0 has no concrete consumer: TS via napi-rs in-process; Python via PyO3 in-process; CLI is sync; default embedder is in-process. Wire-format design without a forcing function is over-design.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-subprocess-bridge-deferral.md:30:**B — MessagePack / CBOR.** Tighter binary; faster; adds dep + tooling complexity; harder to debug. Premature.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-subprocess-bridge-deferral.md:32:**C — Defer entirely (chosen).** No 0.6.0 forcing function. Avoids Phase 3 design + interface work for a speculative feature. Revisit 0.8.0.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-subprocess-bridge-deferral.md:39:- Tracked: `followups.md` FU-WIRE15 (subprocess bridge design — 0.8.0 revisit).

dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism.md:6:blast_radius: architecture.md § 5 + § 8 + § 11 (amend "no sidecar" assertion); design/engine.md (runtime open path); design/bindings.md § 7 (lock contract); src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/database_lock.rs (existing sidecar code retained); src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/runtime.rs (PRAGMA application); REQ-022a/b; REQ-041 (single-file-deploy interpretation refined)
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism.md:55:- Reader connections in the same process use **default (NORMAL) locking_mode** and benefit from WAL multi-reader concurrency. This is required by REQ-018 ("reads do not serialize behind a single reader connection"); applying EXCLUSIVE to readers would defeat WAL's parallel-reader design.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism.md:62:This ADR commits the _invariants_; the concrete step ordering at `Engine.open` is owned by `design/engine.md` (runtime open path).
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism.md:114:- **REQ-041 (single-file-deploy) refinement.** Strict reading of REQ-041 ("operator deploys one binary + one `.sqlite` path") was already a clean-shutdown invariant, not an at-runtime invariant: WAL mode creates `-wal` (and pre-this-ADR also `-shm`); a crash leaves `-wal` orphaned. This ADR adds `.lock` as a third sidecar artifact during operation. After clean shutdown, `db.sqlite` + `db.sqlite.lock` remain; `-wal` auto-deletes; `-shm` was already absent under EXCLUSIVE. Operators must understand `db.sqlite.lock` is part of the database file set. REQ-041 wording does NOT need amendment — REQ-041 commits to "one binary + one path"; the sidecar lockfile is at the same path with a `.lock` suffix and is not an additional operator-managed artifact. **REQ-041 SHOULD gain a Cross-cite to this ADR** so future readers learn of the refined interpretation.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism.md:115:- **`DatabaseLocked` error variant.** Carries optional `holder_pid: Option<u32>`. Surfaces in bindings as a typed error per ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy. Variant added to `design/errors.md` taxonomy on the open-path module-error enum, surfacing on `EngineError` per ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy. (The exact module-error enum name — e.g. `EngineOpenError` — is owned by `design/errors.md`.)
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism.md:116:- **`design/engine.md` runtime open path** spec must implement the 10-step acquisition order in § 3.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism.md:120:- **Cross-binding lock contract** (`design/bindings.md` § 7) refreshed: SDK-native locking + sidecar flock; both layers; same-process two-Engine guaranteed by the union, not by either alone.

(1) Reclassify hybrid fusion+rerank (G9) and vector metadata columns (G10) from "differentiating" to table-stakes for the named consumer class. (2) Direct the deferred canonical-identity substrate (G0) to be designed bi-temporal-aware (G11) so supersession is not built twice.

dev/adr/ADR-0.8.0-canonical-identity-substrate.md:5:desc: Settle the canonical-identity substrate (G0 keystone) for 0.8.0. Inherits Option 2A (design bi-temporal-aware, ship the minimal transaction-time subset) from ADR-0.8.0-agent-memory-retrieval-and-identity. Settles four substrate questions as decisions, authorizes the exact Slice-15 schema delta verbatim (logical_id + superseded_at on canonical_nodes AND canonical_edges; partial-unique-active index; folded G4/G5 indexes; accretion exemption; SCHEMA_VERSION 11→12), states the forward-migration policy, flags the write_cursor-as-row-id deviation for HITL, and names the shadow vec0/FTS5 reconciliation as a reserved Slice-16 follow-on.

RE-POINTED 2026-06-16 from 0.8.2+ → 0.8.5+ (0.8.2–0.8.4 re-sequenced for the graph-adjudication track; lexical-baseline tuning must not be folded into the graph adjudication, where it would handicap the comparison).

dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-typescript-api-shape.md:18:Two binding philosophies. Mirror = same verb names, signatures, error shapes as Python (1:1). Idiomatic = TS conventions (camelCase, Promise everywhere, interface literals). Already constrained by ADR-0.6.0-async-surface Path 2: TS surface is Promise-returning.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:6:blast_radius: requirements.md; acceptance.md; design/engine.md; design/recovery.md; design/errors.md; ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy; FU-VEC13-CORRUPTION; FU-RECOVERY-CORRUPTION-DETECTION
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:18:`Engine.open` runs at process start. It applies schema migrations (REQ-042), warms the embedder (Invariant D, owned by `embedder`), acquires the hybrid database lock from ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism, and applies PRAGMAs. Corruption may surface on the open path through the always-on detection subset owned by `design/recovery.md`: WAL replay errors, header/format mismatch, schema inconsistency, and corrupt stored embedder-profile state.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:28:A separate forcing function: detection cadence ("WHAT do we check, WHEN") is design-owned (design/recovery.md), not ADR-owned. The ADR commits to the user-visible BEHAVIOR on detection; what triggers detection is a separate engineering decision.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:38:Failure surfaces as `EngineOpenError::Corruption(CorruptionDetail)`. This ADR commits to the OUTER variant + the SHAPE of `CorruptionDetail`; the inner enum _variant lists_ (`CorruptionKind`, `OpenStage`, `CorruptionLocator`) extend the ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy variant table and their authoritative enumeration is owned by `design/errors.md` (which extends the taxonomy variant table per its existing protocol).
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:47:    pub kind: CorruptionKind,        // enum; variants in design/errors.md
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:48:    pub stage: OpenStage,            // enum; variants in design/errors.md
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:49:    pub locator: CorruptionLocator,  // enum; variants in design/errors.md
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:64:- `CorruptionLocator` MUST NOT include a free-form `Unspecified` escape hatch. When SQLite reports an opaque error with no usable locator, the variant carries the SQLite extended code (e.g. `OpaqueSqliteError { sqlite_extended_code: i32 }`) so something is always captured. `design/errors.md` MUST justify every locator variant.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:70:`fathomdb recover` is the only path that can mutate a corruption-marked database. Its behavior is owned by `design/recovery.md` and out of this ADR's scope, except for one constraint: the recovery tool MUST require an explicit operator opt-in flag (e.g. `--accept-data-loss`) for any path that is not bit-preserving.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:72:### 4. Detection cadence is design-owned
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:74:This ADR does NOT mandate which checks run at every open or how expensive they may be. `design/recovery.md` owns the cadence policy: which checks are always-on vs doctor-only opt-in. In 0.6.0, opt-in integrity work lives on `doctor check-integrity` only; `Engine.open` has no env/config integrity knob. This ADR's commitment is purely behavioral: IF corruption is detected, REFUSE.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:76:`design/recovery.md` MUST enumerate the always-on detection set. Reducing that set is a behavior change requiring an ADR amendment (anti-regression clause).
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:88:**C — Open read-only on detected corruption.** Permits queries against possibly-inconsistent state. Rejected: hides escalation signal from operators; queries return possibly-wrong data with no error class to dispatch on.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:94:**F — Open succeeds; queries return `Corruption` per request (lazy / per-query corruption signal).** Mirrors how SQLite itself surfaces some `SQLITE_CORRUPT` paths. Rejected: collapses open-time vs query-time error class; forces every caller of every verb (`write`, `search`, `close`) to handle `Corruption`; pollutes the five-verb application surface contract (REQ-053) with a cross-cutting failure mode that is more appropriately surfaced once at open. Operators want a single binary signal at process start, not N signals per query.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:100:- **`design/engine.md` runtime open path** spec must wrap each stage in a corruption-detector that produces `CorruptionDetail` on failure.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:101:- **`design/errors.md` + `design/bindings.md`** must surface `EngineOpenError::Corruption` as a typed binding error (Python: `CorruptionError`; TypeScript: `CorruptionError`); covered transitively by REQ-056 / AC-060a.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:102:- **`design/recovery.md`** owns: which checks run at open (cadence), the doctor-only opt-in integrity surface, what `fathomdb recover` does, and the `--accept-data-loss` opt-in surface.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:103:- **ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy** variant table extended with the open-path `CorruptionKind` enum owned by `design/errors.md`; 0.6.0 `Engine.open` uses the four-kind surface `WalReplayFailure`, `HeaderMalformed`, `SchemaInconsistent`, `EmbedderIdentityDrift`.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior.md:104:- **FU-VEC13-CORRUPTION + FU-RECOVERY-CORRUPTION-DETECTION** resolved by this ADR + design/recovery.md ownership.

dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-projection-model.md:6:blast_radius: design/scheduler.md; design/vector.md; design/projections.md; ADR-0.6.0-async-surface Invariant A; ADR-0.6.0-projection-freshness-sli; ADR-0.6.0-scheduler-shape
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-projection-model.md:14:Phase 2 #16 design ADR. Central design question of the rewrite: when does projection computation happen relative to the originating write?
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-projection-model.md:46:4. **Adapter-level queue-depth monitoring + 429-equivalent shed.** Engine exposes `projection_queue_depth` and `embedder_saturation` metrics (per ADR-0.6.0-scheduler-shape § Observability). SDK bindings (Python and TypeScript) sample these metrics and shed write submissions before memory saturates by returning a typed `EngineError::Overloaded { queue_depth, threshold }`. CLI has no application write surface in 0.6.0. Threshold default + sampling cadence live in `design/scheduler.md`.
dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-projection-model.md:87:- `design/projections.md` documents the push model end-to-end (write → enqueue projection job → embedder dispatch → projection-row commit + cursor advance).

dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope.md:12:**Status:** accepted (HITL 2026-04-27); amended (HITL 2026-05-16) — `--purge-logical-id` and `--restore-logical-id` removed from 0.6.0 recover sub-flag set, originally deferred to 0.7.x (blocked on canonical identity substrate; see `design/recovery.md § Logical-id purge and restore`); further amended (HITL 2026-05-24) — deferral target moved from 0.7.x to **0.8.0** alongside Memex knowledge-store / retrieval features that consume the substrate (`dev/roadmap/0.8.0.md`). 0.7.0 is now perf-only. Further amended (2026-06-06, Slice 34 / reserved-gap-34) — the 0.8.0 op-store diagnostic read-back `fathomdb doctor dump-mutations <collection>` is scoped **IN** under the `doctor` root as a `dump-*` diagnostic over the mutation log (`operational_mutations`). This is **not** a re-opening of Option B: it adds no `search` / `get` / `list` application query surface over `canonical_nodes`; application query verbs still require this ADR to be re-opened. See the Consequences bullet below and `dev/design/slice-34-cli-op-store-readback-design.md`.

The reference code confirms two separate notions: strict required-doc recall for eu8, and a later diagnostic bucket that combines BM25 and dense rank. I’m checking the exact function lines and the qrels shape now so I can distinguish “matches metric” from “hard subset naming may drift.”

   300	
   301	fn parse_evidence(u: &Value) -> Result<EvidenceUnit, String> {
   302	    let evidence_id =
   303	        u.get("evidence_id").and_then(Value::as_str).ok_or("missing `evidence_id`")?.to_string();
   304	    let doc_id = u.get("doc_id").and_then(Value::as_str).ok_or("missing `doc_id`")?.to_string();
   305	    let nec_str = u.get("necessity").and_then(Value::as_str).ok_or("missing `necessity`")?;
   306	    let necessity =
   307	        Necessity::parse(nec_str).ok_or_else(|| format!("unknown necessity `{nec_str}`"))?;
   308	    let locator = u.get("locator").and_then(parse_locator);
   309	    Ok(EvidenceUnit { evidence_id, doc_id, necessity, locator })
   310	}
   311	
   312	/// Parse a `locator` object: required `kind`, optional `spans` (WI-3a). A
   313	/// malformed span entry is skipped (lenient, like the rest of the loader); span
   314	/// *bounds* are checked by [`validate_gold_set`], not here.
   315	fn parse_locator(v: &Value) -> Option<Locator> {
   316	    let m = v.as_object()?;
   317	    let kind = m.get("kind").and_then(Value::as_str)?.to_string();
   318	    let spans = m
   319	        .get("spans")
   320	        .and_then(Value::as_array)
   321	        .map(|arr| arr.iter().filter_map(parse_span).collect::<Vec<Span>>());
   322	    Some(Locator { kind, spans })
   323	}
   324	
   325	fn parse_span(v: &Value) -> Option<Span> {
   326	    let m = v.as_object()?;
   327	    let doc_id = m.get("doc_id").and_then(Value::as_str)?.to_string();
   328	    let start = m.get("start").and_then(Value::as_u64)? as usize;
   329	    let end = m.get("end").and_then(Value::as_u64)? as usize;
   330	    Some(Span { doc_id, start, end })
   331	}
   332	
   333	// ── Denominator derivation — the single seed unit-of-relevance (§(f)) ───────
   334	
   335	/// The authored REQUIRED-evidence denominator for a query (the doc_ids that must
   336	/// be present), as ONE unit of relevance (§(f) seed rule, codex round-5):
   337	/// - if `required_evidence` is present, the `necessity=required` units ARE the
   338	///   denominator (full stop) — even if that yields the empty set;
   339	/// - ONLY when `required_evidence` is entirely absent do `expected_top_k_doc_ids`
   340	///   map exactly once to degenerate whole-body required units (the eu8 reduction
   341	///   fallback). They are NEVER added on top of an evidence-labelled set.
   342	pub fn required_doc_ids(q: &GoldQuery) -> BTreeSet<String> {
   343	    if q.required_evidence.is_empty() {
   344	        return q.expected_top_k_doc_ids.iter().cloned().collect();
   345	    }
   346	    q.required_evidence
   347	        .iter()
   348	        .filter(|e| e.necessity == Necessity::Required)
   349	        .map(|e| e.doc_id.clone())
   350	        .collect()
   351	}
   352	
   353	/// The `supporting` doc_ids — corroborating context, in NEITHER recall number;
   354	/// reported only as the separate supporting-coverage diagnostic (§(a)/(b)).
   355	pub fn supporting_doc_ids(q: &GoldQuery) -> BTreeSet<String> {
   356	    q.required_evidence
   357	        .iter()
   358	        .filter(|e| e.necessity == Necessity::Supporting)
   359	        .map(|e| e.doc_id.clone())
   360	        .collect()
   361	}
   362	
   363	// ── Per-query Evidence Recall@K (§(a)) ───────────────────────────────────────
   364	
   365	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]
   366	pub struct PerQueryRecall {
   367	    /// 1.0 iff EVERY required unit is present in top-K, else 0.0 (the headline,
   368	    /// all-or-nothing). An empty required set is vacuously 1.0 — the aggregator
   369	    /// routes `Negative` queries away from recall and the validator rejects a
   370	    /// non-negative query with an empty denominator, so this only bites
   371	    /// mislabeled data the validator catches.
   372	    pub strict: f64,
   373	    /// `|required ∩ retrieved@K| / |required|` — the graded diagnostic over the
   374	    /// SAME required-only denominator as `strict` (directly comparable).
   375	    pub graded: f64,
   376	    /// `|supporting ∩ retrieved@K| / |supporting|` — separate diagnostic, never
   377	    /// in a recall number. NaN-free: 0.0 when there are no supporting units.
   378	    pub supporting_coverage: f64,
   379	    pub required_n: usize,
   380	    pub required_hits: usize,
   381	}
   382	
   383	/// Evidence Recall@K for one query given the mode's ranked `retrieved_doc_ids`
   384	/// (the harness cuts at `k`). Presence is at doc-body granularity (§(b)).
   385	pub fn evidence_recall_at_k(
   386	    q: &GoldQuery,
   387	    retrieved_doc_ids: &[String],
   388	    k: usize,
   389	) -> PerQueryRecall {
   390	    let topk: HashSet<&String> = retrieved_doc_ids.iter().take(k).collect();
   391	    let required = required_doc_ids(q);
   392	    let supporting = supporting_doc_ids(q);
   393	
   394	    let required_n = required.len();
   395	    let required_hits = required.iter().filter(|d| topk.contains(d)).count();
   396	    let strict = if required_n == 0 || required_hits == required_n { 1.0 } else { 0.0 };
   397	    let graded = if required_n == 0 { 1.0 } else { required_hits as f64 / required_n as f64 };
   398	
   399	    let sup_hits = supporting.iter().filter(|d| topk.contains(d)).count();
   400	    let supporting_coverage =
   401	        if supporting.is_empty() { 0.0 } else { sup_hits as f64 / supporting.len() as f64 };
   402	
   403	    PerQueryRecall { strict, graded, supporting_coverage, required_n, required_hits }
   404	}
   405	
   406	/// Abstention-correctness for a `Negative` ("not-found") query (§(d)): correct
   407	/// iff the retrieval returned NOTHING in top-K. A non-empty top-K is a false
   408	/// positive. Reported separately from recall.
   409	pub fn negative_abstained(retrieved_doc_ids: &[String], k: usize) -> bool {
   410	    retrieved_doc_ids.iter().take(k).next().is_none()
   411	}
   412	
   413	// ── Aggregation (per-class + overall + negative), per K (§(c)/(d)) ──────────
   414	
   415	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]
   416	pub struct ClassAgg {
   417	    pub n: usize,
   418	    pub strict_sum: f64,
   419	    pub graded_sum: f64,
   420	    pub supporting_sum: f64,
   421	}
   422	
   423	impl ClassAgg {
   424	    fn add(&mut self, m: &PerQueryRecall) {
   425	        self.n += 1;
   426	        self.strict_sum += m.strict;
   427	        self.graded_sum += m.graded;
   428	        self.supporting_sum += m.supporting_coverage;
   429	    }
   430	    pub fn strict(&self) -> f64 {

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! EU-7 — real-corpus AC validation + recall@10 anchor measurement.
     2	//!
     3	//! Per `dev/plans/prompts/0.7.1-EU-7-launch.md`. This harness validates
     4	//! AC-013 (vector retrieval latency), AC-013b (recall@10 vs f32 ground
     5	//! truth), and AC-019 (mixed-retrieval stress tail) against the **real
     6	//! default embedder** (`BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5`, candle-transformers,
     7	//! dim 384) over the **real corpus** (`data/corpus-data/raw/*.jsonl`),
     8	//! at dev-box scale.
     9	//!
    10	//! ## What this measures vs. perf_gates.rs
    11	//!
    12	//! `tests/perf_gates.rs::ac_013b_recall_at_10_floor` runs against a
    13	//! synthetic *isotropic* `VaryingEmbedder` and hard-asserts the
    14	//! HITL-locked 0.90 floor. Isotropic random vectors are the noise-
    15	//! limited case for sign-bit ANN; real anisotropic BGE embeddings are
    16	//! *easier* for sign-bit quantization (see
    17	//! `dev/plans/runs/STATUS-perf-vector-quant.md` "Fixture-replacement
    18	//! evaluation", post-correction). This harness measures the **real**
    19	//! number that 0.7.2 PR-2 will use to re-derive `AC013B_RECALL_FLOOR`
    20	//! as `R - 2*sigma`.
    21	//!
    22	//! ## Recall posture (AC-075, 0.8.0 Slice 40 / GA-2 — eu7 is the verdict)
    23	//!
    24	//! As of 0.8.0 Slice 40, this real-embedder harness is the **asserting**
    25	//! recall verdict (AC-075) on the real bge-small embedder over the real
    26	//! corpus. ◆ GA-3 (◆ HITL ruling 2026-06-08): the verdict is a **one-sided,
    27	//! CI-based** gate against the unchanged 0.90 floor — PASS iff the recall 95%
    28	//! bootstrap CI is not significantly below the floor (`recall_ci_hi >=
    29	//! CURRENT_FLOOR`), NOT a point-estimate `recall >= floor` assert. The 0.90
    30	//! floor CONSTANT is unchanged; the measured N=7667 point estimate 0.8960 (CI
    31	//! [0.8640, 0.9250]) clears the gate because ci_hi 0.925 ≥ 0.90. The
    32	//! point-estimate-≥0.90 recovery (the ~4pt 0.7.x→0.8.0 vector-stage drop) is a
    33	//! 0.8.1 item, and this CI form is to be revisited after 0.8.0. It supersedes
    34	//! the informal AC-013b floor assert: the synthetic
    35	//! `perf_gates::ac_013b_recall_at_10_floor` is now REPORT-ONLY.
    36	//!
    37	//! **◆ B-1 correction (GA-2, 2026-06-08).** The system-under-test is the
    38	//! engine's pre-fusion **VECTOR-STAGE** ranking (1-bit sign-quant K=192
    39	//! Hamming + f32 rerank), obtained via the test-only
    40	//! `set_vector_stage_only_for_test` seam — NOT the RRF-fused `search()`
    41	//! output. The ground truth is an exact-f32 VECTOR top-10, so measuring it
    42	//! against the unconditional-RRF hybrid `search()` (Slice 10, `d28d204`)
    43	//! conflated ANN-quantization FIDELITY (what the 0.90 floor gates) with
    44	//! intended fusion divergence — the cause of the Slice-40 Phase-A HALT
    45	//! (recall 0.8710 < 0.90), root-caused in
    46	//! `dev/plans/runs/GA-1-corpus-ab-20260608T012503Z.md`. The corrected
    47	//! vector-stage SUT measures recall@10 = 0.937 (CI 0.913–0.957). The
    48	//! harness ALSO reports the fused-`search()` recall (`EU7_RECALL_FUSED`,
    49	//! ~0.871) as the report-only delta, so the correction is demonstrably
    50	//! load-bearing, not cosmetic. This is the ANN-quantization FIDELITY axis,
    51	//! complementary to and NOT a substitute for the IR/relevance axis (eu8 /
    52	//! the IR-1 `ir-recall-measure.md`). K stays locked at 192;
    53	//! `AC013B_RECALL_FLOOR` is unchanged (its sentinel still pins 0.90).
    54	//! AC-013 latency and AC-019 stress remain REPORTED (dev-box scouting).
    55	//!
    56	//! ## Production-path fidelity
    57	//!
    58	//! The harness constructs the **real** `CandleBgeEmbedder` and supplies
    59	//! it via `EmbedderChoice::Caller`. Because its identity name is
    60	//! `fathomdb-bge-small-en-v1.5`, the engine's mean-centering apply paths
    61	//! engage exactly as they do for `EmbedderChoice::Default` (identity-
    62	//! gated; see `identity_requires_mean_centering`), the mean pins at
    63	//! `MEAN_VEC_PIN_THRESHOLD` (256) docs, and retrieval runs the locked
    64	//! K=192 bit-KNN + f32 rerank pipeline. Holding the embedder `Arc` lets
    65	//! the harness embed queries with the *same model* for the f32
    66	//! ground-truth pass (codex focus: ground truth must use the candidate
    67	//! model, not a different one).
    68	//!
    69	//! ## Gating
    70	//!
    71	//! Requires the `default-embedder` Cargo feature (real candle weights)
    72	//! AND `AGENT_LONG=1` (the embed pass is minutes of wall-clock). The
    73	//! warm-cache contract is the same as `eu5b_lockflip.rs`: weights are
    74	//! pre-fetched; set `FATHOMDB_SKIP_NETWORK_TESTS=1` to skip when the
    75	//! cache is cold and HF is unreachable.
    76	//!
    77	//! Run:
    78	//!   AGENT_LONG=1 cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features default-embedder \
    79	//!     --test eu7_real_corpus_ac -- --nocapture
    80	//!
    81	//! Tunables (env):
    82	//!   EU7_N_VALUES         comma-separated haystack sizes (default "1000,7667")
    83	//!   EU7_QUERIES          query-set size (default 100)
    84	//!   EU7_BOOTSTRAP        bootstrap resamples (default 1000)
    85	//!   EU7_LATENCY_SAMPLES  AC-013 measurement samples (default 1000)
    86	//!   EU7_STRESS_PER_THREAD AC-019 queries per stress thread (default 250)
    87	//!
    88	//! Seeding the real BGE embedder through the projection pipeline runs at
    89	//! ~1.3 docs/sec on a 24-core dev box (embed + sign-bit quantize + mean-
    90	//! centering + WAL commit per doc), so the full 7,667-doc corpus seeds in
    91	//! ~1.5 h. This is expected dev-box cost; canonical N=1M is 0.7.2 PR-3.
    92	
    93	#![cfg(feature = "default-embedder")]
    94	
    95	#[path = "support/corpus_subset.rs"]
    96	mod corpus_subset;
    97	#[path = "support/recall_gate.rs"]
    98	mod recall_gate;
    99	
   100	use std::collections::HashSet;
   101	use std::sync::{Arc, Barrier, Mutex};
   102	use std::thread;
   103	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
   104	
   105	use corpus_subset::{load_subset_or_skip, repo_root, Doc};
   106	use fathomdb_embedder::CandleBgeEmbedder;
   107	use fathomdb_embedder_api::{Embedder, EmbedderError, EmbedderIdentity, Vector};
   108	use fathomdb_engine::{EmbedderChoice, Engine, PreparedWrite};
   109	use serde_json::json;
   110	use tempfile::TempDir;
   111	
   112	/// Serializes `CandleBgeEmbedder::embed` behind a `Mutex`.
   113	///
   114	/// The engine's projection pool runs `PROJECTION_WORKERS` (=2) worker
   115	/// threads that call `embed()` concurrently on one shared instance.
   116	/// candle's own guidance is to guard a shared model (`Arc<RwLock<Model>>`)
   117	/// for concurrent CPU inference; this `Mutex` is that guard, applied
   118	/// harness-side without touching the engine's writer/projection contracts.
   119	/// It is a measurement-fidelity wrapper only: the produced vectors,
   120	/// identity, and mean-centering behaviour are byte-identical to the bare
   420	
   421	struct RecallResult {
   422	    /// AC-075 verdict: recall@10 of the ANN+ VECTOR-STAGE ranking (bit-KNN
   423	    /// K=192 + f32 rerank, pre-fusion) vs the exact-f32 VECTOR top-10 GT.
   424	    mean: f64,
   425	    ci_lo: f64,
   426	    ci_hi: f64,
   427	    sigma: f64,
   428	    /// ◆ B-1 delta (report-only): the same recall@10 method applied to the
   429	    /// production RRF-fused `search()` output (vector ⊕ FTS5) vs the same
   430	    /// vector-only GT. Lower than `mean` because a hybrid result legitimately
   431	    /// diverges from a vector-only top-10 — this is the fusion-divergence the
   432	    /// halted GA-1 attempt mis-measured as a quantization-fidelity drop.
   433	    fused_mean: f64,
   434	}
   435	
   436	/// For each query: embed with the same model, compute the brute-force f32
   437	/// VECTOR top-10 (excluding the target doc) as ground truth, and compare
   438	/// against the engine's ANN+ VECTOR-STAGE top-10 (sign-bit K=192 + f32
   439	/// rerank, pre-fusion, also with the target excluded). recall@10 =
   440	/// |prod ∩ gt| / 10 per query.
   441	///
   442	/// ◆ B-1 correction (GA-2, 2026-06-08): the system-under-test is the
   443	/// engine's pre-fusion VECTOR-branch ranking, obtained via the test-only
   444	/// `set_vector_stage_only_for_test` seam — NOT the RRF-fused `search()`
   445	/// output. The GT is a vector-only top-10, so measuring it against a
   446	/// hybrid (vector ⊕ FTS5) `search()` would conflate ANN-quantization
   447	/// FIDELITY (what the 0.90 floor gates) with intended fusion divergence
   448	/// (the GA-1 finding; see `dev/plans/runs/GA-1-corpus-ab-*.md`). We ALSO
   449	/// measure the fused `search()` output with the identical method and report
   450	/// it as `EU7_RECALL_FUSED` (the report-only delta) so the fused-vs-vector
   451	/// gap is legible — but the asserting verdict is the vector stage.
   452	///
   453	/// Ground truth is computed IN-RUST over `doc_vecs` (the same-model,
   454	/// uncentered, L2-normed embeddings of `bodies`, aligned by index) rather
   455	/// than via a second SQLite connection: a separate `rusqlite::Connection`
   456	/// to the live engine's WAL is a deadlock hazard against the engine's
   457	/// single-writer lock, and in-Rust brute force is both deadlock-free and
   458	/// exactly the f32 ranking the engine's rerank step targets (the engine
   459	/// reranks on uncentered f32; mean-centering only biases the sign-bit
   460	/// candidate stage). Ranking by L2 over unit vectors recovers cosine
   461	/// order (vectors are unit-norm per protocol Invariant 1).
   462	///
   463	/// Target exclusion (codex focus): the query is synthesized from a doc;
   464	/// that doc trivially self-retrieves and would inflate recall by a free
   465	/// shared hit. We drop it from BOTH the ground-truth top-10 (computed as
   466	/// top-11, target removed) and the production results. Because the engine's
   467	/// LIMIT is locked at 10, a query whose target self-retrieves into
   468	/// production's literal top-10 yields at most 9 comparable slots — a small
   469	/// structural bias that pushes recall DOWN (conservative for a floor
   470	/// anchor). Documented in the honesty report.
   548	        // ◆ B-1: the VERDICT SUT is the engine's pre-fusion VECTOR-STAGE
   549	        // ranking (ANN+ bit-KNN K=192 + f32 rerank), via the measurement seam.
   550	        engine.set_vector_stage_only_for_test(true);
   551	        let prod_vec: Vec<String> = engine
   552	            .search(&q.text)
   553	            .expect("prod vector-stage search")
   554	            .results
   555	            .iter()
   556	            .map(|h| h.body.clone())
   557	            .collect();
   558	        // Report-only DELTA SUT: the production RRF-fused `search()` output
   559	        // (vector ⊕ FTS5), measured with the identical method against the same
   560	        // vector-only GT, so the fused-vs-vector gap is legible.
   561	        engine.set_vector_stage_only_for_test(false);
   562	        let prod_fused: Vec<String> = engine
   563	            .search(&q.text)
   564	            .expect("prod fused search")
   565	            .results
   566	            .iter()
   567	            .map(|h| h.body.clone())
   568	            .collect();
   569	
   570	        // OLD recall (diagnostic, on the FUSED output): literal top-10,
   571	        // exclude target after, /10 — kept for continuity of the OLD report.
   572	        let prod_top10: Vec<&str> = prod_fused.iter().take(10).map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();
   573	        if prod_top10.contains(&target) {
   574	            target_in_top10 += 1;
   575	        }
   576	        let hits_old =
   577	            prod_top10.iter().filter(|b| **b != target).filter(|b| gt_old_set.contains(*b)).count();
   578	        per_query_old.push(hits_old as f64 / 10.0);
   579	
   580	        // NEW recall (exclude-before + dedup, take 10, /10) on BOTH SUTs:
   581	        //   per_query_new  = VECTOR-STAGE ranking → the AC-075 verdict.
   582	        //   per_query_fused = RRF-fused search()  → the report-only delta.
   583	        per_query_new.push(recall_new(&prod_vec, &gt_new_set, target));
   584	        per_query_fused.push(recall_new(&prod_fused, &gt_new_set, target));
   585	    }
   586	
   587	    let mean_old = per_query_old.iter().sum::<f64>() / per_query_old.len().max(1) as f64;
   588	    let (lo_old, hi_old, sg_old) = bootstrap_ci(&per_query_old, bootstrap_resamples);
   589	    eprintln!(
   590	        "EU7_RECALL_OLD exclude-after+setGT(fused) recall@10={mean_old:.4} ci=[{lo_old:.4},{hi_old:.4}] sigma={sg_old:.4} target_in_top10={target_in_top10}/{}",
   591	        queries.len()
   592	    );
   593	    // ◆ B-1 verdict: recall@10 of the ANN+ VECTOR STAGE vs exact-f32 vector GT.
   594	    let mean = per_query_new.iter().sum::<f64>() / per_query_new.len().max(1) as f64;
   595	    let (ci_lo, ci_hi, sigma) = bootstrap_ci(&per_query_new, bootstrap_resamples);
   596	    eprintln!(
   597	        "EU7_RECALL_VECTOR exclude-before+dedupGT vector-stage recall@10={mean:.4} ci=[{ci_lo:.4},{ci_hi:.4}] sigma={sigma:.4} (AC-075 VERDICT SUT)"
   598	    );
   599	    // ◆ B-1 report-only delta: the same method on the production RRF-fused
   600	    // search() output. Lower than the vector-stage verdict because a hybrid
   601	    // result legitimately diverges from a vector-only top-10 (the GA-1 finding).
   602	    let fused_mean = per_query_fused.iter().sum::<f64>() / per_query_fused.len().max(1) as f64;
   603	    let (f_lo, f_hi, f_sg) = bootstrap_ci(&per_query_fused, bootstrap_resamples);
   604	    eprintln!(
   605	        "EU7_RECALL_FUSED exclude-before+dedupGT fused-search recall@10={fused_mean:.4} ci=[{f_lo:.4},{f_hi:.4}] sigma={f_sg:.4} (report-only delta; NOT the floor SUT)"
   606	    );
   607	    eprintln!(
   608	        "EU7_RECALL_DELTA vector_stage={mean:.4} fused_search={fused_mean:.4} delta={:.4} \
   609	         (correction is load-bearing: the floor gates the vector stage, not the hybrid output)",
   610	        mean - fused_mean

dev/learnings.md:38:- Runtime DDL on admin path (per-kind `vec_<Kind>` virtual tables). Cite: `dev/notes/0.5.7-corrected-scope.md` Diagnosis + F1 (`bootstrap.rs:1318-1347`). Why it broke: invalidates `prepare_cached` on shared pool → `SQLITE_SCHEMA` (code 17) flood across read path.

dev/acceptance.md:61:| P-PERF-SAMPLES       | AC-012, AC-013, AC-017, AC-019 | Minimum measured samples per percentile calculation                                       | 1,000                                                                                        | ADR-0.6.0-text-query-latency-gates (sets ≥ 1,000 for text); applied uniformly to all latency ACs |
dev/acceptance.md:293:**Measurement:** Per ADR-0.6.0-text-query-latency-gates workload (warmup discard + second-pass measurement, QPS=1, 50–90th percentile token-frequency band); CI gate fails if either percentile exceeds.
dev/acceptance.md:301:**Measurement:** Per ADR-0.6.0-retrieval-latency-gates workload (warmup discard + second-pass, QPS=1); CI gate fails if either percentile exceeds.
dev/acceptance.md:310:**Measurement:** Single CLI execution against the seeded fixture; CI gate fails if wall-clock > 500 ms. (Single-sample assertion sufficient — gate is a hard ceiling, not a percentile.)
dev/acceptance.md:1139:**Assertion:** `eu7` real-corpus recall@10 of the **ANN+ vector stage** (1-bit sign-quant K=192 Hamming + f32 rerank) vs the **exact-f32 VECTOR top-10** of the same embedder ≥ **0.90** — an ANN-quantization **FIDELITY** gate, measured on the **vector stage in isolation** (NOT the RRF-fused `search()` output). MET: bge-small over the real corpus (N≈7,667, K=192) measures vector-stage recall@10 = **0.937** (bootstrap CI 0.913–0.957, σ 0.0116) — full CI clears 0.90. **◆ B-1 rationale:** the eu7 ground truth is a vector-only top-10, but Slice 10 (`d28d204`) made `search()` unconditional RRF-hybrid (vector ⊕ FTS5); measuring a hybrid result against a vector-only GT conflated quantization fidelity with intended fusion divergence (the Slice-40 Phase-A HALT, recall 0.8710; see `dev/plans/runs/GA-1-corpus-ab-20260608T012503Z.md`). The fused-`search()` recall (~0.871) is reported as the report-only delta `EU7_RECALL_FUSED`. The synthetic `ac_013b` is demoted to a REPORT-ONLY quantization-fidelity signal (`RECALL_FIDELITY_INFO`, ~0.73–0.89 on isotropic noise — the noise-limited worst case for sign-bit ANN, not a product floor); the `AC013B_RECALL_FLOOR = 0.90` constant and its sentinel are retained.
dev/acceptance.md:1140:**0.8.0 GA-3 asserting form (◆ HITL ruling 2026-06-08; CI-based reconciliation, floor target 0.90 UNCHANGED):** the 0.8.0 eu7 asserting verdict is "real-embedder eu7 vector-stage recall@10 whose **95% bootstrap CI is not significantly below 0.90** (`recall_ci_hi >= 0.90`)" — a **one-sided** gate (not a two-sided "floor ∈ [ci_lo, ci_hi]" test, which would wrongly fail a comfortably-high recall whose whole CI clears the floor). The prior point-estimate hard-assert (`recall >= 0.90`) PANICKED at the measured N=7667 result; the floor **constant stays 0.90** (`CURRENT_FLOOR`/`AC013B_RECALL_FLOOR` unchanged — not lowered). **Measured result satisfies the gate:** point estimate **0.896**, CI **[0.864, 0.925]** → ci_hi **0.925 ≥ 0.90 ⇒ PASS** (within measurement uncertainty / "rounding-error territory" per the HITL ruling — the 0.90 floor lies inside the 95% CI). The **point-estimate-≥0.90 recovery** and the **~4pt 0.7.x→0.8.0 vector-stage drop** (0.937→0.896) diagnosis are **0.8.1** items. This CI-form is a **0.8.0-scoped reconciliation to be REVISITED after 0.8.0**. Predicate `recall_ci_clears_floor` in `tests/support/recall_gate.rs`, asserted in `eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs`, unit-demonstrated (pass-at-recorded / bite-below-floor) in `tests/ga2_vector_stage_seam.rs`. (The 0.937 cited above is the superseded 0.7.x-inferred number; the real 0.8.0 re-measure is 0.896 — see `dev/plans/runs/GA-signoff-eu7-remeasure-20260608T172804Z.json` + STATUS-0.8.0 § 7.)

dev/DOC-INDEX.md:52:| `dev/design/0.8.2-m1-multihop-harness.md` | **0.8.2 / M1 multi-hop answer-accuracy harness — design + FROZEN pre-registration (AMENDED 2026-06-16; re-frozen 0-rev2 2026-06-19)** (`status: decision-ready`): MuSiQue-Ans 2/3/4-hop datasets, the 5 arms (bm25 / passage-dense / fused(RRF k=60) / fused+rerank / ppr-fusion), the **fixed comparator = `fused-RRF`** (re-frozen at 0-rev2 from the earlier `fused+rerank`, data-justified by the $0 bridge-vs-answer diagnostic; never per-hop max), **`MATERIAL_F1_LIFT = 0.04`** (re-frozen from 0.02/0.03), the **same-answerer** protocol, ablations + bias controls, the budget plan (flash-lite cheap-validate gate + answerer-call model), the dated frozen pre-registration block (primary endpoint = **paired pooled ≥3-hop ΔF1 vs fused-RRF** with bootstrap CI; per-hop = secondary feeding a **trend gate** that vetoes only on a significantly negative slope; EM CI-banded; unanswerable-set carries confident-wrong; decision rule frozen as `eval/m1_decision_rule.py::decide(material, em, trend, confident_wrong, power_ok)`; **whole-rule power-sim** plan) + the falsifiable Slice 5/15/20 AC list. **Applied at Slice 20 → verdict NO-GO.** Cross-links: `dev/plans/plan-0.8.2.md` §4, `dev/roadmap/0.8.2.md`, `runs/0.8.2-m1-FINDINGS.md`, `runs/0.8.2-slice-0-prereg-methodology-review.md` | 0.8.2 Slice 0-rev2 | 2026-06-19 |

Dense/fused arms are in-harness (justified: canonical `.so` has `default-reranker` but not `test-hooks` + no production vector-kind surface). **Slice-15 prerequisite:** PPR arm MUST use the same CLS space | 0.8.2 Slice 5 + fix-2 | 2026-06-18 |

dev/plans/runs/C1-threadsafe2-rebuild-20260504T022727Z.log:38:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FqXbFRfWBQyLSHnrPMGoqT","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n2\t\n3\tOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Cross-vendor convention. This is the canonical agent-instruction file; **no `CLAUDE.md` is maintained** — Claude Code reads `AGENTS.md` natively.\n4\t\n5\tBullet form, prescriptive, ≤300 lines. Link out, do not inline.\n6\t\n7\t## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n8\t\n9\t- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n10\t- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n11\t- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n12\t- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n13\t- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n14\t- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n15\t- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## 1. Invariants — read these first\n20\t\n21\t- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n22\t- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n23\t- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n24\t- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n25\t- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n26\t\n27\t## 2. Repo shape\n28\t\n29\t- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n30\t- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n31\t- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n32\t- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n33\t- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n34\t\n35\t## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n36\t\n37\tUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n38\t\n39\t| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n40\t| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n41\t| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n42\t| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n43\t| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n44\t| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n45\t| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n46\t\n47\tMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n48\t\n49\tRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n50\t\n51\tThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n52\t\n53\t### One-time setup\n54\t\n55\t- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n56\t- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n57\t- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n58\t- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n59\t\n60\t## 4. Verification ordering\n61\t\n62\tRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n63\t\n64\t1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n65\t2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n66\t3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n67\t4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n68\t\n69\tDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n70\t\n71\t## 5. Test discipline\n72\t\n73\t- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n74\t- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n75\t- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n76\t- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n77\t- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n78\t\n79\t## 6. Comment policy\n80\t\n81\t- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n82\t- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n83\t- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n84\t- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n85\t\n86\t## 7. Subagent rules\n87\t\n88\t- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n89\t- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n90\t- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n91\t- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n92\t- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n93\t\n94\t## 8. Iteration discipline\n95\t\n96\t- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n97\t- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n98\t- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n99\t\n100\t## 9. Pointers\n101\t\n102\t- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n103\t- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n104\t- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n105\t- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n106\t- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n107\t- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n108\t\n109\t## 10. Forbidden patterns\n110\t\n111\tPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n112\t\n113\t- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n114\t- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n115\t- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n116\t- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n117\t- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n118\t- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n119\t- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n120\t- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n121\t- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n122\t- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n123\t\n124\t## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n125\t\n126\t`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n127\t\n128\t- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n129\t- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n130\t- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n131\t\n132\tIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n133\t\n134\t## 12. Working with this file\n135\t\n136\t- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n137\t- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n138\t- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n139\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8996a1e6-f0cf-41f6-bd76-ad56a5a9b42e","uuid":"7059d771-1a4a-4040-ad22-da3cf4c091a0","timestamp":"2026-05-04T02:27:33.528Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-pack5-C1-threadsafe2-rebuild-20260504T022727Z/AGENTS.md","content":"# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n\nOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Cross-vendor convention. This is the canonical agent-instruction file; **no `CLAUDE.md` is maintained** — Claude Code reads `AGENTS.md` natively.\n\nBullet form, prescriptive, ≤300 lines. Link out, do not inline.\n\n## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n\n- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Invariants — read these first\n\n- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n\n## 2. Repo shape\n\n- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n\n## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n\nUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n\n| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n\nMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n\nRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n\nThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n\n### One-time setup\n\n- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n\n## 4. Verification ordering\n\nRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n\n1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n\nDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n\n## 5. Test discipline\n\n- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n\n## 6. Comment policy\n\n- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n\n## 7. Subagent rules\n\n- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n\n## 8. Iteration discipline\n\n- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n\n## 9. Pointers\n\n- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n\n## 10. Forbidden patterns\n\nPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n\n- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n\n## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n\n`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n\n- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n\nIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n\n## 12. Working with this file\n\n- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n","numLines":139,"startLine":1,"totalLines":139}}}
