dev/plans/runs/12-S-fix-1-review-20260517T201827Z.log:3041:- Bootstrap behavior is correct. [scripts/bootstrap.sh](/tmp/fdb-12-S-security-fixtures-20260517T195735Z/scripts/bootstrap.sh:47) does `sudo apt-get update -qq` before install, redirects it quietly, and uses `|| true`, so a flaky mirror does not abort bootstrap; install failure degrades to a clear warning at [scripts/bootstrap.sh](/tmp/fdb-12-S-security-fixtures-20260517T195735Z/scripts/bootstrap.sh:54).
dev/plans/runs/12-S-fix-1-review-20260517T201827Z.log:3047:- AC-038 appears untouched by `d25c55d` and `e77265c`; those commits only touch security scripts, fixtures, `agent-verify`, and `bootstrap`. I did not rerun the cargo test in this sandbox.
dev/plans/runs/12-S-fix-1-review-20260517T201827Z.log:3075:- Bootstrap behavior is correct. [scripts/bootstrap.sh](/tmp/fdb-12-S-security-fixtures-20260517T195735Z/scripts/bootstrap.sh:47) does `sudo apt-get update -qq` before install, redirects it quietly, and uses `|| true`, so a flaky mirror does not abort bootstrap; install failure degrades to a clear warning at [scripts/bootstrap.sh](/tmp/fdb-12-S-security-fixtures-20260517T195735Z/scripts/bootstrap.sh:54).
dev/plans/runs/12-S-fix-1-review-20260517T201827Z.log:3081:- AC-038 appears untouched by `d25c55d` and `e77265c`; those commits only touch security scripts, fixtures, `agent-verify`, and `bootstrap`. I did not rerun the cargo test in this sandbox.
dev/plans/runs/0.7.0-PVQ-P2-RED-20260527T144439Z.log:32:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NpKD3PT8ad93TtN442Gkir","type":"tool_result","content":"130\t}\n131\t\n132\t// ── AC-012 / AC-013 / AC-019 retrieval perf-gate fixtures ───────────────────\n133\t//\n134\t// Per `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-D-retrieval-perf-fixtures.md` and\n135\t// ADR-0.6.0-{text-query,retrieval}-latency-gates the canonical fixture\n136\t// scale is 1,000,000 chunk rows / 768-d vectors. Seeding 1M rows under\n137\t// AGENT_LONG=1 on the aarch64 dev runner is hours-of-wall-clock; the\n138\t// canonical x86_64 tier-1 CI runner sets `AC_FULL_SCALE=1` to honor\n139\t// the 1M scale. AGENT_LONG=1 on the dev runner uses the env-tunable\n140\t// scale below (AC-007a/b runner-pin precedent) and asserts the same\n141\t// budget. See `dev/test-plan.md` § Current Perf Attribution and\n142\t// `dev/notes/performance-whitepaper-notes.md` for measured medians.\n143\tconst AC012_DEFAULT_N: usize = 100_000;\n144\tconst AC013_DEFAULT_N: usize = 50_000;\n145\tconst AC019_THREADS: usize = 8;\n146\tconst AC019_QUERIES_PER_THREAD: usize = 250;\n147\tconst AC012_BUDGET_P50: Duration = Duration::from_millis(20);\n148\tconst AC012_BUDGET_P99: Duration = Duration::from_millis(150);\n149\tconst AC013_BUDGET_P50: Duration = Duration::from_millis(50);\n150\tconst AC013_BUDGET_P99: Duration = Duration::from_millis(200);\n151\tconst AC019_STRESS_FLOOR: Duration = Duration::from_millis(150);\n152\tconst AC019_STRESS_MULT: u32 = 10;\n153\tconst RETRIEVAL_VECTOR_DIM: u32 = 768;\n154\t\n155\tfn env_usize(key: &str, default_full: usize, default_short: usize) -> usize {\n156\t    if let Ok(raw) = std::env::var(key) {\n157\t        if let Ok(parsed) = raw.parse::<usize>() {\n158\t            return parsed;\n159\t        }\n160\t    }\n161\t    if std::env::var_os(\"AC_FULL_SCALE\").is_some() {\n162\t        default_full\n163\t    } else {\n164\t        default_short\n165\t    }\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\tfn ac012_corpus_n() -> usize {\n169\t    env_usize(\"AC012_CORPUS_N\", 1_000_000, AC012_DEFAULT_N)\n170\t}\n171\t\n172\tfn ac013_corpus_n() -> usize {\n173\t    env_usize(\"AC013_CORPUS_N\", 1_000_000, AC013_DEFAULT_N)\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\t/// Deterministic seeded LCG. Generates reproducible token streams so the\n177\t/// AC-012 corpus and the held-out query set are both byte-stable across\n178\t/// runs (per ADR-0.6.0-text-query-latency-gates \"synthetic-English-like\n179\t/// text with a Zipfian token-frequency distribution\").\n180\tstruct SeededRng {\n181\t    state: u64,\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\timpl SeededRng {\n185\t    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self {\n186\t        Self { state: seed.wrapping_mul(0x9E37_79B9_7F4A_7C15).wrapping_add(1) }\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    fn next_u64(&mut self) -> u64 {\n190\t        self.state = self.state.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);\n191\t        self.state\n192\t    }\n193\t\n194\t    fn next_in(&mut self, bound: usize) -> usize {\n195\t        (self.next_u64() as usize) % bound\n196\t    }\n197\t\n198\t    fn next_f64(&mut self) -> f64 {\n199\t        (self.next_u64() >> 11) as f64 / ((1u64 << 53) as f64)\n200\t    }\n201\t}\n202\t\n203\t/// Vocabulary of synthetic English-like tokens. Size ~1024 keeps the\n204\t/// FTS index dense; tokens are short, ASCII, lowercase, deterministic.\n205\tfn perf_vocab() -> Vec<String> {\n206\t    let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(1024);\n207\t    for i in 0..1024 {\n208\t        let a = (b'a' + ((i / 26 / 26) % 26) as u8) as char;\n209\t        let b = (b'a' + ((i / 26) % 26) as u8) as char;\n210\t        let c = (b'a' + (i % 26) as u8) as char;\n211\t        out.push(format!(\"{a}{b}{c}{i:04}\"));\n212\t    }\n213\t    out\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\t/// Sample a token index under a Zipfian-ish distribution with shape s=1.0,\n217\t/// using inverse-CDF on a precomputed cumulative weight table. Returns an\n218\t/// index into the vocabulary in [0, vocab_size).\n219\tfn zipf_index(rng: &mut SeededRng, cumulative: &[f64]) -> usize {\n220\t    let r = rng.next_f64() * cumulative[cumulative.len() - 1];\n221\t    match cumulative.binary_search_by(|w| w.partial_cmp(&r).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)) {\n222\t        Ok(idx) => idx,\n223\t        Err(idx) => idx.min(cumulative.len() - 1),\n224\t    }\n225\t}\n226\t\n227\tfn zipf_cumulative(vocab_size: usize) -> Vec<f64> {\n228\t    let mut cumulative = Vec::with_capacity(vocab_size);\n229\t    let mut acc = 0.0_f64;\n230\t    for k in 1..=vocab_size {\n231\t        acc += 1.0_f64 / k as f64;\n232\t        cumulative.push(acc);\n233\t    }\n234\t    cumulative\n235\t}\n236\t\n237\t/// Generate one synthetic chunk body of approximately `target_bytes`\n238\t/// (~500 B per ADR) using the Zipfian sampler. Tokens are space-joined.\n239\tfn synth_chunk_body(rng: &mut SeededRng, vocab: &[String], cumulative: &[f64]) -> String {\n240\t    // Tokens average ~7 chars + 1 separator, target ~500 B => ~63 tokens.\n241\t    let mut body = String::with_capacity(512);\n242\t    let token_count = 55 + rng.next_in(20);\n243\t    for i in 0..token_count {\n244\t        if i > 0 {\n245\t            body.push(' ');\n246\t        }\n247\t        body.push_str(&vocab[zipf_index(rng, cumulative)]);\n248\t    }\n249\t    body\n250\t}\n251\t\n252\t/// Choose a held-out query token from the 50th–90th percentile\n253\t/// term-frequency band per ADR-0.6.0-text-query-latency-gates. Vocab is\n254\t/// indexed by descending frequency rank (rank 0 most frequent), so the\n255\t/// band maps to indices [0.10*vocab, 0.50*vocab) (frequency rank space).\n256\t/// The Zipfian sampler yields rank 0 most often, so this band carves out\n257\t/// the body of the distribution.\n258\tfn ac012_query_token_band(vocab: &[String]) -> Vec<String> {\n259\t    let lo = vocab.len() / 10;\n260\t    let hi = vocab.len() / 2;\n261\t    vocab[lo..hi].to_vec()\n262\t}\n263\t\n264\t/// AC-012 deterministic seeder: 1 chunk body per write batch (4096 nodes\n265\t/// per `engine.write()`). Returns elapsed seed time for diagnostics.\n266\tfn seed_ac012_corpus(engine: &Engine, n: usize) -> Duration {\n267\t    const BATCH: usize = 4096;\n268\t    let vocab = perf_vocab();\n269\t    let cumulative = zipf_cumulative(vocab.len());\n270\t    let mut rng = SeededRng::new(0x0AC0_12C0_12C0);\n271\t    let started = Instant::now();\n272\t    let mut written = 0usize;\n273\t    while written < n {\n274\t        let take = BATCH.min(n - written);\n275\t        let mut batch = Vec::with_capacity(take);\n276\t        for _ in 0..take {\n277\t            batch.push(PreparedWrite::Node {\n278\t                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n279\t                body: synth_chunk_body(&mut rng, &vocab, &cumulative),\n280\t                source_id: None,\n281\t            });\n282\t        }\n283\t        engine.write(&batch).expect(\"ac-012 seed write\");\n284\t        written += take;\n285\t    }\n286\t    // No vector kind configured -> projection runtime still drains FTS index.\n287\t    engine.drain(600_000).expect(\"ac-012 drain\");\n288\t    started.elapsed()\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t/// Deterministic varying-vector embedder. Projects an input token's\n292\t/// stable hash onto a single coordinate per call so vec0 ANN search\n293\t/// returns distinct k=10 neighbors (a constant-vector embedder would\n294\t/// collapse all distances to 0). Reproducible byte-for-byte across runs.\n295\t#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\n296\tstruct VaryingEmbedder {\n297\t    identity: EmbedderIdentity,\n298\t    dim: u32,\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\timpl VaryingEmbedder {\n302\t    fn new(dim: u32) -> Self {\n303\t        Self { identity: EmbedderIdentity::new(\"varying\", \"perf-gates\", dim), dim }\n304\t    }\n305\t\n306\t    fn vector_for(&self, text: &str) -> Vector {\n307\t        let dim = self.dim as usize;\n308\t        let mut v = vec![0.0_f32; dim];\n309\t        // FNV-1a 64-bit on the input; spread across coordinates with\n310\t        // deterministic small magnitudes so distance is meaningful.\n311\t        let mut h: u64 = 0xcbf29ce484222325;\n312\t        for &b in text.as_bytes() {\n313\t            h ^= b as u64;\n314\t            h = h.wrapping_mul(0x100000001b3);\n315\t        }\n316\t        // Place mass on a small handful of coordinates derived from h.\n317\t        for k in 0..6 {\n318\t            let coord = ((h >> (k * 8)) as usize) % dim;\n319\t            let sign = if (h >> (k * 8 + 7)) & 1 == 0 { 1.0 } else { -1.0 };\n320\t            v[coord] += sign * 0.5_f32;\n321\t        }\n322\t        // Normalize-ish so all vectors have similar magnitude.\n323\t        let norm: f32 = v.iter().map(|x| x * x).sum::<f32>().sqrt().max(1e-6);\n324\t        for x in &mut v {\n325\t            *x /= norm;\n326\t        }\n327\t        v\n328\t    }\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\timpl Embedder for VaryingEmbedder {\n332\t    fn identity(&self) -> EmbedderIdentity {\n333\t        self.identity.clone()\n334\t    }\n335\t\n336\t    fn embed(&self, text: &str) -> Result<Vector, EmbedderError> {\n337\t        Ok(self.vector_for(text))\n338\t    }\n339\t}\n340\t\n341\t/// AC-013 deterministic seeder: N vector rows with varying embeddings.\n342\t/// Returns elapsed seed time. Bodies double as both FTS5 documents and\n343\t/// the input to the embedder, so the same fixture supports AC-019's\n344\t/// FTS+vector mixed workload.\n345\tfn seed_ac013_corpus(engine: &Engine, n: usize) -> Duration {\n346\t    const BATCH: usize = 1024;\n347\t    let vocab = perf_vocab();\n348\t    let cumulative = zipf_cumulative(vocab.len());\n349\t    let mut rng = SeededRng::new(0x0AC0_13D0_13D0);\n350\t    engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(\"doc\").expect(\"vector kind\");\n351\t    let started = Instant::now();\n352\t    let mut written = 0usize;\n353\t    while written < n {\n354\t        let take = BATCH.min(n - written);\n355\t        let mut batch = Vec::with_capacity(take);\n356\t        for _ in 0..take {\n357\t            batch.push(PreparedWrite::Node {\n358\t                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n359\t                body: synth_chunk_body(&mut rng, &vocab, &cumulative),\n360\t                source_id: None,\n361\t            });\n362\t        }\n363\t        engine.write(&batch).expect(\"ac-013 seed write\");\n364\t        written += take;\n365\t    }\n366\t    engine.drain(1_800_000).expect(\"ac-013 drain\");\n367\t    started.elapsed()\n368\t}\n369\t\n370\t/// Build a held-out reproducible query set drawn from the same\n371\t/// distribution as the indexed corpus (ADR-0.6.0-retrieval-latency-gates\n372\t/// \"Query vectors drawn from a held-out slice of the same distribution\").\n373\tfn ac013_query_bodies(count: usize) -> Vec<String> {\n374\t    let vocab = perf_vocab();\n375\t    let cumulative = zipf_cumulative(vocab.len());\n376\t    // Use a different seed than the corpus so the query set is held out\n377\t    // (not byte-equal to seeded chunks but drawn from same distribution).\n378\t    let mut rng = SeededRng::new(0x0AC0_130D_EC0D_E000);\n379\t    (0..count).map(|_| synth_chunk_body(&mut rng, &vocab, &cumulative)).collect()\n380\t}\n381\t\n382\t/// Bounded-size histogram for AC-019 tail-latency capture. Single\n383\t/// power-of-two bucketing in microseconds; avoids unbounded\n384\t/// `Vec<Duration>` per the Pack D plan (`Histogram::record` style).\n385\tstruct LatencyHistogram {\n386\t    /// Bucket i spans [2^i us, 2^(i+1) us). 32 buckets covers 1 us .. ~71 minutes.\n387\t    buckets: [u64; 32],\n388\t    count: u64,\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\timpl LatencyHistogram {\n392\t    fn new() -> Self {\n393\t        Self { buckets: [0; 32], count: 0 }\n394\t    }\n395\t\n396\t    fn record(&mut self, d: Duration) {\n397\t        let us = d.as_micros().max(1) as u64;\n398\t        let bucket = (63 - us.leading_zeros()) as usize;\n399\t        let bucket = bucket.min(self.buckets.len() - 1);\n400\t        self.buckets[bucket] += 1;\n401\t        self.count += 1;\n402\t    }\n403\t\n404\t    fn merge(&mut self, other: &LatencyHistogram) {\n405\t        for i in 0..self.buckets.len() {\n406\t            self.buckets[i] += other.buckets[i];\n407\t        }\n408\t        self.count += other.count;\n409\t    }\n410\t\n411\t    /// Return the upper bound of the bucket containing the requested\n412\t    /// percentile (numerator/denominator). Conservative ceiling.\n413\t    fn percentile_ceil(&self, numerator: u64, denominator: u64) -> Duration {\n414\t        if self.count == 0 {\n415\t            return Duration::ZERO;\n416\t        }\n417\t        let target = (self.count * numerator).div_ceil(denominator);\n418\t        let mut acc: u64 = 0;\n419\t        for (i, count) in self.buckets.iter().enumerate() {\n420\t            acc += count;\n421\t            if acc >= target {\n422\t                let upper_us = 1u64 << (i + 1);\n423\t                return Duration::from_micros(upper_us);\n424\t            }\n425\t        }\n426\t        let i = self.buckets.len() - 1;\n427\t        Duration::from_micros(1u64 << (i + 1))\n428\t    }\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t#[test]\n432\tfn ac_012_text_query_latency_on_fts5_path() {\n433\t    if !long_run_enabled() {\n434\t        return;\n435\t    }\n436\t\n437\t    let n = ac012_corpus_n();\n438\t    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"ac012_text_query\");\n439\t    let opened = Engine::open_without_embedder_for_test(&path).expect(\"open\");\n440\t    let seed_elapsed = seed_ac012_corpus(&opened.engine, n);\n441\t\n442\t    // Build the held-out query token band (50th–90th percentile term\n443\t    // frequency per the ADR). Reproducible: seeded from the vocab order.\n444\t    let vocab = perf_vocab();\n445\t    let band = ac012_query_token_band(&vocab);\n446\t    let mut rng = SeededRng::new(0x0AC0_120D_EC0D_E000);\n447\t    let queries: Vec<String> =\n448\t        (0..PERF_SAMPLES).map(|_| band[rng.next_in(band.len())].clone()).collect();\n449\t\n450\t    // Warmup: full pass discarded, per ADR (\"run the full query suite\n451\t    // once and discard; measure on the second pass\").\n452\t    for q in &queries {\n453\t        let _ = opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"warmup search\");\n454\t    }\n455\t\n456\t    // Measurement pass.\n457\t    let mut samples = Vec::with_capacity(PERF_SAMPLES);\n458\t    for q in &queries {\n459\t        let started = Instant::now();\n460\t        let _ = opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"measure search\");\n461\t        samples.push(started.elapsed());\n462\t    }\n463\t\n464\t    let p50 = percentile_ceil(&samples, 50, 100);\n465\t    let p99 = percentile_ceil(&samples, 99, 100);\n466\t    eprintln!(\n467\t        \"AC012_NUMBERS n={n} samples={s} seed_ms={seed} p50_ms={p50} p99_ms={p99}\",\n468\t        s = samples.len(),\n469\t        seed = seed_elapsed.as_millis(),\n470\t        p50 = p50.as_millis(),\n471\t        p99 = p99.as_millis(),\n472\t    );\n473\t\n474\t    assert!(\n475\t        p50 <= AC012_BUDGET_P50,\n476\t        \"AC-012 failed: p50={p50:?} > budget {budget:?} at n={n}\",\n477\t        budget = AC012_BUDGET_P50,\n478\t    );\n479\t    assert!(\n480\t        p99 <= AC012_BUDGET_P99,\n481\t        \"AC-012 failed: p99={p99:?} > budget {budget:?} at n={n}\",\n482\t        budget = AC012_BUDGET_P99,\n483\t    );\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t#[test]\n487\tfn ac_013_vector_retrieval_latency() {\n488\t    if !long_run_enabled() {\n489\t        return;\n490\t    }\n491\t\n492\t    let n = ac013_corpus_n();\n493\t    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"ac013_vector_retrieval\");\n494\t    let embedder = Arc::new(VaryingEmbedder::new(RETRIEVAL_VECTOR_DIM));\n495\t    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n496\t    let seed_elapsed = seed_ac013_corpus(&opened.engine, n);\n497\t\n498\t    let queries = ac013_query_bodies(PERF_SAMPLES);\n499\t\n500\t    for q in &queries {\n501\t        let _ = opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"warmup search\");\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    let mut samples = Vec::with_capacity(PERF_SAMPLES);\n505\t    for q in &queries {\n506\t        let started = Instant::now();\n507\t        let _ = opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"measure search\");\n508\t        samples.push(started.elapsed());\n509\t    }\n510\t\n511\t    let p50 = percentile_ceil(&samples, 50, 100);\n512\t    let p99 = percentile_ceil(&samples, 99, 100);\n513\t    eprintln!(\n514\t        \"AC013_NUMBERS n={n} samples={s} seed_ms={seed} p50_ms={p50} p99_ms={p99}\",\n515\t        s = samples.len(),\n516\t        seed = seed_elapsed.as_millis(),\n517\t        p50 = p50.as_millis(),\n518\t        p99 = p99.as_millis(),\n519\t    );\n520\t\n521\t    assert!(\n522\t        p50 <= AC013_BUDGET_P50,\n523\t        \"AC-013 failed: p50={p50:?} > budget {budget:?} at n={n}\",\n524\t        budget = AC013_BUDGET_P50,\n525\t    );\n526\t    assert!(\n527\t        p99 <= AC013_BUDGET_P99,\n528\t        \"AC-013 failed: p99={p99:?} > budget {budget:?} at n={n}\",\n529\t        budget = AC013_BUDGET_P99,\n530\t    );\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t#[test]\n534\tfn ac_017_vector_projection_freshness_p99_le_five_seconds() {\n535\t    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"projection_freshness\");\n536\t    let embedder = Arc::new(DeterministicEmbedder::new(384, Duration::from_millis(1)));\n537\t    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n538\t    opened.engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(\"doc\").expect(\"vector kind\");\n539\t\n540\t    let mut samples = Vec::with_capacity(PERF_SAMPLES);\n541\t    for i in 0..PERF_SAMPLES {\n542\t        let commit_started = Instant::now();\n543\t        let receipt = opened\n544\t            .engine\n545\t            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n546\t                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n547\t                body: format!(\"projection doc {i}\"),\n548\t                source_id: None,\n549\t            }])"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"878701b8-da54-4911-aa70-258c930a87b2","uuid":"9f1ae0d9-f79f-422a-8573-f41a92b6826f","timestamp":"2026-05-27T14:44:48.046Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-0.7.0-PVQ-P2-RED-20260527T144439Z/src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/perf_gates.rs","content":"}\n\n// ── AC-012 / AC-013 / AC-019 retrieval perf-gate fixtures ───────────────────\n//\n// Per `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-D-retrieval-perf-fixtures.md` and\n// ADR-0.6.0-{text-query,retrieval}-latency-gates the canonical fixture\n// scale is 1,000,000 chunk rows / 768-d vectors. Seeding 1M rows under\n// AGENT_LONG=1 on the aarch64 dev runner is hours-of-wall-clock; the\n// canonical x86_64 tier-1 CI runner sets `AC_FULL_SCALE=1` to honor\n// the 1M scale. AGENT_LONG=1 on the dev runner uses the env-tunable\n// scale below (AC-007a/b runner-pin precedent) and asserts the same\n// budget. See `dev/test-plan.md` § Current Perf Attribution and\n// `dev/notes/performance-whitepaper-notes.md` for measured medians.\nconst AC012_DEFAULT_N: usize = 100_000;\nconst AC013_DEFAULT_N: usize = 50_000;\nconst AC019_THREADS: usize = 8;\nconst AC019_QUERIES_PER_THREAD: usize = 250;\nconst AC012_BUDGET_P50: Duration = Duration::from_millis(20);\nconst AC012_BUDGET_P99: Duration = Duration::from_millis(150);\nconst AC013_BUDGET_P50: Duration = Duration::from_millis(50);\nconst AC013_BUDGET_P99: Duration = Duration::from_millis(200);\nconst AC019_STRESS_FLOOR: Duration = Duration::from_millis(150);\nconst AC019_STRESS_MULT: u32 = 10;\nconst RETRIEVAL_VECTOR_DIM: u32 = 768;\n\nfn env_usize(key: &str, default_full: usize, default_short: usize) -> usize {\n    if let Ok(raw) = std::env::var(key) {\n        if let Ok(parsed) = raw.parse::<usize>() {\n            return parsed;\n        }\n    }\n    if std::env::var_os(\"AC_FULL_SCALE\").is_some() {\n        default_full\n    } else {\n        default_short\n    }\n}\n\nfn ac012_corpus_n() -> usize {\n    env_usize(\"AC012_CORPUS_N\", 1_000_000, AC012_DEFAULT_N)\n}\n\nfn ac013_corpus_n() -> usize {\n    env_usize(\"AC013_CORPUS_N\", 1_000_000, AC013_DEFAULT_N)\n}\n\n/// Deterministic seeded LCG. Generates reproducible token streams so the\n/// AC-012 corpus and the held-out query set are both byte-stable across\n/// runs (per ADR-0.6.0-text-query-latency-gates \"synthetic-English-like\n/// text with a Zipfian token-frequency distribution\").\nstruct SeededRng {\n    state: u64,\n}\n\nimpl SeededRng {\n    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self {\n        Self { state: seed.wrapping_mul(0x9E37_79B9_7F4A_7C15).wrapping_add(1) }\n    }\n\n    fn next_u64(&mut self) -> u64 {\n        self.state = self.state.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);\n        self.state\n    }\n\n    fn next_in(&mut self, bound: usize) -> usize {\n        (self.next_u64() as usize) % bound\n    }\n\n    fn next_f64(&mut self) -> f64 {\n        (self.next_u64() >> 11) as f64 / ((1u64 << 53) as f64)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Vocabulary of synthetic English-like tokens. Size ~1024 keeps the\n/// FTS index dense; tokens are short, ASCII, lowercase, deterministic.\nfn perf_vocab() -> Vec<String> {\n    let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(1024);\n    for i in 0..1024 {\n        let a = (b'a' + ((i / 26 / 26) % 26) as u8) as char;\n        let b = (b'a' + ((i / 26) % 26) as u8) as char;\n        let c = (b'a' + (i % 26) as u8) as char;\n        out.push(format!(\"{a}{b}{c}{i:04}\"));\n    }\n    out\n}\n\n/// Sample a token index under a Zipfian-ish distribution with shape s=1.0,\n/// using inverse-CDF on a precomputed cumulative weight table. Returns an\n/// index into the vocabulary in [0, vocab_size).\nfn zipf_index(rng: &mut SeededRng, cumulative: &[f64]) -> usize {\n    let r = rng.next_f64() * cumulative[cumulative.len() - 1];\n    match cumulative.binary_search_by(|w| w.partial_cmp(&r).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)) {\n        Ok(idx) => idx,\n        Err(idx) => idx.min(cumulative.len() - 1),\n    }\n}\n\nfn zipf_cumulative(vocab_size: usize) -> Vec<f64> {\n    let mut cumulative = Vec::with_capacity(vocab_size);\n    let mut acc = 0.0_f64;\n    for k in 1..=vocab_size {\n        acc += 1.0_f64 / k as f64;\n        cumulative.push(acc);\n    }\n    cumulative\n}\n\n/// Generate one synthetic chunk body of approximately `target_bytes`\n/// (~500 B per ADR) using the Zipfian sampler. Tokens are space-joined.\nfn synth_chunk_body(rng: &mut SeededRng, vocab: &[String], cumulative: &[f64]) -> String {\n    // Tokens average ~7 chars + 1 separator, target ~500 B => ~63 tokens.\n    let mut body = String::with_capacity(512);\n    let token_count = 55 + rng.next_in(20);\n    for i in 0..token_count {\n        if i > 0 {\n            body.push(' ');\n        }\n        body.push_str(&vocab[zipf_index(rng, cumulative)]);\n    }\n    body\n}\n\n/// Choose a held-out query token from the 50th–90th percentile\n/// term-frequency band per ADR-0.6.0-text-query-latency-gates. Vocab is\n/// indexed by descending frequency rank (rank 0 most frequent), so the\n/// band maps to indices [0.10*vocab, 0.50*vocab) (frequency rank space).\n/// The Zipfian sampler yields rank 0 most often, so this band carves out\n/// the body of the distribution.\nfn ac012_query_token_band(vocab: &[String]) -> Vec<String> {\n    let lo = vocab.len() / 10;\n    let hi = vocab.len() / 2;\n    vocab[lo..hi].to_vec()\n}\n\n/// AC-012 deterministic seeder: 1 chunk body per write batch (4096 nodes\n/// per `engine.write()`). Returns elapsed seed time for diagnostics.\nfn seed_ac012_corpus(engine: &Engine, n: usize) -> Duration {\n    const BATCH: usize = 4096;\n    let vocab = perf_vocab();\n    let cumulative = zipf_cumulative(vocab.len());\n    let mut rng = SeededRng::new(0x0AC0_12C0_12C0);\n    let started = Instant::now();\n    let mut written = 0usize;\n    while written < n {\n        let take = BATCH.min(n - written);\n        let mut batch = Vec::with_capacity(take);\n        for _ in 0..take {\n            batch.push(PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: synth_chunk_body(&mut rng, &vocab, &cumulative),\n                source_id: None,\n            });\n        }\n        engine.write(&batch).expect(\"ac-012 seed write\");\n        written += take;\n    }\n    // No vector kind configured -> projection runtime still drains FTS index.\n    engine.drain(600_000).expect(\"ac-012 drain\");\n    started.elapsed()\n}\n\n/// Deterministic varying-vector embedder. Projects an input token's\n/// stable hash onto a single coordinate per call so vec0 ANN search\n/// returns distinct k=10 neighbors (a constant-vector embedder would\n/// collapse all distances to 0). Reproducible byte-for-byte across runs.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\nstruct VaryingEmbedder {\n    identity: EmbedderIdentity,\n    dim: u32,\n}\n\nimpl VaryingEmbedder {\n    fn new(dim: u32) -> Self {\n        Self { identity: EmbedderIdentity::new(\"varying\", \"perf-gates\", dim), dim }\n    }\n\n    fn vector_for(&self, text: &str) -> Vector {\n        let dim = self.dim as usize;\n        let mut v = vec![0.0_f32; dim];\n        // FNV-1a 64-bit on the input; spread across coordinates with\n        // deterministic small magnitudes so distance is meaningful.\n        let mut h: u64 = 0xcbf29ce484222325;\n        for &b in text.as_bytes() {\n            h ^= b as u64;\n            h = h.wrapping_mul(0x100000001b3);\n        }\n        // Place mass on a small handful of coordinates derived from h.\n        for k in 0..6 {\n            let coord = ((h >> (k * 8)) as usize) % dim;\n            let sign = if (h >> (k * 8 + 7)) & 1 == 0 { 1.0 } else { -1.0 };\n            v[coord] += sign * 0.5_f32;\n        }\n        // Normalize-ish so all vectors have similar magnitude.\n        let norm: f32 = v.iter().map(|x| x * x).sum::<f32>().sqrt().max(1e-6);\n        for x in &mut v {\n            *x /= norm;\n        }\n        v\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Embedder for VaryingEmbedder {\n    fn identity(&self) -> EmbedderIdentity {\n        self.identity.clone()\n    }\n\n    fn embed(&self, text: &str) -> Result<Vector, EmbedderError> {\n        Ok(self.vector_for(text))\n    }\n}\n\n/// AC-013 deterministic seeder: N vector rows with varying embeddings.\n/// Returns elapsed seed time. Bodies double as both FTS5 documents and\n/// the input to the embedder, so the same fixture supports AC-019's\n/// FTS+vector mixed workload.\nfn seed_ac013_corpus(engine: &Engine, n: usize) -> Duration {\n    const BATCH: usize = 1024;\n    let vocab = perf_vocab();\n    let cumulative = zipf_cumulative(vocab.len());\n    let mut rng = SeededRng::new(0x0AC0_13D0_13D0);\n    engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(\"doc\").expect(\"vector kind\");\n    let started = Instant::now();\n    let mut written = 0usize;\n    while written < n {\n        let take = BATCH.min(n - written);\n        let mut batch = Vec::with_capacity(take);\n        for _ in 0..take {\n            batch.push(PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: synth_chunk_body(&mut rng, &vocab, &cumulative),\n                source_id: None,\n            });\n        }\n        engine.write(&batch).expect(\"ac-013 seed write\");\n        written += take;\n    }\n    engine.drain(1_800_000).expect(\"ac-013 drain\");\n    started.elapsed()\n}\n\n/// Build a held-out reproducible query set drawn from the same\n/// distribution as the indexed corpus (ADR-0.6.0-retrieval-latency-gates\n/// \"Query vectors drawn from a held-out slice of the same distribution\").\nfn ac013_query_bodies(count: usize) -> Vec<String> {\n    let vocab = perf_vocab();\n    let cumulative = zipf_cumulative(vocab.len());\n    // Use a different seed than the corpus so the query set is held out\n    // (not byte-equal to seeded chunks but drawn from same distribution).\n    let mut rng = SeededRng::new(0x0AC0_130D_EC0D_E000);\n    (0..count).map(|_| synth_chunk_body(&mut rng, &vocab, &cumulative)).collect()\n}\n\n/// Bounded-size histogram for AC-019 tail-latency capture. Single\n/// power-of-two bucketing in microseconds; avoids unbounded\n/// `Vec<Duration>` per the Pack D plan (`Histogram::record` style).\nstruct LatencyHistogram {\n    /// Bucket i spans [2^i us, 2^(i+1) us). 32 buckets covers 1 us .. ~71 minutes.\n    buckets: [u64; 32],\n    count: u64,\n}\n\nimpl LatencyHistogram {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self { buckets: [0; 32], count: 0 }\n    }\n\n    fn record(&mut self, d: Duration) {\n        let us = d.as_micros().max(1) as u64;\n        let bucket = (63 - us.leading_zeros()) as usize;\n        let bucket = bucket.min(self.buckets.len() - 1);\n        self.buckets[bucket] += 1;\n        self.count += 1;\n    }\n\n    fn merge(&mut self, other: &LatencyHistogram) {\n        for i in 0..self.buckets.len() {\n            self.buckets[i] += other.buckets[i];\n        }\n        self.count += other.count;\n    }\n\n    /// Return the upper bound of the bucket containing the requested\n    /// percentile (numerator/denominator). Conservative ceiling.\n    fn percentile_ceil(&self, numerator: u64, denominator: u64) -> Duration {\n        if self.count == 0 {\n            return Duration::ZERO;\n        }\n        let target = (self.count * numerator).div_ceil(denominator);\n        let mut acc: u64 = 0;\n        for (i, count) in self.buckets.iter().enumerate() {\n            acc += count;\n            if acc >= target {\n                let upper_us = 1u64 << (i + 1);\n                return Duration::from_micros(upper_us);\n            }\n        }\n        let i = self.buckets.len() - 1;\n        Duration::from_micros(1u64 << (i + 1))\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn ac_012_text_query_latency_on_fts5_path() {\n    if !long_run_enabled() {\n        return;\n    }\n\n    let n = ac012_corpus_n();\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"ac012_text_query\");\n    let opened = Engine::open_without_embedder_for_test(&path).expect(\"open\");\n    let seed_elapsed = seed_ac012_corpus(&opened.engine, n);\n\n    // Build the held-out query token band (50th–90th percentile term\n    // frequency per the ADR). Reproducible: seeded from the vocab order.\n    let vocab = perf_vocab();\n    let band = ac012_query_token_band(&vocab);\n    let mut rng = SeededRng::new(0x0AC0_120D_EC0D_E000);\n    let queries: Vec<String> =\n        (0..PERF_SAMPLES).map(|_| band[rng.next_in(band.len())].clone()).collect();\n\n    // Warmup: full pass discarded, per ADR (\"run the full query suite\n    // once and discard; measure on the second pass\").\n    for q in &queries {\n        let _ = opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"warmup search\");\n    }\n\n    // Measurement pass.\n    let mut samples = Vec::with_capacity(PERF_SAMPLES);\n    for q in &queries {\n        let started = Instant::now();\n        let _ = opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"measure search\");\n        samples.push(started.elapsed());\n    }\n\n    let p50 = percentile_ceil(&samples, 50, 100);\n    let p99 = percentile_ceil(&samples, 99, 100);\n    eprintln!(\n        \"AC012_NUMBERS n={n} samples={s} seed_ms={seed} p50_ms={p50} p99_ms={p99}\",\n        s = samples.len(),\n        seed = seed_elapsed.as_millis(),\n        p50 = p50.as_millis(),\n        p99 = p99.as_millis(),\n    );\n\n    assert!(\n        p50 <= AC012_BUDGET_P50,\n        \"AC-012 failed: p50={p50:?} > budget {budget:?} at n={n}\",\n        budget = AC012_BUDGET_P50,\n    );\n    assert!(\n        p99 <= AC012_BUDGET_P99,\n        \"AC-012 failed: p99={p99:?} > budget {budget:?} at n={n}\",\n        budget = AC012_BUDGET_P99,\n    );\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn ac_013_vector_retrieval_latency() {\n    if !long_run_enabled() {\n        return;\n    }\n\n    let n = ac013_corpus_n();\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"ac013_vector_retrieval\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(VaryingEmbedder::new(RETRIEVAL_VECTOR_DIM));\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    let seed_elapsed = seed_ac013_corpus(&opened.engine, n);\n\n    let queries = ac013_query_bodies(PERF_SAMPLES);\n\n    for q in &queries {\n        let _ = opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"warmup search\");\n    }\n\n    let mut samples = Vec::with_capacity(PERF_SAMPLES);\n    for q in &queries {\n        let started = Instant::now();\n        let _ = opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"measure search\");\n        samples.push(started.elapsed());\n    }\n\n    let p50 = percentile_ceil(&samples, 50, 100);\n    let p99 = percentile_ceil(&samples, 99, 100);\n    eprintln!(\n        \"AC013_NUMBERS n={n} samples={s} seed_ms={seed} p50_ms={p50} p99_ms={p99}\",\n        s = samples.len(),\n        seed = seed_elapsed.as_millis(),\n        p50 = p50.as_millis(),\n        p99 = p99.as_millis(),\n    );\n\n    assert!(\n        p50 <= AC013_BUDGET_P50,\n        \"AC-013 failed: p50={p50:?} > budget {budget:?} at n={n}\",\n        budget = AC013_BUDGET_P50,\n    );\n    assert!(\n        p99 <= AC013_BUDGET_P99,\n        \"AC-013 failed: p99={p99:?} > budget {budget:?} at n={n}\",\n        budget = AC013_BUDGET_P99,\n    );\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn ac_017_vector_projection_freshness_p99_le_five_seconds() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"projection_freshness\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(DeterministicEmbedder::new(384, Duration::from_millis(1)));\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    opened.engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(\"doc\").expect(\"vector kind\");\n\n    let mut samples = Vec::with_capacity(PERF_SAMPLES);\n    for i in 0..PERF_SAMPLES {\n        let commit_started = Instant::now();\n        let receipt = opened\n            .engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"projection doc {i}\"),\n                source_id: None,\n            }])","numLines":420,"startLine":130,"totalLines":1177}}}

std::sync::{Arc, Barrier, Mutex};\nuse std::thread;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\nuse fathomdb_embedder_api::{Embedder, EmbedderError, EmbedderIdentity, Vector};\nuse fathomdb_engine::{Engine, PreparedWrite};\nuse fathomdb_schema::SQLITE_SUFFIX;\nuse tempfile::TempDir;\n\nconst PERF_SAMPLES: usize = 1_000;\nconst AC020_THREADS: usize = 8;\nconst AC020_ROUNDS_PER_THREAD: usize = 50;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\nstruct DeterministicEmbedder {\n    identity: EmbedderIdentity,\n    vector: Vector,\n    delay: Duration,\n}\n\nimpl DeterministicEmbedder {\n    fn new(dimension: u32, delay: Duration) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            identity: EmbedderIdentity::new(\"deterministic\", \"perf-gates\", dimension),\n            vector: unit_vector(dimension as usize),\n            delay,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Embedder for DeterministicEmbedder {\n    fn identity(&self) -> EmbedderIdentity {\n        self.identity.clone()\n    }\n\n    fn embed(&self, _text: &str) -> Result<Vector, EmbedderError> {\n        thread::sleep(self.delay);\n        Ok(self.vector.clone())\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\nstruct RoutedEmbedder {\n    identity: EmbedderIdentity,\n}\n\nimpl RoutedEmbedder {\n    fn new(dimension: u32) -> Self {\n        Self { identity: EmbedderIdentity::new(\"routed\", \"perf-gates\", dimension) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Embedder for RoutedEmbedder {\n    fn identity(&self) -> EmbedderIdentity {\n        self.identity.clone()\n    }\n\n    fn embed(&self, text: &str) -> Result<Vector, EmbedderError> {\n        let mut vector = vec![0.0_f32; self.identity.dimension as usize];\n        let slot = if text.starts_with(\"semantic-\") || text.starts_with(\"vector-doc-\") {\n            0\n        } else if text.starts_with(\"hybrid-\") || text.starts_with(\"hybrid doc\") {\n            1\n        } else {\n            2\n        };\n        vector[slot] = 1.0;\n        Ok(vector)\n    }\n}\n\nfn fixture_path(name: &str) -> (TempDir, std::path::PathBuf) {\n    let dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n    let path = dir.path().join(format!(\"{name}{SQLITE_SUFFIX}\"));\n    (dir, path)\n}\n\nfn percentile_ceil(samples: &[Duration], numerator: usize, denominator: usize) -> Duration {\n    assert!(!samples.is_empty());\n    let mut sorted = samples.to_vec();\n    sorted.sort_unstable();\n    let index = ((sorted.len() * numerator).div_ceil(denominator)).saturating_sub(1);\n    sorted[index]\n}\n\nfn unit_vector(dimension: usize) -> Vector {\n    let mut values = vec![0.0_f32; dimension];\n    if dimension > 0 {\n        values[0] = 1.0;\n    }\n    values\n}\n\nfn long_run_enabled() -> bool {\n    std::env::var_os(\"AGENT_LONG\").is_some()\n}\n\nfn ac020_queries() -> [&'static str; 4] {\n    [\"semantic-0\", \"hybrid-0\", \"semantic-1\", \"hybrid-1\"]\n}\n\nfn seed_ac020_fixture(engine: &Engine) {\n    engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(\"doc\").expect(\"vector kind\");\n    for i in 0..2 {\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"vector-doc-{i}\"),\n                source_id: None,\n            }])\n            .expect(\"vector-only write\");\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"hybrid doc hybrid-{i}\"),\n                source_id: None,\n            }])\n            .expect(\"hybrid write\");\n    }\n    engine.drain(10_000).expect(\"drain\");\n}\n\nfn run_ac020_mix(engine: &Engine) {\n    for _ in 0..AC020_ROUNDS_PER_THREAD {\n        for query in ac020_queries() {\n            let result = engine.search(query).expect(\"search\");\n            assert!(!result.results.is_empty(), \"read-mix query {query} must yield a result\");\n        }\n    }\n}","numLines":130,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1177}}}

dev/plans/runs/0.7.0-PVQ-P2-RED-20260527T144439Z.log:343:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01192uugREpNPTf4J3Y8u64d","type":"tool_result","content":"90\t\n91\t## Current Perf Attribution\n92\t\n93\t- `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/perf_gates.rs` currently binds\n94\t  AC-017 and AC-018 with active runtime measurements that run under\n95\t  `scripts/agent-verify.sh`.\n96\t- AC-012 / AC-013 / AC-019 protocol-complete fixture harnesses landed\n97\t  in Pack D (Phase 9) at `tests/perf_gates.rs`. All three are\n98\t  `long_run_enabled()`-gated (early-return without `AGENT_LONG=1`) and\n99\t  evidenced exclusively by `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`.\n100\t  - **AC-012** seeder: deterministic seeded-LCG token stream over a\n101\t    1024-token vocabulary with Zipfian (s=1.0) frequency distribution;\n102\t    chunk bodies ≈ 500 B (55–75 tokens); held-out query token band\n103\t    indices [10%, 50%) of vocab — i.e. the 50th–90th percentile term-\n104\t    frequency band per ADR-0.6.0-text-query-latency-gates. Warmup pass\n105\t    discarded; second pass measured (P-PERF-SAMPLES = 1,000).\n106\t  - **AC-013** seeder: same chunk-body generator wired through a\n107\t    deterministic-but-varying 768-d embedder (`VaryingEmbedder`,\n108\t    FNV-1a hash projected to 6 sparse coordinates with unit-norm) so\n109\t    vec0 ANN search returns distinct k=10 neighbors. Held-out query\n110\t    body set drawn from the same distribution with a different LCG\n111\t    seed (mirrors ADR-0.6.0-retrieval-latency-gates \"held-out slice\").\n112\t  - **AC-019** workload: re-runs AC-013's protocol immediately\n113\t    preceding the stress pass (per acceptance.md AC-019 wording),\n114\t    captures `baseline_p99`, then runs `AC019_THREADS=8` concurrent\n115\t    reader threads × `AC019_QUERIES_PER_THREAD=250` mixed FTS5 +\n116\t    vector + canonical reads. Tail latency captured via a bounded\n117\t    32-bucket power-of-two `LatencyHistogram` (microseconds) per the\n118\t    plan's \"no unbounded `Vec<Duration>`\" instruction.\n119\t  - **Scale knobs.** Canonical scale is 1,000,000 chunk rows / 768-d\n120\t    vectors (ADR). The CI runner sets `AC_FULL_SCALE=1` to honor it;\n121\t    `AC012_CORPUS_N` / `AC013_CORPUS_N` env vars override per host\n122\t    (AC-007a/b runner-pin precedent). Default scale on this aarch64\n123\t    Tegra dev runner: AC-012 N=100,000; AC-013 N=50,000. Budgets\n124\t    pinned to ADR values; do not relax.\n125\t  - **Pack D dev-runner status (aarch64 Linux 5.15-tegra,\n126\t    2026-05-05).** AC-012 at default N=100,000: p50=29.7 ms, p99=85\n127\t    ms — p50 RED vs ADR 20 ms; p99 GREEN. AC-013 at N=10,000 (full\n128\t    AGENT_LONG run not feasible on this host): p50=33 ms, p99=48 ms\n129\t    — both GREEN against ADR 50 / 200 ms. AC-013 at N=50,000 not\n130\t    measured: vec0 single-row insertion path on this host took\n131\t    1,800 s wall-clock to seed 10,000 vectors (≈ 5.5 inserts/sec)\n132\t    — engine-surface scaling gap, FLAGGED for orchestrator and Pack 7. AC-019 inherits the AC-013 seed cost; not measured at N=50k.\n133\t    Canonical CI x86_64 tier-1 runner re-measurement is required to\n134\t    close the perf gates.\n135\t  - **Deferral.** AC-012 / AC-013 / AC-019 are **DEFERRED for 0.6.0**\n136\t    as of Pack D close (2026-05-05) — paralleling AC-020. Harnesses\n137\t    are protocol-complete and `AGENT_LONG`-gated; canonical-runner\n138\t    measurement + the vec0 bulk-seed engine-surface gap are early\n139\t    Pack 7 work per\n140\t    `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-7-canonical-perf-measurement.md`.\n141\t    0.6.0 ships with these three gates documented as DEFERRED, not\n142\t    weakened. Budgets stay pinned at ADR values.\n143\t- AC-020 is **DEFERRED for 0.6.0** as of Pack 6.G close (2026-05-04).\n144\t  Implemented as a long-run-only env-gated harness; the documented read\n145\t  mix is 50% vector-only semantic queries (`semantic-*`) and 50% hybrid\n146\t  queries (`hybrid-*`) over a pre-drained vector-indexed fixture, 50\n147\t  rounds per reader thread. Evidenced by `scripts/check.sh` with\n148\t  `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` executes only the early-return path\n149\t  and is not evidence for the AC.\n150\t\n151\t  Latest measured medians (N=5, current `0.6.0-rewrite` tip, this host\n152\t  Linux 5.15-tegra ~3× slower in absolute ms than the Pack 5 reference\n153\t  machine): seq=563 ms, conc=161 ms, bound=105 ms, speedup=3.530×\n154\t  (required ≥ 5.0× packet rule / ≥ 5.33× test bound). AC-017 + AC-018\n155\t  stay green throughout. Best individual run on this host (G.1 run 5):\n156\t  seq=503 / conc=118 / speedup=4.263× — single-run only, not a gate.\n157\t\n158\t  **Deferral rationale.** Pack 5 + Pack 6 + Pack 6.G falsified every\n159\t  canonical-SQLite lever measurable on the AC-020 read-only fixture\n160\t  (mutex track via B.1 / C.1; parse-cost track via E.1; pool-topology\n161\t  track via F.0 KEPT but does not close; allocator-arena via G.1\n162\t  LANDED INCONCLUSIVE; page-cache via G.3.5 SKIP_G4 — `cache_used` =\n163\t  3.35% / `delta_miss_rate` = 0.023% leaves no headroom; WAL atomics +\n164\t  checkpoint = 0% under stack-aware classification). Residual\n165\t  `page_cache` 6.29% conc share is from `pcache1` mutex acquires on\n166\t  every page-fetch (hit-path), which canonical SQLite cannot eliminate\n167\t  without `SQLITE_CONFIG_PCACHE2` custom allocator install. That,\n168\t  WAL2, reader/writer physical separation, and vendor-SQLite swap\n169\t  are all Pack 7 territory — see\n170\t  `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-5-performance-diagnostics.md` §13.\n171\t\n172\t  AC-020 stays RED in CI; the test bound (`tests/perf_gates.rs:245`)\n173\t  is unchanged. 0.6.0 ships with the gate documented as DEFERRED, not\n174\t  weakened. Pack 7 reopens the gate when a measured fix lands.\n175\t\n176\t  Evidence trail:\n177\t  - `dev/notes/performance-whitepaper-notes.md` (§4 kept ledger\n178\t    incl. F.0 / G.1; §5 reverted ledger; §11 Pack 5 narrative;\n179\t    §12 Pack 6 + Pack 6.G synthesis).\n180\t  - `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-5-performance-diagnostics.md` (DoE\n181\t    plan, §12 audit trail, §13 Pack 7 proposed work).\n182\t  - `dev/plans/runs/F0-thread-affine-readers-output.json` (F.0\n183\t    topology + numbers).\n184\t  - `dev/plans/runs/G0-wal-checkpoint-telemetry-output.json` (stack-\n185\t    aware symbol classification).\n186\t  - `dev/plans/runs/G1-reader-lookaside-output.json` (lookaside\n187\t    LANDED INCONCLUSIVE).\n188\t  - `dev/plans/runs/G3_5-cache-pressure-telemetry-output.json`\n189\t    (page-cache lever falsified).\n190\t  - `dev/plans/runs/STATUS.md` (Pack 5 / 6 / 6.G close-state board).\n191\t\n192\t## Component Scaffold Targets\n193\t\n194\tThe implementation module tree for `fathomdb-engine/src/` should be:\n195\t\n196\t- `runtime/` for `Engine.open`, `Engine.close`, config, lock lifecycle, and\n197\t  open reports\n198\t- `writer/` and `reader/` for connection ownership and typed execution\n199\t- `migrations/`, `errors/`, `op_store/`, `embedder/`, `scheduler/`, `vector/`,\n200\t  `projections/`, `retrieval/`, and `lifecycle/` matching `architecture.md`\n201\t- `recovery/` only for engine helpers consumed by the CLI; no SDK recovery\n202\t  surface\n203\t\n204\tThe existing 0.6.0 scaffold crates (`fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`,\n205\t`fathomdb-embedder`, and `src/ts`) should be shaped in place rather than\n206\tcreated from scratch.\n207\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"878701b8-da54-4911-aa70-258c930a87b2","uuid":"0201dfcf-9769-430d-bbf9-19641b4f8bdf","timestamp":"2026-05-27T14:46:45.893Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-0.7.0-PVQ-P2-RED-20260527T144439Z/dev/test-plan.md","content":"\n## Current Perf Attribution\n\n- `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/perf_gates.rs` currently binds\n  AC-017 and AC-018 with active runtime measurements that run under\n  `scripts/agent-verify.sh`.\n- AC-012 / AC-013 / AC-019 protocol-complete fixture harnesses landed\n  in Pack D (Phase 9) at `tests/perf_gates.rs`. All three are\n  `long_run_enabled()`-gated (early-return without `AGENT_LONG=1`) and\n  evidenced exclusively by `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`.\n  - **AC-012** seeder: deterministic seeded-LCG token stream over a\n    1024-token vocabulary with Zipfian (s=1.0) frequency distribution;\n    chunk bodies ≈ 500 B (55–75 tokens); held-out query token band\n    indices [10%, 50%) of vocab — i.e. the 50th–90th percentile term-\n    frequency band per ADR-0.6.0-text-query-latency-gates. Warmup pass\n    discarded; second pass measured (P-PERF-SAMPLES = 1,000).\n  - **AC-013** seeder: same chunk-body generator wired through a\n    deterministic-but-varying 768-d embedder (`VaryingEmbedder`,\n    FNV-1a hash projected to 6 sparse coordinates with unit-norm) so\n    vec0 ANN search returns distinct k=10 neighbors. Held-out query\n    body set drawn from the same distribution with a different LCG\n    seed (mirrors ADR-0.6.0-retrieval-latency-gates \"held-out slice\").\n  - **AC-019** workload: re-runs AC-013's protocol immediately\n    preceding the stress pass (per acceptance.md AC-019 wording),\n    captures `baseline_p99`, then runs `AC019_THREADS=8` concurrent\n    reader threads × `AC019_QUERIES_PER_THREAD=250` mixed FTS5 +\n    vector + canonical reads. Tail latency captured via a bounded\n    32-bucket power-of-two `LatencyHistogram` (microseconds) per the\n    plan's \"no unbounded `Vec<Duration>`\" instruction.\n  - **Scale knobs.** Canonical scale is 1,000,000 chunk rows / 768-d\n    vectors (ADR). The CI runner sets `AC_FULL_SCALE=1` to honor it;\n    `AC012_CORPUS_N` / `AC013_CORPUS_N` env vars override per host\n    (AC-007a/b runner-pin precedent). Default scale on this aarch64\n    Tegra dev runner: AC-012 N=100,000; AC-013 N=50,000. Budgets\n    pinned to ADR values; do not relax.\n  - **Pack D dev-runner status (aarch64 Linux 5.15-tegra,\n    2026-05-05).** AC-012 at default N=100,000: p50=29.7 ms, p99=85\n    ms — p50 RED vs ADR 20 ms; p99 GREEN. AC-013 at N=10,000 (full\n    AGENT_LONG run not feasible on this host): p50=33 ms, p99=48 ms\n    — both GREEN against ADR 50 / 200 ms. AC-013 at N=50,000 not\n    measured: vec0 single-row insertion path on this host took\n    1,800 s wall-clock to seed 10,000 vectors (≈ 5.5 inserts/sec)\n    — engine-surface scaling gap, FLAGGED for orchestrator and Pack 7. AC-019 inherits the AC-013 seed cost; not measured at N=50k.\n    Canonical CI x86_64 tier-1 runner re-measurement is required to\n    close the perf gates.\n  - **Deferral.** AC-012 / AC-013 / AC-019 are **DEFERRED for 0.6.0**\n    as of Pack D close (2026-05-05) — paralleling AC-020. Harnesses\n    are protocol-complete and `AGENT_LONG`-gated; canonical-runner\n    measurement + the vec0 bulk-seed engine-surface gap are early\n    Pack 7 work per\n    `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-7-canonical-perf-measurement.md`.\n    0.6.0 ships with these three gates documented as DEFERRED, not\n    weakened. Budgets stay pinned at ADR values.\n- AC-020 is **DEFERRED for 0.6.0** as of Pack 6.G close (2026-05-04).\n  Implemented as a long-run-only env-gated harness; the documented read\n  mix is 50% vector-only semantic queries (`semantic-*`) and 50% hybrid\n  queries (`hybrid-*`) over a pre-drained vector-indexed fixture, 50\n  rounds per reader thread. Evidenced by `scripts/check.sh` with\n  `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` executes only the early-return path\n  and is not evidence for the AC.\n\n  Latest measured medians (N=5, current `0.6.0-rewrite` tip, this host\n  Linux 5.15-tegra ~3× slower in absolute ms than the Pack 5 reference\n  machine): seq=563 ms, conc=161 ms, bound=105 ms, speedup=3.530×\n  (required ≥ 5.0× packet rule / ≥ 5.33× test bound). AC-017 + AC-018\n  stay green throughout. Best individual run on this host (G.1 run 5):\n  seq=503 / conc=118 / speedup=4.263× — single-run only, not a gate.\n\n  **Deferral rationale.** Pack 5 + Pack 6 + Pack 6.G falsified every\n  canonical-SQLite lever measurable on the AC-020 read-only fixture\n  (mutex track via B.1 / C.1; parse-cost track via E.1; pool-topology\n  track via F.0 KEPT but does not close; allocator-arena via G.1\n  LANDED INCONCLUSIVE; page-cache via G.3.5 SKIP_G4 — `cache_used` =\n  3.35% / `delta_miss_rate` = 0.023% leaves no headroom; WAL atomics +\n  checkpoint = 0% under stack-aware classification). Residual\n  `page_cache` 6.29% conc share is from `pcache1` mutex acquires on\n  every page-fetch (hit-path), which canonical SQLite cannot eliminate\n  without `SQLITE_CONFIG_PCACHE2` custom allocator install. That,\n  WAL2, reader/writer physical separation, and vendor-SQLite swap\n  are all Pack 7 territory — see\n  `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-5-performance-diagnostics.md` §13.\n\n  AC-020 stays RED in CI; the test bound (`tests/perf_gates.rs:245`)\n  is unchanged. 0.6.0 ships with the gate documented as DEFERRED, not\n  weakened. Pack 7 reopens the gate when a measured fix lands.\n\n  Evidence trail:\n  - `dev/notes/performance-whitepaper-notes.md` (§4 kept ledger\n    incl. F.0 / G.1; §5 reverted ledger; §11 Pack 5 narrative;\n    §12 Pack 6 + Pack 6.G synthesis).\n  - `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-5-performance-diagnostics.md` (DoE\n    plan, §12 audit trail, §13 Pack 7 proposed work).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/F0-thread-affine-readers-output.json` (F.0\n    topology + numbers).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/G0-wal-checkpoint-telemetry-output.json` (stack-\n    aware symbol classification).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/G1-reader-lookaside-output.json` (lookaside\n    LANDED INCONCLUSIVE).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/G3_5-cache-pressure-telemetry-output.json`\n    (page-cache lever falsified).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/STATUS.md` (Pack 5 / 6 / 6.G close-state board).\n\n## Component Scaffold Targets\n\nThe implementation module tree for `fathomdb-engine/src/` should be:\n\n- `runtime/` for `Engine.open`, `Engine.close`, config, lock lifecycle, and\n  open reports\n- `writer/` and `reader/` for connection ownership and typed execution\n- `migrations/`, `errors/`, `op_store/`, `embedder/`, `scheduler/`, `vector/`,\n  `projections/`, `retrieval/`, and `lifecycle/` matching `architecture.md`\n- `recovery/` only for engine helpers consumed by the CLI; no SDK recovery\n  surface\n\nThe existing 0.6.0 scaffold crates (`fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`,\n`fathomdb-embedder`, and `src/ts`) should be shaped in place rather than\ncreated from scratch.\n","numLines":118,"startLine":90,"totalLines":207}}}
