                                           | `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/op_store.rs`; `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-cli/tests/recovery_cli.rs`                                                                                                                                                       |\n\n## Required Fixtures\n\nThe lock-blocking fixture set from `acceptance.md` is implemented once and\nreused across suites:\n\n- 1M chunk-row corpus with FTS5 + `vec0` indexes\n- 1GB seeded DB\n- open-path corruption matrix: WAL replay, header probe, schema probe,\n  embedder-profile corruption\n- power-cut and OS-crash harnesses\n- shadow-table and page-corruption injection tools\n- deterministic slow CTE, poison operation, mixed retrieval stress, read-mix,\n  compressed provenance workload, vector/FTS 100-query suites\n- AST scanner, removal-detect linter, cargo/pip skew fixtures, synthetic\n  changelog fixtures, netns-deny-egress, and no-listen syscall capture\n\n## Implementation Order\n\n1. Replace scaffold property tests with failing tests for public surface,\n   locking, cursor, and migration contracts.\n2. Add CLI recovery/doctor tests before implementing CLI verbs.\n3. Add durability/perf/soak harnesses behind explicit CI labels so normal\n   `agent-verify` can stay fast while release gates still execute the full\n   acceptance set.\n\n   **Gate boundary — agent-verify vs check.sh AGENT_LONG=1:**\n   - `scripts/agent-verify.sh` (fast local loop) runs lint → typecheck →\n     unit/integration tests EXCLUDING long-run variants. It does NOT\n     exercise: AC-021's 60 s spec-conforming window, AC-059b's\n     ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture\n     (`cursor_read_after_write::projection_cursor_bounds_observed_row_count`),\n     and AC-020's sequential-vs-8-reader perf comparison once that\n     read-mix fixture is landed. `agent-verify` runs the AC-021 5 s\n     smoke variant only; the smoke run\n     does not satisfy AC-021's measurement protocol on its own.\n   - `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1` (full evidence gate) runs\n     everything `agent-verify` runs PLUS the long-run variants: AC-021's\n     60 s spec-conforming window, AC-059b's ~1000-iteration race\n     fixture, and AC-020's sequential-vs-8-reader comparison once the\n     read-mix fixture is protocol-complete. AC-059b has no smoke\n     variant — its evidence comes exclusively from this gate.\n\n   A reviewer reading just this section should be able to attribute each\n   long-run AC's evidence to a single command.\n\n4. Keep thresholds in `acceptance.md`; tests read or restate those parameters\n   but do not invent new gates.\n\n## Current Perf Attribution\n\n- `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/perf_gates.rs` currently binds\n  AC-017 and AC-018 with active runtime measurements that run under\n  `scripts/agent-verify.sh`.\n- AC-012 / AC-013 / AC-019 protocol-complete fixture harnesses landed\n  in Pack D (Phase 9) at `tests/perf_gates.rs`. All three are\n  `long_run_enabled()`-gated (early-return without `AGENT_LONG=1`) and\n  evidenced exclusively by `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`.\n  - **AC-012** seeder: deterministic seeded-LCG token stream over a\n    1024-token vocabulary with Zipfian (s=1.0) frequency distribution;\n    chunk bodies ≈ 500 B (55–75 tokens); held-out query token band\n    indices [10%, 50%) of vocab — i.e. the 50th–90th percentile term-\n    frequency band per ADR-0.6.0-text-query-latency-gates. Warmup pass\n    discarded; second pass measured (P-PERF-SAMPLES = 1,000).\n  - **AC-013** seeder: same chunk-body generator wired through a\n    deterministic-but-varying 768-d embedder (`VaryingEmbedder`,\n    FNV-1a hash projected to 6 sparse coordinates with unit-norm) so\n    vec0 ANN search returns distinct k=10 neighbors. Held-out query\n    body set drawn from the same distribution with a different LCG\n    seed (mirrors ADR-0.6.0-retrieval-latency-gates \"held-out slice\").\n  - **AC-013b** recall@10 floor (Pack 2 RED, 2026-05-27): reuses\n    `seed_ac013_corpus` + `ac013_query_bodies` so latency and recall\n    share a single corpus. Per query: re-embeds via `VaryingEmbedder`,\n    runs `engine.search` for production top-10, runs the brute-force\n    f32 ground-truth SQL (`SELECT rowid FROM vector_default WHERE\n    embedding MATCH vec_f32(?1) ORDER BY distance LIMIT 10`) via a\n    raw read-only `rusqlite::Connection` on the same DB path, looks\n    up bodies through `canonical_nodes.write_cursor`, and reports\n    `RECALL_NUMBERS` with mean recall@10 over `PERF_SAMPLES`. Floor\n    HITL-locked at ≥ 0.90 (`dev/plans/0.7.0-HITL-recommendations.md`\n    line 89; ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant § 2). Pre-Pack-2-IMPL the\n    production path is byte-equal to the ground-truth SQL so recall =\n    1.0; the test exists as the canonical regression gate for the\n    bit-KNN + f32 rerank rewrite in P2-IMPL.\n  - **AC-019** workload: re-runs AC-013's protocol immediately\n    preceding the stress pass (per acceptance.md AC-019 wording),\n    captures `baseline_p99`, then runs `AC019_THREADS=8` concurrent\n    reader threads × `AC019_QUERIES_PER_THREAD=250` mixed FTS5 +\n    vector + canonical reads. Tail latency captured via a bounded\n    32-bucket power-of-two `LatencyHistogram` (microseconds) per the\n    plan's \"no unbounded `Vec<Duration>`\" instruction.\n  - **Scale knobs.** Canonical scale is 1,000,000 chunk rows / 768-d\n    vectors (ADR). The CI runner sets `AC_FULL_SCALE=1` to honor it;\n    `AC012_CORPUS_N` / `AC013_CORPUS_N` env vars override per host\n    (AC-007a/b runner-pin precedent). Default scale on this aarch64\n    Tegra dev runner: AC-012 N=100,000; AC-013 N=50,000. Budgets\n    pinned to ADR values; do not relax.\n  - **Pack D dev-runner status (aarch64 Linux 5.15-tegra,\n    2026-05-05).** AC-012 at default N=100,000: p50=29.7 ms, p99=85\n    ms — p50 RED vs ADR 20 ms; p99 GREEN. AC-013 at N=10,000 (full\n    AGENT_LONG run not feasible on this host): p50=33 ms, p99=48 ms\n    — both GREEN against ADR 50 / 200 ms. AC-013 at N=50,000 not\n    measured: vec0 single-row insertion path on this host took\n    1,800 s wall-clock to seed 10,000 vectors (≈ 5.5 inserts/sec)\n    — engine-surface scaling gap, FLAGGED for orchestrator and Pack 7. AC-019 inherits the AC-013 seed cost; not measured at N=50k.\n    Canonical CI x86_64 tier-1 runner re-measurement is required to\n    close the perf gates.\n  - **Deferral.** AC-012 / AC-013 / AC-019 are **DEFERRED for 0.6.0**\n    as of Pack D close (2026-05-05) — paralleling AC-020. Harnesses\n    are protocol-complete and `AGENT_LONG`-gated; canonical-runner\n    measurement + the vec0 bulk-seed engine-surface gap are early\n    Pack 7 work per\n    `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-7-canonical-perf-measurement.md`.\n    0.6.0 ships with these three gates documented as DEFERRED, not\n    weakened. Budgets stay pinned at ADR values.\n- AC-020 is **DEFERRED for 0.6.0** as of Pack 6.G close (2026-05-04).\n  Implemented as a long-run-only env-gated harness; the documented read\n  mix is 50% vector-only semantic queries (`semantic-*`) and 50% hybrid\n  queries (`hybrid-*`) over a pre-drained vector-indexed fixture, 50\n  rounds per reader thread. Evidenced by `scripts/check.sh` with\n  `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` executes only the early-return path\n  and is not evidence for the AC.\n\n  Latest measured medians (N=5, current `0.6.0-rewrite` tip, this host\n  Linux 5.15-tegra ~3× slower in absolute ms than the Pack 5 reference\n  machine): seq=563 ms, conc=161 ms, bound=105 ms, speedup=3.530×\n  (required ≥ 5.0× packet rule / ≥ 5.33× test bound). AC-017 + AC-018\n  stay green throughout. Best individual run on this host (G.1 run 5):\n  seq=503 / conc=118 / speedup=4.263× — single-run only, not a gate.\n\n  **Deferral rationale.** Pack 5 + Pack 6 + Pack 6.G falsified every\n  canonical-SQLite lever measurable on the AC-020 read-only fixture\n  (mutex track via B.1 / C.1; parse-cost track via E.1; pool-topology\n  track via F.0 KEPT but does not close; allocator-arena via G.1\n  LANDED INCONCLUSIVE; page-cache via G.3.5 SKIP_G4 — `cache_used` =\n  3.35% / `delta_miss_rate` = 0.023% leaves no headroom; WAL atomics +\n  checkpoint = 0% under stack-aware classification). Residual\n  `page_cache` 6.29% conc share is from `pcache1` mutex acquires on\n  every page-fetch (hit-path), which canonical SQLite cannot eliminate\n  without `SQLITE_CONFIG_PCACHE2` custom allocator install. That,\n  WAL2, reader/writer physical separation, and vendor-SQLite swap\n  are all Pack 7 territory — see\n  `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-5-performance-diagnostics.md` §13.\n\n  AC-020 stays RED in CI; the test bound (`tests/perf_gates.rs:245`)\n  is unchanged. 0.6.0 ships with the gate documented as DEFERRED, not\n  weakened. Pack 7 reopens the gate when a measured fix lands.\n\n  Evidence trail:\n  - `dev/notes/performance-whitepaper-notes.md` (§4 kept ledger\n    incl. F.0 / G.1; §5 reverted ledger; §11 Pack 5 narrative;\n    §12 Pack 6 + Pack 6.G synthesis).\n  - `dev/plans/0.6.0-Phase-9-Pack-5-performance-diagnostics.md` (DoE\n    plan, §12 audit trail, §13 Pack 7 proposed work).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/F0-thread-affine-readers-output.json` (F.0\n    topology + numbers).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/G0-wal-checkpoint-telemetry-output.json` (stack-\n    aware symbol classification).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/G1-reader-lookaside-output.json` (lookaside\n    LANDED INCONCLUSIVE).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/G3_5-cache-pressure-telemetry-output.json`\n    (page-cache lever falsified).\n  - `dev/plans/runs/STATUS.md` (Pack 5 / 6 / 6.G close-state board).\n\n## Component Scaffold Targets\n\nThe implementation module tree for `fathomdb-engine/src/` should be:\n\n- `runtime/` for `Engine.open`, `Engine.close`, config, lock lifecycle, and\n  open reports\n- `writer/` and `reader/` for connection ownership and typed execution\n- `migrations/`, `errors/`, `op_store/`, `embedder/`, `scheduler/`, `vector/`,\n  `projections/`, `retrieval/`, and `lifecycle/` matching `architecture.md`\n- `recovery/` only for engine helpers consumed by the CLI; no SDK recovery\n  surface\n\nThe existing 0.6.0 scaffold crates (`fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`,\n`fathomdb-embedder`, and `src/ts`) should be shaped in place rather than\ncreated from scratch.\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":135,"oldLines":7,"newStart":135,"newLines":13,"lines":["     `AC012_CORPUS_N` / `AC013_CORPUS_N` env vars override per host","     (AC-007a/b runner-pin precedent). Default scale on this aarch64","     Tegra dev runner: AC-012 N=100,000; AC-013 N=50,000. Budgets","-    pinned to ADR values; do not relax.","+    pinned to ADR values; do not relax. **AC-013 budget re-pinned","+    2026-05-27 (Pack 2 RED) to p50 ≤ 80 ms / p99 ≤ 300 ms — the","+    HITL-locked target for the bit-KNN + f32 rerank reader rewrite","+    in P2-IMPL. Canonical-CI N=1M measured 2048 / 2327 ms on the","+    f32-brute-force path","+    (`dev/plans/runs/0.7.0-PERF-EXP-W4.1-ac013-canonical-output.json`),","+    so the new budget is RED at canonical scale until P2-IMPL lands.**","   - **Pack D dev-runner status (aarch64 Linux 5.15-tegra,","     2026-05-05).** AC-012 at default N=100,000: p50=29.7 ms, p99=85","     ms — p50 RED vs ADR 20 ms; p99 GREEN. AC-013 at N=10,000 (full"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
dev/plans/runs/0.8.0-slice-40-ga3-codex-review-20260608T194500Z.md:61: **Assertion:** `eu7` real-corpus recall@10 of the **ANN+ vector stage** (1-bit sign-quant K=192 Hamming + f32 rerank) vs the **exact-f32 VECTOR top-10** of the same embedder ≥ **0.90** — an ANN-quantization **FIDELITY** gate, measured on the **vector stage in isolation** (NOT the RRF-fused `search()` output). MET: bge-small over the real corpus (N≈7,667, K=192) measures vector-stage recall@10 = **0.937** (bootstrap CI 0.913–0.957, σ 0.0116) — full CI clears 0.90. **◆ B-1 rationale:** the eu7 ground truth is a vector-only top-10, but Slice 10 (`d28d204`) made `search()` unconditional RRF-hybrid (vector ⊕ FTS5); measuring a hybrid result against a vector-only GT conflated quantization fidelity with intended fusion divergence (the Slice-40 Phase-A HALT, recall 0.8710; see `dev/plans/runs/GA-1-corpus-ab-20260608T012503Z.md`). The fused-`search()` recall (~0.871) is reported as the report-only delta `EU7_RECALL_FUSED`. The synthetic `ac_013b` is demoted to a REPORT-ONLY quantization-fidelity signal (`RECALL_FIDELITY_INFO`, ~0.73–0.89 on isotropic noise — the noise-limited worst case for sign-bit ANN, not a product floor); the `AC013B_RECALL_FLOOR = 0.90` constant and its sentinel are retained.
dev/plans/runs/0.8.0-slice-40-ga3-codex-review-20260608T194500Z.md:62:+**0.8.0 GA-3 asserting form (◆ HITL ruling 2026-06-08; CI-based reconciliation, floor target 0.90 UNCHANGED):** the 0.8.0 eu7 asserting verdict is "real-embedder eu7 vector-stage recall@10 whose **95% bootstrap CI is not significantly below 0.90** (`recall_ci_hi >= 0.90`)" — a **one-sided** gate (not a two-sided "floor ∈ [ci_lo, ci_hi]" test, which would wrongly fail a comfortably-high recall whose whole CI clears the floor). The prior point-estimate hard-assert (`recall >= 0.90`) PANICKED at the measured N=7667 result; the floor **constant stays 0.90** (`CURRENT_FLOOR`/`AC013B_RECALL_FLOOR` unchanged — not lowered). **Measured result satisfies the gate:** point estimate **0.896**, CI **[0.864, 0.925]** → ci_hi **0.925 ≥ 0.90 ⇒ PASS** (within measurement uncertainty / "rounding-error territory" per the HITL ruling — the 0.90 floor lies inside the 95% CI). The **point-estimate-≥0.90 recovery** and the **~4pt 0.7.x→0.8.0 vector-stage drop** (0.937→0.896) diagnosis are **0.8.1** items. This CI-form is a **0.8.0-scoped reconciliation to be REVISITED after 0.8.0**. Predicate `recall_ci_clears_floor` in `tests/support/recall_gate.rs`, asserted in `eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs`, unit-demonstrated (pass-at-recorded / bite-below-floor) in `tests/ga2_vector_stage_seam.rs`. (The 0.937 cited above is the superseded 0.7.x-inferred number; the real 0.8.0 re-measure is 0.896 — see `dev/plans/runs/GA-signoff-eu7-remeasure-20260608T172804Z.json` + STATUS-0.8.0 § 7.)

dev/plans/runs/0.8.0-slice-40-ga3-codex-review-20260608T194500Z.md:456:./dev/acceptance.md:1140:**0.8.0 GA-3 asserting form (◆ HITL ruling 2026-06-08; CI-based reconciliation, floor target 0.90 UNCHANGED):** the 0.8.0 eu7 asserting verdict is "real-embedder eu7 vector-stage recall@10 whose **95% bootstrap CI is not significantly below 0.90** (`recall_ci_hi >= 0.90`)" — a **one-sided** gate (not a two-sided "floor ∈ [ci_lo, ci_hi]" test, which would wrongly fail a comfortably-high recall whose whole CI clears the floor). The prior point-estimate hard-assert (`recall >= 0.90`) PANICKED at the measured N=7667 result; the floor **constant stays 0.90** (`CURRENT_FLOOR`/`AC013B_RECALL_FLOOR` unchanged — not lowered). **Measured result satisfies the gate:** point estimate **0.896**, CI **[0.864, 0.925]** → ci_hi **0.925 ≥ 0.90 ⇒ PASS** (within measurement uncertainty / "rounding-error territory" per the HITL ruling — the 0.90 floor lies inside the 95% CI). The **point-estimate-≥0.90 recovery** and the **~4pt 0.7.x→0.8.0 vector-stage drop** (0.937→0.896) diagnosis are **0.8.1** items. This CI-form is a **0.8.0-scoped reconciliation to be REVISITED after 0.8.0**. dev/plans/runs/0.8.2-slice-0-rev-review-20260617T014318Z.log:882:+| `dev/design/0.8.2-m1-multihop-harness.md` | **0.8.2 / M1 multi-hop answer-accuracy harness — design + FROZEN pre-registration (AMENDED 2026-06-16, 6 methodology amendments)** (`status: decision-ready`): MuSiQue-Ans 2/3/4-hop datasets, the 5 arms (bm25 / passage-dense / fused(RRF k=60) / **fused+rerank** / ppr-fusion), the **fixed comparator = `fused+rerank`** (never per-hop max), the **same-answerer** protocol, ablations + bias controls, the budget plan (flash-lite cheap-validate gate + answerer-call model), the dated AMENDED frozen pre-registration block (primary endpoint = **paired pooled ≥3-hop ΔF1 vs fused+rerank** with bootstrap CI; per-hop = secondary feeding a **trend gate** that vetoes only on a significantly negative slope; EM CI-banded; unanswerable-set carries confident-wrong; decision rule frozen as `eval/m1_decision_rule.py::decide(material, em, trend, confident_wrong, power_ok)`; **whole-rule power-sim** plan) + the falsifiable Slice 5/15/20 AC list. Cross-links: `dev/plans/plan-0.8.2.md` §4, `dev/roadmap/0.8.2.md` §3, `runs/0.8.2-slice-0-prereg-methodology-review.md` | 0.8.2 Slice 0-rev | 2026-06-16 |

dev/plans/runs/0.8.2-slice-0-rev-review-20260617T014318Z.log:1408:  Because the amended design adds `frozen-field: trend-test` as the only place that freezes how `trend.neg_significant` is computed, leaving it out of `REQUIRED_FROZEN_FIELDS` means `lint_preregistration()` will still pass if that section is deleted or undated. In that scenario Slice 20 would still have a frozen boolean gate in code but no machine-checked preregistration for the bootstrap slope test that feeds it, undermining the stated “Each frozen-field line below is required” guarantee.
dev/plans/runs/0.8.2-slice-0-rev-review-20260617T014318Z.log:1414:  Because the amended design adds `frozen-field: trend-test` as the only place that freezes how `trend.neg_significant` is computed, leaving it out of `REQUIRED_FROZEN_FIELDS` means `lint_preregistration()` will still pass if that section is deleted or undated. dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-slice-0-review-20260621T201312Z.log:40: | `dev/design/0.8.2-m1-multihop-harness.md` | **0.8.2 / M1 multi-hop answer-accuracy harness — design + FROZEN pre-registration (AMENDED 2026-06-16; re-frozen 0-rev2 2026-06-19)** (`status: decision-ready`): MuSiQue-Ans 2/3/4-hop datasets, the 5 arms (bm25 / passage-dense / fused(RRF k=60) / fused+rerank / ppr-fusion), the **fixed comparator = `fused-RRF`** (re-frozen at 0-rev2 from the earlier `fused+rerank`, data-justified by the $0 bridge-vs-answer diagnostic; never per-hop max), **`MATERIAL_F1_LIFT = 0.04`** (re-frozen from 0.02/0.03), the **same-answerer** protocol, ablations + bias controls, the budget plan (flash-lite cheap-validate gate + answerer-call model), the dated frozen pre-registration block (primary endpoint = **paired pooled ≥3-hop ΔF1 vs fused-RRF** with bootstrap CI; per-hop = secondary feeding a **trend gate** that vetoes only on a significantly negative slope; EM CI-banded; unanswerable-set carries confident-wrong; decision rule frozen as `eval/m1_decision_rule.py::decide(material, em, trend, confident_wrong, power_ok)`; **whole-rule power-sim** plan) + the falsifiable Slice 5/15/20 AC list. **Applied at Slice 20 → verdict NO-GO.** Cross-links: `dev/plans/plan-0.8.2.md` §4, `dev/roadmap/0.8.2.md`, `runs/0.8.2-m1-FINDINGS.md`, `runs/0.8.2-slice-0-prereg-methodology-review.md` | 0.8.2 Slice 0-rev2 | 2026-06-19 |
dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-slice-0-review-20260621T201312Z.log:49:+| `src/python/eval/decision_rule_083.py` (+ `tests/test_decision_rule_083.py`) | **0.8.3 frozen RESOLUTION decision rule** (Slice 0) — the executable pre-registration extending `m1_decision_rule.py`'s style (pure stdlib, deterministic, consumes already-computed summary stats; **no `fathomdb`/`scipy`/`numpy`/`networkx`/RNG/I-O**): `EPS_NEAR_PARITY = 0.05`, `MEMORY_CLASSES`, `decide_083` (**BLOCK** on eu7 < 0.90 / latency first → per-class power guard `mde > ε` → **REACHED** iff every class `ci_lo ≥ −ε`; `surpass_candidates` = classes `ci_lo > 0`; `below_parity:`/`underpowered:` binding-constraint), `probe_15a_pass`/`probe_15b_pass` (the §5 $0-probe criteria), and `lint_preregistration_083` (asserts the design carries every dated `frozen-field:`). Tests run under plain `pytest` (no extension build; `FATHOMDB_TESTS_NO_REBUILD=1`) | 0.8.3 Slice 0 | 2026-06-21 |

dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-slice-0-review-20260621T201312Z.log:2810:- [P2] Drop raw-point comparison from the 15a pass gate — /home/coreyt/projects/fathomdb/src/python/eval/decision_rule_083.py:232-233
dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-slice-0-review-20260621T201312Z.log:2811:  `probe_15a_pass` is supposed to implement the preregistered rule "margin CI lower bound > 0" on both metrics, but these lines add an extra `cand > base` point-estimate check. That changes the rule for any caller that passes rounded summary points: a candidate can legitimately have `eu8_margin_ci_lo > 0` / `hard_margin_ci_lo > 0` and still tie after rounding, which will now be reported as FAIL and incorrectly suppress the embedder promotion decision for Slice 20.

dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-slice-0-review-20260621T201312Z.log:2820:  `probe_15a_pass` is supposed to implement the preregistered rule "margin CI lower bound > 0" on both metrics, but these lines add an extra `cand > base` point-estimate check. That changes the rule for any caller that passes rounded summary points: a candidate can legitimately have `eu8_margin_ci_lo > 0` / `hard_margin_ci_lo > 0` and still tie after rounding, which will now be reported as FAIL and incorrectly suppress the embedder promotion decision for Slice 20.

dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-slice-5-fix-1-review-20260621T215345Z.log:670:./dev/plans/runs/C1-threadsafe2-rebuild-20260504T022727Z.log:38:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FqXbFRfWBQyLSHnrPMGoqT","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n2\t\n3\tOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Cross-vendor convention. This is the canonical agent-instruction file; **no `CLAUDE.md` is maintained** — Claude Code reads `AGENTS.md` natively.\n4\t\n5\tBullet form, prescriptive, ≤300 lines. Link out, do not inline.\n6\t\n7\t## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n8\t\n9\t- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n10\t- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n11\t- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n12\t- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n13\t- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n14\t- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n15\t- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## 1. Invariants — read these first\n20\t\n21\t- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n22\t- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n23\t- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n24\t- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n25\t- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n26\t\n27\t## 2. Repo shape\n28\t\n29\t- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n30\t- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n31\t- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n32\t- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n33\t- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n34\t\n35\t## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n36\t\n37\tUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n38\t\n39\t| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n40\t| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n41\t| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n42\t| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n43\t| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n44\t| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n45\t| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n46\t\n47\tMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n48\t\n49\tRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n50\t\n51\tThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n52\t\n53\t### One-time setup\n54\t\n55\t- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n56\t- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n57\t- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n58\t- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n59\t\n60\t## 4. Verification ordering\n61\t\n62\tRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n63\t\n64\t1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n65\t2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n66\t3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n67\t4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n68\t\n69\tDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n70\t\n71\t## 5. Test discipline\n72\t\n73\t- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n74\t- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n75\t- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n76\t- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n77\t- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n78\t\n79\t## 6. Comment policy\n80\t\n81\t- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n82\t- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n83\t- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n84\t- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n85\t\n86\t## 7. Subagent rules\n87\t\n88\t- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n89\t- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n90\t- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n91\t- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n92\t- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n93\t\n94\t## 8. Iteration discipline\n95\t\n96\t- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n97\t- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n98\t- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the botnd the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n78\t\n79\t## 6. Comment policy\n80\t\n81\t- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n82\t- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n83\t- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n84\t- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n85\t\n86\t## 7. Subagent rules\n87\t\n88\t- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n89\t- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n90\t- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n91\t- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n92\t- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n93\t\n94\t## 8. Iteration discipline\n95\t\n96\t- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n97\t- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n98\t- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n99\t\n100\t## 9. Pointers\n101\t\n102\t- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n103\t- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n104\t- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n105\t- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n106\t- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n107\t- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n108\t\n109\t## 10. Forbidden patterns\n110\t\n111\tPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n112\t\n113\t- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n114\t- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n115\t- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n116\t- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n117\t- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n118\t- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n119\t- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n120\t- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n121\t- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n122\t- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n123\t\n124\t## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n125\t\n126\t`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n127\t\n128\t- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n129\t- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n130\t- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n131\t\n132\tIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n133\t\n134\t## 12. Working with this file\n135\t\n136\t- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n137\t- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n138\t- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n139\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8996a1e6-f0cf-41f6-bd76-ad56a5a9b42e","uuid":"7059d771-1a4a-4040-ad22-da3cf4c091a0","timestamp":"2026-05-04T02:27:33.528Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-pack5-C1-threadsafe2-rebuild-20260504T022727Z/AGENTS.md","content":"# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n\nOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Cross-vendor convention. This is the canonical agent-instruction file; **no `CLAUDE.md` is maintained** — Claude Code reads `AGENTS.md` natively.\n\nBullet form, prescriptive, ≤300 lines. Link out, do not inline.\n\n## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n\n- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Invariants — read these first\n\n- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n\n## 2. Repo shape\n\n- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n\n## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n\nUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n\n| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n\nMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n\nRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n\nThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n\n### One-time setup\n\n- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n\n## 4. Verification ordering\n\nRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n\n1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n\nDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n\n## 5. Test discipline\n\n- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n\n## 6. Comment policy\n\n- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n\n## 7. Subagent rules\n\n- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n\n## 8. Iteration discipline\n\n- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n\n## 9. Pointers\n\n- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n\n## 10. Forbidden patterns\n\nPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n\n- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n\n## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n\n`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n\n- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n\nIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n\n## 12. Working with this file\n\n- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n","numLines":139,"startLine":1,"totalLines":139}}}

Names should carry the meaning.\n83\t- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n84\t- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n85\t\n86\t## 7. Subagent rules\n87\t\n88\t- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n89\t- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n90\t- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n91\t- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n92\t- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n93\t\n94\t## 8. Iteration discipline\n95\t\n96\t- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n97\t- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n98\t- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n99\t\n100\t## 9. Pointers\n101\t\n102\t- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n103\t- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n104\t- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n105\t- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n106\t- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n107\t- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n108\t\n109\t## 10. Forbidden patterns\n110\t\n111\tPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n112\t\n113\t- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n114\t- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n115\t- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n116\t- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n117\t- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n118\t- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n119\t- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n120\t- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n121\t- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n122\t- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n123\t\n124\t## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n125\t\n126\t`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n127\t\n128\t- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n129\t- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n130\t- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n131\t\n132\tIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n133\t\n134\t## 12. Working with this file\n135\t\n136\t- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n137\t- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n138\t- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n139\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8996a1e6-f0cf-41f6-bd76-ad56a5a9b42e","uuid":"7059d771-1a4a-4040-ad22-da3cf4c091a0","timestamp":"2026-05-04T02:27:33.528Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-pack5-C1-threadsafe2-rebuild-20260504T022727Z/AGENTS.md","content":"# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n\nOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Cross-vendor convention. This is the canonical agent-instruction file; **no `CLAUDE.md` is maintained** — Claude Code reads `AGENTS.md` natively.\n\nBullet form, prescriptive, ≤300 lines. Link out, do not inline.\n\n## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n\n- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Invariants — read these first\n\n- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n\n## 2. Repo shape\n\n- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n\n## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n\nUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n\n| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n\nMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n\nRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n\nThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n\n### One-time setup\n\n- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n\n## 4. Verification ordering\n\nRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n\n1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n\nDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n\n## 5. Test discipline\n\n- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n\n## 6. Comment policy\n\n- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n\n## 7. Subagent rules\n\n- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n\n## 8. Iteration discipline\n\n- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n\n## 9. Pointers\n\n- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n\n## 10. Forbidden patterns\n\nPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n\n- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n\n## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n\n`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n\n- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n\nIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n\n## 12. Working with this file\n\n- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n","numLines":139,"startLine":1,"totalLines":139}}}

Do not rely on chat to remember.\n98\t- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n99\t\n100\t## 9. Pointers\n101\t\n102\t- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n103\t- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n104\t- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n105\t- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n106\t- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n107\t- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n108\t\n109\t## 10. Forbidden patterns\n110\t\n111\tPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n112\t\n113\t- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n114\t- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n115\t- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n116\t- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n117\t- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n118\t- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n119\t- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n120\t- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n121\t- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n122\t- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n123\t\n124\t## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n125\t\n126\t`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n127\t\n128\t- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n129\t- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n130\t- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n131\t\n132\tIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n133\t\n134\t## 12. Working with this file\n135\t\n136\t- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n137\t- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n138\t- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n139\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6045b345-74e5-4485-9471-2a1561990851","uuid":"49aec118-a290-40bf-aaad-82d0c3a80a5b","timestamp":"2026-05-15T23:41:16.781Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-10b-A-low-risk-seams-20260515T234104Z/AGENTS.md","content":"# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n\nOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Cross-vendor convention. This is the canonical agent-instruction file; **no `CLAUDE.md` is maintained** — Claude Code reads `AGENTS.md` natively.\n\nBullet form, prescriptive, ≤300 lines. Link out, do not inline.\n\n## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n\n- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Invariants — read these first\n\n- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n\n## 2. Repo shape\n\n- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n\n## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n\nUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n\n| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n\nMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n\nRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n\nThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n\n### One-time setup\n\n- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n\n## 4. Verification ordering\n\nRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n\n1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n\nDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n\n## 5. Test discipline\n\n- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n\n## 6. Comment policy\n\n- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n\n## 7. Subagent rules\n\n- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n\n## 8. Iteration discipline\n\n- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n\n## 9. Pointers\n\n- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n\n## 10. Forbidden patterns\n\nPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n\n- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n\n## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n\n`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n\n- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n\nIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n\n## 12. Working with this file\n\n- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n","numLines":139,"startLine":1,"totalLines":139}}}
