(`feedback_tdd.md`)\n24\t- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n25\t- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n26\t\n27\t## 2. Repo shape\n28\t\n29\t- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n30\t- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n31\t- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n32\t- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n33\t- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n34\t\n35\t## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n36\t\n37\tUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n38\t\n39\t| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n40\t| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n41\t| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n42\t| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n43\t| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n44\t| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n45\t| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n46\t\n47\tMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n48\t\n49\tRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n50\t\n51\tThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n52\t\n53\t### One-time setup\n54\t\n55\t- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n56\t- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n57\t- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n58\t- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n59\t\n60\t## 4. Verification ordering\n61\t\n62\tRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n63\t\n64\t1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n65\t2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n66\t3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n67\t4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n68\t\n69\tDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n70\t\n71\t## 5. Test discipline\n72\t\n73\t- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n74\t- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n75\t- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n76\t- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n77\t- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n78\t\n79\t## 6. Comment policy\n80\t\n81\t- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n82\t- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n83\t- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n84\t- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n85\t\n86\t## 7. Subagent rules\n87\t\n88\t- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n89\t- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n90\t- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n91\t- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n92\t- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n93\t\n94\t## 8. Iteration discipline\n95\t\n96\t- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n97\t- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n98\t- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n99\t\n100\t## 9. Pointers\n101\t\n102\t- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n103\t- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n104\t- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n105\t- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n106\t- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n107\t- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n108\t\n109\t## 10. Forbidden patterns\n110\t\n111\tPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n112\t\n113\t- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n114\t- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n115\t- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n116\t- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n117\t- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n118\t- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n119\t- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n120\t- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n121\t- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n122\t- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n123\t\n124\t## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n125\t\n126\t`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n127\t\n128\t- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n129\t- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n130\t- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n131\t\n132\tIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n133\t\n134\t## 12. Working with this file\n135\t\n136\t- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n137\t- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n138\t- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n139\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8996a1e6-f0cf-41f6-bd76-ad56a5a9b42e","uuid":"7059d771-1a4a-4040-ad22-da3cf4c091a0","timestamp":"2026-05-04T02:27:33.528Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-pack5-C1-threadsafe2-rebuild-20260504T022727Z/AGENTS.md","content":"# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n\nOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Cross-vendor convention. This is the canonical agent-instruction file; **no `CLAUDE.md` is maintained** — Claude Code reads `AGENTS.md` natively.\n\nBullet form, prescriptive, ≤300 lines. Link out, do not inline.\n\n## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n\n- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Invariants — read these first\n\n- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n\n## 2. Repo shape\n\n- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n\n## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n\nUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n\n| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n\nMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n\nRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n\nThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n\n### One-time setup\n\n- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n\n## 4. Verification ordering\n\nRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n\n1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n\nDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n\n## 5. Test discipline\n\n- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n\n## 6. Comment policy\n\n- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n\n## 7. Subagent rules\n\n- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n\n## 8. Iteration discipline\n\n- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n\n## 9. Pointers\n\n- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n\n## 10. Forbidden patterns\n\nPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n\n- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n\n`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n\n- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n\nIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n\n## 12. Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n38\t\n39\t| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n40\t| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n41\t| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n42\t| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n43\t| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n44\t| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n45\t| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n46\t\n47\tMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n48\t\n49\tRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n50\t\n51\tThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n52\t\n53\t### One-time setup\n54\t\n55\t- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n56\t- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n57\t- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n58\t- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n59\t\n60\t## 4. Verification ordering\n61\t\n62\tRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n63\t\n64\t1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n65\t2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n66\t3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n67\t4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n68\t\n69\tDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n70\t\n71\t## 5. Test discipline\n72\t\n73\t- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n74\t- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n75\t- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n76\t- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n77\t- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n78\t\n79\t## 6. Comment policy\n80\t\n81\t- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n82\t- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n83\t- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n84\t- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n85\t\n86\t## 7. Subagent rules\n87\t\n88\t- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n89\t- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n90\t- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n91\t- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n92\t- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n93\t\n94\t## 8. Iteration discipline\n95\t\n96\t- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n97\t- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n98\t- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n99\t\n100\t## 9. Pointers\n101\t\n102\t- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n103\t- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n104\t- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n105\t- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n106\t- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n107\t- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n108\t\n109\t## 10. Forbidden patterns\n110\t\n111\tPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n112\t\n113\t- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n114\t- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n115\t- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n116\t- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n117\t- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n118\t- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n119\t- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n120\t- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n121\t- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n122\t- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n123\t\n124\t## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n125\t\n126\t`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n127\t\n128\t- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n129\t- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n130\t- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n131\t\n132\tIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n133\t\n134\t## 12. Working with this file\n135\t\n136\t- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n137\t- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n138\t- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.\n139\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6045b345-74e5-4485-9471-2a1561990851","uuid":"49aec118-a290-40bf-aaad-82d0c3a80a5b","timestamp":"2026-05-15T23:41:16.781Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-10b-A-low-risk-seams-20260515T234104Z/AGENTS.md","content":"# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n\nOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Cross-vendor convention. This is the canonical agent-instruction file; **no `CLAUDE.md` is maintained** — Claude Code reads `AGENTS.md` natively.\n\nBullet form, prescriptive, ≤300 lines. Link out, do not inline.\n\n## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n\n- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Invariants — read these first\n\n- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n\n## 2. Repo shape\n\n- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n\n## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n\nUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n\n| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n\nMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n\nRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n\nThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n\n### One-time setup\n\n- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n\n## 4. Verification ordering\n\nRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n\n1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n\nDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n\n## 5. Test discipline\n\n- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n\n## 6. Comment policy\n\n- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n\n## 7. Subagent rules\n\n- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n\n## 8. Iteration discipline\n\n- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n\n## 9. Pointers\n\n- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n\n## 10. Forbidden patterns\n\nPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n\n- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n\n## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n\n`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n\n- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n\nIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n\n## 12. Working with this file\n\n- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n52\t\n53\t### One-time setup\n54\t\n55\t- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n56\t- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n57\t- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n58\t- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n59\t\n60\t## 4. Verification ordering\n61\t\n62\tRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n63\t\n64\t1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n65\t2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n66\t3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n67\t4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n68\t\n69\tDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n70\t\n71\t## 5. Test discipline\n72\t\n73\t- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n74\t- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n75\t- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n76\t- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n77\t- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n78\t\n79\t## 6. Comment policy\n80\t\n81\t- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n82\t- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n83\t- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n84\t- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n85\t\n86\t## 7. Subagent rules\n87\t\n88\t- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n89\t- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n90\t- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n91\t- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n92\t- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n93\t\n94\t## 8. Iteration discipline\n95\t\n96\t- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n97\t- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n98\t- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n99\t\n100\t## 9. Pointers"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"38adc285-4437-4bbf-a855-0849e65f1132","uuid":"6284fa65-d046-4b43-b811-5abe25f073f0","timestamp":"2026-05-04T02:49:13.612Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-pack5-E1-prepare-cached-readers-20260504T024906Z/AGENTS.md","content":"# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n\nOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Cross-vendor convention. This is the canonical agent-instruction file; **no `CLAUDE.md` is maintained** — Claude Code reads `AGENTS.md` natively.\n\nBullet form, prescriptive, ≤300 lines. Link out, do not inline.\n\n## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n\n- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Invariants — read these first\n\n- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n\n## 2. Repo shape\n\n- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n\n## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n\nUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n\n| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n\nMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n\nRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n\nThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n\n### One-time setup\n\n- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n\n## 4. Verification ordering\n\nRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n\n1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n\nDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n\n## 5. Test discipline\n\n- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n\n## 6. Comment policy\n\n- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n\n## 7. Subagent rules\n\n- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n\n## 8. Iteration discipline\n\n- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n\n## 9. Pointers","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":139}}}

Names should carry the meaning.\n- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n\n## 7. Subagent rules\n\n- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n\n## 8. Iteration discipline\n\n- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n\n## 9. Pointers\n\n- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n\n## 10. Forbidden patterns\n\nPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n\n- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n\n## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n\n`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n\n- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n\nIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n\n## 12. Working with this file\n\n- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d1c788b7-12bf-4bce-9fc4-3a0fdd43bef6","uuid":"5648ac8c-1fe2-4bb2-972e-c773beb5859d","timestamp":"2026-05-16T21:04:27.032Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"# AGENTS.md — FathomDB\n\nOperating manual for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot) working in this repo. Link out, do not inline.\n\n## 0. System layers — where each rule fits\n\n- **Invariants layer** — this file (front-loaded, cached, ≤300, bullets).\n- **Index layer** — `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`, `dev/interfaces/*.md`. Linked, never inlined.\n- **Retrieval layer** — grep / glob / read / LSP / tree-sitter map on demand. No local vector index.\n- **Execution layer** — typed dev-loop verbs under `scripts/agent-*.sh` emit structured JSON. Tests are oracle.\n- **Persistence layer** — `dev/progress/<release>.md` + ADR supersession. Compaction-safe.\n- **Permission layer** — three-tier sandbox + worktree-per-task + egress allowlist (`.claude/settings.json`).\n- **Topology layer** — main thread orchestrates; implementer (worktree) + code-reviewer fan out at clean seams; single-agent for shared-state edits.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Invariants — read these first\n\n- **Memory first.** Read `MEMORY.md` and the `feedback_*.md` files it points to before planning any change. They encode prior corrections that override default behavior.\n- **ADRs are authoritative.** Decisions live in `dev/adr/`. Index: `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`. Do not contradict an accepted ADR; propose a successor instead.\n- **TDD is mandatory.** Failing test first; red → green → refactor. Mechanical version bumps and renames are the only exception. (`feedback_tdd.md`)\n- **Stale > missing.** A wrong comment, doc, or ADR is more harmful than its absence. If you cannot maintain something, delete it.\n- **Public surface is contract.** Anything in `dev/interfaces/` or `pub` Rust APIs is a contract; changes need an ADR or interface-doc update in the same PR.\n\n## 2. Repo shape\n\n- **Rust workspace** under `src/rust/crates/` — 7 crates: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, `fathomdb-embedder-api`.\n- **Python bindings** under `src/python/` (package: `fathomdb`).\n- **TypeScript bindings** under `src/ts/`.\n- **Public docs** under `docs/` (MkDocs-built).\n- **Internal engineering docs** under `dev/`: `adr/`, `interfaces/`, `progress/`, `plans/`, `tmp/`.\n\n## 3. Build / test / lint commands\n\nUse the typed dev-loop verbs (Phase 2). Each emits **concise output on pass, structured diagnostic on fail**, with full output spilled to `/tmp/fathomdb-agent-<verb>-<pid>.log` when capped.\n\n| Verb      | Script                         | Purpose                                                           |\n| --------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| build     | `./scripts/agent-build.sh`     | Compile workspace (Rust + Python install + TS build if installed) |\n| lint      | `./scripts/agent-lint.sh`      | clippy + ruff + markdownlint + prettier --check + lychee          |\n| typecheck | `./scripts/agent-typecheck.sh` | cargo check + pyright + tsc --noEmit                              |\n| test      | `./scripts/agent-test.sh`      | cargo test + pytest                                               |\n| verify    | `./scripts/agent-verify.sh`    | lint → typecheck → test (short-circuits on first fail)            |\n\nMarkdown lint covers `AGENTS.md`, `dev/plans/`, `dev/progress/`, README files, and root metadata. Pre-existing legacy under `dev/adr/`, `dev/design/`, `dev/agents/`, `dev/deps/`, etc. is excluded — clean up incrementally when touching those files. Auto-fix: `npm run format:md` (prettier --write) + `./node_modules/.bin/markdownlint-cli2 --fix`.\n\nRun `./scripts/agent-verify.sh` after every meaningful edit. Do not ship a PR with verify failing.\n\nThe broader CI gate is `./scripts/check.sh` (adds mkdocs build); the agent-loop gate is `agent-verify.sh`. Long-run test variants (e.g. AC-021 60 s window, AC-059b ~1000-iteration cursor-race fixture) are exercised only via `scripts/check.sh` with `AGENT_LONG=1`; `agent-verify.sh` skips them for runtime budget.\n\n### One-time setup\n\n- Rust toolchain: stable per `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`. clippy + rustfmt come with rustup defaults.\n- Python dev tooling: `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'` — installs `pytest`, `hypothesis`, `ruff`, `pyright`. Without this, the Python lint/typecheck/test steps emit a skip notice and pass without exercising.\n- TypeScript: `cd src/ts && npm install` if you intend to touch TS. Without this, TS verbs skip.\n- Markdown tooling: `npm install` at repo root — installs `markdownlint-cli2` + `prettier`. `cargo install --locked lychee` for link checking (one-time). All wired up by `./scripts/bootstrap.sh`.\n\n## 4. Verification ordering\n\nRun in latency order; short-circuit on first failure:\n\n1. **lint** (clippy / ruff) — fastest signal, catches most style + correctness issues\n2. **typecheck** (cargo check / pyright / tsc) — catches type errors before tests run them\n3. **unit tests** (`agent-test.sh`)\n4. **integration tests** — opt-in, gated by feature/env flag; not part of `agent-verify`\n\nDo not paraphrase, summarize, or shorten compiler diagnostics — pass them through unaltered. Rust diagnostics in particular are best-in-class; Anthropic's RustAssistant numbers depend on them being unaltered.\n\n## 5. Test discipline\n\n- **Failing test first.** Write the failing test, commit it (or stage it visible to the reviewer), then implement.\n- **Test files are read-only during fix-to-spec.** Do not edit a test to make a failing build pass; fix the code.\n- **Property-based tests required** for codec / projection / recovery / round-trip layers. Rust: `proptest` (dev-dep on `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-engine`, `fathomdb-query`). Python: `hypothesis` (in `[dev]` and `[test]` optional deps; install via `pip install -e 'src/python[dev]'`). Scaffolds: `src/rust/crates/<crate>/tests/property_template.rs` and `src/python/tests/test_property_template.py` — replace the trivial property with real round-trip / invariant assertions when domain types land.\n- **No agent-generated oracles.** Tests must encode human intent; do not regenerate snapshot or golden tests autonomously.\n- **Retry budget.** If you hit the same failure mode twice, stop. Re-read the failing test, the relevant ADR, and the relevant `feedback_*.md`. Do not loop a third time without re-thinking; if necessary, `/clear` and reset.\n\n## 6. Comment policy\n\n- **Public-API docstrings:** required for `pub` Rust items, top-level Python functions/classes, exported TS symbols. Document contract: inputs, outputs, errors, panics, invariants.\n- **Internal helpers:** no docstrings unless behavior is non-obvious. Names should carry the meaning.\n- **Inline comments:** why / invariants / hazards only. Never restate what code does. Never reference the current task or PR. (Per CLAUDE-default rules and `feedback_reliability_principles.md`.)\n- **Stale comment > delete.** If a comment no longer matches the code, delete it; do not \"update later.\"\n\n## 7. Subagent rules\n\n- **Main thread orchestrates.** Do not spawn an \"orchestrator\" subagent — the main thread _is_ the orchestrator. (`feedback_orchestrator_thread.md`)\n- **Releases:** main thread plans; delegate coding to `implementer` (in worktree); request diff review from `code-reviewer`. (`feedback_orchestrate_releases.md`)\n- **Subagents win for fan-out.** Parallel research, format-strict review, output isolation. Examples: searching across crates, auditing a diff, generating an ADR draft.\n- **Subagents lose for shared-state edits.** A multi-agent edit pipeline drops tacit context at every handoff. Single-agent for any edit on shared mutable state.\n- **Worktrees** are the unit of isolation. Implementer subagents always operate in a fresh worktree; main thread never edits in a subagent's worktree.\n\n## 8. Iteration discipline\n\n- **Cap retry-budget at ~2 same-issue corrections.** Beyond that, stop, externalize plan to `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`, `/clear`, restart with the plan in front of you.\n- **Compact-aware.** Anything that must survive compaction goes on disk: ADRs, progress logs, plan files, MEMORY entries. Do not rely on chat to remember.\n- **Front-load invariants, end-load tasks.** When prompting yourself or constructing context, put rules near the top, the immediate task near the bottom (lost-in-the-middle).\n\n## 9. Pointers\n\n- **Decisions:** `dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-decision-index.md`\n- **Interfaces:** `dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript,cli,wire}.md`\n- **Progress log:** `dev/progress/0.6.0.md`\n- **Plans:** `dev/plans/`\n- **Research:** `dev/notes/context-research-agentic-best-practices.md` (the best-practices synthesis this file operationalizes)\n- **Memory:** `MEMORY.md` (auto-loaded into Claude Code session start)\n\n## 10. Forbidden patterns\n\nPulled from MEMORY `feedback_*.md`. Violations are correctness bugs, not style preferences.\n\n- **No mocking the database.** Integration tests hit a real database. (`feedback_tdd.md`-adjacent; prior incident.)\n- **No skipping hooks.** Never `--no-verify`, never `--no-gpg-sign` unless explicitly approved.\n- **No backwards-compatibility shims** in pre-1.0 rewrites.\n- **No data migrations** in managed-vec projection releases. (`feedback_no_data_migration.md`)\n- **No `c_char` hardcoded as `i8` or `u8`** at C interop boundaries. (`feedback_cross_platform_rust.md`)\n- **No `pip install` + manual `cargo build` + `cp`** for Python native modules — use `pip install -e python/`. (`feedback_python_native_build.md`)\n- **No `yaml.safe_load` as workflow validator** — use `actionlint`. (`feedback_workflow_validation.md`)\n- **No \"fix in 0.7\"** for reliability bugs. Net-negative LoC on reliability releases. (`feedback_reliability_principles.md`)\n- **No \"green CI = done\"** for releases — install the published wheel and run end-to-end open/close/exit before declaring done. (`feedback_release_verification.md`)\n- **Vector identity belongs to the embedder.** (`project_vector_identity_invariant.md`)\n\n## 11. Permission model (for the Claude Code harness)\n\n`.claude/settings.json` enforces a 3-tier model:\n\n- **read-only** (auto-allowed): `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, plus `Bash` for the agent-loop verbs and read-only git/cargo/uv operations.\n- **workspace-write** (auto-allowed): `Edit`, `Write` within the repo; `git add`, `git commit`, `git restore`.\n- **escalated** (denied or asked): destructive ops (`rm`, `mv`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean`); network beyond the package registries (`curl`, `wget`); `git push` requires confirmation.\n\nIf the agent needs an op outside this model, surface it to the user; do not look for workarounds.\n\n## 12. Working with this file\n\n- This file should stay ≤300 lines. If it grows, link out to a scoped doc and reference it.\n- Per-crate `AGENTS.md` files are **not maintained** until each crate has non-scaffold content. Stale > missing.\n- Update this file in the same PR as any change that invalidates one of its rules.","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

then\n  python_bin=\".venv/bin/python\"\nelif command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  python_bin=\"$(command -v python3)\"\nfi\n\nif [ -n \"$python_bin\" ] && \"$python_bin\" -c 'import pytest' >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -d src/python/tests ]; then\n  run_capped test-python \"$python_bin\" -m pytest -q src/python/tests\nelse\n  skip_notice test-python \"pytest not installed or no tests dir\"\nfi\n\n# TypeScript\nif [ -d src/ts/node_modules ]; then\n  echo \"Installing TypeScript dev tooling...\"\n  (cd src/ts && npm install --silent)\nfi\n\n# Repo-wide markdown tooling (markdownlint-cli2 + prettier).\nif [ -f package.json ] && [ ! -d node_modules ]; then\n  run_capped test-python \"$python_bin\" -m pytest -q src/python/tests\nelse\n  skip_notice test-python \"pytest not installed or no tests dir\"\nfi\n\n# TypeScript\nif [ -d src/ts/node_modules ]; then\n  run_capped test-ts bash -c 'cd src/ts && npm test --silent'\nelse\n  skip_notice test-ts \"src/ts/node_modules not installed\"\nfi\n---\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\nset -euo pipefail\n\ncd \"$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)\"\n\necho \"FathomDB scaffold bootstrap\"\necho \"Public docs live in docs/ and build with MkDocs.\"\necho \"Internal engineering docs live in dev/.\"\necho \"Rust workspace members live under src/rust/crates/.\"\necho \"Run scripts/agent-verify.sh during the agent loop, scripts/check.sh as the broader CI gate.\"\n\n# Repo-tracked git hooks (pre-commit fmt/lint, pre-push agent-verify).\nscripts/install-hooks.sh\n\n# Python dev tooling — pytest, hypothesis, ruff, pyright.\nif [ -f src/python/pyproject.toml ]; then\n  echo \"Installing Python dev tooling into .venv (pytest + hypothesis + ruff + pyright)...\"\n  python3 -m venv .venv\n  .venv/bin/python -m pip install --quiet --upgrade pip\n  .venv/bin/python -m pip install --quiet -e 'src/python[dev]'\n  .venv/bin/python -c 'import pytest, hypothesis'\n  .venv/bin/pyright -p src/python >/dev/null\nfi\n\n# TypeScript dev tooling.\nif [ -f src/ts/package.json ] && [ ! -d src/ts/node_modules ]; Pinned: yaml.safe_load passes\n# schema-invalid syntax that GitHub silently rejects, so we need a real\n# linter for .github/workflows/*.yml. -d src/ts/node_modules ]; then\n  echo \"Installing TypeScript dev tooling...\"\n  (cd src/ts && npm install --silent)\nfi\n\n# Repo-wide markdown tooling (markdownlint-cli2 + prettier).\nif [ -f package.json ] && [ ! -d node_modules ]; then\n  echo \"Installing markdown dev tooling (markdownlint-cli2 + prettier)...\"\n  npm install --silent\nfi\n\n# Lychee link checker (Rust binary).\nif ! command -v lychee >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  echo \"Installing lychee link checker...\"\n  cargo install --locked --quiet lychee\nfi\n\n# actionlint — workflow validator. Pass-through diagnostics unparaphrased on failure.\nset -euo pipefail\n\nSCRIPT_DIR=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")\" && pwd)\"\n# shellcheck source=lib/agent-output.sh\n. \"$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/agent-output.sh\"\ncd_repo_root\n\n# Rust: clippy with -D warnings (treat warnings as errors)\nrun_capped lint-rust cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --quiet -- -D warnings\n\n# Rust: format check\nrun_capped lint-rustfmt cargo fmt --all --check\n\n# Migration authoring policy\nrun_capped lint-migrations \"$SCRIPT_DIR/agent-lint-migrations.sh\"\n\n# Python: ruff if available\nruff_bin=\"\"\nif [ -x .venv/bin/ruff ]; then\n  ruff_bin=\".venv/bin/ruff\"\nelif command -v ruff >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  ruff_bin=\"$(command -v ruff)\"\nfi\n\nif [ -n \"$ruff_bin\" ]; then\n  run_capped lint-python \"$ruff_bin\" check src/python\nelse\n  skip_notice lint-python \"ruff not installed\"\nfi\n\n# TypeScript: ESLint not configured yet\nskip_notice lint-ts \"ESLint not configured\"\n\n# Workflows: actionlint is the canonical validator per feedback_workflow_validation\n# (yaml.safe_load passes schema-invalid syntax GitHub silently rejects).\nif command -v actionlint >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  run_capped lint-actions actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml\nelse\n  skip_notice lint-actions \"actionlint not installed (run scripts/bootstrap.sh)\"\nfi\n\n# Markdown: structural + format + link integrity\n\"$SCRIPT_DIR/agent-lint-md.sh\"","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"17a0ef0a-e71e-4f47-89a1-81a3e1f65155","uuid":"6eb4844e-12c8-4e93-ac53-f2af94e2b6fb","timestamp":"2026-05-17T19:57:48.717Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# Lint all language surfaces.
