(file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"17a0ef0a-e71e-4f47-89a1-81a3e1f65155","uuid":"8c96d692-399d-42ab-aa4b-a0c577c022d4","timestamp":"2026-05-17T20:02:57.341Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-12-S-security-fixtures-20260517T195735Z/scripts/bootstrap.sh","oldString":"# Lychee link checker (Rust binary).\nif ! command -v lychee >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  echo \"Installing lychee link checker...\"\n  cargo install --locked --quiet lychee\nfi\n\n# actionlint","newString":"# Lychee link checker (Rust binary).\nif ! command -v lychee >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  echo \"Installing lychee link checker...\"\n  cargo install --locked --quiet lychee\nfi\n\n# strace — required by the AC-036 no-listen and AC-037 netns-deny-egress\n# security fixtures under scripts/security/. ~50KB, unprivileged at\n# runtime. Skip silently if apt isn't available (non-Debian hosts); the\n# fixtures will report a BLOCKER exit themselves.\nif ! command -v strace >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n    echo \"Installing strace (AC-036/AC-037 security fixtures)...\"\n    sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y strace >/dev/null 2>&1 || \\\n      echo \"strace install failed; AC-036/AC-037 will report BLOCKER until installed\" >&2\n  else\n    echo \"strace not installed and apt-get unavailable; install via host package manager\" >&2\n    echo \"  (required by scripts/security/check-no-listen.sh + check-netns-deny-egress.sh)\" >&2\n  fi\nfi\n\n# actionlint","originalFile":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\nset -euo pipefail\n\ncd \"$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)\"\n\necho \"FathomDB scaffold bootstrap\"\necho \"Public docs live in docs/ and build with MkDocs.\"\necho \"Internal engineering docs live in dev/.\"\necho \"Rust workspace members live under src/rust/crates/.\"\necho \"Run scripts/agent-verify.sh during the agent loop, scripts/check.sh as the broader CI gate.\"\n\n# Repo-tracked git hooks (pre-commit fmt/lint, pre-push agent-verify).\nscripts/install-hooks.sh\n\n# Python dev tooling — pytest, hypothesis, ruff, pyright.\nif [ -f src/python/pyproject.toml ]; then\n  echo \"Installing Python dev tooling into .venv (pytest + hypothesis + ruff + pyright)...\"\n  python3 -m venv .venv\n  .venv/bin/python -m pip install --quiet --upgrade pip\n  .venv/bin/python -m pip install --quiet -e 'src/python[dev]'\n  .venv/bin/python -c 'import pytest, hypothesis'\n  .venv/bin/pyright -p src/python >/dev/null\nfi\n\n# TypeScript dev tooling.\nif [ -f src/ts/package.json ] && [ ! -d src/ts/node_modules ]; then\n  echo \"Installing TypeScript dev tooling...\"\n  (cd src/ts && npm install --silent)\nfi\n\n# Repo-wide markdown tooling (markdownlint-cli2 + prettier).\nif [ -f package.json ] && [ ! -d node_modules ]; then\n  echo \"Installing markdown dev tooling (markdownlint-cli2 + prettier)...\"\n  npm install --silent\nfi\n\n# Lychee link checker (Rust binary).\nif ! command -v lychee >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  echo \"Installing lychee link checker...\"\n  cargo install --locked --quiet lychee\nfi\n\n# actionlint — workflow validator. Pinned: yaml.safe_load passes\n# schema-invalid syntax that GitHub silently rejects, so we need a real\n# linter for .github/workflows/*.yml. Version pin matches a recent stable\n# release; bump deliberately, not drifted.\nif ! command -v actionlint >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  if command -v go >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n    echo \"Installing actionlint v1.7.7 via go install...\"\n    GO111MODULE=on go install github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.7\n  else\n    echo \"actionlint not installed and go toolchain unavailable; install actionlint manually\" >&2\n    echo \"  see https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint/releases (pin v1.7.7)\" >&2\n  fi\nfi\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":40,"oldLines":6,"newStart":40,"newLines":21,"lines":["   cargo install --locked --quiet lychee"," fi"," ","+# strace — required by the AC-036 no-listen and AC-037 netns-deny-egress","+# security fixtures under scripts/security/. ~50KB, unprivileged at","+# runtime. Skip silently if apt isn't available (non-Debian hosts); the","+# fixtures will report a BLOCKER exit themselves.","+if ! command -v strace >/dev/null 2>&1; then","+  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then","+    echo \"Installing strace (AC-036/AC-037 security fixtures)...\"","+    sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y strace >/dev/null 2>&1 || \\","+      echo \"strace install failed; AC-036/AC-037 will report BLOCKER until installed\" >&2","+  else","+    echo \"strace not installed and apt-get unavailable; install via host package manager\" >&2","+    echo \"  (required by scripts/security/check-no-listen.sh + check-netns-deny-egress.sh)\" >&2","+  fi","+fi","+"," # actionlint — workflow validator. Pinned: yaml.safe_load passes"," # schema-invalid syntax that GitHub silently rejects, so we need a real"," # linter for .github/workflows/*.yml. dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism.md:109:**H — Process-global Rust registry keyed by canonical path (no OS lock).** A `OnceLock<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, Weak<EngineRuntime>>>>` or similar at the crate level that refuses a second `Engine.open` for the same canonical path. Rejected: covers same-process only, not cross-process; cross-binding (Python and TypeScript loaded as separate cdylib instances within one operator process) bypasses it because each cdylib has its own static — separate `OnceLock`s, separate registries, no coordination; not load-bearing for the failure modes that matter. Useful only as an additional in-language ergonomic guard, not as the primary mechanism.

dev/plans/runs/0.6.1-AXIS-E-PIN-review-20260524T205304Z.log:1959:dev/plans/runs/11c-set-version-20260516T221655Z.log:57:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TSzGE9Xs9xm54tEQ5bPDc1","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\ttitle: Release Subsystem Design\n3\tdate: 2026-05-12\n4\ttarget_release: 0.6.0\n5\tdesc: Version axes, tiered publish order, multi-registry publish discipline, and post-publish verification\n6\tblast_radius: release workflows; REQ-047..REQ-052; scripts/set-version.sh; .github/workflows/release.yml; dev/plans/ci-deferred.md\n7\tstatus: locked\n8\t---\n9\t\n10\t# Release Design\n11\t\n12\tThis file owns release-gate mechanics: version axes, sibling-package\n13\tco-tagging, tiered publish order, registry-installed smoke verification, and\n14\tatomic completion of the multi-registry publish flow.\n15\t\n16\t## Version axes (2026-05-12)\n17\t\n18\t0.6.0 ships **two independent version axes**, not one global workspace\n19\tversion:\n20\t\n21\t- **Axis W (workspace).** Lockstep version for the runtime/binding/CLI\n22\t  surface: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`,\n23\t  `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, plus the\n24\t  Python (`src/python/`) and TypeScript (`src/ts/`) binding packages.\n25\t- **Axis E (embedder-api).** Independent semver for\n26\t  `fathomdb-embedder-api` only. Tagged and published on every workspace\n27\t  release per REQ-048, but the embedder-api version bumps only when its\n28\t  trait surface changes.\n29\t\n30\tRationale: `fathomdb-embedder-api` is the semver-stable trait surface\n31\tper ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology (sibling-crate amendment) and\n32\tADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol. Lockstep-bumping it on every workspace\n33\trelease would create false version churn for downstream embedder\n34\tconsumers pinning the trait, and would break the version-skew-resolution\n35\tintent of REQ-047. Two axes preserve `fathomdb-embedder` /\n36\t`fathomdb-embedder-api` consumer ergonomics without giving up workspace\n37\tlockstep elsewhere.\n38\t\n39\tBoth axes share a single tag prefix (`v<axis-W-version>`) per release;\n40\tthe embedder-api crate carries its independent `version` field in its\n41\town `Cargo.toml`. Version-consistency checks (`set-version.sh\n42\t--check-files`) verify Axis W lockstep across all Axis-W manifests and\n43\tverify Axis E sits at its independently declared version.\n44\t\n45\t## Tiered publish order\n46\t\n47\tThe publish order is a strict topological sort of the workspace crate\n48\tdependency graph plus the two binding packages. Index-propagation sleeps\n49\tsit between tiers (pattern from pre-0.6.0 `.github/workflows/release.yml`).\n50\t\n51\t| Tier | Targets                                                      | Why                                                                                                                                                                 |\n52\t| ---- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n53\t| T1   | `fathomdb-embedder-api`                                      | Leaf. Axis E. Must publish first so every later crate can resolve it on crates.io.                                                                                  |\n54\t| T2   | `fathomdb-schema`                                            | Leaf (no in-workspace deps beyond external crates).                                                                                                                 |\n55\t| T3   | `fathomdb-query`                                             | Depends on `fathomdb-schema` (per ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology consequences) and `fathomdb-embedder-api` indirectly via retrieval glue.                                 |\n56\t| T4   | `fathomdb-engine`                                            | Depends on T1+T2+T3. Largest crate; publishes only after its deps are resolvable on crates.io.                                                                      |\n57\t| T5   | `fathomdb-embedder`                                          | Depends on `fathomdb-embedder-api` (Axis E). Independent of `fathomdb-engine`.                                                                                      |\n58\t| T6   | `fathomdb`                                                   | Facade. Depends on `fathomdb-engine`.                                                                                                                               |\n59\t| T7   | `fathomdb-cli`                                               | Depends on `fathomdb` facade per ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology amendment 2026-05-11.                                                                                     |\n60\t| T8   | Python wheel (`src/python/`); TypeScript package (`src/ts/`) | Both wrap `fathomdb-engine` directly (PyO3 / napi-rs); they can publish in parallel after T4 resolves on crates.io. PyPI / npm publishing is independent of T5..T7. |\n61\t\n62\tCross-ecosystem gate: every tier must succeed before the next tier\n63\tstarts. `all-builds-passed` gates Tier T1 from starting until every\n64\tmatrix build for the release has produced an artifact (no \"publish T1\n65\twhile the napi-rs prebuild is still red\"). Cross-registry atomicity is\n66\tcovered by the post-publish smoke step below.\n67\t\n68\tTier inter-step sleep: keep the pre-0.6.0 60-second\n69\tcrates.io-index-propagation sleep between tiers. Without the sleep,\n70\tlater tiers race the index and fail dependency resolution.\n71\t\n72\t## Sibling-package co-tagging\n73\t\n74\tPer REQ-048, `fathomdb-embedder-api` and `fathomdb-embedder` carry the\n75\tsame git tag as the workspace release even though `fathomdb-embedder-api`\n76\truns on its own version axis. The tag prefix is workspace (Axis W);\n77\t`fathomdb-embedder-api` records its independent version inside the tag\n78\tcommit via its `Cargo.toml`.\n79\t\n80\t## Post-publish smoke\n81\t\n82\tPer `feedback_release_verification`, \"green CI + published wheel\" is not\n83\tdone. Release-evidence sweep installs the published wheel from PyPI and\n84\truns an end-to-end open + close + exit smoke before the release is\n85\tdeclared signed. Equivalent npm smoke applies for `@fathomdb/...`\n86\tpublishes. Crate publishes are smoked via `cargo install fathomdb-cli`\n87\tfollowed by `fathomdb doctor check-integrity --json` against a fresh\n88\tfixture database.\n89\t\n90\t## Sources\n91\t\n92\t- `dev/plans/ci-deferred.md` enumerates the pre-0.6.0 workflows being\n93\t  restored.\n94\t- `ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology` (incl. 2026-04-27 sibling amendment + 2026-05-11\n95\t  CLI-via-facade amendment) owns the crate set.\n96\t- `ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol` owns the `fathomdb-embedder-api` trait\n97\t  stability posture.\n98\t- REQ-047, REQ-048, REQ-049, REQ-050, REQ-052.\n99\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4cda15f5-dd21-46d9-b4db-04d61e252748","uuid":"eb05b2c9-c034-4bcc-97b7-b02b04c4861e","timestamp":"2026-05-16T22:17:09.206Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-11c-set-version-20260516T221655Z/dev/design/release.md","content":"---\ntitle: Release Subsystem Design\ndate: 2026-05-12\ntarget_release: 0.6.0\ndesc: Version axes, tiered publish order, multi-registry publish discipline, and post-publish verification\nblast_radius: release workflows; REQ-047..REQ-052; scripts/set-version.sh; .github/workflows/release.yml; dev/plans/ci-deferred.md\nstatus: locked\n---\n\n# Release Design\n\nThis file owns release-gate mechanics: version axes, sibling-package\nco-tagging, tiered publish order, registry-installed smoke verification, and\natomic completion of the multi-registry publish flow.\n\n## Version axes (2026-05-12)\n\n0.6.0 ships **two independent version axes**, not one global workspace\nversion:\n\n- **Axis W (workspace).** Lockstep version for the runtime/binding/CLI\n  surface: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`,\n  `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, plus the\n  Python (`src/python/`) and TypeScript (`src/ts/`) binding packages.\n- **Axis E (embedder-api).** Independent semver for\n  `fathomdb-embedder-api` only. Tagged and published on every workspace\n  release per REQ-048, but the embedder-api version bumps only when its\n  trait surface changes.\n\nRationale: `fathomdb-embedder-api` is the semver-stable trait surface\nper ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology (sibling-crate amendment) and\nADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol. Lockstep-bumping it on every workspace\nrelease would create false version churn for downstream embedder\nconsumers pinning the trait, and would break the version-skew-resolution\nintent of REQ-047. Two axes preserve `fathomdb-embedder` /\n`fathomdb-embedder-api` consumer ergonomics without giving up workspace\nlockstep elsewhere.\n\nBoth axes share a single tag prefix (`v<axis-W-version>`) per release;\nthe embedder-api crate carries its independent `version` field in its\nown `Cargo.toml`. Version-consistency checks (`set-version.sh\n--check-files`) verify Axis W lockstep across all Axis-W manifests and\nverify Axis E sits at its independently declared version.\n\n## Tiered publish order\n\nThe publish order is a strict topological sort of the workspace crate\ndependency graph plus the two binding packages. Index-propagation sleeps\nsit between tiers (pattern from pre-0.6.0 `.github/workflows/release.yml`).\n\n| Tier | Targets                                                      | Why                                                                                                                                                                 |\n| ---- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| T1   | `fathomdb-embedder-api`                                      | Leaf. Axis E. Must publish first so every later crate can resolve it on crates.io.                                                                                  |\n| T2   | `fathomdb-schema`                                            | Leaf (no in-workspace deps beyond external crates).                                                                                                                 |\n| T3   | `fathomdb-query`                                             | Depends on `fathomdb-schema` (per ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology consequences) and `fathomdb-embedder-api` indirectly via retrieval glue.                                 |\n| T4   | `fathomdb-engine`                                            | Depends on T1+T2+T3. Largest crate; publishes only after its deps are resolvable on crates.io.                                                                      |\n| T5   | `fathomdb-embedder`                                          | Depends on `fathomdb-embedder-api` (Axis E). Independent of `fathomdb-engine`.                                                                                      |\n| T6   | `fathomdb`                                                   | Facade. Depends on `fathomdb-engine`.                                                                                                                               |\n| T7   | `fathomdb-cli`                                               | Depends on `fathomdb` facade per ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology amendment 2026-05-11.                                                                                     |\n| T8   | Python wheel (`src/python/`); TypeScript package (`src/ts/`) | Both wrap `fathomdb-engine` directly (PyO3 / napi-rs); they can publish in parallel after T4 resolves on crates.io. PyPI / npm publishing is independent of T5..T7. |\n\nCross-ecosystem gate: every tier must succeed before the next tier\nstarts. `all-builds-passed` gates Tier T1 from starting until every\nmatrix build for the release has produced an artifact (no \"publish T1\nwhile the napi-rs prebuild is still red\"). Cross-registry atomicity is\ncovered by the post-publish smoke step below.\n\nTier inter-step sleep: keep the pre-0.6.0 60-second\ncrates.io-index-propagation sleep between tiers. Without the sleep,\nlater tiers race the index and fail dependency resolution.\n\n## Sibling-package co-tagging\n\nPer REQ-048, `fathomdb-embedder-api` and `fathomdb-embedder` carry the\nsame git tag as the workspace release even though `fathomdb-embedder-api`\nruns on its own version axis. The tag prefix is workspace (Axis W);\n`fathomdb-embedder-api` records its independent version inside the tag\ncommit via its `Cargo.toml`.\n\n## Post-publish smoke\n\nPer `feedback_release_verification`, \"green CI + published wheel\" is not\ndone. Release-evidence sweep installs the published wheel from PyPI and\nruns an end-to-end open + close + exit smoke before the release is\ndeclared signed. Equivalent npm smoke applies for `@fathomdb/...`\npublishes. Crate publishes are smoked via `cargo install fathomdb-cli`\nfollowed by `fathomdb doctor check-integrity --json` against a fresh\nfixture database.\n\n## Sources\n\n- `dev/plans/ci-deferred.md` enumerates the pre-0.6.0 workflows being\n  restored.\n- `ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology` (incl. 2026-04-27 sibling amendment + 2026-05-11\n  CLI-via-facade amendment) owns the crate set.\n- `ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol` owns the `fathomdb-embedder-api` trait\n  stability posture.\n- REQ-047, REQ-048, REQ-049, REQ-050, REQ-052.\n","numLines":99,"startLine":1,"totalLines":99}}}

Release-evidence sweep installs the published wheel from PyPI and\nruns an end-to-end open + close + exit smoke before the release is\ndeclared signed. Equivalent npm smoke applies for `fathomdb` publishes.\nThe npm package is published as bare `fathomdb` (not a `@fathomdb/`\nscope) per 11d Blocker 7 — single brand across crates, wheel, and npm.\nCrate publishes are smoked via `cargo install fathomdb-cli`\nfollowed by `fathomdb doctor check-integrity --json` against a fresh\nfixture database.","originalFile":"---\ntitle: Release Subsystem Design\ndate: 2026-05-12\ntarget_release: 0.6.0\ndesc: Version axes, tiered publish order, multi-registry publish discipline, and post-publish verification\nblast_radius: release workflows; REQ-047..REQ-052; scripts/set-version.sh; .github/workflows/release.yml; dev/plans/ci-deferred.md\nstatus: locked\n---\n\n# Release Design\n\nThis file owns release-gate mechanics: version axes, sibling-package\nco-tagging, tiered publish order, registry-installed smoke verification, and\natomic completion of the multi-registry publish flow.\n\n## Version axes (2026-05-12)\n\n0.6.0 ships **two independent version axes**, not one global workspace\nversion:\n\n- **Axis W (workspace).** Lockstep version for the runtime/binding/CLI\n  surface: `fathomdb`, `fathomdb-cli`, `fathomdb-engine`,\n  `fathomdb-query`, `fathomdb-schema`, `fathomdb-embedder`, plus the\n  Python (`src/python/`) and TypeScript (`src/ts/`) binding packages.\n- **Axis E (embedder-api).** Independent semver for\n  `fathomdb-embedder-api` only. Tagged and published on every workspace\n  release per REQ-048, but the embedder-api version bumps only when its\n  trait surface changes.\n\nRationale: `fathomdb-embedder-api` is the semver-stable trait surface\nper ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology (sibling-crate amendment) and\nADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol. Lockstep-bumping it on every workspace\nrelease would create false version churn for downstream embedder\nconsumers pinning the trait, and would break the version-skew-resolution\nintent of REQ-047. Two axes preserve `fathomdb-embedder` /\n`fathomdb-embedder-api` consumer ergonomics without giving up workspace\nlockstep elsewhere.\n\nBoth axes share a single tag prefix (`v<axis-W-version>`) per release;\nthe embedder-api crate carries its independent `version` field in its\nown `Cargo.toml`. Version-consistency checks (`set-version.sh\n--check-files`) verify Axis W lockstep across all Axis-W manifests and\nverify Axis E sits at its independently declared version.\n\n## Tiered publish order\n\nThe publish order is a strict topological sort of the workspace crate\ndependency graph plus the two binding packages. Index-propagation sleeps\nsit between tiers (pattern from pre-0.6.0 `.github/workflows/release.yml`).\n\n| Tier | Targets                                                      | Why                                                                                                                                                                 |\n| ---- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| T1   | `fathomdb-embedder-api`                                      | Leaf. Axis E. Must publish first so every later crate can resolve it on crates.io.                                                                                  |\n| T2   | `fathomdb-schema`                                            | Leaf (no in-workspace deps beyond external crates).                                                                                                                 |\n| T3   | `fathomdb-query`                                             | Depends on `fathomdb-schema` (per ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology consequences) and `fathomdb-embedder-api` indirectly via retrieval glue.                                 |\n| T4   | `fathomdb-engine`                                            | Depends on T1+T2+T3. Largest crate; publishes only after its deps are resolvable on crates.io.                                                                      |\n| T5   | `fathomdb-embedder`                                          | Depends on `fathomdb-embedder-api` (Axis E). Independent of `fathomdb-engine`.                                                                                      |\n| T6   | `fathomdb`                                                   | Facade. Depends on `fathomdb-engine`.                                                                                                                               |\n| T7   | `fathomdb-cli`                                               | Depends on `fathomdb` facade per ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology amendment 2026-05-11.                                                                                     |\n| T8   | Python wheel (`src/python/`); TypeScript package (`src/ts/`) | Both wrap `fathomdb-engine` directly (PyO3 / napi-rs); they can publish in parallel after T4 resolves on crates.io. PyPI / npm publishing is independent of T5..T7. |\n\nCross-ecosystem gate: every tier must succeed before the next tier\nstarts. `all-builds-passed` gates Tier T1 from starting until every\nmatrix build for the release has produced an artifact (no \"publish T1\nwhile the napi-rs prebuild is still red\"). Cross-registry atomicity is\ncovered by the post-publish smoke step below.\n\nTier inter-step sleep: keep the pre-0.6.0 60-second\ncrates.io-index-propagation sleep between tiers. Without the sleep,\nlater tiers race the index and fail dependency resolution.\n\n## Sibling-package co-tagging\n\nPer REQ-048, `fathomdb-embedder-api` and `fathomdb-embedder` carry the\nsame git tag as the workspace release even though `fathomdb-embedder-api`\nruns on its own version axis. The tag prefix is workspace (Axis W);\n`fathomdb-embedder-api` records its independent version inside the tag\ncommit via its `Cargo.toml`.\n\n## Post-publish smoke\n\nPer `feedback_release_verification`, \"green CI + published wheel\" is not\ndone. Release-evidence sweep installs the published wheel from PyPI and\nruns an end-to-end open + close + exit smoke before the release is\ndeclared signed. Equivalent npm smoke applies for `@fathomdb/...`\npublishes. Crate publishes are smoked via `cargo install fathomdb-cli`\nfollowed by `fathomdb doctor check-integrity --json` against a fresh\nfixture database.\n\n## Sources\n\n- `dev/plans/ci-deferred.md` enumerates the pre-0.6.0 workflows being\n  restored.\n- `ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology` (incl. 2026-04-27 sibling amendment + 2026-05-11\n  CLI-via-facade amendment) owns the crate set.\n- `ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol` owns the `fathomdb-embedder-api` trait\n  stability posture.\n- REQ-047, REQ-048, REQ-049, REQ-050, REQ-052.\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":82,"oldLines":8,"newStart":82,"newLines":10,"lines":[" Per `feedback_release_verification`, \"green CI + published wheel\" is not"," done. Independent of `fathomdb-engine`.                                                                                      |\n| T6   | `fathomdb`                                                   | Facade. Depends on `fathomdb-engine`.                                                                                                                               |\n| T7   | `fathomdb-cli`                                               | Depends on `fathomdb` facade per ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology amendment 2026-05-11.                                                                                     |\n| T8   | Python wheel (`src/python/`); TypeScript package (`src/ts/`) | Both wrap `fathomdb-engine` directly (PyO3 / napi-rs); they can publish in parallel after T4 resolves on crates.io. PyPI / npm publishing is independent of T5..T7. |\n\nCross-ecosystem gate: every tier must succeed before the next tier\nstarts. `all-builds-passed` gates Tier T1 from starting until every\nmatrix build for the release has produced an artifact (no \"publish T1\nwhile the napi-rs prebuild is still red\"). Cross-registry atomicity is\ncovered by the post-publish smoke step below.\n\nTier inter-step sleep: keep the pre-0.6.0 60-second\ncrates.io-index-propagation sleep between tiers. Without the sleep,\nlater tiers race the index and fail dependency resolution.\n\n## Sibling-package co-tagging\n\nPer REQ-048, `fathomdb-embedder-api` and `fathomdb-embedder` carry the\nsame git tag as the workspace release even though `fathomdb-embedder-api`\nruns on its own version axis. The tag prefix is workspace (Axis W);\n`fathomdb-embedder-api` records its independent version inside the tag\ncommit via its `Cargo.toml`.\n\n## Post-publish smoke\n\nPer `feedback_release_verification`, \"green CI + published wheel\" is not\ndone. Release-evidence sweep installs the published wheel from PyPI and\nruns an end-to-end open + close + exit smoke before the release is\ndeclared signed. Equivalent npm smoke applies for `@fathomdb/...`\npublishes. Crate publishes are smoked via `cargo install fathomdb-cli`\nfollowed by `fathomdb doctor check-integrity --json` against a fresh\nfixture database.\n\n## Sources\n\n- `dev/plans/ci-deferred.md` enumerates the pre-0.6.0 workflows being\n  restored.\n- `ADR-0.6.0-crate-topology` (incl. 2026-04-27 sibling amendment + 2026-05-11\n  CLI-via-facade amendment) owns the crate set.\n- `ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol` owns the `fathomdb-embedder-api` trait\n  stability posture.\n- REQ-047, REQ-048, REQ-049, REQ-050, REQ-052.\n","numLines":99,"startLine":1,"totalLines":99}}}

dev/plans/prompts/0.7.1-EMBEDDER-UNDEFER-HANDOFF.md:52:**Why first**: ADR-0.6.0-default-embedder.md was written before the 0.7.0 PVQ pipeline existed. The 384-dim, sign-bit + f32-rerank combination at K=64 has not been measured for BGE-small. STATUS-perf-vector-quant cites e5-base-v2 at 74.8% recall@10 (768d, K=64×4) — a 384d model probably degrades further. Implementing the wrong model is a 3-release problem to undo.
dev/plans/prompts/0.7.1-EMBEDDER-UNDEFER-HANDOFF.md:59:3. Candidates (all in-process / pure-Rust-feasible via candle / wheel-size-tolerable): **bge-small-en-v1.5 (384d)**, **all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384d)**, **e5-small-v2 (384d)**, **gte-small (384d)**, **bge-base-en-v1.5 (768d)** as upper-size bound.
dev/plans/prompts/0.7.1-EMBEDDER-UNDEFER-HANDOFF.md:60:4. If no 384d candidate hits ≥0.85 recall@10 at K=64, evaluate: (a) raise K (cost ≈ linear extra f32-rerank), (b) post-quantization centering (subtract corpus mean before sign — one extra f32 vector in `_fathomdb_embedder_profiles`, no learned rotation, cheap), (c) jump to 768d (bge-base, +wheel-size).
dev/plans/prompts/0.7.1-EMBEDDER-UNDEFER-HANDOFF.md:71:**Success criteria**: HITL has a clear pick-one between (a) BGE-small at the chosen K, (b) a different 384d model at the chosen K, (c) bge-base 768d, (d) accept "BGE-small with documented recall caveat."

Installed mem0ai 2.0.7 + chromadb + sentence-transformers into an ISOLATED env (--target /tmp/d0b-mem0-env on PYTHONPATH; canonical .venv NOT polluted). try_build() -> build_local_mem0_config (airlock qwen3.6-27b LLM + local bge-small embedder + on-disk chroma, per-run isolated; NO Mem0 cloud) constructs a working adapter that ingested + retrieved live in both cheap-validate and pilot. dev/plans/runs/E1-prepare-cached-readers-20260504T024906Z.log:4848:{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01ArMM1zdqBLexFb2hESu56y","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GXotBcsgv9yLD4ZAX2LxvG","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/fdb-pack5-E1-prepare-cached-readers-20260504T024906Z/src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/prepare_cached_readers.rs","content":"use std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::thread;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse fathomdb_embedder_api::{Embedder, EmbedderError, EmbedderIdentity, Vector};\nuse fathomdb_engine::{Engine, PreparedWrite};\nuse fathomdb_schema::SQLITE_SUFFIX;\nuse tempfile::TempDir;\n\nconst SEARCH_ITERATIONS: usize = 100;\n\n/// 3-dimensional embedder that routes by query prefix, matching ac_020 fixture.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\nstruct RoutedEmbedder3 {\n    identity: EmbedderIdentity,\n}\n\nimpl RoutedEmbedder3 {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self { identity: EmbedderIdentity::new(\"routed3\", \"prepare-cached-readers\", 3) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Embedder for RoutedEmbedder3 {\n    fn identity(&self) -> EmbedderIdentity {\n        self.identity.clone()\n    }\n\n    fn embed(&self, text: &str) -> Result<Vector, EmbedderError> {\n        let mut v = vec![0.0_f32; 3];\n        let slot = if text.starts_with(\"semantic-\") { 0 } else if text.starts_with(\"hybrid-\") { 1 } else { 2 };\n        v[slot] = 1.0;\n        Ok(v)\n    }\n}\n\nfn fixture_path(name: &str) -> (TempDir, std::path::PathBuf) {\n    let dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n    let path = dir.path().join(format!(\"{name}{SQLITE_SUFFIX}\"));\n    (dir, path)\n}\n\nfn seed_fixture(engine: &Engine) {\n    engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(\"doc\").expect(\"vector kind\");\n    for i in 0..2 {\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"semantic-{i} text\"),\n            }])\n            .expect(\"write\");\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"hybrid-{i} text\"),\n            }])\n            .expect(\"write\");\n    }\n    engine.drain(10_000).expect(\"drain\");\n}\n\n/// Verify that the 4 read-path search statements use `prepare_cached` so each\n/// reader connection compiles each SQL at most once.\n///\n/// Baseline (before E.1): `tx.prepare()` on every search → 4 fresh prepares\n/// per call → 100 iterations × 3 active statements = 300 cache misses (fails).\n///\n/// After E.1: `prepare_cached` → 1 miss per unique SQL per reader connection\n/// borrowed. Single-threaded test uses ≤ 1 reader → ≤ 4 total misses (passes).\n/// The bound 32 (8 readers × 4 stmts) covers the multi-reader case.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_statement_cache_populated_after_100_searches() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_readers\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    for _ in 0..SEARCH_ITERATIONS {\n        let result = opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n        assert!(!result.results.is_empty(), \"search must return results\");\n    }\n\n    let misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 total prepare cache misses (8 readers × 4 stmts); got {misses}. \\\n         Baseline (no caching) would be ~300 misses for 100 iterations.\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Same invariant, exercised with all 4 query types from the AC-020 mix to\n/// confirm caching holds regardless of which vector-path branches activate.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_all_query_types_cached() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_readers_mix\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    let queries = [\"semantic-0\", \"hybrid-0\", \"semantic-1\", \"hybrid-1\"];\n    for _ in 0..25 {\n        for q in queries {\n            opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"search\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    let misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 prepare cache misses across 100 searches (4 queries × 25 iters); \\\n         got {misses}\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Concurrent variant: 8 threads each run 50 searches. Total cache misses must\n/// be ≤ 32 (8 reader connections × 4 statements), matching READER_POOL_SIZE.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_concurrent_pool_miss_bound() {\n    const THREADS: usize = 8;\n    const ROUNDS: usize = 50;\n\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_concurrent\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    let engine = Arc::new(opened.engine);\n    engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    let barrier = Arc::new(std::sync::Barrier::new(THREADS + 1));\n    let mut handles = Vec::with_capacity(THREADS);\n    for _ in 0..THREADS {\n        let eng = Arc::clone(&engine);\n        let bar = Arc::clone(&barrier);\n        handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {\n            bar.wait();\n            for _ in 0..ROUNDS {\n                eng.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n            }\n        }));\n    }\n    barrier.wait();\n    for h in handles {\n        h.join().expect(\"thread\");\n    }\n\n    let misses = engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    // Each of the 8 reader connections may cold-start its cache → 8 × 3 active\n    // stmts = 24 expected, ≤ 32 to account for pool reuse ordering.\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 cache misses ({THREADS} readers × 4 stmts); got {misses}\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Regression: after 1 000 searches, miss count must not grow beyond the\n/// initial warm-up budget, proving the cache stays warm.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_miss_count_saturates() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_saturation\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    for _ in 0..1_000 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n\n    let misses_1000 = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    // Reset and do just the warm-up pass to get the initial miss count.\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n    let misses_warmup = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    assert!(\n        misses_1000 <= misses_warmup + 2,\n        \"miss count should saturate after warm-up; \\\n         1000-iter misses={misses_1000} vs 10-iter warm-up misses={misses_warmup}\"\n    );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3198,"cache_read_input_tokens":91350,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3198},"output_tokens":27,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"diagnostics":null,"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"38adc285-4437-4bbf-a855-0849e65f1132","uuid":"04dd07d2-3ceb-40f5-832c-82b001566ee8"}

3];\n        let slot = if text.starts_with(\"semantic-\") { 0 } else if text.starts_with(\"hybrid-\") { 1 } else { 2 };\n        v[slot] = 1.0;\n        Ok(v)\n    }\n}\n\nfn fixture_path(name: &str) -> (TempDir, std::path::PathBuf) {\n    let dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n    let path = dir.path().join(format!(\"{name}{SQLITE_SUFFIX}\"));\n    (dir, path)\n}\n\nfn seed_fixture(engine: &Engine) {\n    engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(\"doc\").expect(\"vector kind\");\n    for i in 0..2 {\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"semantic-{i} text\"),\n            }])\n            .expect(\"write\");\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"hybrid-{i} text\"),\n            }])\n            .expect(\"write\");\n    }\n    engine.drain(10_000).expect(\"drain\");\n}\n\n/// Verify that the 4 read-path search statements use `prepare_cached` so each\n/// reader connection compiles each SQL at most once.\n///\n/// Baseline (before E.1): `tx.prepare()` on every search → 4 fresh prepares\n/// per call → 100 iterations × 3 active statements = 300 cache misses (fails).\n///\n/// After E.1: `prepare_cached` → 1 miss per unique SQL per reader connection\n/// borrowed. Single-threaded test uses ≤ 1 reader → ≤ 4 total misses (passes).\n/// The bound 32 (8 readers × 4 stmts) covers the multi-reader case.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_statement_cache_populated_after_100_searches() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_readers\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    for _ in 0..SEARCH_ITERATIONS {\n        let result = opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n        assert!(!result.results.is_empty(), \"search must return results\");\n    }\n\n    let misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 total prepare cache misses (8 readers × 4 stmts); got {misses}. \\\n         Baseline (no caching) would be ~300 misses for 100 iterations.\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Same invariant, exercised with all 4 query types from the AC-020 mix to\n/// confirm caching holds regardless of which vector-path branches activate.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_all_query_types_cached() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_readers_mix\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    let queries = [\"semantic-0\", \"hybrid-0\", \"semantic-1\", \"hybrid-1\"];\n    for _ in 0..25 {\n        for q in queries {\n            opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"search\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    let misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 prepare cache misses across 100 searches (4 queries × 25 iters); \\\n         got {misses}\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Concurrent variant: 8 threads each run 50 searches. Total cache misses must\n/// be ≤ 32 (8 reader connections × 4 statements), matching READER_POOL_SIZE.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_concurrent_pool_miss_bound() {\n    const THREADS: usize = 8;\n    const ROUNDS: usize = 50;\n\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_concurrent\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    let engine = Arc::new(opened.engine);\n    engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    let barrier = Arc::new(std::sync::Barrier::new(THREADS + 1));\n    let mut handles = Vec::with_capacity(THREADS);\n    for _ in 0..THREADS {\n        let eng = Arc::clone(&engine);\n        let bar = Arc::clone(&barrier);\n        handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {\n            bar.wait();\n            for _ in 0..ROUNDS {\n                eng.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n            }\n        }));\n    }\n    barrier.wait();\n    for h in handles {\n        h.join().expect(\"thread\");\n    }\n\n    let misses = engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    // Each of the 8 reader connections may cold-start its cache → 8 × 3 active\n    // stmts = 24 expected, ≤ 32 to account for pool reuse ordering.\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 cache misses ({THREADS} readers × 4 stmts); got {misses}\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Regression: after 1 000 searches, miss count must not grow beyond the\n/// initial warm-up budget, proving the cache stays warm.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_miss_count_saturates() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_saturation\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    for _ in 0..1_000 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n\n    let misses_1000 = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    // Reset and do just the warm-up pass to get the initial miss count.\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n    let misses_warmup = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    assert!(\n        misses_1000 <= misses_warmup + 2,\n        \"miss count should saturate after warm-up; dev/plans/runs/E1-prepare-cached-readers-20260504T024906Z.log:5298:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014Y9YDaQNtGE8iVoK8nboJg","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /tmp/fdb-pack5-E1-prepare-cached-readers-20260504T024906Z/src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/prepare_cached_readers.rs has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"38adc285-4437-4bbf-a855-0849e65f1132","uuid":"3837b01e-3126-448e-a917-891628ae9a63","timestamp":"2026-05-04T03:03:26.724Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-pack5-E1-prepare-cached-readers-20260504T024906Z/src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/prepare_cached_readers.rs","oldString":"/// Regression: after 1 000 searches, miss count must not grow beyond the\n/// initial warm-up budget, proving the cache stays warm.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_miss_count_saturates() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_saturation\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    for _ in 0..1_000 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n\n    let misses_1000 = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    // Reset and do just the warm-up pass to get the initial miss count.\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n    let misses_warmup = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    assert!(\n        misses_1000 <= misses_warmup + 2,\n        \"miss count should saturate after warm-up; \\\n         1000-iter misses={misses_1000} vs 10-iter warm-up misses={misses_warmup}\"\n    );\n}","newString":"/// Regression: once the cache is warm, subsequent searches must produce zero\n/// new prepare misses, proving the cache stays hot indefinitely.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_miss_count_saturates() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_saturation\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    // Warm-up phase: cache fills after the first few searches.\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n\n    // Post-warm-up: counter reset; all subsequent searches must be cache hits.\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    for _ in 0..1_000 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n    let post_warmup_misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    assert_eq!(\n        post_warmup_misses, 0,\n        \"after cache warm-up, 1000 searches must produce 0 new prepare misses; \\\n         got {post_warmup_misses}\"\n    );\n}","originalFile":"use std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::thread;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse fathomdb_embedder_api::{Embedder, EmbedderError, EmbedderIdentity, Vector};\nuse fathomdb_engine::{Engine, PreparedWrite};\nuse fathomdb_schema::SQLITE_SUFFIX;\nuse tempfile::TempDir;\n\nconst SEARCH_ITERATIONS: usize = 100;\n\n/// 3-dimensional embedder that routes by query prefix, matching ac_020 fixture.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\nstruct RoutedEmbedder3 {\n    identity: EmbedderIdentity,\n}\n\nimpl RoutedEmbedder3 {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self { identity: EmbedderIdentity::new(\"routed3\", \"prepare-cached-readers\", 3) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Embedder for RoutedEmbedder3 {\n    fn identity(&self) -> EmbedderIdentity {\n        self.identity.clone()\n    }\n\n    fn embed(&self, text: &str) -> Result<Vector, EmbedderError> {\n        let mut v = vec![0.0_f32; 3];\n        let slot = if text.starts_with(\"semantic-\") { 0 } else if text.starts_with(\"hybrid-\") { 1 } else { 2 };\n        v[slot] = 1.0;\n        Ok(v)\n    }\n}\n\nfn fixture_path(name: &str) -> (TempDir, std::path::PathBuf) {\n    let dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n    let path = dir.path().join(format!(\"{name}{SQLITE_SUFFIX}\"));\n    (dir, path)\n}\n\nfn seed_fixture(engine: &Engine) {\n    engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(\"doc\").expect(\"vector kind\");\n    for i in 0..2 {\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"semantic-{i} text\"),\n            }])\n            .expect(\"write\");\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"hybrid-{i} text\"),\n            }])\n            .expect(\"write\");\n    }\n    engine.drain(10_000).expect(\"drain\");\n}\n\n/// Verify that the 4 read-path search statements use `prepare_cached` so each\n/// reader connection compiles each SQL at most once.\n///\n/// Baseline (before E.1): `tx.prepare()` on every search → 4 fresh prepares\n/// per call → 100 iterations × 3 active statements = 300 cache misses (fails).\n///\n/// After E.1: `prepare_cached` → 1 miss per unique SQL per reader connection\n/// borrowed. Single-threaded test uses ≤ 1 reader → ≤ 4 total misses (passes).\n/// The bound 32 (8 readers × 4 stmts) covers the multi-reader case.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_statement_cache_populated_after_100_searches() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_readers\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    for _ in 0..SEARCH_ITERATIONS {\n        let result = opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n        assert!(!result.results.is_empty(), \"search must return results\");\n    }\n\n    let misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 total prepare cache misses (8 readers × 4 stmts); got {misses}. \\\n         Baseline (no caching) would be ~300 misses for 100 iterations.\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Same invariant, exercised with all 4 query types from the AC-020 mix to\n/// confirm caching holds regardless of which vector-path branches activate.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_all_query_types_cached() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_readers_mix\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    let queries = [\"semantic-0\", \"hybrid-0\", \"semantic-1\", \"hybrid-1\"];\n    for _ in 0..25 {\n        for q in queries {\n            opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"search\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    let misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 prepare cache misses across 100 searches (4 queries × 25 iters); \\\n         got {misses}\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Concurrent variant: 8 threads each run 50 searches. Total cache misses must\n/// be ≤ 32 (8 reader connections × 4 statements), matching READER_POOL_SIZE.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_concurrent_pool_miss_bound() {\n    const THREADS: usize = 8;\n    const ROUNDS: usize = 50;\n\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_concurrent\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    let engine = Arc::new(opened.engine);\n    engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    let barrier = Arc::new(std::sync::Barrier::new(THREADS + 1));\n    let mut handles = Vec::with_capacity(THREADS);\n    for _ in 0..THREADS {\n        let eng = Arc::clone(&engine);\n        let bar = Arc::clone(&barrier);\n        handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {\n            bar.wait();\n            for _ in 0..ROUNDS {\n                eng.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n            }\n        }));\n    }\n    barrier.wait();\n    for h in handles {\n        h.join().expect(\"thread\");\n    }\n\n    let misses = engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    // Each of the 8 reader connections may cold-start its cache → 8 × 3 active\n    // stmts = 24 expected, ≤ 32 to account for pool reuse ordering.\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 cache misses ({THREADS} readers × 4 stmts); got {misses}\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Regression: after 1 000 searches, miss count must not grow beyond the\n/// initial warm-up budget, proving the cache stays warm.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_miss_count_saturates() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_saturation\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    for _ in 0..1_000 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n\n    let misses_1000 = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    // Reset and do just the warm-up pass to get the initial miss count.\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n    let misses_warmup = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    assert!(\n        misses_1000 <= misses_warmup + 2,\n        \"miss count should saturate after warm-up; \\\n         1000-iter misses={misses_1000} vs 10-iter warm-up misses={misses_warmup}\"\n    );\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":157,"oldLines":8,"newStart":157,"newLines":8,"lines":["     );"," }"," ","-/// Regression: after 1 000 searches, miss count must not grow beyond the","-/// initial warm-up budget, proving the cache stays warm.","+/// Regression: once the cache is warm, subsequent searches must produce zero","+/// new prepare misses, proving the cache stays hot indefinitely."," #[test]"," fn prepare_cached_readers_miss_count_saturates() {","     let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_saturation\");"]},{"oldStart":166,"oldLines":24,"newStart":166,"newLines":21,"lines":["     let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");","     seed_fixture(&opened.engine);"," ","-    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();","-","-    for _ in 0..1_000 {","+    // Warm-up phase: cache fills after the first few searches.","+    for _ in 0..10 {","         opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");","     }"," ","-    let misses_1000 = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();","-","-    // Reset and do just the warm-up pass to get the initial miss count.","+    // Post-warm-up: counter reset; all subsequent searches must be cache hits.","     opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();","-    for _ in 0..10 {","+    for _ in 0..1_000 {","         opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");","     }","-    let misses_warmup = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();","+    let post_warmup_misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();"," ","-    assert!(","-        misses_1000 <= misses_warmup + 2,","-        \"miss count should saturate after warm-up; \\","-         1000-iter misses={misses_1000} vs 10-iter warm-up misses={misses_warmup}\"","+    assert_eq!(","+        post_warmup_misses, 0,","+        \"after cache warm-up, 1000 searches must produce 0 new prepare misses; \\","+         got {post_warmup_misses}\"","     );"," }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

3];\n        let slot = if text.starts_with(\"semantic-\") { 0 } else if text.starts_with(\"hybrid-\") { 1 } else { 2 };\n        v[slot] = 1.0;\n        Ok(v)\n    }\n}\n\nfn fixture_path(name: &str) -> (TempDir, std::path::PathBuf) {\n    let dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n    let path = dir.path().join(format!(\"{name}{SQLITE_SUFFIX}\"));\n    (dir, path)\n}\n\nfn seed_fixture(engine: &Engine) {\n    engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(\"doc\").expect(\"vector kind\");\n    for i in 0..2 {\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"semantic-{i} text\"),\n            }])\n            .expect(\"write\");\n        engine\n            .write(&[PreparedWrite::Node {\n                kind: \"doc\".to_string(),\n                body: format!(\"hybrid-{i} text\"),\n            }])\n            .expect(\"write\");\n    }\n    engine.drain(10_000).expect(\"drain\");\n}\n\n/// Verify that the 4 read-path search statements use `prepare_cached` so each\n/// reader connection compiles each SQL at most once.\n///\n/// Baseline (before E.1): `tx.prepare()` on every search → 4 fresh prepares\n/// per call → 100 iterations × 3 active statements = 300 cache misses (fails).\n///\n/// After E.1: `prepare_cached` → 1 miss per unique SQL per reader connection\n/// borrowed. Single-threaded test uses ≤ 1 reader → ≤ 4 total misses (passes).\n/// The bound 32 (8 readers × 4 stmts) covers the multi-reader case.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_statement_cache_populated_after_100_searches() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_readers\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    for _ in 0..SEARCH_ITERATIONS {\n        let result = opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n        assert!(!result.results.is_empty(), \"search must return results\");\n    }\n\n    let misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 total prepare cache misses (8 readers × 4 stmts); got {misses}. \\\n         Baseline (no caching) would be ~300 misses for 100 iterations.\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Same invariant, exercised with all 4 query types from the AC-020 mix to\n/// confirm caching holds regardless of which vector-path branches activate.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_all_query_types_cached() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_readers_mix\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    let queries = [\"semantic-0\", \"hybrid-0\", \"semantic-1\", \"hybrid-1\"];\n    for _ in 0..25 {\n        for q in queries {\n            opened.engine.search(q).expect(\"search\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    let misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 prepare cache misses across 100 searches (4 queries × 25 iters); \\\n         got {misses}\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Concurrent variant: 8 threads each run 50 searches. Total cache misses must\n/// be ≤ 32 (8 reader connections × 4 statements), matching READER_POOL_SIZE.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_concurrent_pool_miss_bound() {\n    const THREADS: usize = 8;\n    const ROUNDS: usize = 50;\n\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_concurrent\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    let engine = Arc::new(opened.engine);\n    engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    let barrier = Arc::new(std::sync::Barrier::new(THREADS + 1));\n    let mut handles = Vec::with_capacity(THREADS);\n    for _ in 0..THREADS {\n        let eng = Arc::clone(&engine);\n        let bar = Arc::clone(&barrier);\n        handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {\n            bar.wait();\n            for _ in 0..ROUNDS {\n                eng.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n            }\n        }));\n    }\n    barrier.wait();\n    for h in handles {\n        h.join().expect(\"thread\");\n    }\n\n    let misses = engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    // Each of the 8 reader connections may cold-start its cache → 8 × 3 active\n    // stmts = 24 expected, ≤ 32 to account for pool reuse ordering.\n    assert!(\n        misses <= 32,\n        \"expected ≤ 32 cache misses ({THREADS} readers × 4 stmts); got {misses}\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Regression: once the cache is warm, subsequent searches must produce zero\n/// new prepare misses, proving the cache stays hot indefinitely.\n#[test]\nfn prepare_cached_readers_miss_count_saturates() {\n    let (_dir, path) = fixture_path(\"prepare_cached_saturation\");\n    let embedder = Arc::new(RoutedEmbedder3::new());\n    let opened = Engine::open_with_embedder_for_test(&path, embedder).expect(\"open\");\n    seed_fixture(&opened.engine);\n\n    // Warm-up phase: cache fills after the first few searches.\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n\n    // Post-warm-up: counter reset; all subsequent searches must be cache hits.\n    opened.engine.reset_search_prepare_count_for_test();\n    for _ in 0..1_000 {\n        opened.engine.search(\"semantic-0\").expect(\"search\");\n    }\n    let post_warmup_misses = opened.engine.search_prepare_count_for_test();\n\n    assert_eq!(\n        post_warmup_misses, 0,\n        \"after cache warm-up, 1000 searches must produce 0 new prepare misses; \\\n         got {post_warmup_misses}\"\n    );\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":1,"oldLines":6,"newStart":1,"newLines":5,"lines":[" use std::sync::Arc;"," use std::thread;","-use std::time::Duration;"," "," use fathomdb_embedder_api::{Embedder, EmbedderError, EmbedderIdentity, Vector};"," use fathomdb_engine::{Engine, PreparedWrite};"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

dev/plans/runs/0.7.2-PR-2bc-S3-output.json:13:  "notes": "PR-2 reframe: kept the 0.90 recall floor (unchanged at perf_gates.rs:159) and amended ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md to cite the CORRECTED ANN/quantization-FIDELITY measurement (recall@10 = 0.937, CI 0.913-0.957, sigma 0.0116; bge-small candle, EU-7 N=7667, K=192, standard ANN method: exclude query-source before top-10 + dedup-by-id GT). Full CI clears 0.90; R-2sigma = 0.914 -> floor NOT raised (headroom for production variance + pending N=1M PR-3). The earlier 0.828 documented as a conservative-measurement artifact (+7.8pp), citing 0.7.2-PR-2c-recall-rootcause.md. ANN-vs-IR distinction kept sharp: the floor is fidelity (GT = exact f32 top-10 over same model), explicitly NOT IR-relevance. EU-8 IR ceiling recall@10 = 0.571 recorded as a separate embedder/task ceiling (NOT a gate), citing 0.7.2-EU-8-ir-recall-results.md; lever is a better embedder/graph, not K/ANN tuning. Added fast non-gated sentinel ac_013b_floor_matches_adr asserting the constant == 0.90 tied to ADR §2 point 4. cargo build --tests clean; sentinel passes. No IR gate introduced. No §6/§7 HITL gate conflicts with keeping 0.90."
