dev/plans/runs/0.7.0-PVQ-S0-20260527T130459Z.log:117:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01W6dw58GVZo5GWi69vxzNNQ","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\ttitle: 0.7.0 vector-retrieval cost research\n3\tdate: 2026-05-26\n4\tupdated: 2026-05-27 (HITL responses incorporated; Pack 1/Pack 2 split)\n5\ttype: research-only (no code, no slice plan, no ADR)\n6\tstatus: HITL-RESPONDED — ready for implementation handoff\n7\tinputs:\n8\t  - dev/plans/prompts/0.7.0-VECTOR-COST-RESEARCH.md\n9\t  - dev/plans/0.7.0-HITL-recommendations.md\n10\t  - dev/plans/runs/0.7.0-PERF-EXP-W4.1-ac013-canonical-output.json\n11\t---\n12\t\n13\t# Vector-retrieval cost research (AC-013 / AC-019)\n14\t\n15\t## Problem restated\n16\t\n17\tCanonical CI under the locked W4.1-stacked-O1 (PCACHE2) stack measures\n18\tAC-013 at **p50 = 2048 ms, p99 = 2327 ms** for 1 M rows × dim 768 f32,\n19\tseeded by `VaryingEmbedder` (`tests/perf_gates.rs:296`). ADR-draft\n20\tbudgets are 80 / 300 ms — measurement is **25–40× over**.\n21\t\n22\tRoot cause is a per-query O(N) full scan through `vec0`. The hot SQL\n23\tis in `read_search_in_tx` at\n24\t`src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs:2317-2323`:\n25\t\n26\t```\n27\tSELECT rowid FROM vector_default\n28\tWHERE embedding MATCH vec_f32(?1)\n29\tORDER BY distance\n30\tLIMIT 10\n31\t```\n32\t\n33\t`vector_default` is declared at\n34\t`src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs:3278-3283` as\n35\t\n36\t```\n37\tCREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vector_default\n38\tUSING vec0(embedding float[<dim>])\n39\t```\n40\t\n41\ti.e. a single global vec0 table, no metadata columns, no partition\n42\tkeys, full-precision f32. `register_sqlite_vec_extension()` at\n43\t`src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs:3107-3117` registers the\n44\textension via `sqlite3_auto_extension`. The pinned upstream is\n45\t**`sqlite-vec = \"=0.1.7\"`** (`crates/fathomdb-engine/Cargo.toml:18`)\n46\twith `libsqlite3-sys` shipped by `rusqlite 0.31 + bundled`\n47\t(`Cargo.toml:14`).\n48\t\n49\tThe single-writer + projection-cursor freshness contract is owned by\n50\tthe writer loop (`src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs:2174,\n51\t2242, 2846`). Any vector-encoding change must round-trip through\n52\t`PreparedWrite::Node` and `_fathomdb_vector_rows`, not bypass it.\n53\t\n54\t---\n55\t\n56\t## TIER 1 — scan optimizations on existing vec0\n57\t\n58\tPer-query work at 1 M × 768 × 4 B = 3 GB scanned; 2048 ms ⇒ ~1.5 GB/s\n59\teffective bandwidth (close to single-thread memory-read ceiling). Any\n60\ttechnique that **shrinks the bytes-per-row** or **shrinks the row\n61\tcount** translates ~linearly to latency.\n62\t\n63\t### 1. Metadata prefiltering\n64\t\n65\t- **Mechanism.** `vec0` (sqlite-vec ≥ 0.1.6) supports auxiliary\n66\t  metadata columns and partition-key columns; KNN can be pre-filtered\n67\t  with `WHERE kind = ?` so distance compute runs only over the\n68\t  matching chunk(s). Cited:\n69\t  [alexgarcia.xyz blog — metadata release](https://alexgarcia.xyz/blog/2024/sqlite-vec-metadata-release/index.html);\n70\t  vec0 features page lists up to 16 metadata columns and 4 partition\n71\t  keys.\n72\t- **Expected gain.** Scales with selectivity. If FathomDB carries 8\n73\t  vector kinds with rough uniformity, prefiltering by `kind` cuts\n74\t  scanned rows ~8× ⇒ ~256 ms. AC-013's fixture seeds a *single*\n75\t  `\"doc\"` kind (`tests/perf_gates.rs:350`), so the benchmark itself\n76\t  gets **no benefit** from `kind` prefiltering. Real workloads do.\n77\t- **Implementation cost.** Schema migration (add metadata + partition\n78\t  cols to `vector_default` or split into per-kind tables);\n79\t  `engine.search()` API extension to accept filter predicates;\n80\t  ingest path change in `_fathomdb_vector_rows` insert\n81\t  (`lib.rs:2846`). Moderate.\n82\t- **Recall / correctness.** Exact within the filter set — semantics\n83\t  change (results restricted), not approximated.\n84\t- **Compatibility.** Plays cleanly with writer lock + projection\n85\t  cursor; vec0 partition keys are chunk-organised so writes scale\n86\t  per partition.\n87\t\n88\t**Verdict on its own: insufficient for AC-013's synthetic\n89\tsingle-kind fixture; valuable for real workloads but not load-bearing\n90\tfor the gate.**\n91\t\n92\t### 2. Partition keys (physical / logical)\n93\t\n94\t- **Mechanism.** Partition-key columns on `vec0` (sqlite-vec\n95\t  feature; max 4) physically chunk storage. Logical alternative:\n96\t  N per-kind `vec0` tables.\n97\t- **Gain.** Same scaling as metadata prefilter; for AC-013's\n98\t  single-kind fixture, zero. For domain workloads with k=8 partitions\n99\t  → ~8× cheaper scans (~250 ms).\n100\t- **Cost.** Either a multi-table search planner (logical) or schema\n101\t  migration (partition_key). Logical option additionally creates a\n102\t  fan-out merge problem at the search layer.\n103\t- **Recall.** Exact within partition set.\n104\t- **Compatibility.** Per-table flavour is incompatible with the\n105\t  current single `vector_default` table assumption baked into\n106\t  `read_search_in_tx`; partition_key flavour is a simpler change.\n107\t\n108\t**Verdict: same as #1 — orthogonal to the gate, but worth shipping\n109\ttogether if we do a vec0 migration anyway.**\n110\t\n111\t### 3. Smaller embeddings (lower dim)\n112\t\n113\t- **Mechanism.** dim 768 → 384 (MiniLM-L6) halves distance compute;\n114\t  → 256 cuts 3×; → 128 cuts 6×.\n115\t- **Gain (alone).** 384 ⇒ ~1024 ms; 128 ⇒ ~340 ms. Neither closes\n116\t  the 25–40× gap by itself.\n117\t- **Cost.** This is **the embedder identity**, not a vec0 setting.\n118\t  `EmbedderIdentity` is profile-pinned via\n119\t  `_fathomdb_embedder_profiles` (`lib.rs:3248-3260`); switching\n120\t  embedders requires a corpus rebuild and a profile rotation. Not a\n121\t  knob we can flip without writer cooperation and a re-embedding\n122\t  pass.\n123\t- **Recall.** Domain-dependent; MTEB shows ~3–6 pp recall drop from\n124\t  768→384 for general-purpose embedders.\n125\t- **Compatibility.** Touches the embedder contract and the schema\n126\t  pinning logic. **HITL Q1: do we have the appetite to switch the\n127\t  reference embedder in 0.7.0?** Default assumption: no.\n128\t\n129\t**Verdict: out of scope for 0.7.0 unless HITL signals otherwise.**\n130\t\n131\t### 4. Binary / int8 quantization (THE LEVER)\n132\t\n133\t- **Mechanism.** Replace the f32 column with `bit[768]` or\n134\t  `int8[768]`. sqlite-vec supports both natively in `vec0`\n135\t  (`bit[N]`, `int8[N]` declarations; cited:\n136\t  [sqlite-vec API reference / DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/3-api-reference);\n137\t  [binary quantization guide](https://alexgarcia.xyz/sqlite-vec/guides/binary-quant.html)).\n138\t  Binary distance becomes Hamming (popcount xor) on 96 bytes/row\n139\t  vs 3072 bytes/row of f32 — **32× narrower**, plus 4× lighter\n140\t  arithmetic. Int8 is 4× narrower with SIMD-able dot products.\n141\t- **Gain.** Binary: 32× storage × ~1× arithmetic-density advantage\n142\t  ⇒ 30–50× in practice on memory-bound scans. Published benches\n143\t  (Kyle Howells, Cohere) show 15–40× faster than f32 brute force at\n144\t  ~85–95 % recall@10 with **rerank-on-f32**. Expected p50 if we\n145\t  binarize: **~50–100 ms** for 1 M rows. Int8 alone: 4–8× ⇒\n146\t  ~250–500 ms (borderline on the p99 = 300 ms budget).\n147\t- **Cost.** Schema migration (add `embedding_bin bit[768]` column\n148\t  or a sibling `vector_default_bin` table); ingest path adds a\n149\t  `vec_quantize_binary()` call alongside the f32 insert; query path\n150\t  becomes a two-phase: **bit-distance prefilter → f32 rerank** of\n151\t  the top ~256 candidates. The f32 column is retained for rerank\n152\t  (so storage grows 1.03×, not shrinks). All changes are SQL-level\n153\t  and application-side; no SQLite build flags. sqlite-vec ≥ 0.1.7\n154\t  (our pinned version) already ships the relevant types per\n155\t  [release notes](https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/releases) —\n156\t  no upgrade required to land bit/int8, though upgrading to 0.1.9\n157\t  is low-risk and unlocks the constraint-column KNN refinements.\n158\t- **Recall.** Binary alone: ~70–80 % @ k=10. Binary + f32 rerank\n159\t  on top-K (K = 4–8 × k): ~95–99 % @ k=10, equivalent to brute\n160\t  force for most practical purposes. **HITL Q2: what's the recall\n161\t  floor we'll enforce in the AC-013 RED test?** Suggested: ≥ 90 %\n162\t  recall@10 vs f32 ground truth on the AC-013 fixture.\n163\t- **Compatibility.** Single-writer lock and projection cursor are\n164\t  not affected — both columns commit in the same writer\n165\t  transaction. `VaryingEmbedder` produces normalised f32; binary\n166\t  quantization preserves the sign-mass pattern it encodes, so the\n167\t  fixture remains discriminative.\n168\t\n169\t**Verdict: binary + f32 rerank is the single highest-leverage Tier-1\n170\ttechnique. Plausibly closes the gate alone.**\n171\t\n172\t### 5. Matryoshka / truncated dimensions\n173\t\n174\t- **Mechanism.** Run KNN against a 128-d prefix, rerank on full 768.\n175\t- **Gain.** ~6× on the prefilter pass ⇒ ~340 ms. Less leverage than\n176\t  binary; binary already wins on bandwidth.\n177\t- **Cost.** Requires a Matryoshka-trained embedder (`nomic-embed\n178\t  v1.5`, `text-embedding-3-*`). VaryingEmbedder is not Matryoshka;\n179\t  the AC-013 fixture wouldn't exhibit the recall property.\n180\t  sqlite-vec exposes `vec_slice()` but no documented native first-K\n181\t  dim KNN.\n182\t- **Recall.** Embedder-dependent.\n183\t- **Compatibility.** Same embedder-identity coupling as #3.\n184\t\n185\t**Verdict: dominated by binary quantization for our setting. Skip.**\n186\t\n187\t### 6. Caching / candidate limiting\n188\t\n189\t- **Mechanism.** Result cache (no help on AC-013's uniform random\n190\t  queries — held-out distribution per `ac013_query_bodies`,\n191\t  `tests/perf_gates.rs:373`). `LIMIT 10` is already applied\n192\t  (`lib.rs:2322`) but `vec0`'s brute-force scan still visits all\n193\t  rows to compute the top-10 ordering. No early-exit on a\n194\t  brute-force scan.\n195\t- **Gain.** Zero on AC-013.\n196\t- **Cost.** N/A.\n197\t\n198\t**Verdict: not load-bearing.**\n199\t\n200\t### Tier 1 combined estimate\n201\t\n202\tStacking the realistic non-embedder-switching levers — **binary\n203\tquantization + f32 rerank** (#4) as the primary, with **metadata\n204\tprefilter / partition_key** (#1 + #2) as an architectural neighbour\n205\tthat ships for free in the same migration — gives:\n206\t\n207\t- **p50 expectation: 50–100 ms** (within the 80 ms p50 budget on\n208\t  the optimistic side, ~10 ms over on the pessimistic).\n209\t- **p99 expectation: 100–200 ms** (well under the 300 ms p99\n210\t  budget).\n211\t- **Recall: ≥ 95 % @ k=10** with K-rerank = 64, depending on\n212\t  workload.\n213\t\n214\t**This plausibly closes the 25–40× gap.** Tier 2 (vectorlite) and\n215\tTier 3 (broader ANN) become unnecessary for 0.7.0.\n216\t\n217\tThe one residual concern: the AC-013 fixture's `VaryingEmbedder`\n218\tconcentrates mass on 6 coordinates out of 768\n219\t(`tests/perf_gates.rs:317`), which is *atypically* friendly to\n220\tbinary quantization (sign pattern dominates). Real embedders will\n221\tshow somewhat less favourable ratios. The fixture itself should\n222\tstill GREEN; HITL should note that AC-013's headroom may be\n223\toverstated relative to production workloads.\n224\t\n225\t---\n226\t\n227\t## TIER 2 — vectorlite (HNSW SQLite extension)\n228\t\n229\tInvestigated for completeness. **Skip in 0.7.0** if Tier 1 closes\n230\tthe gap.\n231\t\n232\t- **Maintenance.** `1yefuwang1/vectorlite`, Apache-2.0, latest\n233\t  release **v0.2.0 (2024-08-19)** per the GitHub repo\n234\t  ([vectorlite](https://github.com/1yefuwang1/vectorlite)). No\n235\t  releases in the ~21 months since. 361 stars / 10 forks. Maturity:\n236\t  research-grade.\n237\t- **API.** Loadable extension, virtual table (`USING vectorlite(...)`).\n238\t  Would slot into `register_sqlite_vec_extension()` as a sibling\n239\t  registration (different entry point).\n240\t- **Coexistence with sqlite-vec.** No conflict — different virtual\n241\t  table names. But a vectorlite table is a separate corpus; we'd\n242\t  either dual-write (storage 2×) or migrate.\n243\t- **HNSW params.** `M` default 16, `ef_construction` 200, `ef`\n244\t  query-time default 10. Sane defaults; would need tuning for dim 768.\n245\t- **Benchmarks.** Author's published benches stop at 3000 vectors\n246\t  (recall 36–99 % depending on `ef`; latency 13 µs – 1.4 ms). **No\n247\t  1 M × 768 benchmark.** Extrapolation: HNSW is log-time, so the\n248\t  step from 3 K → 1 M is ~2× per `log N` factor; plausibly 50–500 µs\n249\t  per query, well under budget. But unbenchmarked at our scale.\n250\t- **Storage.** HNSW typical 1.5–2× overhead; at 3 GB raw, +1.5–3 GB.\n251\t  Acceptable but non-trivial for embedded-DB posture.\n252\t- **Single-writer / lifecycle.** Vectorlite stores its HNSW graph\n253\t  inside the SQLite file via the virtual table's shadow tables —\n254\t  no external files. Compatible with `database_lock.rs`. Build is\n255\t  synchronous on insert (no async rebuild) — fits the writer\n256\t  transactional model.\n257\t- **Snapshot / projection cursor.** Inserts commit synchronously\n258\t  with the writer txn; AC-017 cursor freshness unaffected.\n259\t- **Fallback.** No documented opt-out; would need to retain\n260\t  `vec0` brute-force in parallel for recall validation.\n261\t- **libsqlite3-sys 0.28 compatibility.** Not specifically tested\n262\t  upstream; vectorlite requires SQLite ≥ 3.38 for metadata\n263\t  filtering. rusqlite 0.31 / bundled ships SQLite ≥ 3.44 → OK.\n264\t\n265\t**Verdict: shippable with caveats (stale maintenance + unbenchmarked\n266\tat our scale + architectural-lever cost), but unnecessary if Tier 1\n267\tcloses the gap. If Tier 1 falls short, vectorlite is a credible\n268\tTier 2 lever.**\n269\t\n270\tSlice scope if pursued: 1 slice (extension registration + schema\n271\tmigration + dual-corpus rebuild). Adds an architectural lever on\n272\ttop of PCACHE2 in 0.7.0 — **HITL \"one architectural lever per\n273\trelease\" rule applies; this would require an exception.**\n274\t\n275\t---\n276\t\n277\t## TIER 3 — broader ANN architecture\n278\t\n279\tPursued only as a sanity check; Tier 1 wins.\n280\t\n281\t- **sqlite-vec upstream ANN.** v0.1.10-alpha.1 introduced\n282\t  experimental ANN indexes (`rescore`, `ivf` not-enabled,\n283\t  `DiskANN`) per\n284\t  [GitHub releases](https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/releases).\n285\t  Status: alpha, not production. **Not 0.7.0-eligible.** Worth\n286\t  tracking for 0.8.0 / 0.9.0.\n287\t- **Algorithm choice.** For our dim 768 / 1 M / read-heavy /\n288\t  embedded-single-process workload, HNSW is the obvious default;\n289\t  IVF wins at much larger N; PQ wins under tight memory budgets.\n290\t  Binary quantization (Tier 1 #4) *is* a scalar-quantization ANN\n291\t  technique applied at brute-force scan time, which is why it\n292\t  works without a graph index.\n293\t- **Integration shapes** (A. inside vec0 future, B. vectorlite-\n294\t  style external vtab, C. Rust-side `usearch` / `instant-distance`,\n295\t  D. hybrid). C is the most interesting alternative if Tiers 1+2\n296\t  both fail: `usearch` is actively maintained, has Rust bindings,\n297\t  and supports binary + matryoshka natively. Would require\n298\t  rebuilding the FathomDB search verb to leave SQLite for the KNN\n299\t  step — large blast radius.\n300\t- **Recall validation.** RED-test would express a recall floor\n301\t  against f32 brute-force ground truth on a held-out query set;\n302\t  bound: `recall@10 ≥ 0.90` (HITL to confirm).\n303\t- **Slice scope.** Multi-phase, almost certainly slips to 0.7.1.\n304\t\n305\t**Verdict: do not pursue in 0.7.0.**\n306\t\n307\t---\n308\t\n309\t## HITL responses (locked 2026-05-27)\n310\t\n311\t1. **Recall floor for AC-013: ≥ 90 % recall@10** vs f32 ground\n312\t   truth on the AC-013 held-out query set, as an initial floor.\n313\t   Lock as a RED test alongside the latency budget. Tighten in a\n314\t   later release if real-workload recall headroom permits.\n315\t2. **Embedder switch: KEEP existing embedder.** Tier-1 #3\n316\t   (smaller embeddings) and Tier-1 #5 (matryoshka) are\n317\t   out-of-scope for 0.7.0. The `VaryingEmbedder` /\n318\t   `EmbedderIdentity` contract is unchanged.\n319\t3. **Metadata + partition_key in 0.7.0: YES.** Bundle with the\n320\t   binary-quant migration in Pack 1 (see below). The schema\n321\t   doesn't move the AC-013 gate but is the correct shape for\n322\t   real workloads and avoids a second migration in 0.7.1.\n323\t\n324\t---\n325\t\n326\t## Drafter's recommendation — locked plan\n327\t\n328\t**Tier 1 wins. Tiers 2 and 3 are not pursued in 0.7.0.**\n329\t\n330\tShip in **two packs** within 0.7.0. Both packs live under the\n331\texisting 0.7.0 release line; the split exists for review\n332\tgranularity, not for release-boundary reasons.\n333\t\n334\t### Pack 1 — schema migration + ingest double-write\n335\t\n336\tLands the new schema and the new ingest path. No reader behavior\n337\tchange yet; queries still go through the existing f32 path.\n338\t\n339\t- **Add `bit[768]` column** (or sibling `vector_default_bin`\n340\t  vec0 table) alongside the current f32 `embedding` column.\n341\t  sqlite-vec 0.1.7 already supports `bit[N]` natively\n342\t  ([release notes](https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/releases))\n343\t  — no upstream upgrade required.\n344\t- **Add metadata columns + partition_key** to the vec0 schema\n345\t  (Tier 1 #1 + #2). HITL-confirmed to ship in 0.7.0. Doesn't\n346\t  help the AC-013 gate (single-kind fixture) but is the correct\n347\t  shape for real workloads and avoids a second migration in\n348\t  0.7.1. Partition_key candidate: vector kind from\n349\t  `_fathomdb_vector_rows` (chunk-organised storage). Metadata\n350\t  columns: at minimum a `kind` column; broader metadata schema\n351\t  per the corpus / API work tracked separately.\n352\t- **Ingest path change** in the writer (`lib.rs:2846` insert\n353\t  site for `_fathomdb_vector_rows`): on every write, also\n354\t  compute `vec_quantize_binary(embedding)` and insert into the\n355\t  bit column. This is the \"double-write\" — both columns commit\n356\t  in the same writer transaction, so the writer-lock and\n357\t  projection-cursor freshness contract are unchanged.\n358\t- **Schema migration** for existing data: rebuild the bit\n359\t  column from the existing f32 corpus at upgrade time (one-pass\n360\t  `INSERT ... SELECT vec_quantize_binary(embedding) FROM\n361\t  vector_default`). Migration cost: ~3 GB read + ~96 MB write\n362\t  for a 1 M row corpus; well under a minute on disk.\n363\t- **Tests:** Pack 1 adds no new RED gate; existing AC-013\n364\t  remains pinned at the unindexed 2048/2327 numbers until\n365\t  Pack 2 lands. Pack 1's correctness gate is the migration\n366\t  round-trip test (insert f32, read back bit column,\n367\t  `vec_distance_hamming` matches the expected popcount).\n368\t\n369\t### Pack 2 — query-path swap + RED tests\n370\t\n371\tSwitches the reader to the two-phase shape and pins the new\n372\tbudgets.\n373\t\n374\t- **`read_search_in_tx` query rewrite** at `lib.rs:2317-2323`:\n375\t  two-phase query, bit-distance KNN top-K (K=64) → f32 rerank\n376\t  for final top-10. K = 64 picked from binary-quant literature\n377\t  (Kyle Howells, Cohere) to land recall@10 ≥ 95 % at ~50–100 ms;\n378\t  may be tunable.\n379\t- **AC-013 latency budget re-pin** in `tests/perf_gates.rs:149-150`:\n380\t  p50 ≤ 80 ms, p99 ≤ 300 ms (per ADR-0.7.0 draft). RED test\n381\t  must fail at canonical-CI scale before Pack 2 code change.\n382\t- **New AC recall-floor RED test** asserting recall@10 ≥ 0.90\n383\t  vs f32 brute-force ground truth on the AC-013 fixture\n384\t  (HITL-locked). Ground truth computed at test-setup time\n385\t  via a brute-force pass; cached as expected top-10 IDs per\n386\t  query. The brute-force pass uses the existing f32 column\n387\t  retained for rerank — this is one of the column's two\n388\t  jobs.\n389\t- **AC-019 verdict** under the new query path: re-measure;\n390\t  bound expression (`10× baseline`) stays.\n391\t\n392\t### Architectural-lever accounting\n393\t\n394\tThe binary-quant + rerank query shape is a **data-encoding\n395\tchange** (schema + query rewrite), not an architectural lever\n396\tin the sense of the `feedback_reliability_principles` rule\n397\t(PCACHE2 was the architectural lever — replaces SQLite's page\n398\tcache). The HITL doc records this disposition; if a future\n399\treviewer disagrees and counts it as a second lever, the fallback\n400\tis to ship Pack 1 in 0.7.0 and Pack 2 in 0.7.1. Drafter's\n401\trecommendation: ship both in 0.7.0.\n402\t\n403\t### Out of scope for both packs\n404\t\n405\t- Embedder change (HITL confirmed: keep existing embedder).\n406\t- ANN graph index (vectorlite, sqlite-vec ANN alpha, usearch /\n407\t  instant-distance / Rust-side index). Tracked in\n408\t  `dev/notes/pcache2-followups.md` for post-0.7.0 if real-workload\n409\t  telemetry shows the binary-quant scan is insufficient.\n410\t\n411\t## Sources\n412\t\n413\t- [sqlite-vec releases](https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/releases)\n414\t- [sqlite-vec metadata + filtering blog post](https://alexgarcia.xyz/blog/2024/sqlite-vec-metadata-release/index.html)\n415\t- [sqlite-vec binary quantization guide](https://alexgarcia.xyz/sqlite-vec/guides/binary-quant.html)\n416\t- [sqlite-vec API reference (DeepWiki)](https://deepwiki.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/3-api-reference)\n417\t- [vectorlite GitHub repo](https://github.com/1yefuwang1/vectorlite)\n418\t- [Kyle Howells — Binary quantized embeddings](https://ikyle.me/blog/2025/binary-quantized-embeddings)\n419\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"aa315b5f-03de-4674-91e7-1c237d33c95a","uuid":"bfad3451-2373-4099-bc34-683888e3acc2","timestamp":"2026-05-27T13:12:09.707Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-0.7.0-PVQ-S0-20260527T130459Z/dev/notes/0.7.0-vector-cost-research.md","content":"---\ntitle: 0.7.0 vector-retrieval cost research\ndate: 2026-05-26\nupdated: 2026-05-27 (HITL responses incorporated; Pack 1/Pack 2 split)\ntype: research-only (no code, no slice plan, no ADR)\nstatus: HITL-RESPONDED — ready for implementation handoff\ninputs:\n  - dev/plans/prompts/0.7.0-VECTOR-COST-RESEARCH.md\n  - dev/plans/0.7.0-HITL-recommendations.md\n  - dev/plans/runs/0.7.0-PERF-EXP-W4.1-ac013-canonical-output.json\n---\n\n# Vector-retrieval cost research (AC-013 / AC-019)\n\n## Problem restated\n\nCanonical CI under the locked W4.1-stacked-O1 (PCACHE2) stack measures\nAC-013 at **p50 = 2048 ms, p99 = 2327 ms** for 1 M rows × dim 768 f32,\nseeded by `VaryingEmbedder` (`tests/perf_gates.rs:296`). ADR-draft\nbudgets are 80 / 300 ms — measurement is **25–40× over**.\n\nRoot cause is a per-query O(N) full scan through `vec0`. The hot SQL\nis in `read_search_in_tx` at\n`src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs:2317-2323`:\n\n```\nSELECT rowid FROM vector_default\nWHERE embedding MATCH vec_f32(?1)\nORDER BY distance\nLIMIT 10\n```\n\n`vector_default` is declared at\n`src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs:3278-3283` as\n\n```\nCREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vector_default\nUSING vec0(embedding float[<dim>])\n```\n\ni.e. a single global vec0 table, no metadata columns, no partition\nkeys, full-precision f32. `register_sqlite_vec_extension()` at\n`src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs:3107-3117` registers the\nextension via `sqlite3_auto_extension`. The pinned upstream is\n**`sqlite-vec = \"=0.1.7\"`** (`crates/fathomdb-engine/Cargo.toml:18`)\nwith `libsqlite3-sys` shipped by `rusqlite 0.31 + bundled`\n(`Cargo.toml:14`).\n\nThe single-writer + projection-cursor freshness contract is owned by\nthe writer loop (`src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs:2174,\n2242, 2846`). Any vector-encoding change must round-trip through\n`PreparedWrite::Node` and `_fathomdb_vector_rows`, not bypass it.\n\n---\n\n## TIER 1 — scan optimizations on existing vec0\n\nPer-query work at 1 M × 768 × 4 B = 3 GB scanned; 2048 ms ⇒ ~1.5 GB/s\neffective bandwidth (close to single-thread memory-read ceiling). Any\ntechnique that **shrinks the bytes-per-row** or **shrinks the row\ncount** translates ~linearly to latency.\n\n### 1. Metadata prefiltering\n\n- **Mechanism.** `vec0` (sqlite-vec ≥ 0.1.6) supports auxiliary\n  metadata columns and partition-key columns; KNN can be pre-filtered\n  with `WHERE kind = ?` so distance compute runs only over the\n  matching chunk(s). Cited:\n  [alexgarcia.xyz blog — metadata release](https://alexgarcia.xyz/blog/2024/sqlite-vec-metadata-release/index.html);\n  vec0 features page lists up to 16 metadata columns and 4 partition\n  keys.\n- **Expected gain.** Scales with selectivity. If FathomDB carries 8\n  vector kinds with rough uniformity, prefiltering by `kind` cuts\n  scanned rows ~8× ⇒ ~256 ms. AC-013's fixture seeds a *single*\n  `\"doc\"` kind (`tests/perf_gates.rs:350`), so the benchmark itself\n  gets **no benefit** from `kind` prefiltering. Real workloads do.\n- **Implementation cost.** Schema migration (add metadata + partition\n  cols to `vector_default` or split into per-kind tables);\n  `engine.search()` API extension to accept filter predicates;\n  ingest path change in `_fathomdb_vector_rows` insert\n  (`lib.rs:2846`). Moderate.\n- **Recall / correctness.** Exact within the filter set — semantics\n  change (results restricted), not approximated.\n- **Compatibility.** Plays cleanly with writer lock + projection\n  cursor; vec0 partition keys are chunk-organised so writes scale\n  per partition.\n\n**Verdict on its own: insufficient for AC-013's synthetic\nsingle-kind fixture; valuable for real workloads but not load-bearing\nfor the gate.**\n\n### 2. Partition keys (physical / logical)\n\n- **Mechanism.** Partition-key columns on `vec0` (sqlite-vec\n  feature; max 4) physically chunk storage. Logical alternative:\n  N per-kind `vec0` tables.\n- **Gain.** Same scaling as metadata prefilter; for AC-013's\n  single-kind fixture, zero. For domain workloads with k=8 partitions\n  → ~8× cheaper scans (~250 ms).\n- **Cost.** Either a multi-table search planner (logical) or schema\n  migration (partition_key). Logical option additionally creates a\n  fan-out merge problem at the search layer.\n- **Recall.** Exact within partition set.\n- **Compatibility.** Per-table flavour is incompatible with the\n  current single `vector_default` table assumption baked into\n  `read_search_in_tx`; partition_key flavour is a simpler change.\n\n**Verdict: same as #1 — orthogonal to the gate, but worth shipping\ntogether if we do a vec0 migration anyway.**\n\n### 3. Smaller embeddings (lower dim)\n\n- **Mechanism.** dim 768 → 384 (MiniLM-L6) halves distance compute;\n  → 256 cuts 3×; → 128 cuts 6×.\n- **Gain (alone).** 384 ⇒ ~1024 ms; 128 ⇒ ~340 ms. Neither closes\n  the 25–40× gap by itself.\n- **Cost.** This is **the embedder identity**, not a vec0 setting.\n  `EmbedderIdentity` is profile-pinned via\n  `_fathomdb_embedder_profiles` (`lib.rs:3248-3260`); switching\n  embedders requires a corpus rebuild and a profile rotation. Not a\n  knob we can flip without writer cooperation and a re-embedding\n  pass.\n- **Recall.** Domain-dependent; MTEB shows ~3–6 pp recall drop from\n  768→384 for general-purpose embedders.\n- **Compatibility.** Touches the embedder contract and the schema\n  pinning logic. **HITL Q1: do we have the appetite to switch the\n  reference embedder in 0.7.0?** Default assumption: no.\n\n**Verdict: out of scope for 0.7.0 unless HITL signals otherwise.**\n\n### 4. Binary / int8 quantization (THE LEVER)\n\n- **Mechanism.** Replace the f32 column with `bit[768]` or\n  `int8[768]`. sqlite-vec supports both natively in `vec0`\n  (`bit[N]`, `int8[N]` declarations; cited:\n  [sqlite-vec API reference / DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/3-api-reference);\n  [binary quantization guide](https://alexgarcia.xyz/sqlite-vec/guides/binary-quant.html)).\n  Binary distance becomes Hamming (popcount xor) on 96 bytes/row\n  vs 3072 bytes/row of f32 — **32× narrower**, plus 4× lighter\n  arithmetic. Int8 is 4× narrower with SIMD-able dot products.\n- **Gain.** Binary: 32× storage × ~1× arithmetic-density advantage\n  ⇒ 30–50× in practice on memory-bound scans. Published benches\n  (Kyle Howells, Cohere) show 15–40× faster than f32 brute force at\n  ~85–95 % recall@10 with **rerank-on-f32**. Expected p50 if we\n  binarize: **~50–100 ms** for 1 M rows. Int8 alone: 4–8× ⇒\n  ~250–500 ms (borderline on the p99 = 300 ms budget).\n- **Cost.** Schema migration (add `embedding_bin bit[768]` column\n  or a sibling `vector_default_bin` table); ingest path adds a\n  `vec_quantize_binary()` call alongside the f32 insert; query path\n  becomes a two-phase: **bit-distance prefilter → f32 rerank** of\n  the top ~256 candidates. The f32 column is retained for rerank\n  (so storage grows 1.03×, not shrinks). All changes are SQL-level\n  and application-side; no SQLite build flags. sqlite-vec ≥ 0.1.7\n  (our pinned version) already ships the relevant types per\n  [release notes](https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/releases) —\n  no upgrade required to land bit/int8, though upgrading to 0.1.9\n  is low-risk and unlocks the constraint-column KNN refinements.\n- **Recall.** Binary alone: ~70–80 % @ k=10. Binary + f32 rerank\n  on top-K (K = 4–8 × k): ~95–99 % @ k=10, equivalent to brute\n  force for most practical purposes. **HITL Q2: what's the recall\n  floor we'll enforce in the AC-013 RED test?** Suggested: ≥ 90 %\n  recall@10 vs f32 ground truth on the AC-013 fixture.\n- **Compatibility.** Single-writer lock and projection cursor are\n  not affected — both columns commit in the same writer\n  transaction. `VaryingEmbedder` produces normalised f32; binary\n  quantization preserves the sign-mass pattern it encodes, so the\n  fixture remains discriminative.\n\n**Verdict: binary + f32 rerank is the single highest-leverage Tier-1\ntechnique. Plausibly closes the gate alone.**\n\n### 5. Matryoshka / truncated dimensions\n\n- **Mechanism.** Run KNN against a 128-d prefix, rerank on full 768.\n- **Gain.** ~6× on the prefilter pass ⇒ ~340 ms. Less leverage than\n  binary; binary already wins on bandwidth.\n- **Cost.** Requires a Matryoshka-trained embedder (`nomic-embed\n  v1.5`, `text-embedding-3-*`). VaryingEmbedder is not Matryoshka;\n  the AC-013 fixture wouldn't exhibit the recall property.\n  sqlite-vec exposes `vec_slice()` but no documented native first-K\n  dim KNN.\n- **Recall.** Embedder-dependent.\n- **Compatibility.** Same embedder-identity coupling as #3.\n\n**Verdict: dominated by binary quantization for our setting. Skip.**\n\n### 6. Caching / candidate limiting\n\n- **Mechanism.** Result cache (no help on AC-013's uniform random\n  queries — held-out distribution per `ac013_query_bodies`,\n  `tests/perf_gates.rs:373`). `LIMIT 10` is already applied\n  (`lib.rs:2322`) but `vec0`'s brute-force scan still visits all\n  rows to compute the top-10 ordering. No early-exit on a\n  brute-force scan.\n- **Gain.** Zero on AC-013.\n- **Cost.** N/A.\n\n**Verdict: not load-bearing.**\n\n### Tier 1 combined estimate\n\nStacking the realistic non-embedder-switching levers — **binary\nquantization + f32 rerank** (#4) as the primary, with **metadata\nprefilter / partition_key** (#1 + #2) as an architectural neighbour\nthat ships for free in the same migration — gives:\n\n- **p50 expectation: 50–100 ms** (within the 80 ms p50 budget on\n  the optimistic side, ~10 ms over on the pessimistic).\n- **p99 expectation: 100–200 ms** (well under the 300 ms p99\n  budget).\n- **Recall: ≥ 95 % @ k=10** with K-rerank = 64, depending on\n  workload.\n\n**This plausibly closes the 25–40× gap.** Tier 2 (vectorlite) and\nTier 3 (broader ANN) become unnecessary for 0.7.0.\n\nThe one residual concern: the AC-013 fixture's `VaryingEmbedder`\nconcentrates mass on 6 coordinates out of 768\n(`tests/perf_gates.rs:317`), which is *atypically* friendly to\nbinary quantization (sign pattern dominates). Real embedders will\nshow somewhat less favourable ratios. The fixture itself should\nstill GREEN; HITL should note that AC-013's headroom may be\noverstated relative to production workloads.\n\n---\n\n## TIER 2 — vectorlite (HNSW SQLite extension)\n\nInvestigated for completeness. **Skip in 0.7.0** if Tier 1 closes\nthe gap.\n\n- **Maintenance.** `1yefuwang1/vectorlite`, Apache-2.0, latest\n  release **v0.2.0 (2024-08-19)** per the GitHub repo\n  ([vectorlite](https://github.com/1yefuwang1/vectorlite)). No\n  releases in the ~21 months since. 361 stars / 10 forks. Maturity:\n  research-grade.\n- **API.** Loadable extension, virtual table (`USING vectorlite(...)`).\n  Would slot into `register_sqlite_vec_extension()` as a sibling\n  registration (different entry point).\n- **Coexistence with sqlite-vec.** No conflict — different virtual\n  table names. But a vectorlite table is a separate corpus; we'd\n  either dual-write (storage 2×) or migrate.\n- **HNSW params.** `M` default 16, `ef_construction` 200, `ef`\n  query-time default 10. Sane defaults; would need tuning for dim 768.\n- **Benchmarks.** Author's published benches stop at 3000 vectors\n  (recall 36–99 % depending on `ef`; latency 13 µs – 1.4 ms). **No\n  1 M × 768 benchmark.** Extrapolation: HNSW is log-time, so the\n  step from 3 K → 1 M is ~2× per `log N` factor; plausibly 50–500 µs\n  per query, well under budget. But unbenchmarked at our scale.\n- **Storage.** HNSW typical 1.5–2× overhead; at 3 GB raw, +1.5–3 GB.\n  Acceptable but non-trivial for embedded-DB posture.\n- **Single-writer / lifecycle.** Vectorlite stores its HNSW graph\n  inside the SQLite file via the virtual table's shadow tables —\n  no external files. Compatible with `database_lock.rs`. Build is\n  synchronous on insert (no async rebuild) — fits the writer\n  transactional model.\n- **Snapshot / projection cursor.** Inserts commit synchronously\n  with the writer txn; AC-017 cursor freshness unaffected.\n- **Fallback.** No documented opt-out; would need to retain\n  `vec0` brute-force in parallel for recall validation.\n- **libsqlite3-sys 0.28 compatibility.** Not specifically tested\n  upstream; vectorlite requires SQLite ≥ 3.38 for metadata\n  filtering. rusqlite 0.31 / bundled ships SQLite ≥ 3.44 → OK.\n\n**Verdict: shippable with caveats (stale maintenance + unbenchmarked\nat our scale + architectural-lever cost), but unnecessary if Tier 1\ncloses the gap. If Tier 1 falls short, vectorlite is a credible\nTier 2 lever.**\n\nSlice scope if pursued: 1 slice (extension registration + schema\nmigration + dual-corpus rebuild). Adds an architectural lever on\ntop of PCACHE2 in 0.7.0 — **HITL \"one architectural lever per\nrelease\" rule applies; this would require an exception.**\n\n---\n\n## TIER 3 — broader ANN architecture\n\nPursued only as a sanity check; Tier 1 wins.\n\n- **sqlite-vec upstream ANN.** v0.1.10-alpha.1 introduced\n  experimental ANN indexes (`rescore`, `ivf` not-enabled,\n  `DiskANN`) per\n  [GitHub releases](https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/releases).\n  Status: alpha, not production. **Not 0.7.0-eligible.** Worth\n  tracking for 0.8.0 / 0.9.0.\n- **Algorithm choice.** For our dim 768 / 1 M / read-heavy /\n  embedded-single-process workload, HNSW is the obvious default;\n  IVF wins at much larger N; PQ wins under tight memory budgets.\n  Binary quantization (Tier 1 #4) *is* a scalar-quantization ANN\n  technique applied at brute-force scan time, which is why it\n  works without a graph index.\n- **Integration shapes** (A. inside vec0 future, B. vectorlite-\n  style external vtab, C. Rust-side `usearch` / `instant-distance`,\n  D. hybrid). C is the most interesting alternative if Tiers 1+2\n  both fail: `usearch` is actively maintained, has Rust bindings,\n  and supports binary + matryoshka natively. Would require\n  rebuilding the FathomDB search verb to leave SQLite for the KNN\n  step — large blast radius.\n- **Recall validation.** RED-test would express a recall floor\n  against f32 brute-force ground truth on a held-out query set;\n  bound: `recall@10 ≥ 0.90` (HITL to confirm).\n- **Slice scope.** Multi-phase, almost certainly slips to 0.7.1.\n\n**Verdict: do not pursue in 0.7.0.**\n\n---\n\n## HITL responses (locked 2026-05-27)\n\n1. **Recall floor for AC-013: ≥ 90 % recall@10** vs f32 ground\n   truth on the AC-013 held-out query set, as an initial floor.\n   Lock as a RED test alongside the latency budget. Tighten in a\n   later release if real-workload recall headroom permits.\n2. **Embedder switch: KEEP existing embedder.** Tier-1 #3\n   (smaller embeddings) and Tier-1 #5 (matryoshka) are\n   out-of-scope for 0.7.0. The `VaryingEmbedder` /\n   `EmbedderIdentity` contract is unchanged.\n3. **Metadata + partition_key in 0.7.0: YES.** Bundle with the\n   binary-quant migration in Pack 1 (see below). The schema\n   doesn't move the AC-013 gate but is the correct shape for\n   real workloads and avoids a second migration in 0.7.1.\n\n---\n\n## Drafter's recommendation — locked plan\n\n**Tier 1 wins. Tiers 2 and 3 are not pursued in 0.7.0.**\n\nShip in **two packs** within 0.7.0. Both packs live under the\nexisting 0.7.0 release line; the split exists for review\ngranularity, not for release-boundary reasons.\n\n### Pack 1 — schema migration + ingest double-write\n\nLands the new schema and the new ingest path. No reader behavior\nchange yet; queries still go through the existing f32 path.\n\n- **Add `bit[768]` column** (or sibling `vector_default_bin`\n  vec0 table) alongside the current f32 `embedding` column.\n  sqlite-vec 0.1.7 already supports `bit[N]` natively\n  ([release notes](https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/releases))\n  — no upstream upgrade required.\n- **Add metadata columns + partition_key** to the vec0 schema\n  (Tier 1 #1 + #2). HITL-confirmed to ship in 0.7.0. Doesn't\n  help the AC-013 gate (single-kind fixture) but is the correct\n  shape for real workloads and avoids a second migration in\n  0.7.1. Partition_key candidate: vector kind from\n  `_fathomdb_vector_rows` (chunk-organised storage). Metadata\n  columns: at minimum a `kind` column; broader metadata schema\n  per the corpus / API work tracked separately.\n- **Ingest path change** in the writer (`lib.rs:2846` insert\n  site for `_fathomdb_vector_rows`): on every write, also\n  compute `vec_quantize_binary(embedding)` and insert into the\n  bit column. This is the \"double-write\" — both columns commit\n  in the same writer transaction, so the writer-lock and\n  projection-cursor freshness contract are unchanged.\n- **Schema migration** for existing data: rebuild the bit\n  column from the existing f32 corpus at upgrade time (one-pass\n  `INSERT ... SELECT vec_quantize_binary(embedding) FROM\n  vector_default`). Migration cost: ~3 GB read + ~96 MB write\n  for a 1 M row corpus; well under a minute on disk.\n- **Tests:** Pack 1 adds no new RED gate; existing AC-013\n  remains pinned at the unindexed 2048/2327 numbers until\n  Pack 2 lands. Pack 1's correctness gate is the migration\n  round-trip test (insert f32, read back bit column,\n  `vec_distance_hamming` matches the expected popcount).\n\n### Pack 2 — query-path swap + RED tests\n\nSwitches the reader to the two-phase shape and pins the new\nbudgets.\n\n- **`read_search_in_tx` query rewrite** at `lib.rs:2317-2323`:\n  two-phase query, bit-distance KNN top-K (K=64) → f32 rerank\n  for final top-10. K = 64 picked from binary-quant literature\n  (Kyle Howells, Cohere) to land recall@10 ≥ 95 % at ~50–100 ms;\n  may be tunable.\n- **AC-013 latency budget re-pin** in `tests/perf_gates.rs:149-150`:\n  p50 ≤ 80 ms, p99 ≤ 300 ms (per ADR-0.7.0 draft). RED test\n  must fail at canonical-CI scale before Pack 2 code change.\n- **New AC recall-floor RED test** asserting recall@10 ≥ 0.90\n  vs f32 brute-force ground truth on the AC-013 fixture\n  (HITL-locked). Ground truth computed at test-setup time\n  via a brute-force pass; cached as expected top-10 IDs per\n  query. The brute-force pass uses the existing f32 column\n  retained for rerank — this is one of the column's two\n  jobs.\n- **AC-019 verdict** under the new query path: re-measure;\n  bound expression (`10× baseline`) stays.\n\n### Architectural-lever accounting\n\nThe binary-quant + rerank query shape is a **data-encoding\nchange** (schema + query rewrite), not an architectural lever\nin the sense of the `feedback_reliability_principles` rule\n(PCACHE2 was the architectural lever — replaces SQLite's page\ncache). The HITL doc records this disposition; if a future\nreviewer disagrees and counts it as a second lever, the fallback\nis to ship Pack 1 in 0.7.0 and Pack 2 in 0.7.1. Drafter's\nrecommendation: ship both in 0.7.0.\n\n### Out of scope for both packs\n\n- Embedder change (HITL confirmed: keep existing embedder).\n- ANN graph index (vectorlite, sqlite-vec ANN alpha, usearch /\n  instant-distance / Rust-side index). Tracked in\n  `dev/notes/pcache2-followups.md` for post-0.7.0 if real-workload\n  telemetry shows the binary-quant scan is insufficient.\n\n## Sources\n\n- [sqlite-vec releases](https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/releases)\n- [sqlite-vec metadata + filtering blog post](https://alexgarcia.xyz/blog/2024/sqlite-vec-metadata-release/index.html)\n- [sqlite-vec binary quantization guide](https://alexgarcia.xyz/sqlite-vec/guides/binary-quant.html)\n- [sqlite-vec API reference (DeepWiki)](https://deepwiki.com/asg017/sqlite-vec/3-api-reference)\n- [vectorlite GitHub repo](https://github.com/1yefuwang1/vectorlite)\n- [Kyle Howells — Binary quantized embeddings](https://ikyle.me/blog/2025/binary-quantized-embeddings)\n","numLines":419,"startLine":1,"totalLines":419}}}
