Failure modes + recovery\n\n| Failure mode                            | Surfacing                                                                                                          | Bindings contract                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |\n| --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| Lock contention on open                 | `DatabaseLocked` per § 3 + § 7                                                                                     | Every binding maps identically; no retry loop in the binding.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |\n| Corruption on open                      | `CorruptionError` carrying `detail` per § 3 + ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior                                   | Every binding surfaces `detail` as a structurally-typed object (not a stringified blob) exposing `recovery_hint.code` (idiomatic casing per binding) as the stable dispatch key, per ADR-corruption-open-behavior § 2. Exact attribute spelling owned by `interfaces/{python,ts}.md`. Bindings do NOT auto-invoke recovery. |\n| Embedder identity mismatch              | `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError` per § 3                                                                            | Bindings do NOT auto-rebuild and 0.6.0 provides no open-time bypass. Caller must resolve the mismatch outside the SDK surface.                                                                                                                                                                                              |\n| Schema migration failure                | `MigrationError` per § 3                                                                                           | Bindings surface per-step duration + failure reason from the engine's structured open result (REQ-042).                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |\n| Write validation failure (JSON Schema)  | `SchemaValidationError` per § 3                                                                                    | Surfaces save-time, pre-commit (AC-060b); engine state is unchanged after rejection. Bindings do NOT pre-validate.                                                                                                                                                                                                          |\n| Engine.close while writes in flight     | `ClosingError` per § 3 (variant per ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy § Consequences)                                       | Bindings surface as a typed close-race error; `Engine.close` itself is required (REQ-020a; AC-022a) and bindings do not auto-retry.                                                                                                                                                                                         |\n| Backpressure exhaustion / overload      | `OverloadedError` per § 3 (variant per ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy + ADR-0.6.0-projection-model layer-4 backpressure) | Bindings surface as a typed overload error; do NOT auto-retry; caller decides retry policy.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |\n| User-supplied embedder buggy / blocking | Engine watchdog enforces per-call timeout per Invariant D (default 30 s); surfaces as `EmbedderError`.             | Bindings document Invariant B + C constraints in their `Embedder` impl docs; tests verify a buggy embedder cannot deadlock the engine.                                                                                                                                                                                      |\n\n## 12. Boundaries with `interfaces/*.md`\n\n| Lives here (`design/bindings.md`)                                           | Lives there (`interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md`)                                  |\n| --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n| Surface-set parity claim across all bindings                                | The exact public symbol list per binding                                       |\n| Error-mapping protocol (the matrix itself lives in `design/errors.md`)      | Per-language stability posture, deprecation notes, exception hierarchy diagram |\n| Async dispatch model + how Invariants A–D manifest per binding              | Per-symbol async/sync signature, parameter types, return types                 |\n| Marshalling protocol (typed boundary; vector encoding; JSON-Schema cadence) | Per-language type names, type aliases, idiomatic accessors                     |\n| Embedder identity invariant across bindings                                 | Per-binding `Embedder` trait/protocol shape and impl examples                  |\n| Config knob parity claim                                                    | Per-language config object shape, kwargs spelling, CLI flag name               |\n| Lock contract across bindings                                               | (Not duplicated; cite this file.)                                              |\n| Logging schema invariance                                                   | Per-binding subscriber registration call signature                             |\n| Build/packaging strategy across bindings                                    | (Not duplicated; cite this file.)                                              |\n| Recovery non-presence claim                                                 | Per-binding surface enumeration that incidentally satisfies it                 |\n\n## 13. ADR + REQ + AC trace\n\n**ADRs owned/cross-cited by this design doc:**\n\n- ADR-0.6.0-async-surface (Invariants A–D; Path 1 / Path 2)\n- ADR-0.6.0-python-api-shape\n- ADR-0.6.0-typescript-api-shape\n- ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope\n- ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy (variant table = canonical mapping source)\n- ADR-0.6.0-prepared-write-shape (typed write boundary materialization in bindings)\n- ADR-0.6.0-typed-write-boundary\n- ADR-0.6.0-zerocopy-blob (vector encoding boundary)\n- ADR-0.6.0-vector-identity-embedder-owned + ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol (cross-binding identity invariant)\n- ADR-0.6.0-json-schema-policy (save-time validation behavior)\n- ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior (cross-binding corruption surface)\n- ADR-0.6.0-tier1-ci-platforms (per-binding smoke gate)\n- ADR-0.6.0-deprecation-policy-0-5-names\n\n**REQs covered:**\n\n- REQ-053 (five-verb SDK surface) — § 1\n- REQ-054 (recovery CLI-only) — § 10\n- REQ-037 (SDK unreachability of recovery) — § 10\n- REQ-056 (typed errors) — § 3\n- REQ-020a (close releases lock) — § 7\n- REQ-031d (refuse-on-corruption) — § 11 + § 3\n- REQ-042 (open reports migration progress) — § 11\n- REQ-046a/b (deprecation discipline at the bindings facade) — delegated to `interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md` for messaging shape; this file commits no deprecation contract\n- REQ-047 (embedder version-skew detection at resolution) — § 5 (via ADR-0.6.0-vector-identity-embedder-owned + the `fathomdb-embedder-api` semver-pinned trait crate per architecture.md § 1; skew detected at link/resolution time, not by bindings at runtime)\n\n**ACs cited:**\n\n- AC-001 (lifecycle phase tag enum) — § 8\n- AC-002 (no log files without subscriber) — § 8\n- AC-022a (close releases lock) — § 7\n- AC-035a/b/c/d (corruption refuse + shape + lock release + recovery CLI-only) — § 7, § 10, § 11\n- AC-056 (registry-installed wheel smoke gate) — § 9\n- AC-057a (five-verb SDK surface set) — § 1, § 10\n- AC-060a (typed errors per variant) — § 3\n- AC-060b (JSON-Schema save-time cadence) — § 4\n\n## 14. Invariants summary (no speculative knobs)\n\nThe bindings layer is normative on:\n\n1. Surface-set parity across SDK bindings (five verbs; no recovery on SDK).\n2. Error-mapping _protocol_ (one class per variant; typed attrs; single rooted hierarchy; no string-pattern dispatch). The matrix itself lives in `design/errors.md`.\n3. Dispatch model and Invariant A–D no-escape-hatch posture.\n4. Typed write boundary across bindings.\n5. JSON-Schema save-time validation cadence (no binding-side pre-validation).\n6. Cross-binding embedder identity.\n7. Engine-config knob symmetry across SDK bindings; binding-runtime mechanics\n   like the TS dispatch pool are not canonical engine knobs.\n8. Lock contract uniformity (sidecar flock per ADR #31, supersedes #30; writer connection uses default (NORMAL) locking_mode in WAL — EXCLUSIVE was dropped to admit the reader pool).\n9. Logging engine-event payload wire-stability (host-native fields derived by adapter).\n10. Recovery non-presence on SDK surface.\n\nThe bindings layer is _non-normative_ on (delegated to per-language `interfaces/*.md`): exact symbol names, parameter spellings, idiomatic casing, deprecation messaging, per-language documentation tone, hierarchy diagram presentation, ergonomic shortcuts that do not change semantics.\n\nThis file commits no knobs that are not already committed by an upstream ADR. It does not introduce a new `BindingsConfig` struct, a new dispatch model, or a new error class.\n\n## 15. Followups out of scope here\n\n- Subprocess bridge wire format (FU-WIRE15; ADR-0.6.0-subprocess-bridge-deferral; revisit 0.8.0).\n- Embedder cancellation semantics (cited under ADR-0.6.0-async-surface followups).\n- Asyncio-first Python API (not 0.6.0; sync is settled).\n- Streaming result cursors across the FFI boundary (rejected for 0.6.0; results are owned/materialized per § 4).\n\n## 16. Critic + lock protocol\n\nPer `plan.md` Phase 3 step 4, this draft is critic-passed by `architecture-inspector` (cross-cutting design with strong ADR coupling). Findings applied; HITL gate before status flip to `locked`.\n","numLines":320,"startLine":1,"totalLines":320}}}
dev/plans/runs/0.6.1-DEPENDABOT-review-20260524T203227Z.log:2577:dev/plans/runs/0.6.1-planning-review-20260523T162359Z.log:4438:dev/plans/runs/12-TX-ts-python-parity-20260517T204414Z.log:233:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KJpffbaHNFrZQPawKNS8qC","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\ttitle: Bindings Subsystem Design\n3\tdate: 2026-04-29\n4\ttarget_release: 0.6.0\n5\tdesc: Cross-language binding strategy (Python, TypeScript, CLI) — concerns that span all bindings collectively, distinct from per-surface signatures owned by interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md\n6\tblast_radius: src/rust/crates/fathomdb (Rust facade); src/rust/crates/fathomdb-embedder-api (semver-pinned trait crate; REQ-047 link-time check); src/python/ (PyO3 cdylib); src/ts/ (napi-rs cdylib); src/rust/crates/fathomdb-cli (binary); interfaces/{python,ts,cli,rust,wire}.md (per-surface signatures); design/errors.md (variant→class matrix owner); design/release.md (CI smoke gate owner); ADR-error-taxonomy + ADR-async-surface + ADR-python-api-shape + ADR-typescript-api-shape + ADR-cli-scope + ADR-embedder-protocol + ADR-prepared-write-shape + ADR-corruption-open-behavior; dev/design-logging-and-tracing.md (Tier 1/2 carryovers cited in § 8); build pipelines (pip + npm)\n7\tstatus: locked\n8\t---\n9\t\n10\t# Bindings — Design\n11\t\n12\tThis file is **cross-cutting**. It commits to invariants that hold _across_ every public binding (Python, TypeScript, CLI) collectively, plus the protocol that connects each binding to the underlying Rust engine. Per-binding signatures, examples, and error cases live in `interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md`; this file does not duplicate those.\n13\t\n14\t## 0. Value-test against `interfaces/`\n15\t\n16\tPer `plan.md` Phase 3 step 4, `design/bindings.md` is written first to test whether it fills a role distinct from per-surface `interfaces/*.md`. Verdict: **distinct role; KEEP**. The following concerns are _properties of bindings collectively_, not statements about a single binding's surface, and have no clean home in any per-language interface file:\n17\t\n18\t| Cross-cutting concern                                                                                                                   | Why not in `interfaces/<lang>.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |\n19\t| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n20\t| Surface-set parity (every SDK binding exposes the same five verbs and _only_ those five)                                                | Per-surface files would each enumerate their own list; a parity claim across bindings cannot be expressed by any single file.                                                                                                                        |\n21\t| Error-mapping _protocol_ (one class per variant; typed attrs not stringified args; single rooted hierarchy; no string-pattern dispatch) | The protocol commitments are properties OF the bindings collectively. The mapping _matrix itself_ lives in `design/errors.md` (architecture.md § 2) and is cited from interfaces/\\*.md; this file commits the protocol that any matrix must satisfy. |\n22\t| Async dispatch model and where Invariants A–D land per binding                                                                          | A property of the engine boundary expressed differently per language; the _protocol_ (e.g. \"Promise edges live in TS only; Python is sync\") is shared.                                                                                               |\n23\t| Embedder identity + cross-language consistency (same DB opened from Python and TS resolves the same `EmbedderIdentity`)                 | Multi-binding invariant; not a per-language signature.                                                                                                                                                                                               |\n24\t| Lock + process-exclusivity contract spanning bindings                                                                                   | Same DB file opened from Python in process A and TS in process B must observe the engine's exclusive WAL lock identically.                                                                                                                           |\n25\t| Build/packaging contracts (`pip install -e src/python/`; `npm install` against napi-rs `src/ts/`; CLI binary release)                   | Operations on the binding _shipping path_, not on the binding's runtime surface.                                                                                                                                                                     |\n26\t| Recovery-surface unreachability across all SDK bindings (REQ-037 / REQ-054 / REQ-031d)                                                  | A _non-presence_ claim that holds across every SDK; awkward to assert in any single per-language file.                                                                                                                                               |\n27\t\n28\tPer-binding _signatures_ still belong in `interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md`. This file commits to _protocol_, not to syntax.\n29\t\n30\t## 1. Surface-set parity invariant\n31\t\n32\tEvery **SDK** binding's public top-level surface MUST be exactly the five-verb canonical set in that binding's idiomatic casing:\n33\t\n34\t```text\n35\tEngine.open    admin.configure    write    search    close\n36\t```\n37\t\n38\t(REQ-053; gated by AC-057a.) The parity claim is symmetric _across SDK bindings_ (Python + TypeScript): a verb appears in every SDK binding or in none. Adding a verb requires updating all SDK bindings together; per-SDK-binding surface sets are not allowed to drift.\n39\t\n40\tRust is a stable public facade contract, but it is not part of the\n41\tPython/TypeScript SDK parity set. `interfaces/rust.md` owns the Rust facade\n42\tshape.\n43\t\n44\tCLI is a separate, non-SDK surface (per ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope, architecture.md § 1: \"Does NOT mirror full SDK 5-verb surface\"). CLI exposes a structurally distinct operator command set (`fathomdb doctor <verb>` and `fathomdb recover`). CLI parity with SDK is NOT required and NOT promised; adding an SDK verb does not imply adding a CLI command.\n45\t\n46\tThe SDK surface MUST NOT contain any name in `{recover, restore, repair, fix, rebuild, doctor}` (REQ-037, REQ-054, REQ-031d). These verbs exist exclusively on the CLI surface.\n47\t\n48\tREQ-030's bounded-completion surface is **not** a sixth top-level SDK verb.\n49\tIt is an `Engine` instance method. The per-language method spelling is owned by\n50\t`interfaces/{rust,python,typescript}.md`; this file commits only that it must\n51\tnot widen the top-level parity set from five verbs to six.\n52\t\n53\t## 2. Lifecycle dispatch model\n54\t\n55\tEach binding owns the dispatch model for its language. The protocol that connects them to the engine is shared.\n56\t\n57\t| Binding    | Dispatch model                                                                   | Engine call shape                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Owning ADR                                                                |\n58\t| ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n59\t| Python     | Sync surface (Path 1)                                                            | Direct PyO3 call into Rust; engine is sync; Python `asyncio` users wrap with `run_in_executor` (documented pattern, not first-party API).                                                                                                                                                      | ADR-0.6.0-python-api-shape; ADR-0.6.0-async-surface (Path 1 + Invariants) |\n60\t| TypeScript | Promise surface (Path 2)                                                         | napi-rs `ThreadsafeFunction` + TS binding-owned Rust handoff pool sized `num_cpus::get()`. Decoupled from libuv's `fs/dns/crypto` thread pool. TS may expose adapter-specific sizing control, but that control is owned by the TS binding runtime rather than the canonical engine-config set. | ADR-0.6.0-typescript-api-shape; ADR-0.6.0-async-surface (Path 2)          |\n61\t| CLI        | Sync subcommand entry; binary returns process exit code per ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope. | Direct sync call into Rust; same engine as SDK.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope                                                       |\n62\t\n63\tAsync invariants A–D from ADR-0.6.0-async-surface manifest in every binding. The invariants themselves are owned by that ADR; this file commits only the _binding-side_ assertion that no binding exposes an escape hatch:\n64\t\n65\t- **Invariant A (scheduler post-commit).** Cite ADR-async-surface § Decision. Bindings expose no escape hatch: bindings do not introduce additional locking around `write`, do not pre-dispatch scheduler work from the caller thread, and do not provide a \"skip-scheduler\" path.\n66\t- **Invariant B (engine-owned embedder thread).** Cite ADR-async-surface § Decision. Bindings expose no escape hatch: embedder calls always run on the engine-owned embedder pool, never on the binding's caller thread (Python GIL-holder, TS libuv worker, CLI main thread).\n67\t- **Invariant C (embedder-protocol no-reentrancy).** Cite ADR-async-surface + ADR-embedder-protocol. Bindings document this constraint in their `Embedder` impl docs; tests verify a buggy user-supplied embedder cannot deadlock the engine. Bindings expose no escape hatch (no \"reentrant embedder\" config flag).\n68\t- **Invariant D (eager model warmup + per-call timeout).** Cite ADR-async-surface § Decision (eager warmup at `Engine.open`; per-call timeout default 30s). Bindings expose no escape hatch: no cold-load path, no per-call-timeout override that disables the watchdog. Reporting of model-load duration is part of `Engine.open`'s structured result, the shape of which is owned by `design/engine.md`.\n69\t\n70\t## 3. Error-mapping protocol\n71\t\n72\tThe Rust error variant set is owned by **`ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy`** (BEHAVIOR commitment). The variant→class mapping matrix (Rust variant → Python exception → TS error class) is owned by **`design/errors.md`** per architecture.md § 2 (which assigns the `errors` module to that file). This file commits only the _protocol_ claims that the mapping must satisfy.\n73\t\n74\tProtocol commitments (apply to every binding):\n75\t\n76\t- **One concrete class per variant.** Never a generic `EngineError` with a `kind` discriminator string. Tests dispatch on `except <Specific>` (Python) and `instanceof <Specific>` (TS) without inspecting messages. (REQ-056; AC-060a.)\n77\t- **Single rooted hierarchy.** Every binding's exception/error hierarchy roots at one base class (Python: `fathomdb.EngineError`; TS: `FathomDbError`) so callers can catch broad failures while still narrowing per variant.\n78\t- **Typed attributes, not stringified payloads.** Variant fields surface as typed attributes on the exception/error object, not as `args[0]` tuples or stringified blobs. The exact attribute spelling (idiomatic per binding) is owned by `interfaces/{python,ts}.md`; this file commits that the attribute exists and is structurally typed.\n79\t- **Stable dispatch key for `CorruptionError`.** `recovery_hint.code` (idiomatic casing per binding) is the stable dispatch key per ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior § 2; `recovery_hint.doc_anchor` is the doc pointer. Bindings do not flatten or stringify `recovery_hint`.\n80\t- **CLI is structural.** Each variant maps to a stable exit code + a structured stderr line carrying the variant's stable code (e.g. `recovery_hint.code` for `Corruption`). The exit-code table itself lives in `interfaces/cli.md`; the variant→exit-code authority is in `design/errors.md`.\n81\t- **Top-level Rust error type plurality.** The Rust surface exposes more than one top-level error type (`EngineError` for runtime ops, `EngineOpenError` for `Engine.open` per ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior). `design/errors.md` enumerates which top-level Rust types exist and how each variant routes to a binding class; Python and TypeScript expose one catch-all base while preserving distinct leaf classes.\n82\t\n83\tDecision note:\n84\t\n85\t- 0.6.0 uses one exported catch-all base type across Python and TypeScript.\n86\t- TypeScript's catch-all base is `FathomDbError`; every concrete leaf class in\n87\t  `design/errors.md` extends it.\n88\t\n89\tThis file does NOT enumerate the variant→class matrix; that authority lives in `design/errors.md` and is cited by `interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md`. Per-language interface files do not duplicate the matrix.\n90\t\n91\t## 4. Marshalling strategy\n92\t\n93\t`PreparedWrite` (per ADR-0.6.0-prepared-write-shape) is the only typed shape the engine accepts. Bindings construct `Vec<PreparedWrite>` from idiomatic per-language inputs and never touch raw SQL.\n94\t\n95\t| Direction                                                                                  | Boundary                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Owning ADR                                                        |\n96\t| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n97\t| Caller → engine: `PreparedWrite::Node` / `Edge` / `OpStore(OpStoreInsert)` / `AdminSchema` | Per-binding builder constructs the variant. No raw SQL accepted.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | ADR-0.6.0-typed-write-boundary; ADR-0.6.0-prepared-write-shape    |\n98\t| Caller → engine: vector input                                                              | Bindings accept idiomatic typed arrays. When the input is already LE-f32 contiguous (NumPy `ndarray[float32]`, TS `Float32Array`), the binding zerocopy-casts to `&[u8]` per ADR-0.6.0-zerocopy-blob (no copy). Non-contiguous inputs (e.g. Python `list[float]`) are converted once into an LE-f32 buffer at the binding boundary. | ADR-0.6.0-zerocopy-blob; ADR-0.6.0-vector-identity-embedder-owned |\n99\t| Caller → engine: op-store payload                                                          | Bindings accept idiomatic dict / object; bindings serialize to JSON; engine validates against `schema_id` save-time before commit.                                                                                                                                                                                                  | ADR-0.6.0-json-schema-policy; AC-060b                             |\n100\t| Engine → caller: search results                                                            | Engine returns owned typed result rows; bindings expose them as idiomatic types (Python `list[dict]`/dataclass; TS object). No lazy cursors crossing the FFI boundary.                                                                                                                                                              | ADR-0.6.0-retrieval-pipeline-shape                                |\n101\t| Engine → caller: errors                                                                    | Per § 3.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy                                          |\n102\t\n103\tJSON-Schema validation behavior (AC-060b) is invariant across bindings:\n104\tvalidation fires save-time, pre-commit, on the writer thread; failure surfaces\n105\tas `SchemaValidationError`; no open-time re-validation; bindings do _not_ run\n106\ttheir own pre-engine validation pass (single source of truth).\n107\t\n108\t## 5. Embedder identity invariant\n109\t\n110\tPer ADR-0.6.0-vector-identity-embedder-owned + ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol, vector identity is owned by the embedder, not by per-DB config. Cross-binding consequence:\n111\t\n112\t- A DB written by Python with embedder identity `E` and re-opened by TypeScript MUST resolve the same `EmbedderIdentity`. Bindings do not pin or override identity at open.\n113\t- Identity-mismatch surfaces uniformly as `EmbedderIdentityMismatch` (per § 3 mapping). Bindings do not auto-rebuild on mismatch (consistent with ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior's no-auto-recover posture for open failures).\n114\t- A user-supplied embedder (Python or TS impl) declares its `EmbedderIdentity` per ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol Invariants 1–4. Binding-side bindings do _not_ synthesize identity from binding metadata; identity is the embedder's responsibility.\n115\t- REQ-047 (embedder version-skew detection) is satisfied at link/resolution time by the semver-pinned `fathomdb-embedder-api` trait crate (architecture.md § 1) plus the embedder-owned `EmbedderIdentity` carried in stored vectors. Bindings do NOT perform runtime version-skew checks; they consume the typed mismatch error surfaced by the engine.\n116\t\n117\t## 6. SDK symmetry, CLI boundary, and config classes\n118\t\n119\tTwo different symmetry rules exist and must not be conflated:\n120\t\n121\t- **SDK surface symmetry.** Python and TypeScript expose the same canonical SDK\n122\t  verb set. This is the hard parity rule from § 1.\n123\t- **Engine-config symmetry.** Engine-owned knobs named in `design/engine.md`\n124\t  must be reachable from every SDK binding in idiomatic form.\n125\t\n126\tCLI is outside both parity claims. It is a separate operator surface and does\n127\tnot promise flag-for-flag mirroring of SDK verbs or SDK config.\n128\t\n129\tThe engine-config symmetry rule applies only to knobs that change engine\n130\tbehavior after the binding boundary. It does **not** apply to binding-runtime\n131\tmechanics that exist only to bridge one host runtime into the engine.\n132\t\n133\tExamples:\n134\t\n135\t- `embedder_pool_size` — engine-owned runtime knob; symmetry required across\n136\t  SDK bindings.\n137\t- `scheduler_runtime_threads` — engine-owned runtime knob; symmetry required\n138\t  across SDK bindings.\n139\t- Python `run_in_executor` usage — caller-side runtime pattern, not an engine\n140\t  knob.\n141\t- TypeScript `ThreadsafeFunction` pool sizing — TS binding runtime mechanic per\n142\t  ADR-0.6.0-async-surface. A TS binding may surface it near `Engine.open`, but\n143\t  it is not part of the canonical engine-config set and does not create a\n144\t  Python-parity obligation.\n145\t\n146\tCLI inherits engine defaults unless `interfaces/cli.md` explicitly grants a\n147\tflag. That inheritance posture does not weaken the SDK symmetry rule because\n148\tCLI is not an SDK binding.\n149\t\n150\t## 7. Lock + process-exclusivity contract\n151\t\n152\tPer `architecture.md` § 5 + ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism-reader-pool-revision (#31, supersedes #30), only one `Engine` instance per database file may be open at a time. The lock mechanism is the sidecar `{database_path}.lock` flock (Rust std `File::try_lock`, per-OFD exclusion semantics). Sidecar = pre-open fail-fast + operator-diagnostic PID + load-bearing layer for both cross-process and same-process two-`Engine` exclusion. The writer connection runs WAL with default (NORMAL) locking_mode; `PRAGMA locking_mode=EXCLUSIVE` is NOT applied — it is incompatible with the Phase 8 reader pool because EXCLUSIVE causes SQLite to skip the WAL shared-memory wal-index. Reader connections use default (NORMAL) locking_mode and benefit from WAL multi-reader concurrency (REQ-018). `-shm` is created as a normal WAL artifact.\n153\t\n154\tCross-binding consequences:\n155\t\n156\t- Python in process A holding `Engine.open(path)` → TS in process B calling `Engine.open(path)` MUST fail with `DatabaseLocked { holder_pid }` regardless of binding identity. The sidecar flock is the load-bearing layer for cross-process exclusion; it surfaces BEFORE SQLite I/O begins, so Engine.open does not pay migration / embedder warmup cost on a doomed open.\n157\t- The lock's lifetime is bound to the `Engine` instance; closing or dropping the engine releases it (drops the sidecar lock fd + closes SQLite connections). `Engine.close` is required (REQ-020a; AC-022a).\n158\t- Same-process two-`Engine` (Python `Engine` and TS `Engine` in the _same_ process targeting the _same_ path) is also forbidden. The second `Engine.open` opens a NEW `File` handle for the sidecar; per-OFD `flock` semantics return `WouldBlock`, surfacing as `DatabaseLocked`. Bindings do NOT maintain an in-process registry of held paths.\n159\t- Path canonicalization: `Engine.open` canonicalizes the parent directory and appends the leaf filename before deriving `{...}.lock` path, defeating symlink + bind-mount aliasing. Bindings do not perform their own canonicalization.\n160\t\n161\tThe corruption-on-open path (ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior § 5; AC-035c) MUST release the sidecar lock + close any opened SQLite connection before returning `CorruptionError`. Bindings inherit this guarantee; no binding-level workaround is permitted.\n162\t\n163\t## 8. Logging / tracing subscriber attachment\n164\t\n165\tThe engine emits structured tracing events (per `dev/design-logging-and-tracing.md` Tier 1/2 carryovers). Binding adapters attach a host subscriber:\n166\t\n167\t- Python: caller registers a `logging`-backed adapter via a binding-provided helper that maps tracing events into Python `LogRecord`s.\n168\t- TypeScript: caller registers a callback invoked per event.\n169\t- CLI: when run in human-facing mode, attaches a console subscriber; when run in machine-facing `--json` mode, emits the verb-owned JSON shape from `interfaces/cli.md` / `design/recovery.md`. `doctor check-integrity` is a single JSON object; other verbs own their own machine-readable contract.\n170\t\n171\tAcross bindings, the _engine event payload_ is wire-stable: same field names,\n172\tsame types, same lifecycle phase tag enum (AC-001). The host adapter MAY\n173\ttranslate or derive **host-native required fields** from the engine event when\n174\tthe host logger backend requires them. Engine event fields appear under a\n175\tstable `fathomdb` payload key in the host record so downstream filters can\n176\ttarget them precisely; the engine never emits a host-named field directly.\n177\t\n178\t### `fathomdb` payload envelope\n179\t\n180\tWithin the host record, the stable `fathomdb` payload key carries one of these\n181\tsurface shapes:\n182\t\n183\t- response-cycle event: `phase` plus producer-owned operation context\n184\t- diagnostic event: `source`, `category`, and producer-owned detail payload\n185\t- counter snapshot: `counter_snapshot`\n186\t- profile record: `profile_record`\n187\t- stress-failure payload: `stress_failure`\n188\t- migration step event: `migration_step`\n189\t\n190\tOwnership split:\n191\t\n192\t- `design/lifecycle.md` owns `phase`, `source`, `category`,\n193\t  `counter_snapshot`, `profile_record`, and `stress_failure`\n194\t- `design/migrations.md` owns `migration_step`\n195\t- binding adapters own how these are embedded into host-native logger records\n196\t\n197\tAcceptance note: current ACs lock the typed payload members more directly than\n198\tthe outer `fathomdb` envelope key. Do not rename the envelope key without\n199\tupdating both this file and the interface docs.\n200\t\n201\tDefault subscriber posture: when no subscriber is registered, the engine writes nothing — no log files, no stderr noise (AC-002).\n202\t\n203\t## 9. Build / packaging strategy\n204\t\n205\tBinding-shipping protocol — the canonical build entry point per binding. (CI smoke gates and post-publish verification are owned by `design/release.md` per architecture.md § 2; this file commits only the per-binding build path.)\n206\t\n207\t| Binding    | Build path                                                                                | Authoritative source                                       |\n208\t| ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |\n209\t| Python     | `pip install -e src/python/` (PyO3 cdylib package `fathomdb` under `src/python/`)         | architecture.md § 1; memory `feedback_python_native_build` |\n210\t| TypeScript | `npm install` against `src/ts/` (napi-rs cdylib package `fathomdb` under `src/ts/`)       | architecture.md § 1; ADR-0.6.0-typescript-api-shape        |\n211\t| CLI        | `cargo build --release -p fathomdb-cli`; release artifact is a single binary per platform | ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope; ADR-0.6.0-tier1-ci-platforms          |\n212\t\n213\tCI build, per-binding smoke testing, and post-publish verification (memory `feedback_release_verification`; AC-056) are owned by `design/release.md`. Developer ergonomics (e.g. \"do not manually `cargo build && cp`\") are out of scope here and live in `src/python/README.md` / contributor docs.\n214\t\n215\t## 10. Recovery surface unreachability\n216\t\n217\tThe bindings collectively MUST NOT expose any recovery verb on the SDK surface. (REQ-037, REQ-054, REQ-031d; gated by AC-057a + AC-035d.) Recovery is invoked exclusively via the CLI binary `fathomdb recover` and `fathomdb doctor <verb>`. The application SDK contains no path that mutates a corruption-marked database.\n218\t\n219\tThis is a _non-presence_ claim, not a per-binding signature, and is therefore owned here rather than in `interfaces/{python,ts}.md`.\n220\t\n221\t## 11. Failure modes + recovery\n222\t\n223\t| Failure mode                            | Surfacing                                                                                                          | Bindings contract                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |\n224\t| --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n225\t| Lock contention on open                 | `DatabaseLocked` per § 3 + § 7                                                                                     | Every binding maps identically; no retry loop in the binding.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |\n226\t| Corruption on open                      | `CorruptionError` carrying `detail` per § 3 + ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior                                   | Every binding surfaces `detail` as a structurally-typed object (not a stringified blob) exposing `recovery_hint.code` (idiomatic casing per binding) as the stable dispatch key, per ADR-corruption-open-behavior § 2. Exact attribute spelling owned by `interfaces/{python,ts}.md`. Bindings do NOT auto-invoke recovery. |\n227\t| Embedder identity mismatch              | `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError` per § 3                                                                            | Bindings do NOT auto-rebuild and 0.6.0 provides no open-time bypass. Caller must resolve the mismatch outside the SDK surface.                                                                                                                                                                                              |\n228\t| Schema migration failure                | `MigrationError` per § 3                                                                                           | Bindings surface per-step duration + failure reason from the engine's structured open result (REQ-042).                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |\n229\t| Write validation failure (JSON Schema)  | `SchemaValidationError` per § 3                                                                                    | Surfaces save-time, pre-commit (AC-060b); engine state is unchanged after rejection. Bindings do NOT pre-validate.                                                                                                                                                                                                          |\n230\t| Engine.close while writes in flight     | `ClosingError` per § 3 (variant per ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy § Consequences)                                       | Bindings surface as a typed close-race error; `Engine.close` itself is required (REQ-020a; AC-022a) and bindings do not auto-retry.                                                                                                                                                                                         |\n231\t| Backpressure exhaustion / overload      | `OverloadedError` per § 3 (variant per ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy + ADR-0.6.0-projection-model layer-4 backpressure) | Bindings surface as a typed overload error; do NOT auto-retry; caller decides retry policy.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |\n232\t| User-supplied embedder buggy / blocking | Engine watchdog enforces per-call timeout per Invariant D (default 30 s); surfaces as `EmbedderError`.             | Bindings document Invariant B + C constraints in their `Embedder` impl docs; tests verify a buggy embedder cannot deadlock the engine.                                                                                                                                                                                      |\n233\t\n234\t## 12. Boundaries with `interfaces/*.md`\n235\t\n236\t| Lives here (`design/bindings.md`)                                           | Lives there (`interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md`)                                  |\n237\t| --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n238\t| Surface-set parity claim across all bindings                                | The exact public symbol list per binding                                       |\n239\t| Error-mapping protocol (the matrix itself lives in `design/errors.md`)      | Per-language stability posture, deprecation notes, exception hierarchy diagram |\n240\t| Async dispatch model + how Invariants A–D manifest per binding              | Per-symbol async/sync signature, parameter types, return types                 |\n241\t| Marshalling protocol (typed boundary; vector encoding; JSON-Schema cadence) | Per-language type names, type aliases, idiomatic accessors                     |\n242\t| Embedder identity invariant across bindings                                 | Per-binding `Embedder` trait/protocol shape and impl examples                  |\n243\t| Config knob parity claim                                                    | Per-language config object shape, kwargs spelling, CLI flag name               |\n244\t| Lock contract across bindings                                               | (Not duplicated; cite this file.)                                              |\n245\t| Logging schema invariance                                                   | Per-binding subscriber registration call signature                             |\n246\t| Build/packaging strategy across bindings                                    | (Not duplicated; cite this file.)                                              |\n247\t| Recovery non-presence claim                                                 | Per-binding surface enumeration that incidentally satisfies it                 |\n248\t\n249\t## 13. ADR + REQ + AC trace\n250\t\n251\t**ADRs owned/cross-cited by this design doc:**\n252\t\n253\t- ADR-0.6.0-async-surface (Invariants A–D; Path 1 / Path 2)\n254\t- ADR-0.6.0-python-api-shape\n255\t- ADR-0.6.0-typescript-api-shape\n256\t- ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope\n257\t- ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy (variant table = canonical mapping source)\n258\t- ADR-0.6.0-prepared-write-shape (typed write boundary materialization in bindings)\n259\t- ADR-0.6.0-typed-write-boundary\n260\t- ADR-0.6.0-zerocopy-blob (vector encoding boundary)\n261\t- ADR-0.6.0-vector-identity-embedder-owned + ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol (cross-binding identity invariant)\n262\t- ADR-0.6.0-json-schema-policy (save-time validation behavior)\n263\t- ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior (cross-binding corruption surface)\n264\t- ADR-0.6.0-tier1-ci-platforms (per-binding smoke gate)\n265\t- ADR-0.6.0-deprecation-policy-0-5-names\n266\t\n267\t**REQs covered:**\n268\t\n269\t- REQ-053 (five-verb SDK surface) — § 1\n270\t- REQ-054 (recovery CLI-only) — § 10\n271\t- REQ-037 (SDK unreachability of recovery) — § 10\n272\t- REQ-056 (typed errors) — § 3\n273\t- REQ-020a (close releases lock) — § 7\n274\t- REQ-031d (refuse-on-corruption) — § 11 + § 3\n275\t- REQ-042 (open reports migration progress) — § 11\n276\t- REQ-046a/b (deprecation discipline at the bindings facade) — delegated to `interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md` for messaging shape; this file commits no deprecation contract\n277\t- REQ-047 (embedder version-skew detection at resolution) — § 5 (via ADR-0.6.0-vector-identity-embedder-owned + the `fathomdb-embedder-api` semver-pinned trait crate per architecture.md § 1; skew detected at link/resolution time, not by bindings at runtime)\n278\t\n279\t**ACs cited:**\n280\t\n281\t- AC-001 (lifecycle phase tag enum) — § 8\n282\t- AC-002 (no log files without subscriber) — § 8\n283\t- AC-022a (close releases lock) — § 7\n284\t- AC-035a/b/c/d (corruption refuse + shape + lock release + recovery CLI-only) — § 7, § 10, § 11\n285\t- AC-056 (registry-installed wheel smoke gate) — § 9\n286\t- AC-057a (five-verb SDK surface set) — § 1, § 10\n287\t- AC-060a (typed errors per variant) — § 3\n288\t- AC-060b (JSON-Schema save-time cadence) — § 4\n289\t\n290\t## 14. Invariants summary (no speculative knobs)\n291\t\n292\tThe bindings layer is normative on:\n293\t\n294\t1. Surface-set parity across SDK bindings (five verbs; no recovery on SDK).\n295\t2. Error-mapping _protocol_ (one class per variant; typed attrs; single rooted hierarchy; no string-pattern dispatch). The matrix itself lives in `design/errors.md`.\n296\t3. Dispatch model and Invariant A–D no-escape-hatch posture.\n297\t4. Typed write boundary across bindings.\n298\t5. JSON-Schema save-time validation cadence (no binding-side pre-validation).\n299\t6. Cross-binding embedder identity.\n300\t7. Engine-config knob symmetry across SDK bindings; binding-runtime mechanics\n301\t   like the TS dispatch pool are not canonical engine knobs.\n302\t8. Lock contract uniformity (sidecar flock per ADR #31, supersedes #30; writer connection uses default (NORMAL) locking_mode in WAL — EXCLUSIVE was dropped to admit the reader pool).\n303\t9. Logging engine-event payload wire-stability (host-native fields derived by adapter).\n304\t10. Recovery non-presence on SDK surface.\n305\t\n306\tThe bindings layer is _non-normative_ on (delegated to per-language `interfaces/*.md`): exact symbol names, parameter spellings, idiomatic casing, deprecation messaging, per-language documentation tone, hierarchy diagram presentation, ergonomic shortcuts that do not change semantics.\n307\t\n308\tThis file commits no knobs that are not already committed by an upstream ADR. It does not introduce a new `BindingsConfig` struct, a new dispatch model, or a new error class.\n309\t\n310\t## 15. Followups out of scope here\n311\t\n312\t- Subprocess bridge wire format (FU-WIRE15; ADR-0.6.0-subprocess-bridge-deferral; revisit 0.8.0).\n313\t- Embedder cancellation semantics (cited under ADR-0.6.0-async-surface followups).\n314\t- Asyncio-first Python API (not 0.6.0; sync is settled).\n315\t- Streaming result cursors across the FFI boundary (rejected for 0.6.0; results are owned/materialized per § 4).\n316\t\n317\t## 16. Critic + lock protocol\n318\t\n319\tPer `plan.md` Phase 3 step 4, this draft is critic-passed by `architecture-inspector` (cross-cutting design with strong ADR coupling). Findings applied; HITL gate before status flip to `locked`.\n320\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e03e003f-16a4-48ce-9857-522e8e7e136e","uuid":"f496b184-50cf-41a8-b14d-260adedb878e","timestamp":"2026-05-17T20:44:50.286Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/fdb-12-TX-ts-python-parity-20260517T204414Z/dev/design/bindings.md","content":"---\ntitle: Bindings Subsystem Design\ndate: 2026-04-29\ntarget_release: 0.6.0\ndesc: Cross-language binding strategy (Python, TypeScript, CLI) — concerns that span all bindings collectively, distinct from per-surface signatures owned by interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md\nblast_radius: src/rust/crates/fathomdb (Rust facade); src/rust/crates/fathomdb-embedder-api (semver-pinned trait crate; REQ-047 link-time check); src/python/ (PyO3 cdylib); src/ts/ (napi-rs cdylib); src/rust/crates/fathomdb-cli (binary); interfaces/{python,ts,cli,rust,wire}.md (per-surface signatures); design/errors.md (variant→class matrix owner); design/release.md (CI smoke gate owner); ADR-error-taxonomy + ADR-async-surface + ADR-python-api-shape + ADR-typescript-api-shape + ADR-cli-scope + ADR-embedder-protocol + ADR-prepared-write-shape + ADR-corruption-open-behavior; dev/design-logging-and-tracing.md (Tier 1/2 carryovers cited in § 8); build pipelines (pip + npm)\nstatus: locked\n---\n\n# Bindings — Design\n\nThis file is **cross-cutting**. It commits to invariants that hold _across_ every public binding (Python, TypeScript, CLI) collectively, plus the protocol that connects each binding to the underlying Rust engine. Per-binding signatures, examples, and error cases live in `interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md`; this file does not duplicate those.\n\n## 0. Value-test against `interfaces/`\n\nPer `plan.md` Phase 3 step 4, `design/bindings.md` is written first to test whether it fills a role distinct from per-surface `interfaces/*.md`. Verdict: **distinct role; KEEP**. The following concerns are _properties of bindings collectively_, not statements about a single binding's surface, and have no clean home in any per-language interface file:\n\n| Cross-cutting concern                                                                                                                   | Why not in `interfaces/<lang>.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |\n| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| Surface-set parity (every SDK binding exposes the same five verbs and _only_ those five)                                                | Per-surface files would each enumerate their own list; a parity claim across bindings cannot be expressed by any single file.                                                                                                                        |\n| Error-mapping _protocol_ (one class per variant; typed attrs not stringified args; single rooted hierarchy; no string-pattern dispatch) | The protocol commitments are properties OF the bindings collectively. The mapping _matrix itself_ lives in `design/errors.md` (architecture.md § 2) and is cited from interfaces/\\*.md; this file commits the protocol that any matrix must satisfy. |\n| Async dispatch model and where Invariants A–D land per binding                                                                          | A property of the engine boundary expressed differently per language; the _protocol_ (e.g. \"Promise edges live in TS only; Python is sync\") is shared.                                                                                               |\n| Embedder identity + cross-language consistency (same DB opened from Python and TS resolves the same `EmbedderIdentity`)                 | Multi-binding invariant; not a per-language signature.                                                                                                                                                                                               |\n| Lock + process-exclusivity contract spanning bindings                                                                                   | Same DB file opened from Python in process A and TS in process B must observe the engine's exclusive WAL lock identically.                                                                                                                           |\n| Build/packaging contracts (`pip install -e src/python/`; `npm install` against napi-rs `src/ts/`; CLI binary release)                   | Operations on the binding _shipping path_, not on the binding's runtime surface.                                                                                                                                                                     |\n| Recovery-surface unreachability across all SDK bindings (REQ-037 / REQ-054 / REQ-031d)                                                  | A _non-presence_ claim that holds across every SDK; awkward to assert in any single per-language file.                                                                                                                                               |\n\nPer-binding _signatures_ still belong in `interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md`. This file commits to _protocol_, not to syntax.\n\n## 1. Surface-set parity invariant\n\nEvery **SDK** binding's public top-level surface MUST be exactly the five-verb canonical set in that binding's idiomatic casing:\n\n```text\nEngine.open    admin.configure    write    search    close\n```\n\n(REQ-053; gated by AC-057a.) The parity claim is symmetric _across SDK bindings_ (Python + TypeScript): a verb appears in every SDK binding or in none. Adding a verb requires updating all SDK bindings together; per-SDK-binding surface sets are not allowed to drift.\n\nRust is a stable public facade contract, but it is not part of the\nPython/TypeScript SDK parity set. `interfaces/rust.md` owns the Rust facade\nshape.\n\nCLI is a separate, non-SDK surface (per ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope, architecture.md § 1: \"Does NOT mirror full SDK 5-verb surface\"). CLI exposes a structurally distinct operator command set (`fathomdb doctor <verb>` and `fathomdb recover`). CLI parity with SDK is NOT required and NOT promised; adding an SDK verb does not imply adding a CLI command.\n\nThe SDK surface MUST NOT contain any name in `{recover, restore, repair, fix, rebuild, doctor}` (REQ-037, REQ-054, REQ-031d). These verbs exist exclusively on the CLI surface.\n\nREQ-030's bounded-completion surface is **not** a sixth top-level SDK verb.\nIt is an `Engine` instance method. The per-language method spelling is owned by\n`interfaces/{rust,python,typescript}.md`; this file commits only that it must\nnot widen the top-level parity set from five verbs to six.\n\n## 2. Lifecycle dispatch model\n\nEach binding owns the dispatch model for its language. The protocol that connects them to the engine is shared.\n\n| Binding    | Dispatch model                                                                   | Engine call shape                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Owning ADR                                                                |\n| ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| Python     | Sync surface (Path 1)                                                            | Direct PyO3 call into Rust; engine is sync; Python `asyncio` users wrap with `run_in_executor` (documented pattern, not first-party API).                                                                                                                                                      | ADR-0.6.0-python-api-shape; ADR-0.6.0-async-surface (Path 1 + Invariants) |\n| TypeScript | Promise surface (Path 2)                                                         | napi-rs `ThreadsafeFunction` + TS binding-owned Rust handoff pool sized `num_cpus::get()`. Decoupled from libuv's `fs/dns/crypto` thread pool. TS may expose adapter-specific sizing control, but that control is owned by the TS binding runtime rather than the canonical engine-config set. | ADR-0.6.0-typescript-api-shape; ADR-0.6.0-async-surface (Path 2)          |\n| CLI        | Sync subcommand entry; binary returns process exit code per ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope. | Direct sync call into Rust; same engine as SDK.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope                                                       |\n\nAsync invariants A–D from ADR-0.6.0-async-surface manifest in every binding. The invariants themselves are owned by that ADR; this file commits only the _binding-side_ assertion that no binding exposes an escape hatch:\n\n- **Invariant A (scheduler post-commit).** Cite ADR-async-surface § Decision. Bindings expose no escape hatch: bindings do not introduce additional locking around `write`, do not pre-dispatch scheduler work from the caller thread, and do not provide a \"skip-scheduler\" path.\n- **Invariant B (engine-owned embedder thread).** Cite ADR-async-surface § Decision. Bindings expose no escape hatch: embedder calls always run on the engine-owned embedder pool, never on the binding's caller thread (Python GIL-holder, TS libuv worker, CLI main thread).\n- **Invariant C (embedder-protocol no-reentrancy).** Cite ADR-async-surface + ADR-embedder-protocol. Bindings document this constraint in their `Embedder` impl docs; tests verify a buggy user-supplied embedder cannot deadlock the engine. Bindings expose no escape hatch (no \"reentrant embedder\" config flag).\n- **Invariant D (eager model warmup + per-call timeout).** Cite ADR-async-surface § Decision (eager warmup at `Engine.open`; per-call timeout default 30s). Bindings expose no escape hatch: no cold-load path, no per-call-timeout override that disables the watchdog. Reporting of model-load duration is part of `Engine.open`'s structured result, the shape of which is owned by `design/engine.md`.\n\n## 3. Error-mapping protocol\n\nThe Rust error variant set is owned by **`ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy`** (BEHAVIOR commitment). The variant→class mapping matrix (Rust variant → Python exception → TS error class) is owned by **`design/errors.md`** per architecture.md § 2 (which assigns the `errors` module to that file). This file commits only the _protocol_ claims that the mapping must satisfy.\n\nProtocol commitments (apply to every binding):\n\n- **One concrete class per variant.** Never a generic `EngineError` with a `kind` discriminator string. Tests dispatch on `except <Specific>` (Python) and `instanceof <Specific>` (TS) without inspecting messages. (REQ-056; AC-060a.)\n- **Single rooted hierarchy.** Every binding's exception/error hierarchy roots at one base class (Python: `fathomdb.EngineError`; TS: `FathomDbError`) so callers can catch broad failures while still narrowing per variant.\n- **Typed attributes, not stringified payloads.** Variant fields surface as typed attributes on the exception/error object, not as `args[0]` tuples or stringified blobs. The exact attribute spelling (idiomatic per binding) is owned by `interfaces/{python,ts}.md`; this file commits that the attribute exists and is structurally typed.\n- **Stable dispatch key for `CorruptionError`.** `recovery_hint.code` (idiomatic casing per binding) is the stable dispatch key per ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior § 2; `recovery_hint.doc_anchor` is the doc pointer. Bindings do not flatten or stringify `recovery_hint`.\n- **CLI is structural.** Each variant maps to a stable exit code + a structured stderr line carrying the variant's stable code (e.g. `recovery_hint.code` for `Corruption`). The exit-code table itself lives in `interfaces/cli.md`; the variant→exit-code authority is in `design/errors.md`.\n- **Top-level Rust error type plurality.** The Rust surface exposes more than one top-level error type (`EngineError` for runtime ops, `EngineOpenError` for `Engine.open` per ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior). `design/errors.md` enumerates which top-level Rust types exist and how each variant routes to a binding class; Python and TypeScript expose one catch-all base while preserving distinct leaf classes.\n\nDecision note:\n\n- 0.6.0 uses one exported catch-all base type across Python and TypeScript.\n- TypeScript's catch-all base is `FathomDbError`; every concrete leaf class in\n  `design/errors.md` extends it.\n\nThis file does NOT enumerate the variant→class matrix; that authority lives in `design/errors.md` and is cited by `interfaces/{python,ts,cli}.md`. Per-language interface files do not duplicate the matrix.\n\n## 4. Marshalling strategy\n\n`PreparedWrite` (per ADR-0.6.0-prepared-write-shape) is the only typed shape the engine accepts. Bindings construct `Vec<PreparedWrite>` from idiomatic per-language inputs and never touch raw SQL.\n\n| Direction                                                                                  | Boundary                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Owning ADR                                                        |\n| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| Caller → engine: `PreparedWrite::Node` / `Edge` / `OpStore(OpStoreInsert)` / `AdminSchema` | Per-binding builder constructs the variant. No raw SQL accepted.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | ADR-0.6.0-typed-write-boundary; ADR-0.6.0-prepared-write-shape    |\n| Caller → engine: vector input                                                              | Bindings accept idiomatic typed arrays. When the input is already LE-f32 contiguous (NumPy `ndarray[float32]`, TS `Float32Array`), the binding zerocopy-casts to `&[u8]` per ADR-0.6.0-zerocopy-blob (no copy). Non-contiguous inputs (e.g. Python `list[float]`) are converted once into an LE-f32 buffer at the binding boundary. | ADR-0.6.0-zerocopy-blob; ADR-0.6.0-vector-identity-embedder-owned |\n| Caller → engine: op-store payload                                                          | Bindings accept idiomatic dict / object; bindings serialize to JSON; engine validates against `schema_id` save-time before commit.                                                                                                                                                                                                  | ADR-0.6.0-json-schema-policy; AC-060b                             |\n| Engine → caller: search results                                                            | Engine returns owned typed result rows; bindings expose them as idiomatic types (Python `list[dict]`/dataclass; TS object). No lazy cursors crossing the FFI boundary.                                                                                                                                                              | ADR-0.6.0-retrieval-pipeline-shape                                |\n| Engine → caller: errors                                                                    | Per § 3.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | ADR-0.6.0-error-taxonomy                                          |\n\nJSON-Schema validation behavior (AC-060b) is invariant across bindings:\nvalidation fires save-time, pre-commit, on the writer thread; failure surfaces\nas `SchemaValidationError`; no open-time re-validation; bindings do _not_ run\ntheir own pre-engine validation pass (single source of truth).\n\n## 5. Embedder identity invariant\n\nPer ADR-0.6.0-vector-identity-embedder-owned + ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol, vector identity is owned by the embedder, not by per-DB config. Cross-binding consequence:\n\n- A DB written by Python with embedder identity `E` and re-opened by TypeScript MUST resolve the same `EmbedderIdentity`. Bindings do not pin or override identity at open.\n- Identity-mismatch surfaces uniformly as `EmbedderIdentityMismatch` (per § 3 mapping). Bindings do not auto-rebuild on mismatch (consistent with ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior's no-auto-recover posture for open failures).\n- A user-supplied embedder (Python or TS impl) declares its `EmbedderIdentity` per ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol Invariants 1–4. Binding-side bindings do _not_ synthesize identity from binding metadata; identity is the embedder's responsibility.\n- REQ-047 (embedder version-skew detection) is satisfied at link/resolution time by the semver-pinned `fathomdb-embedder-api` trait crate (architecture.md § 1) plus the embedder-owned `EmbedderIdentity` carried in stored vectors. Bindings do NOT perform runtime version-skew checks; they consume the typed mismatch error surfaced by the engine.\n\n## 6. SDK symmetry, CLI boundary, and config classes\n\nTwo different symmetry rules exist and must not be conflated:\n\n- **SDK surface symmetry.** Python and TypeScript expose the same canonical SDK\n  verb set. This is the hard parity rule from § 1.\n- **Engine-config symmetry.** Engine-owned knobs named in `design/engine.md`\n  must be reachable from every SDK binding in idiomatic form.\n\nCLI is outside both parity claims. It is a separate operator surface and does\nnot promise flag-for-flag mirroring of SDK verbs or SDK config.\n\nThe engine-config symmetry rule applies only to knobs that change engine\nbehavior after the binding boundary. It does **not** apply to binding-runtime\nmechanics that exist only to bridge one host runtime into the engine.\n\nExamples:\n\n- `embedder_pool_size` — engine-owned runtime knob; symmetry required across\n  SDK bindings.\n- `scheduler_runtime_threads` — engine-owned runtime knob; symmetry required\n  across SDK bindings.\n- Python `run_in_executor` usage — caller-side runtime pattern, not an engine\n  knob.\n- TypeScript `ThreadsafeFunction` pool sizing — TS binding runtime mechanic per\n  ADR-0.6.0-async-surface. A TS binding may surface it near `Engine.open`, but\n  it is not part of the canonical engine-config set and does not create a\n  Python-parity obligation.\n\nCLI inherits engine defaults unless `interfaces/cli.md` explicitly grants a\nflag. That inheritance posture does not weaken the SDK symmetry rule because\nCLI is not an SDK binding.\n\n## 7. Lock + process-exclusivity contract\n\nPer `architecture.md` § 5 + ADR-0.6.0-database-lock-mechanism-reader-pool-revision (#31, supersedes #30), only one `Engine` instance per database file may be open at a time. The lock mechanism is the sidecar `{database_path}.lock` flock (Rust std `File::try_lock`, per-OFD exclusion semantics). Sidecar = pre-open fail-fast + operator-diagnostic PID + load-bearing layer for both cross-process and same-process two-`Engine` exclusion. The writer connection runs WAL with default (NORMAL) locking_mode; `PRAGMA locking_mode=EXCLUSIVE` is NOT applied — it is incompatible with the Phase 8 reader pool because EXCLUSIVE causes SQLite to skip the WAL shared-memory wal-index. Reader connections use default (NORMAL) locking_mode and benefit from WAL multi-reader concurrency (REQ-018). `-shm` is created as a normal WAL artifact.\n\nCross-binding consequences:\n\n- Python in process A holding `Engine.open(path)` → TS in process B calling `Engine.open(path)` MUST fail with `DatabaseLocked { holder_pid }` regardless of binding identity. The sidecar flock is the load-bearing layer for cross-process exclusion; it surfaces BEFORE SQLite I/O begins, so Engine.open does not pay migration / embedder warmup cost on a doomed open.\n- The lock's lifetime is bound to the `Engine` instance; closing or dropping the engine releases it (drops the sidecar lock fd + closes SQLite connections). `Engine.close` is required (REQ-020a; AC-022a).\n- Same-process two-`Engine` (Python `Engine` and TS `Engine` in the _same_ process targeting the _same_ path) is also forbidden. The second `Engine.open` opens a NEW `File` handle for the sidecar; per-OFD `flock` semantics return `WouldBlock`, surfacing as `DatabaseLocked`. Bindings do NOT maintain an in-process registry of held paths.\n- Path canonicalization: `Engine.open` canonicalizes the parent directory and appends the leaf filename before deriving `{...}.lock` path, defeating symlink + bind-mount aliasing. Bindings do not perform their own canonicalization.\n\nThe corruption-on-open path (ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior § 5; AC-035c) MUST release the sidecar lock + close any opened SQLite connection before returning `CorruptionError`. Bindings inherit this guarantee; no binding-level workaround is permitted.\n\n## 8. Logging / tracing subscriber attachment\n\nThe engine emits structured tracing events (per `dev/design-logging-and-tracing.md` Tier 1/2 carryovers). Binding adapters attach a host subscriber:\n\n- Python: caller registers a `logging`-backed adapter via a binding-provided helper that maps tracing events into Python `LogRecord`s.\n- TypeScript: caller registers a callback invoked per event.\n- CLI: when run in human-facing mode, attaches a console subscriber; when run in machine-facing `--json` mode, emits the verb-owned JSON shape from `interfaces/cli.md` / `design/recovery.md`. `doctor check-integrity` is a single JSON object; other verbs own their own machine-readable contract.\n\nAcross bindings, the _engine event payload_ is wire-stable: same field names,\nsame types, same lifecycle phase tag enum (AC-001). The host adapter MAY\ntranslate or derive **host-native required fields** from the engine event when\nthe host logger backend requires them. Engine event fields appear under a\nstable `fathomdb` payload key in the host record so downstream filters can\ntarget them precisely; the engine never emits a host-named field directly.\n\n### `fathomdb` payload envelope\n\nWithin the host record, the stable `fathomdb` payload key carries one of these\nsurface shapes:\n\n- response-cycle event: `phase` plus producer-owned operation context\n- diagnostic event: `source`, `category`, and producer-owned detail payload\n- counter snapshot: `counter_snapshot`\n- profile record: `profile_record`\n- stress-failure payload: `stress_failure`\n- migration step event: `migration_step`\n\nOwnership split:\n\n- `design/lifecycle.md` owns `phase`, `source`, `category`,\n  `counter_snapshot`, `profile_record`, and `stress_failure`\n- `design/migrations.md` owns `migration_step`\n- binding adapters own how these are embedded into host-native logger records\n\nAcceptance note: current ACs lock the typed payload members more directly than\nthe outer `fathomdb` envelope key. Do not rename the envelope key without\nupdating both this file and the interface docs.\n\nDefault subscriber posture: when no subscriber is registered, the engine writes nothing — no log files, no stderr noise (AC-002).\n\n## 9. Build / packaging strategy\n\nBinding-shipping protocol — the canonical build entry point per binding. (CI smoke gates and post-publish verification are owned by `design/release.md` per architecture.md § 2; this file commits only the per-binding build path.)\n\n| Binding    | Build path                                                                                | Authoritative source                                       |\n| ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |\n| Python     | `pip install -e src/python/` (PyO3 cdylib package `fathomdb` under `src/python/`)         | architecture.md § 1; memory `feedback_python_native_build` |\n| TypeScript | `npm install` against `src/ts/` (napi-rs cdylib package `fathomdb` under `src/ts/`)       | architecture.md § 1; ADR-0.6.0-typescript-api-shape        |\n| CLI        | `cargo build --release -p fathomdb-cli`; release artifact is a single binary per platform | ADR-0.6.0-cli-scope; ADR-0.6.0-tier1-ci-platforms          |\n\nCI build, per-binding smoke testing, and post-publish verification (memory `feedback_release_verification`; AC-056) are owned by `design/release.md`. Developer ergonomics (e.g. \"do not manually `cargo build && cp`\") are out of scope here and live in `src/python/README.md` / contributor docs.\n\n## 10. Recovery surface unreachability\n\nThe bindings collectively MUST NOT expose any recovery verb on the SDK surface. (REQ-037, REQ-054, REQ-031d; gated by AC-057a + AC-035d.) Recovery is invoked exclusively via the CLI binary `fathomdb recover` and `fathomdb doctor <verb>`. The application SDK contains no path that mutates a corruption-marked database.\n\nThis is a _non-presence_ claim, not a per-binding signature, and is therefore owned here rather than in `interfaces/{python,ts}.md`.\n\n## 11. Failure modes + recovery\n\n| Failure mode                            | Surfacing                                                                                                          | Bindings contract                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |\n| --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| Lock contention on open                 | `DatabaseLocked` per § 3 + § 7                                                                                     | Every binding maps identically; no retry loop in the binding.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |\n| Corruption on open                      | `CorruptionError` carrying `detail` per § 3 + ADR-0.6.0-corruption-open-behavior                                   | Every binding surfaces `detail` as a structurally-typed object (not a stringified blob) exposing `recovery_hint.code` (idiomatic casing per binding) as the stable dispatch key, per ADR-corruption-open-behavior § 2. Exact attribute spelling owned by `interfaces/{python,ts}.md`. Bindings do NOT auto-invoke recovery. |\n| Embedder identity mismatch              | `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError` per § 3                                                                            | Bindings do NOT auto-rebuild and 0.6.0 provides no open-time bypass.
