   263	# --- determinism ------------------------------------------------------------ #
   264	def test_determinism_same_input_same_result() -> None:
   265	    a = decide_083(_all(), eu7_recall=0.92, latency_ok=True)
   266	    b = decide_083(_all(), eu7_recall=0.92, latency_ok=True)
   267	    assert a == b
   268	
   269	
   270	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   271	# Probe 15a — embedder-ceiling (PRIMARY). Clears iff beats CLS-corrected
   272	# bge-small on BOTH eu8 and the hard subset by a CI-lower-> 0 margin, is
   273	# CPU-feasible, AND is 1-bit-survivable (projected eu7 >= 0.90).
   274	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   275	def _cand_15a(**overrides: float) -> dict[str, float]:
   276	    cand = {
   277	        "eu8": 0.60,
   278	        "hard": 0.50,
   279	        "eu8_margin_ci_lo": 0.01,
   280	        "hard_margin_ci_lo": 0.01,
   281	        "cpu_feasible": True,
   282	        "projected_eu7": 0.91,
   283	    }
   284	    cand.update(overrides)
   285	    return cand
   286	
   287	
   288	_BASE_15A = {"eu8": 0.571, "hard": 0.45}
   289	
   290	
   291	def test_probe_15a_all_criteria_pass() -> None:
   292	    assert probe_15a_pass(_cand_15a(), _BASE_15A) is True
   293	
   294	
   295	def test_probe_15a_fails_when_eu8_margin_ci_not_above_zero() -> None:
   296	    assert probe_15a_pass(_cand_15a(eu8_margin_ci_lo=0.0), _BASE_15A) is False
   297	
   298	
   299	def test_probe_15a_fails_when_hard_margin_ci_not_above_zero() -> None:
   300	    assert probe_15a_pass(_cand_15a(hard_margin_ci_lo=-0.01), _BASE_15A) is False
   301	
   302	
   303	def test_probe_15a_point_tie_with_positive_margin_ci_passes() -> None:
   304	    # Frozen design §5 gates 15a on the paired margin CI lower bound > 0 on BOTH
   305	    # eu8 and the hard subset (plus cpu_feasible + 1-bit-survivable) — NOT on a raw
   306	    # candidate-point > base comparison. A candidate whose point estimate TIES the
   307	    # base (c_eu8 == base.eu8, c_hard == base.hard) but whose margin CI lower bounds
   308	    # are both > 0 must CLEAR (the unregistered raw-point check is removed; the old
   309	    # code returned False here = a rounding-induced false negative).
   310	    cand = _cand_15a(eu8=_BASE_15A["eu8"], hard=_BASE_15A["hard"])
   311	    assert probe_15a_pass(cand, _BASE_15A) is True
   312	
   313	
   314	def test_probe_15a_fails_when_not_cpu_feasible() -> None:
   315	    assert probe_15a_pass(_cand_15a(cpu_feasible=False), _BASE_15A) is False
   316	
   317	
   318	def test_probe_15a_fails_when_not_one_bit_survivable() -> None:

   321	
   322	def test_probe_15a_projected_eu7_exactly_floor_survives() -> None:

    80	   re-derivation + full re-embed) — `ADR-0.6.0-vector-identity-embedder-owned.md`.
    81	5. **>512-token context** is the lever most directly targeting the long-transcript
    82	   truncation finding — *if* candle-compatible.
    83	
    84	## §1 — Is bge-small at its limit? No.
    85	
    86	- bge-small-en-v1.5: 33M params, 384-d, **512-token** max, CLS-pooled, MIT; MTEB
    87	  Retrieval ~51.7 (v1, https://huggingface.co/BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5). Sept 2023.
    88	- Short-context (512-tok) dense retrievers lose the most on **long documents**;
    89	  truncation degradation scales with the fraction of relevant content past the
    90	  cutoff (LongEmbed, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2404.12096; Late Chunking,
    91	  https://arxiv.org/html/2409.04701). BGE's own guidance: for short-query→long-doc,
    92	  add the query instruction; for long inputs use **BGE-M3** (8192 tok), not
    93	  bge-small (https://huggingface.co/BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5).
    94	- **Verdict:** median-rank-99 on long-transcript summary queries is far below
    95	  bge-small's documented ceiling and points to **mean-pool mismatch + truncation**,
    96	  not the model. (No bge-small-specific "summary-query" ablation was found; the
    97	  long-doc degradation evidence is for the short-context class generally.)
    98	
    99	## §2 — Candle compatibility (the binding filter) — verified from candle source
   100	
   101	`candle-transformers/src/models/mod.rs` (main, June 2026) has dedicated modules for:
   102	`bert`, `modernbert`, `xlm_roberta`, `jina_bert`, `nomic_bert`, `distilbert`,
   103	`stella_en_v5`, `nvembed_v2`
   104	(https://github.com/huggingface/candle/blob/main/candle-transformers/src/models/mod.rs).
   105	Runnable embedding **examples** exist for bert, modernbert, nomic-bert, jina-bert,
   106	stella, gte-qwen, nvembed.
   107	
   108	| Architecture | candle-native? | Affected models |
   109	|---|---|---|
   110	| BERT | ✅ (canonical) | bge-*, gte-small, arctic-embed-s/m/m-v1.5, e5-*, mxbai |
   111	| ModernBERT | ✅ module+example | modernbert-embed-base, **gte-modernbert-base** |
   112	| nomic-bert (RoPE/SwiGLU) | ✅ module+example (targets nomic-embed-v1.5) | nomic-embed-text-v1.5 |
   113	| JinaBERT (ALiBi) | ✅ module+example | jina-embeddings-v2-small/base |
   114	| XLM-RoBERTa | ✅ module (no example) | multilingual-e5, arctic-embed-l-v2.0 (long-ctx XLM-R — verify) |
   115	| **GTE "transformer++" encoder** (RoPE+GLU) | ❌ **no module** | **gte-base-en-v1.5, arctic-embed-m-v2.0** → custom code needed |
   116	
   117	So **gte-base-en-v1.5** and **arctic-embed-m-v2.0** are effectively out (no candle
   118	GTE encoder); **gte-modernbert-base** is in (it's ModernBERT, not the gte encoder).
   119	
   120	## §3 — Binary-quantization robustness (corroborates the e5 rejection)
   121	
   122	Per HF "Binary and Scalar Embedding Quantization" (Mar 2024,
   123	https://huggingface.co/blog/embedding-quantization), % of fp32 retrieval retained
   124	under **1-bit binary**:
   125	
   126	| model | binary retention |
   127	|---|---|
   128	| mxbai-embed-large-v1 | 96.45% |
   129	| Cohere embed-v3 | 94.6% |
   130	| all-MiniLM-L6-v2 | 93.79% |
   131	| nomic-embed-text-v1.5 | 87.7% |
   132	| **e5-base-v2** | **74.77%** (collapses) |
   155	| **bge-small-en-v1.5** (current) | 33M | 384 | 512 | BERT ✅ | MIT | ~51.7 | empirically yes |
   156	| arctic-embed-s | 33M | **384** | 512 | BERT ✅ | Apache | **51.98** | unverified (e5-derived) |
   157	| gte-small | 33M | **384** | 512 | BERT ✅ | MIT | 49.46 | unverified |
   158	| **nomic-embed-text-v1.5** | 137M | 768 → **256/512** MRL | **8192** | nomic-bert ✅ | Apache | 62.28@768 / 61.04@256 | **87.7%** |
   159	| modernbert-embed-base | 149M | 768 → **256** MRL | **8192** | ModernBERT ✅ | Apache | ~53 (unconf.) | MRL ⇒ likely |
   160	| gte-modernbert-base | 149M | 768 | **8192** | ModernBERT ✅ | Apache | **55.33** | no MRL ⇒ unverified |
   161	| jina-embeddings-v2-small-en | 33M | 512 | **8192** (ALiBi) | jina_bert ✅ | Apache | unconf. | unverified |
   162	| mxbai-embed-large-v1 | 335M | 1024 → MRL | 512 | BERT-large ✅ | Apache | 54.39 | **96.45%** (best) |
   163	| bge-base-en-v1.5 | 109M | 768 | 512 | BERT ✅ | MIT | 53.25 | likely (bge family) |
   164	
   165	Excluded: **gte-base-en-v1.5**, **arctic-embed-m-v2.0** (no candle GTE encoder);
   166	**jina-embeddings-v3** (CC-BY-NC, non-commercial — license-blocked); static
   167	model2vec/potion (MIT, ~30 MB, but retrieval only ~82% of all-MiniLM and you'd hand-
   168	write the EmbeddingBag forward — a fast-first-stage option, not a quality upgrade).
   169	
   170	Note: drop-in 384-d swaps (arctic-embed-s, gte-small) are **marginal** over a
   171	*correctly-pooled* bge-small and are not MRL (binary risk). The real upgrades are the
   172	**MRL + long-context** models, which require a dimension/schema migration.
   263	Ran the full-corpus dense diagnostic with `nomic-embed-text-v1.5` (768-d, candle
   264	`nomic_bert`, `search_document:`/`search_query:` prefixes) vs the bge-mean baseline,
   265	same corpus + same 128/96 geometry.
   266	
   267	| class | metric | bge mean | nomic |
   268	|---|---|---|---|
   269	| exact_fact | median / top-50 | 2 / 78% | **1 / 84%** (+6) |
   270	| exploratory | median rank | 99 | **135** (worse) |
   271	| exploratory | top-50 | 37% | **32%** (worse) |
   272	| exploratory | semantic / hard | 142 / 596 | **76 / 662** (worse) |
   273	
   274	Speed (measured, same chunks): nomic ~310 ms/passage @ ~245% CPU vs bge ~225 ms @
   275	~150% — **~1.4× wall-clock, ~2.2× CPU**, ~4× weights on disk (522 MB vs 133 MB).
   276	
   277	**Conclusions:**
   278	1. **Stronger ≠ better here.** Nomic (MTEB retrieval 62.28 vs bge 51.68) is clearly
   279	   better on **exact_fact** (median rank 1, +6 pts top-50) but **worse on
   280	   exploratory** (median 99→135). So the exploratory weakness is **not** a
   281	   model-capacity problem a bigger model fixes.
   282	2. **The exact_fact win is moot for the hybrid:** exact_fact is already lexically
   283	   solved (BM25 median rank 1); a better vector arm there adds nothing the fusion
   284	   needs.
   285	3. **Most likely it's a GRANULARITY / task-structure problem, not a model one.** The
   286	   diagnostic embeds ~128-word chunks, which (a) **neutralizes nomic's headline
   287	   advantage** — its 8192-token long context is wasted on 128-word windows — and
   288	   (b) is hostile to discourse/summary queries, whose answer spans the *whole
   289	   discussion*, not a single chunk. Max-pool over short chunks can't represent
   290	   "what did they decide about X" over a meeting transcript. Both models hit this
   291	   wall; the lexical (BM25) arm remains the best exploratory component (median rank
   292	   26 vs dense 99–135).
   293	4. **Decision:** nomic is **not** worth ~2× compute / ~4× model size — it doesn't
   294	   move the class we care about (exploratory) and only helps an already-solved one.
   295	   Stay on bge-small (Mean pooling); for exploratory, lean on the lexical arm.

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba dev/adr/ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md | sed -n '140,180p'; nl -ba dev/adr/ADR-0.6.0-default-embedder.md | sed -n '160,192p'" in /tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da
 succeeded in 0ms:
   140	   GATED on the real-embedder eu7 VECTOR STAGE, not the synthetic
   141	   ac_013b, and not the RRF-fused `search()` output.** The 0.90
   142	   floor and the `AC013B_RECALL_FLOOR` constant are unchanged (no
   143	   numeric change); what changes is *which test, and which
   144	   system-under-test, enforces it*. The asserting recall verdict
   145	   moved from the synthetic `perf_gates::ac_013b_recall_at_10_floor`
   146	   to the real-embedder `eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs`, which now
   147	   hard-asserts recall@10 ≥ 0.90 on real bge-small (measured 0.937).
   148	   **Why the vector stage, not `search()`:** Slice 10 (`d28d204`)
   149	   made `search()` an unconditional RRF fusion of the vector and
   150	   FTS5 branches. eu7's ground truth is the exact-f32 *vector*
   151	   top-10, so comparing it against the *hybrid* `search()` output
   152	   conflates ANN-quantization FIDELITY (what this floor is defined
   153	   to measure) with intended fusion divergence — that conflation
   154	   produced the Slice-40 Phase-A HALT (recall 0.8710 < 0.90), which
   155	   GA-1 root-caused to fusion divergence on a byte-identical corpus,
   156	   NOT a quantization regression (`dev/plans/runs/GA-1-corpus-ab-20260608T012503Z.md`).
   157	   The HITL ◆ B-1 ruling (Option 1, 2026-06-08) corrected eu7 to
   158	   measure the pre-fusion vector branch in isolation via a test-only
   159	   engine seam (`set_vector_stage_only_for_test`); the fused-`search()`
   160	   recall (~0.871) is now a report-only delta (`EU7_RECALL_FUSED`).
   161	   The synthetic `ac_013b` is demoted to REPORT-ONLY: its isotropic
   162	   `VaryingEmbedder` measures a quantization-FIDELITY number
   163	   (~0.73–0.89, the noise-limited worst case for sign-bit ANN) that
   164	   does not — and was never meant to — clear a 0.90 *product* floor,
   165	   so asserting it on that fixture was a mis-calibration. The fast
   166	   sentinel `ac_013b_floor_matches_adr` still pins the 0.90 constant
   167	   to this ADR. HITL-approved 2026-06-06 / ◆ B-1 2026-06-08; AC-075
   168	   minted at the 0.8.0 GA gated slice (`dev/acceptance.md` § AC-075).
   169	   The 0.7.2 validation below remains the evidentiary basis for the
   170	   number. This FIDELITY gate is complementary to, not a substitute
   171	   for, the IR/relevance axis (eu8 / IR-1).
   172	
   173	   **0.8.0 GA-3 amendment (◆ HITL ruling 2026-06-08) — the eu7
   174	   AC-075 assert is now a ONE-SIDED, CI-based gate; the 0.90 floor
   175	   CONSTANT is UNCHANGED.** The required real 0.8.0 re-measure came
   176	   back at vector-stage recall@10 = **0.896** (CI [0.864, 0.925],
   177	   σ 0.0157) @ N=7667 — the 0.937 above was a 0.7.x-inferred number,
   178	   so this is a real ~4pt 0.7.x→0.8.0 ANN-fidelity drop, NOT a
   179	   measurement artifact (`dev/plans/runs/GA-signoff-eu7-remeasure-20260608T172804Z.json`).
   180	   The point-estimate hard-assert PANICKED at 0.896. HITL accepted
   160	
   161	**Chosen configuration:**
   162	
   163	| Knob                   | Value                                |
   164	|------------------------|--------------------------------------|
   165	| Model                  | `BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5`             |
   166	| Dimension              | 384                                  |
   167	| Pooling                | mean-pool over attention mask (EMB-1) |
   168	| Post-pool norm         | L2 (EMB-2)                           |
   169	| Rerank K (sign-bit → f32) | **192** (revised 2026-05-29 from K=128; see research §5.4) |
   170	| Mean-centering         | **ON** — subtract corpus-mean f32 vector before sign-quantize, at both write and query |
   171	
   172	**New architectural element introduced by 0.7.1 (mean-centering).** The
   173	corpus-mean f32 vector (1536 bytes at 384d) is a per-workspace constant
   174	stored alongside the index in `_fathomdb_embedder_profiles`. It is
   175	subtracted from each vector before the sign-bit quantization step on both
   176	the write path (`run_projection_job`) and the query path (synchronous
   177	caller-thread embed). The f32 rerank uses the un-centered f32 vector — the
   178	centering is purely a sign-bit-quantization bias-correction, not a change
   179	to the geometry of the f32 space. Recomputation policy and persistence
   180	schema are an EU-2 design decision (forward-cited). The pin threshold is
   181	locked at `MEAN_VEC_PIN_THRESHOLD = 256` in EU-2, with the statistical
   182	and operational justification (per-axis sample SE σ/16 ≈ ±6% at N=256,
   183	diminishing returns past N=512, workload-fit against agentic-memory
   184	workspace sizes, topic-drift caveat) recorded in
   185	`dev/design/embedder.md` §0.3.
   186	
   187	**Why this model + K + mc combination.** Empirically (N=100 synthetic
   188	queries against the 7,667-doc 0.7.0 corpus): bge-small + mc at K=192 reaches
   189	recall@10 = **0.933** (95% CI 0.912–0.953; lower CI bound clears the 0.90
   190	floor); bge-base at K=96 reaches 0.914 at ~2× storage and ~3× per-query
   191	embed latency. CIs overlap on point estimate but the bge-small + K=192
   192	path delivers ~0.033 cushion above the floor at materially lower cost.

codex
**Findings**

- **Q1 eu8 definition: OK.** The design’s strict all-of doc-id recall@10, averaged outside `negative`, matches the eu8/doc-id reduction of Evidence Recall. `required_doc_ids()` falls back to `expected_top_k_doc_ids` when no evidence labels exist, and `evidence_recall_at_k()` is strict all-of over top-K: [ir_eval.rs:335](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/support/ir_eval.rs:335), [ir_eval.rs:385](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/support/ir_eval.rs:385). Negative queries are routed out of recall aggregation: [ir_eval.rs:493](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/support/ir_eval.rs:493). Design lines match: [0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:99](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:99).

- **Q2 HARD subset: CONCERN.** The model-independence principle is right: using only `bm25_rank is None or > cap` avoids the circular dense-rank branch in `bucket()` ([ir_c_gold_diagnostics.rs:198](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/ir_c_gold_diagnostics.rs:198), design [line 106](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:106)). But the design is loose enough to drift: it permits “deterministic rank-BM25 over the same tokenization” as an alternative to FTS5 `bm25()` ([line 110](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:110)), then admits tokenizer mismatch risk ([line 225](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:225)). Also, “gold doc” is singular ([line 107](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:107)) while eu8 qrels are plural; if any query has multiple required docs, best-gold-doc BM25 rank can mark a query lexical even when BM25 misses other required docs. The “actual count governs” rule is honest, but not sufficient unless the cap, tokenizer, and multi-doc reduction are pinned.

- **Q3 projected_eu7: CONCERN.** The SUT/GT shape is correct: 1-bit sign quant, Hamming K=192, f32 rerank, recall@10 against the same model’s exact-f32 vector top-10 matches eu7’s fidelity axis ([eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs:436](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs:436), [ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md:148](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/adr/ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md:148)). Per-candidate measurement is necessary because the ground truth is model-owned. The concern: the design calls it “exactly as `eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs`” while gating a point `projected_eu7 >= 0.90` ([design line 131](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:131)); eu7 AC-075 is now CI-based, not point-estimate based ([ADR line 173](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/adr/ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md:173)). The frozen probe rule does use a point float, so this is a wording/methodology caveat, not a consume-rule violation.

- **Q4 paired bootstrap gate: OK.** The gated quantity is the right one: paired per-query `cand_hit - base_hit`, resample query indices, mean delta, percentile 95% CI lower bound ([design line 141](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:141)). It matches `probe_15a_pass`, which gates only `eu8_margin_ci_lo > 0` and `hard_margin_ci_lo > 0` plus CPU/eu7 ([decision_rule_083.py:202](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/src/python/eval/decision_rule_083.py:202)). Raw-point gating is explicitly removed in both code comments and tests: [decision_rule_083.py:221](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/src/python/eval/decision_rule_083.py:221), [test_decision_rule_083.py:303](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/src/python/tests/test_decision_rule_083.py:303).

- **Q5 pooling/prefix table: CONCERN.** BGE CLS + query instruction and E5 mean + `query:`/`passage:` are defensible. Nomic prefixes match the stated task-prefix convention. The weak point is `gte-base-en-v1.5`: the design treats it as CPU/ONNX-class and possibly CLS-pooled ([line 71](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:71)), but repo research says this exact GTE v1.5 architecture lacks a candle-native module and was previously excluded as requiring custom code: [IR-C-embedder-options-research.md:115](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/notes/IR-C-embedder-options-research.md:115), [line 165](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/notes/IR-C-embedder-options-research.md:165). If GTE wins the Python probe but cannot become the Slice-20 in-library embedder without new model work, the probe has picked an infeasible candidate.

- **Q6 misleading verdict / discipline: CONCERN.** Three concrete false-verdict paths remain. First, `cpu_feasible` is a frozen gate input but the design gives only “AC012/AC013 ballpark,” not a numeric threshold ([line 137](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:137)); that makes a pass/fail input judgment-call-shaped. Second, the design claims offline while allowing first-run HF downloads ([line 168](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:168)); that is $0 but not strictly offline/deterministic. Third, byte-identical torch CPU vectors are asserted ([line 164](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:164)) without a fully pinned runtime/BLAS/threading lock. Consume-don’t-redefine discipline itself is good: the design repeatedly says to call `probe_15a_pass` and not touch the frozen module ([line 47](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:47), [line 186](/tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da/dev/design/0.8.3-slice-15a-embedder-probe.md:186)).
